description: Description This indication is invoked a specific number of FlexRay cycles before the NM will go into state BusSleepMode. It is also called in case that an indication priorly given is not valid any more (e.g. due to a node which keeps the bus awake now).
parameters: Parameters valid 0 : A priorly given indication is not valid any more 1 : Indication that NM state BusSleepMode will be entered in a specific number of FlexRay cycles deltaCycles Number of FlexRay cycles until the NM state BusSleepMode will be entered (equal to parameter ReadyPreSleepCnt in INI file) absCycle FlexRay cycle at which the NM state BusSleepMode will be entered
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmCarWakeUpIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_CarWakeUpInd( dword slotId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM PDU that has the car wake-up bit set. Is only called if NM_CAR_WAKEUP_RX_ENABLED=1.
parameters: Parameters slotId The slot ID of the message. identifier The NM source identifier in the NM PDU or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 if the Repeat-Message bit is set, 0 otherwise. data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Start up the node when the car wake-up bit is received void Fr Nm_CarWakeUpInd( dword slotId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[]) { Write( " Fr Nm_CarWakeUpInd: from node %d", identifier ); Fr Nm_NetworkRequest(); }

name: NmCheckRemoteSleepIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_CheckRemoteSleepInd( )
description: Description Check if remote sleep (all nodes except this one allow transition to Bus-Sleep) has been detected, i.e. this node is the last one keeping the bus awake.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 remote sleep is indicated 0 remote sleep is not indicated

name: NmConfigureILNotificationsFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_ConfigureILNotifications(dword direction, dword mode)
description: Description This function influences the coupling between NM and IL. The coupling normally takes care of forwarding certain events automatically. Implementing CAPL callback functions of the IL and NM may break open this coupling.
parameters: Parameters direction specifies which events are affected 0 : Events from NM to IL (e.g. network mode indication) 1 : Events from IL to NM (e.g. network request due to clamp 15) mode specifies the according effect, the value is interpreted bitwise Bit 0 0 : Open – events are not propagated automatically 1 : Fixed – events are propagated automatically even in case of according CAPL callback functions (does not work for direction = 1)
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong parameter values or illegal combination)

name: NmControlBitVectorIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_ControlBitVectorInd(dword sourceId, dword controlBits)
description: Description This function is called if a NM dataPDu with a non-zero Control-Bit-Vector is received. In addition to the vector itself, the identifier of the sender node is given.
parameters: Parameters sourceId The NM source identifier in the NM PDU or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. controlBits The Control Bit Vector received.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Print the identifier of the sending node in the Write Window. void Fr Nm_ControlBitVectorInd( dword sourceId, dword controlBits) { Write( " Fr Nm_ControlBitVectorInd: from node %d, bits %02x" , sourceId, controlBits); }

name: NmCtrlSimulationOffFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_CtrlSimulationOff( )
description: Description This function will turn off the complete NM simulation by the modeling library . While the NM simulation is off, any API call (except Fr Nm_CtrlSimulationOn() ) will not have any effect and the modeling library will not sent any NM PDU !
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmCtrlSimulationOnFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_CtrlSimulationOn( )
description: Description This function will turn on the complete NM simulation by the modeling library . While the NM simulation is off, any API call (except Fr Nm_CtrlSimulationOn() ) will not have any effect and the modeling library will not sent any NM PDU !
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmDisablePartialNetworkingFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_DisablePartialNetworking( )
description: Description Stop using partial networking for this node. All relevant parameters must be available ( INI Files Partial Network ) or the function call will fail. Please also consider the restrictions stated in that section when activating the PN feature from CAPL.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmDisablePassiveModeFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_DisablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Stop using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmEnablePartialNetworkingFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_EnablePartialNetworking( )
description: Description Start using partial networking for this node. All relevant parameters must be available ( INI Files Partial Network ) or the function call will fail. Please also consider the restrictions stated in that section when activating the PN feature from CAPL.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmEnablePassiveModeFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_EnablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Start using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 1 : OK 0 0 : not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmFIIgnoreRepeatMessageBitFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr NmFI_IgnoreRepeatMessageBit(long value)
description: Description Defines the reaction upon received repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters 0 normal behavior 1 repeat message bit will be ignored
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetCbvPositionFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetCbvPosition( )
description: Description Returns the byte position (zero-based numbering) of the Control Bit Vector (CBV) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain CBV. ≥ 0 Byte position of CBV within NM PDU.

name: NmGetControlBitVectorFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_GetControlBitVector(dword &controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits Reference parameter to read the CBV.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (Simulation is not enabled, or no CBV is used)
example: Example dword out; long ret; ret = Fr Nm_GetControlBitVector(out); if (ret == 1) { write("CBV value: %u", out); }

name: NmGetDLLInfoFR320
syntax: Function Syntax dword Fr Nm_GetDLLInfo(dword selector)
description: Description Return additional information about the modeling library .
parameters: Parameters selector Selects different return values (see below).
returnValues: Return Values selector = 0 The modeling library 's file version as BCD, e.g. 0x010203 for V1.2.3 selector = 1 The modeling library 's major version selector = 2 The modeling library 's minor version selector = 3 The modeling library 's build number

name: NmGetLocalNodeIdentifierFR320
syntax: Function Syntax dword Fr Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Return the NM identifier of this node.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Node identifier, range 0x00 – 0xFF

name: NmGetNodeIdentifierFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Get the NM identifier of the node that sent the last received NM message.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 .. 0xFF node identifier -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetNodeNameFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_GetNodeName(char bufferOut[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description The FIBEX database name of this node is copied into the provided buffer.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Output buffer for the node name. bufferSize Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetNodeStateFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetNodeState(dword nodeId)
description: Description Check if the node with the given NM identifier has sent a NM message the last time the network was in Repeat Message state. Precondition for retrieval of meaningful information in a node is a preceding call of the function Fr Nm_RepeatMessageRequest and the completion of the subsequent repeat message phase.
parameters: Parameters nodeId Target node ID, range 0x00 – 0xFF
returnValues: Return Values 2 information is not (yet) available (e.g. node detection not started or ongoing) 1 target node has been detected 0 target node has not been detected -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPduDataFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetPduData(BYTE pduOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the last received NM PDU completely into the buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPnAffiliationFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetPnAffiliation(long partialNetworkId)
description: Description When using partial networking, a node filters NM messages according to the request mask of the message and its own filter mask. Via this function it is possible to retrieve information about the partial networks contained in the filter mask.
parameters: Parameters partialNetworkId Specifies for which partial network the affiliation should be retrieved.
returnValues: Return Values 0 partial network not relevant for node 1 partial network relevant for node -1 error (e.g. illegal partial network, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPncBitVectorLengthFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetPncBitVectorLength( )
description: Description Returns the length (in bytes) of the Partial Network Cluster (PNC) bit vector.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Partial networking is not used. ≥ 0 Offset of the PNC bit vector (in bytes).

name: NmGetPncBitVectorOffsetFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetPncBitVectorOffset( )
description: Description Returns the offset (in bytes) of the Partial Network Cluster (PNC) bit vector within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Partial networking is not used. ≥ 0 Offset of the PNC bit vector (in bytes).

name: NmGetPnCurrentExternalRequestBitsFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetPnCurrentExternalRequestBits(long flags, byte bufferOut[], dword bufferSize, dword numberOfBitsOut []))
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about the external request status of all partial networks. Information about a single partial network can be retrieved via function Fr Nm_IsPnCurrentlyRequested .
parameters: Parameters flags 0 : local scope, i.e. consider only partial networks relevant for the node (result depends on filter mask) 1 : network wide scope bufferOut Byte array in which the request status of the partial networks will be written. bufferSize Size of the byte array in number of bytes. numberOfBitsOut Number of bits needed and/or written to the byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (e.g. partial networking not available) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPnCurrentInternalRequestBitsFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetPnCurrentInternalRequestBits(BYTE bufferOut[], dword bufferSize, dword numberOfBitsOut []))
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about the internal request status of all partial networks. Information about a single partial network can be retrieved via function Fr Nm_IsPnCurrentlyRequested .
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Byte array in which the request status of the partial networks will be written. bufferSize Size of the byte array in number of bytes. numberOfBitsOut Number of bits needed and/or written to the byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (e.g. partial networking not available) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPnRequestBitsDefaultFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetPnRequestBitsDefault( BYTE bufferOut[], dword bufferSize, dword pnRequestBitsLengthOut[])
description: Description The default partial network request bits (the PN info range ) of this node are copied into the provided buffer.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Output buffer for the PN request bits. bufferSize Size of the output buffer. pnRequestBitsLengthOut Number of bits in PN request mask.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (partial networking is either not available (i.e. some relevant parameters have not been configured) or the buffer has an insufficient size) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetSniPositionFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetSniPosition ( )
description: Description Returns the byte position PDU (zero-based numbering) of the Source Node Identifier (SNI) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain SNI. ≥ 0 Byte position of the SNI within NM PDU.

name: NmGetStateFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetState( )
description: Description The returned value indicates the NM state the node is currently in.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 NM_STATE_UNINIT 1 NM_STATE_BUS_SLEEP 2 NM_STATE_PREPARE_BUS_SLEEP (never reached on FlexRay!) 3 NM_STATE_READY_SLEEP 4 NM_STATE_NORMAL_OPERATION 5 NM_STATE_REPEAT_MESSAGE 6 NM_STATE_SYNCHRONIZE -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetUserDataFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetUserData(BYTE dataOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the user data of the last received NM PDU into the output buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmInhibitActiveWakeUpBitFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_InhibitActiveWakeUpBit (long value)
description: Description By default, the active wake-up bit in the Control Bit Vector of the NM PDU is set. This function allows to prevent this. Function can be used in on preStart.
parameters: Parameters value 0 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is set (default). 1 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is not set.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmIsPnCurrentlyRequestedFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_IsPnCurrentlyRequested(long partialNetworkId, long flags)
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about the status of a certain partial network. Thereby it is possible to differentiate between the scope of the node and the network scope.
parameters: Parameters partialNetworkId Specifies for which partial network the status information should be retrieved. flags 0 : local scope, i.e. consider only partial networks relevant for the node (result depends on filter mask) 1 : network wide scope
returnValues: Return Values -1 error (e.g. illegal partial network, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive) In all other cases the return value has to be interpreted bitwise. Bit 0 0 : current node does not request partial network itself 1 : current node requests partial network itself Bit 1 0 : other nodes do not request partial network 1 : at least one other node requests partial network

name: NmNetworkModeIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_NetworkModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicate that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Fr Nm_NetworkModeInd() { Write( "Nm_NetworkModeInd: Network Mode"); }

name: NmNetworkReleaseFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_NetworkRelease( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node doesn't need the network, and allows it to enter Bus-Sleep mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkRequestFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_NetworkRequest( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node needs the network.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkStartIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_NetworkStartInd( )
description: Description A NM PDU has been received while the node is in Bus-Sleep mode. Typically Fr Nm_PassiveStartUp( ) will be called to let the node enter Repeat-Message state.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Call start-up function to change to Repeat Message state. void Fr Nm_NetworkStartInd() { Write( " Fr Nm_NetworkStartInd: Network started"); Fr Nm_PassiveStartUp(); }

name: NmPassiveStartUpFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_PassiveStartUp( )
description: Description Make the NM leave the Bus-Sleep mode and enter the Repeat Message state, without actually requesting the bus.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmPduRxInd2FR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PduRxInd2(dword busContext, dword shortId, dword longId, char name[], dword sourceNodeIdentifier, long repeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM message from another node. The arguments are taken from the NM message directly.
parameters: Parameters busContext Bus context of the node layer calling this function. shortId Short header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. longId Long header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. name Name of the AUTOSAR PDU. sourceNodeIdentifier The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 : f the Repeat-Message bit is set 0 : otherwise data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPduRxIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PduRxInd( dword slotId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM data PDU from another node. The arguments are taken from the NM message directly.
parameters: Parameters slotId The slot ID of the message. identifier The NM source identifier in the NM PDU or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 if the Repeat-Message bit is set, 0 otherwise. data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Report the NM message received in the Write Window. void Fr Nm_PduRxInd( dword slotId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[]) { Write( " Fr Nm_PduRxInd: NM id=%d, RM=%d, %d byte" identifier, bRepeatMessageBit, elcount(data) ); }

name: NmPduTxIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PduTxInd(dword channelMask, dword slotId, dword pduType, BYTE data[], long identifier, long repeatMessageBit, long vote)
description: Description This function is called after a NM PDU was sent on the bus.
parameters: Parameters channelMask 1 : FR A 2 : FR B slotID Slot in which the PDU was sent. pduType 1 : Vote PDU 2 : Data PDU 3 : Vote + Data PDU (for scheduling variant 1 and 2) data Content of the NM PDU (empty in case of pduType = 1). identifier NM source identifier in the NM PDU (-1 in case of pduType = 1). repeatMessageBit Repeat message bit in the NM PDU (-1 in case of pduType = 1). vote 0 : NM PDU is a vote PDU and does not vote to keep the bus awake 1 : NM PDU is a vote PDU and votes to keep the bus
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPNReleasedIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PNReleaseInd(long partialNetworkId)
description: Description Indication that a certain partial network was released. The indication is called as soon as a certain time elapsed without any NM message requesting the partial network. This time is specified with the parameter NM_PN_RESET_TIME.
parameters: Parameters networkId Specifies which partial network was released.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPNRequestIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PNRequestInd(long partialNetworkId)
description: Description Indication that a certain partial network is now requested. The indication will be only called if the partial network is included in the filter mask of the node.
parameters: Parameters networkId Specifies which partial network was requested.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPreSendIndExtFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_PreSendIndExt(WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[], dword channelMask, dword pduType)
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function to change the actual message sent or even suppress it.
parameters: Parameters dlcInOut (alterable) The DLC of the message to be sent. If the DLC is set to a value greater than 254 , the message will not be sent at all. data (alterable) The message data to be sent. channelMask (not alterable) 1 : FR A 2 : FR B pduType (not alterable) 1 : Vote PDU 2 : Data PDU 3 : Sync PDU
returnValues: Return Values > 0 message will be sent otherwise message will not be sent

name: NmPreSendIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PreSendInd(WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[])
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function to change the actual message sent or even suppress it .
parameters: Parameters dlcInOut The DLC of the message to be sent (alterable). If the DLC is set to a value greater than 254, the message will not be sent at all. data The message data to be sent (alterable).
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Every NM message sent by this node is patched to be 7 bytes long, and byte 6 is set to 0xFF. void Fr Nm_PreSendInd( WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[]) { dlcInOut[0] = 7; // change the DLC of the message sent data[6] = 0xFF; // patch a value }

name: NmReinitSleepTimerFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_ReinitSleepTimer(long isActive)
description: Description This function reinitializes the timer until NM releases the network. Calling has no effect if there is no interaction layer.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmRemoteSleepIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_RemoteSleepInd(long flag)
description: Description If no other node on the network has sent a NM PDU for a configurable amount of time, this function is called with flag==1. If later another node's NM PDU is seen on the network, this function is called again with flag==0.
parameters: Parameters flag The indication type: 0 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is left 1 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is entered
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate whether other nodes allow the network to go to sleep. void Fr Nm_RemoteSleepInd( long flag) { if( flag) Write( " Fr Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Other nodes ready to sleep"); else Write( " Fr Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Another node requests network"); }

name: NmRepeatMessageRequestFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_RepeatMessageRequest( )
description: Description Make the NM leave Normal Operation or Ready Sleep state and enter Repeat Message state, and set the repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmRequestBusSynchFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_RequestBusSynch( )
description: Description Send a NM message immediately to resynchronize all NM timeouts in the nodes on the network. The NM cycle timer is not restarted, i.e. the message is sent independently of the NM message cycle.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSendImmediateNmPDUFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(dword flags, BYTE data[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description Send a NM Data PDU with the provided payload on next opportunity of FlexRay schedule for this PDU.
parameters: Parameters flags Currently unused. data Data bytes including the protocol bytes of NM PDU. bufferSize Size of data buffer in bytes (value must be identical to regular NM PDU size).
returnValues: Return Values -1 invalid 0 not executed 1 OK 2 not available
example: Example on key 'x' { long result; BYTE pduData[8]; //modify bytes as desired, e.g. pduData[ Fr Nm_GetSniPosition()] = Fr Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier(); pduData[ Fr Nm_GetCbvPosition()] = 0x40; result = Fr Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(0, pduData, elcount(pduData)); if (result == 1) { write("Sending immediate Fr NM PDU successfully executed"); } }

name: NmSetApplicationActivityFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_SetApplicationActivity(long isActive)
description: Description This function sets the NM's application activity. If the flag is set (isActive = 1) and the network is sleeping, nothing will happen. But if the network wakes up later, it will stay active until the flag is reset again. If the flag is set and the network is active, it will stay active until the flag is reset again.
parameters: Parameters isActive 0 : no activity 1 : activity
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (no change of isActive )

name: NmSetAutoStartParamFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_SetAutoStartParam(dword autoStartParam)
description: Description Define the behavior of NM at measurement start. If used at all, the function must be called within the on preStart event handler of a CAPL Program.
parameters: Parameters autoStartParam Determines the start up behavior: 0 : No automatic network request at measurement start by NM (network requests via CAPL or from the interaction layer might still lead to a transition into repeat message at measurement start) 1 : Automatic network request at measurement start 2 : Automatic network request if interaction layer is present, no automatic network request in case interaction layer is absent
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetCarWakeUpBitFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_SetCarWakeUpBit(long value)
description: Description Modify the car wake-up bit in the user data. Is only available when the car wake-up bit is configured (else, 0 will be returned).
parameters: Parameters value The desired boolean value (0 = false, >0 = true).
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSetControlBitVectorFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_SetControlBitVector(dword controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits The desired CBV, which is interpreted bit-wise.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetILSleepTimeFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_SetILSleepTime(dword timeMS)
description: Description This function changes the value for next reinitialization of the sleep timer. An on-going timer is not influenced.
parameters: Parameters timeMS new value of the timer in milliseconds
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (no IL) -1 error

name: NmSetPnRequestBitsFR320
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_SetPnRequestBits(BYTE requestVector[])
description: Description Set the partial network request bits (the PN info range ) within the user data. All relevant parameters must be available ( INI Files Partial Network ) or the function call will fail.
parameters: Parameters requestVector The desired PN request bits, which are interpreted bit-wise. Must have the same length as the configured PN info range.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSetUserDataFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_SetUserData(BYTE userData[], dword count)
description: Description Set the user data this node sends in its NM PDU to the byte sequence given, where only the bytes after node identifier or Control Bit Vector are changed.
parameters: Parameters userData User data to be set. bufferSize Length of the user data buffer (cannot exceed the configured user data length).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetVerbosityFR320
syntax: Function Syntax dword Fr Nm_SetVerbosity(dword selector)
description: Description Set the modeling library 's verbosity. The higher the value, the more information is printed in the Write Window.
parameters: Parameters level The desired verbosity level: 0 : Report only critical errors 1 : Report errors resulting from misconfiguration etc. 2 : Warn if an unexpected protocol state is reached and report missing mandatory callbacks 3 : Inform of missing optional callbacks and minor issues >3 : Report low-level events like timer setup and expiration
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmStateChangeNotificationFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_StateChangeNotification(long previousState, long currentState)
description: Description Every time the NM state machine changes from one state to another, this function will be called if it is defined.
parameters: Parameters previousState The NM state which is left. currentState The NM state which is entered. (Please refer to the NM State functions for the table of values.)
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the old and new state in the Write Window. void Fr Nm_StateChangeNotification( long previousState, long currentState) { Write( "Nm_StateChangeNotification: From %d to %d" , previousState, currentState); }

name: NmSynchronizedPncShutdownPduRxIndFR320
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_SynchronizedPncShutdownPduRxInd(dword busContext, dword shortId, dword longId, char name[], dword flags, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call of this function indicates the reception of a NM PDU with a valid synchronized PNC shutdown in the Control Bit Vector (CBV). A synchronized PNC shutdown is only valid if the PNI bit as well as the bit for synchronized shutdown are set simultaneously.
parameters: Parameters busContext Bus context of the modeling library calling this function (only relevant for gateway nodes). shortId If AUTOSAR PDUs are used: short header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. Otherwise: slot ID of received NM frame. longId If AUTOSAR PDUs are used: long header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. Otherwise: slot ID of received NM frame. name If AUTOSAR PDUs are used: name of the AUTOSAR PDU. Otherwise: empty string. flags Currently unused. data Data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmBusSleepModeIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_BusSleepModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicates that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Udp Nm_BusSleepModeInd() { Write( "Nm_BusSleepModeInd: Sleep Mode"); }

name: NmCarWakeUpIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_CarWakeUpInd(dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM message that has the car wake-up bit set. Is only called if NM_CAR_WAKEUP_RX_ENABLED=1.
parameters: Parameters identifier The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 if the Repeat-Message bit is set, 0 otherwise. data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Start up the node when the car wake-up bit is received void Udp Nm_CarWakeUpInd(dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[]) { Write( " Udp Nm_CarWakeUpInd: from node %d", identifier ); Udp Nm_NetworkRequest(); }

name: NmCheckRemoteSleepIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_CheckRemoteSleepInd( )
description: Description Check if remote sleep (all nodes except this one allow transition to Bus-Sleep) has been detected, i.e. this node is the last one keeping the bus awake.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 remote sleep is indicated 0 remote sleep is not indicated

name: NmConfigureILNotificationsUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_ConfigureILNotifications(dword direction, dword mode)
description: Description This function influences the coupling between NM and IL. The coupling normally takes care of forwarding certain events automatically. Implementing CAPL callback functions of the IL and NM may break open this coupling.
parameters: Parameters direction specifies which events are affected 0 : Events from NM to IL (e.g. network mode indication) 1 : Events from IL to NM (e.g. network request due to clamp 15) mode specifies the according effect, the value is interpreted bitwise Bit 0 0 : Open – events are not propagated automatically 1 : Fixed – events are propagated automatically even in case of according CAPL callback functions (does not work for direction = 1)
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong parameter values or illegal combination)

name: NmControlBitVectorIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_ControlBitVectorInd(dword sourceId, dword controlBits)
description: Description This function is called if a NM message with a non-zero Control-Bit-Vector is received. In addition to the vector itself, the identifier of the sender node is given.
parameters: Parameters sourceId The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. controlBits The Control Bit Vector received.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Print the identifier of the sending node in the Write Window. void Udp Nm_ControlBitVectorInd( dword sourceId, dword controlBits) { Write( " Udp Nm_ControlBitVectorInd: from node %d, bits %02x" , sourceId, controlBits); }

name: NmCtrlSimulationOffUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_CtrlSimulationOff( )
description: Description This function will turn off the complete NM simulation by the modeling library . While the NM simulation is off, any API call (except Udp Nm_CtrlSimulationOn() ) will not have any effect and the modeling library will not sent any NM messages !
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmCtrlSimulationOnUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_CtrlSimulationOn( )
description: Description This function will turn on the complete NM simulation by the modeling library . While the NM simulation is off, any API call (except Udp Nm_CtrlSimulationOn() ) will not have any effect and the modeling library will not sent any NM messages !
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmDisableCommunicationUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_DisableCommunication( )
description: Description Turn the sending of NM messages off. The node will proceed as if the sending of the messages succeeded, though. This is needed for the ISO14229 Communication Control service.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong NM state) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmDisablePartialNetworkingUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_DisablePartialNetworking( )
description: Description Stop using partial networking for this node. All relevant parameters must be available ( INI Files Partial Network ) or the function call will fail. Please also consider the restrictions stated in that section when activating the PN feature from CAPL.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmDisablePassiveModeUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_DisablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Stop using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmEnableCommunicationUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_EnableCommunication( )
description: Description Turn the sending of NM messages on. The node will proceed as if the sending of the messages succeeded, though. This is needed for the ISO14229 Communication Control service.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong NM state) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmEnablePartialNetworkingUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_EnablePartialNetworking( )
description: Description Start using partial networking for this node. All relevant parameters must be available ( INI Files Partial Network ) or the function call will fail. Please also consider the restrictions stated in that section when activating the PN feature from CAPL.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmEnablePassiveModeUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_EnablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Start using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 1 : OK 0 0 : not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmFIIgnoreRepeatMessageBitUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp NmFI_IgnoreRepeatMessageBit(long value)
description: Description Defines the reaction upon received repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters 0 normal behavior 1 repeat message bit will be ignored
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetCbvPositionUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetCbvPosition( )
description: Description Returns the byte position (zero-based numbering) of the Control Bit Vector (CBV) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain CBV. ≥ 0 Byte position of CBV within NM PDU.

name: NmGetControlBitVectorUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_GetControlBitVector(dword &controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits Reference parameter to read the CBV.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (Simulation is not enabled, or no CBV is used)
example: Example dword out; long ret; ret = Udp Nm_GetControlBitVector(out); if (ret == 1) { write("CBV value: %u", out); }

name: NmGetLocalNodeIdentifierUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax dword Udp Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Return the NM identifier of this node.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Node identifier, range 0x00 – 0xFF

name: NmGetNodeIdentifierUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Get the NM identifier of the node that sent the last received NM message.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 .. 0xFF node identifier -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetNodeNameUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_GetNodeName(char bufferOut[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description The database name of this node is copied into the provided buffer.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Output buffer for the node name. bufferSize Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetNodeStateUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetNodeState(dword nodeId)
description: Description Check if the node with the given NM identifier has sent a NM message the last time the network was in Repeat Message state. Precondition for retrieval of meaningful information in a node is a preceding call of the function and the completion of the subsequent repeat message phase.
parameters: Parameters nodeId Target node ID, range 0x00 – 0xFF
returnValues: Return Values 2 information is not (yet) available (e.g. node detection not started or ongoing) 1 target node has been detected 0 target node has not been detected -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPduDataUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetPduData(BYTE pduOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the last received NM completely into the buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPnAffiliationUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetPnAffiliation(long partialNetworkId)
description: Description When using partial networking, a node filters NM messages according to the request mask of the message and its own filter mask. Via this function it is possible to retrieve information about the partial networks contained in the filter mask.
parameters: Parameters partialNetworkId Specifies for which partial network the affiliation should be retrieved.
returnValues: Return Values 0 partial network not relevant for node 1 partial network relevant for node -1 error (e.g. illegal partial network, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPncBitVectorLengthUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetPncBitVectorLength( )
description: Description Returns the length (in bytes) of the Partial Network Cluster (PNC) bit vector.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Partial networking is not used. ≥ 0 Offset of the PNC bit vector (in bytes).

name: NmGetPncBitVectorOffsetUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetPncBitVectorOffset( )
description: Description Returns the offset (in bytes) of the Partial Network Cluster (PNC) bit vector within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Partial networking is not used. ≥ 0 Offset of the PNC bit vector (in bytes).

name: NmGetPnCurrentExternalRequestBitsUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetPnCurrentExternalRequestBits(long flags, byte bufferOut[], dword bufferSize, dword numberOfBitsOut []))
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about the external request status of all partial networks. Information about a single partial network can be retrieved via function Udp Nm_IsPnCurrentlyRequested .
parameters: Parameters flags 0 : local scope, i.e. consider only partial networks relevant for the node (result depends on filter mask) 1 : network wide scope bufferOut Byte array in which the request status of the partial networks will be written. bufferSize Size of the byte array in number of bytes. numberOfBitsOut Number of bits needed and/or written to the byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (e.g. partial networking not available) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPnCurrentInternalRequestBitsUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetPnCurrentInternalRequestBits(BYTE bufferOut[], dword bufferSize, dword numberOfBitsOut []))
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about the internal request status of all partial networks. Information about a single partial network can be retrieved via function Udp Nm_IsPnCurrentlyRequested .
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Byte array in which the request status of the partial networks will be written. bufferSize Size of the byte array in number of bytes. numberOfBitsOut Number of bits needed and/or written to the byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (e.g. partial networking not available) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPnRequestBitsDefaultUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetPnRequestBitsDefault( BYTE bufferOut[], dword bufferSize, dword pnRequestBitsLengthOut[])
description: Description The default partial network request bits (the PN info range ) of this node are copied into the provided buffer.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Output buffer for the PN request bits. bufferSize Size of the output buffer. pnRequestBitsLengthOut Number of bits in PN request mask.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (partial networking is either not available (i.e. some relevant parameters have not been configured) or the buffer has an insufficient size) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetRequestStateUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetRequestState( )
description: Description The returned value indicates the NM request state the node is currently in.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 NM_REQUEST_STATE_RELEASED 1 NM_REQUEST_STATE_REQUESTED 2 NM_REQUEST_STATE_PASSIVE -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetSniPositionUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetSniPosition ( )
description: Description Returns the byte position PDU (zero-based numbering) of the Source Node Identifier (SNI) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain SNI. ≥ 0 Byte position of the SNI within NM PDU.

name: NmGetStateUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetState( )
description: Description The returned value indicates the NM state the node is currently in.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 NM_STATE_UNINIT 1 NM_STATE_BUS_SLEEP 2 NM_STATE_PREPARE_BUS_SLEEP 3 NM_STATE_READY_SLEEP 4 NM_STATE_NORMAL_OPERATION 5 NM_STATE_REPEAT_MESSAGE -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetUserDataUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_GetUserData(BYTE dataOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the user data of the last received NM into the output buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmInhibitActiveWakeUpBitUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_InhibitActiveWakeUpBit (long value)
description: Description By default, the active wake-up bit in the Control Bit Vector of the NM PDU is set. This function allows to prevent this. Function can be used in on preStart.
parameters: Parameters value 0 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is set (default). 1 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is not set.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmIsPnCurrentlyRequestedUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_IsPnCurrentlyRequested(long partialNetworkId, long flags)
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about the status of a certain partial network. Thereby it is possible to differentiate between the scope of the node and the network scope.
parameters: Parameters partialNetworkId Specifies for which partial network the status information should be retrieved. flags 0 : local scope, i.e. consider only partial networks relevant for the node (result depends on filter mask) 1 : network wide scope
returnValues: Return Values -1 error (e.g. illegal partial network, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive) In all other cases the return value has to be interpreted bitwise. Bit 0 0 : current node does not request partial network itself 1 : current node requests partial network itself Bit 1 0 : other nodes do not request partial network 1 : at least one other node requests partial network

name: NmIsPnEnabledUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp NM_IsPnEnabled( )
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about whether partial networking is currently switched on or off for this node.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 partial networking is switched on for this node 0 partial networking is switched off for this node -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkModeIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_NetworkModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicate that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Udp Nm_NetworkModeInd() { Write( "Nm_NetworkModeInd: Network Mode"); }

name: NmNetworkReleaseUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_NetworkRelease( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node doesn't need the network, and allows it to enter Bus-Sleep mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkRequestUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_NetworkRequest( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node needs the network.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkStartIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_NetworkStartInd( )
description: Description A NM message has been received while the node is in Bus-Sleep mode. Typically Udp Nm_PassiveStartUp( ) will be called to let the node enter Repeat-Message state.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Call start-up function to change to Repeat Message state. void Udp Nm_NetworkStartInd() { Write( " Udp Nm_NetworkStartInd: Network started"); Udp Nm_PassiveStartUp(); }

name: NmPassiveStartUpUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_PassiveStartUp( )
description: Description Make the NM leave the Bus-Sleep mode and enter the Repeat Message state, without actually requesting the bus.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmPduRxIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_PduRxInd(dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM message from another node. The arguments are taken from the NM message directly.
parameters: Parameters identifier The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 if the Repeat-Message bit is set, 0 otherwise. data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Report the NM message received in the Write Window. void Udp Nm_PduRxInd(dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[]) { Write( " Udp Nm_PduRxInd: NM id=%d, RM=%d, %d byte" identifier, bRepeatMessageBit, elcount(data) ); }

name: NmPNReleasedIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_PNReleaseInd(long partialNetworkId)
description: Description Indication that a certain partial network was released. The indication is called as soon as a certain time elapsed without any NM message requesting the partial network. This time is specified with the parameter NM_PN_RESET_TIME.
parameters: Parameters networkId Specifies which partial network was released.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPNRequestIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_PNRequestInd(long partialNetworkId)
description: Description Indication that a certain partial network is now requested. The indication will be only called if the partial network is included in the filter mask of the node.
parameters: Parameters networkId Specifies which partial network was requested.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPrepareBusSleepModeIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_PrepareBusSleepModeInd( )
description: Description These function indicate that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Udp Nm_PrepareBusSleepModeInd() { Write( " Udp Nm_PrepareBusSleepModeInd: Prepare Sleep Mode"); }

name: NmPreSendIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_PreSendInd(WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[])
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function.
parameters: Parameters dlcInOut The of the message to be sent (alterable). data The message data to be sent (alterable).
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Every NM message sent by this node is patched to be 7 bytes long, and byte 6 is set to 0xFF. void Udp Nm_PreSendInd( WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[]) { dlcInOut[0] = 7; // change the of the message sent data[6] = 0xFF; // patch a value }

name: NmReinitSleepTimerUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_ReinitSleepTimer(long isActive)
description: Description This function reinitializes the timer until NM releases the network. Calling has no effect if there is no interaction layer.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmRemoteSleepIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_RemoteSleepInd(long flag)
description: Description If no other node on the network has sent a NM message for a configurable amount of time, this function is called with flag==1. If later another node's NM message is seen on the network, this function is called again with flag==0.
parameters: Parameters flag The indication type: 0 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is left 1 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is entered
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate whether other nodes allow the network to go to sleep. void Udp Nm_RemoteSleepInd( long flag) { if( flag) Write( " Udp Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Other nodes ready to sleep"); else Write( " Udp Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Another node requests network"); }

name: NmRepeatMessageRequestUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_RepeatMessageRequest( )
description: Description Make the NM leave Normal Operation or Ready Sleep state and enter Repeat Message state, and set the repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSendImmediateNmPDUUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(dword flags, BYTE data[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description Send immediately a NM PDU with the provided payload.
parameters: Parameters flags Currently unused. data Data bytes including the protocol bytes of NM PDU. bufferSize Size of data buffer in bytes (value must be identical to regular NM PDU size).
returnValues: Return Values -1 invalid 0 not executed 1 OK 2 not available
example: Example on key 'x' { long result; BYTE pduData[8]; //modify bytes as desired, e.g. pduData[ Udp Nm_GetSniPosition()] = Udp Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier(); pduData[ Udp Nm_GetCbvPosition()] = 0x40; result = Udp Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(0, pduData, elcount(pduData)); if (result == 1) { write("Sending immediate Udp NM PDU successfully executed"); } }

name: NmSetApplicationActivityUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_SetApplicationActivity(long isActive)
description: Description This function sets the NM's application activity. If the flag is set (isActive = 1) and the network is sleeping, nothing will happen. But if the network wakes up later, it will stay active until the flag is reset again. If the flag is set and the network is active, it will stay active until the flag is reset again.
parameters: Parameters isActive 0 : no activity 1 : activity
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (no change of isActive )

name: NmSetAutoStartParamUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_SetAutoStartParam(dword autoStartParam)
description: Description Define the behavior of NM at measurement start. If used at all, the function must be called within the on preStart event handler of a CAPL Program.
parameters: Parameters autoStartParam Determines the start up behavior: 0 : No automatic network request at measurement start by NM (network requests via CAPL or from the interaction layer might still lead to a transition into repeat message at measurement start) 1 : Automatic network request at measurement start 2 : Automatic network request if interaction layer is present, no automatic network request in case interaction layer is absent
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetCarWakeUpBitUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_SetCarWakeUpBit(long value)
description: Description Modify the car wake-up bit in the user data. Is only available when the car wake-up bit is configured (else, 0 will be returned).
parameters: Parameters value The desired boolean value (0 = false, >0 = true).
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSetControlBitVectorUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_SetControlBitVector(dword controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits The desired CBV, which is interpreted bit-wise.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetILSleepTimeUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_SetILSleepTime(dword timeMS)
description: Description This function changes the value for next reinitialization of the sleep timer. An on-going timer is not influenced.
parameters: Parameters timeMS new value of the timer in milliseconds
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (no IL) -1 error

name: NmSetPnRequestBitsUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_SetPnRequestBits(BYTE requestVector[])
description: Description Set the partial network request bits (the PN info range ) within the user data. All relevant parameters must be available ( INI Files Partial Network ) or the function call will fail.
parameters: Parameters requestVector The desired PN request bits, which are interpreted bit-wise. Must have the same length as the configured PN info range.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSetUserDataUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_SetUserData(BYTE userData[], dword count)
description: Description Set the user data this node sends in its NM PDU to the byte sequence given, where only the bytes after node identifier or Control Bit Vector are changed.
parameters: Parameters userData User data to be set. bufferSize Length of the user data buffer (cannot exceed the configured user data length).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetVerbosityUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax dword Udp Nm_SetVerbosity(dword selector)
description: Description Set the modeling library 's verbosity. The higher the value, the more information is printed in the Write Window.
parameters: Parameters level The desired verbosity level: 0 : Report only critical errors 1 : Report errors resulting from misconfiguration etc. 2 : Warn if an unexpected protocol state is reached and report missing mandatory callbacks 3 : Inform of missing optional callbacks and minor issues >3 : Report low-level events like timer setup and expiration
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmStateChangeNotificationUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_StateChangeNotification(long previousState, long currentState)
description: Description Every time the NM state machine changes from one state to another, this function will be called if it is defined.
parameters: Parameters previousState The NM state which is left. currentState The NM state which is entered. (Please refer to the NM State functions for the table of values.)
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the old and new state in the Write Window. void Udp Nm_StateChangeNotification( long previousState, long currentState) { Write( "Nm_StateChangeNotification: From %d to %d" , previousState, currentState); }

name: NmSynchronizedPncShutdownPduRxIndUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax void Udp Nm_SynchronizedPncShutdownPduRxInd(dword busContext, dword shortId, dword longId, char name[], dword flags, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call of this function indicates the reception of a NM PDU with a valid synchronized PNC shutdown in the Control Bit Vector (CBV). A synchronized PNC shutdown is only valid if the PNI bit as well as the bit for synchronized shutdown are set simultaneously.
parameters: Parameters busContext Bus context of the modeling library calling this function (only relevant for gateway nodes). shortId Short header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. longId Long header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. name Name of the AUTOSAR PDU. flags Currently unused. data Data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmTransmitUDPNM
syntax: Function Syntax long Udp Nm_Transmit( )
description: Description Send a NM message immediately and restart the NM cycle timer.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values The specification defines that Udp Nm_Transmit shall only be available under certain conditions. This is indicated by the return value: 1 OK 0 not executed (sending not possible because of the node state) 2 feature is not activated ( non-standard behaviour ) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: AddConnSlotsASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _AddConnSlots( long connHandle, dword localSlotId, long countSend, dword remoteSlotId, long countReceive);
description: Description Assign already reserved slots to an existing connection. It is possible to set one of the count values to 0 (the slot id is ignored in that case) to indicate that no slot for that direction should be added.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. localSlotId The slot ID that is used for sending. countSend The number of consecutive slots that are used for sending. remoteSlotId The slot ID that is used for receiving. countReceive The number of consecutive slots that are used for receiving.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Create a connection that sends frames in the slots 141, 143 and 147, assuming that the slots have been reserved before. connHandle = FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( 141, 150); FrTP _AddConnSlots( connHandle, 143, 1, -1, 0); FrTP _AddConnSlots( connHandle, 147, 1, -1, 0);

name: CreateConnUnicast1ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( dword localSlotId, WORD remoteSlotId);
description: Description This function will create a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the specified slots and return its connection handle. Local slots are used by this node to send its TP frames, and the node listens to TP frames received on the remote slots.
parameters: Parameters localSlotId The slot ID that is used for sending. remoteSlotId The slot ID that is used for receiving.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Example 1 Creation of a unicast connection that sends PDUs in slot 123 and receives PDUs in slot 234. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { gConnHandle = FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( 123, 234); } Example 2 Assign slots reserved in on Start to a connection created later. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( 130, 10); FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( 140, 0, 1); } On key '1' { gConnHandle = FrTP_CreateConnUnicast( 130, 5, 140, 1); }

name: CreateConnUnicast2ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( dword localSlotId, long localStartCycle, long localCycleRepetition, dword remoteSlotId, long remoteStartCycle, long remoteCycleRepetition);
description: Description This function will create a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the specified slots and return its connection handle. Local slots are used by this node to send its TP frames, and the node listens to TP frames received on the remote slots.
parameters: Parameters localSlotId The slot ID that is used for sending. ocalStartCycle The offset in the cycle multiplexing [0 – localCycleRepetition]. localCycleRepetition The repetion factor of the cycle multiplexing (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64). remoteSlotId The slot ID that is used for receiving. remoteStartCycle The offset in the cycle multiplexing [0 – remoteCycleRepetition]. remoteCycleRepetition The repetion factor of the cycle multiplexing (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64).
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Example 1 Creation of a unicast connection that sends PDUs in slot 123 and receives PDUs in slot 234. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { gConnHandle = FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( 123, 234); } Example 2 Assign slots reserved in on Start to a connection created later. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( 130, 10); FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( 140, 0, 1); } On key '1' { gConnHandle = FrTP_CreateConnUnicast( 130, 5, 140, 1); }

name: CreateConnUnicast3ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( dword localSlotId, long countSend, dword remoteSlotId, long countReceive);
description: Description This function will create a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the specified slots and return its connection handle. Local slots are used by this node to send its TP frames, and the node listens to TP frames received on the remote slots.
parameters: Parameters localSlotId The slot ID that is used for sending. countSend The number of consecutive slots that are used for sending. remoteSlotId The slot ID that is used for receiving. countReceive The number of consecutive slots that are used for receiving.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Example 1 Creation of a unicast connection that sends PDUs in slot 123 and receives PDUs in slot 234. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { gConnHandle = FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( 123, 234); } Example 2 Assign slots reserved in on Start to a connection created later. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( 130, 10); FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( 140, 0, 1); } On key '1' { gConnHandle = FrTP_CreateConnUnicast( 130, 5, 140, 1); }

name: DataRequestASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _DataRequest( long connHandle, byte data[], dword dataLength);
description: Description Send data to the peer. Attention: After return of the function the sending might still be in progress! The return value will indicate if the request could be processed correctly (0), or if an error occurred.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. data Byte array that contains the data. dataLength Length of the data to send and minimum size of the data array.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Send the content of a byte array to the peer. Byte data[20]; // Fill data! if( 0 != FrTP _DataRequest( gConnHandle, data, elcount(data))) { write( "Request to send data failed!"); }

name: DeleteConnASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _DeleteConn( long connHandle);
description: Description Destroy a connection to free its slots or PDUs . They can be assigned to other connections later, though the direction (sending or receiving) cannot be changed.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: ErrorIndASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _ErrorInd( long connHandle, dword error);
description: Description An error occurred for the connection indicated. Please refer to Error Codes for details.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example A warning is written to the Write Window when an error occurs. void FrTP _ErrorInd( long connHandle, dword error) { write( "Error %d for connection %d", error, connHandle); }

name: FirstFrameIndASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _FirstFrameInd( long connHandle, dword length);
description: Description This function allows the CAPL program to determine the start of a segmented data reception.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the events in the Write Window. void FrTP _FirstFrameInd( long connHandle, dword length) { write( "Peer has started transfer of %d byte to this node" , length); }

name: FrameRxIndASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP_FrameRxInd( long connHandle, long typeOfCPDU, BYTE buffer[]);
description: Description This CAPL callback function is called by the DLL whenever a C-PDU is received on its observed slots. During this callback additional information of the frame can be retrieved by calling functions from the group Retrieve Additional Information .
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. typeOfCPDU The type (interpretation) of the received PDU: -1 : Unknown (unexpected) 0 : Un-segmented SF (Single Frame) 1 : Segmented FF (First Frame) 2 : CF ( Continuous Frame) 3 : FC/ACK_RET (Flow Control or Acknowledge/Retry Frame) buffer Received payload of the PDU.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: FrTPAddConnReceiveSlotASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_AddConnReceiveSlot( long connHandle, dword flags, char aRXFrameOrPDUName[]);
description: Description This function adds one Rx frame or PDU to an existing connection(cf. function group Creation of Connections on PDUs or Frames ).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. flags The bit mask control the behavior of this function. Mask Mode 0x0001 If bit is set then a frame is specified; otherwise a PDU or frame (depending on database type). 0x0002 If bit is set then delete all existing PDUs/frames per one direction depending on function, before adding the specified one.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPAddConnSendSlotASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_AddConnSendSlot( long connHandle, dword flags, char aTXFrameOrPDUName[]);
description: Description This function adds one Tx frame or PDU to an existing connection (cf. function group Creation of Connections on PDUs or Frames ). The frame or PDU possibly needs to be reserved before (cf. function group Reservation of Slots ).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. flags The bit mask control the behavior of this function. Mask Mode 0x0001 If bit is set then a frame is specified; otherwise a PDU or frame (depending on database type). 0x0002 If bit is set then delete all existing PDUs/frames per one direction depending on function, before adding the specified one.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPCopyDataReceivedASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_CopyDataReceived( long connHandle, dword posFirstByte, BYTE dataOut[], dword nrOfBytesToCopy);
description: Description While data is received on a connection, it is possible to copy it into a buffer at any time.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. posFirstByte Location of the first byte to copy in the received data. If no bytes are available at this position, an error is returned. dataOut Buffer the bytes are copied into. nrOfBytesToCopy Maximum number of bytes to copy, i.e. if fewer bytes are available, only those will be copied.
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This are the number of bytes that are copied into the buffer. <0 An error occurred, e.g. no data available at the given position. (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example During the indication of a First Frame, it is possible to access the first bytes of data. void FrTP_FirstFrameInd(long connectionHdl, dword dataLength) { BYTE data[100]; FrTP_CopyDataReceived(connectionHdl, 0, data, elcount(data)); // process first data bytes ... }

name: FrTPCreatePDUConnUnicast1ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_CreateConnUnicast( dword flags, char aTXFrameOrPDUName[], char aRXFrameOrPDUName[]);
description: Description This function creates a unicast (one-to-one) connection with optional one Tx and one Rx frame or PDU or frame of any type. Further frames or PDUs can be added. (Cf. function group Slot Assignment ).
parameters: Parameters flags The bit mask control the behavior of this function: Mask : 0x0001 Mode : If bit is set then a frame is specified; otherwise a PDU or frame (depending on database type). aTXFrameOrPDUName The optional name of a Tx frame or PDU in the communication database. aRXFrameOrPDUName The optional name of a Rx frame or PDU in the communication database.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Given a set of PDU definitions in a FIBEX database, CAPL can assign PDUs to connections. Sample Definitions Sender Node PDU name Length in byte Gateway TPL_PhysReq0 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq1 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq2 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq3 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq4 10 Gateway TPL_FuncReq0 15 Gateway TPL_FuncReq1 15 ABC TPL_PhysRespABC0 10 ABC TPL_PhysRespABC1 10 DEF TPL_PhysRespDEF0 10 DEF TPL_PhysRespDEF1 10 GHI TPL_PhysRespGHI0 10 GHI TPL_PhysRespGHI1 10

name: FrTPCreatePDUConnUnicast2ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_CreatePDUConnUnicast( dword sendPDUlen, dword maxSendPDU);
description: Description This function creates a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the next maxSendPDU TPL or DIAGNOSTICS PDUs for sending, that have a length of sendPDUlen byte. Note that it is possible to set the number of send PDUs to 0, e.g. when receiving multicast data.
parameters: Parameters sendPDUlen The PDU will be identified by its payload length that must match this value. maxSendPDU The number of PDUs to search for.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Given a set of PDU definitions in a FIBEX database, CAPL can assign PDUs to connections. Sample Definitions Sender Node PDU name Length in byte Gateway TPL_PhysReq0 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq1 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq2 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq3 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq4 10 Gateway TPL_FuncReq0 15 Gateway TPL_FuncReq1 15 ABC TPL_PhysRespABC0 10 ABC TPL_PhysRespABC1 10 DEF TPL_PhysRespDEF0 10 DEF TPL_PhysRespDEF1 10 GHI TPL_PhysRespGHI0 10 GHI TPL_PhysRespGHI1 10

name: FrTPCreatePDUConnUnicast3ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_CreatePDUConnUnicast( char sendPDUNameArray[][], dword nameLength, dword numPDUs);
description: Description This function creates a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the TPL or DIAGNOSTICS PDUs with the given names for sending.
parameters: Parameters sendPDUNameArray A two dimensional array containing the names of the PDUs that are used for sending. nameLength The maximum name length in the array (size of first dimension). numPDUs The number of PDUs that are defined in the array (size of second dimension).
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Given a set of PDU definitions in a FIBEX database, CAPL can assign symbolically PDUs for sending to connections. // Reserve PDU TPL_FuncReq1 for sending const dword cPDUNameLen = 257; const dword cNumSendPDUs = 1; // This is the PDU that is used for sending on the connection: FrPDU TPL_FuncReq1 diagPDU1; long gDiagConnHandle = -1; long sendPDULen; char sendPDUNames[cNumSendPDUs][cPDUNameLen]; strncpy(sendPDUNames[0], diagPDU1.name, lcount(diagPDU1.name)); sendPDULen = diagPDU1.FR_PDULength; gDiagConnHandle = FrTP_CreatePDUConnUnicast(sendPDUNames, cPDUNameLen, cNumSendPDUs); FrTP_SetMaxPDULength(gDiagConnHandle, sendPDULen);

name: FrTPFIClearAllasr
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_ClearAll( long connHandle);
description: Description Deletes all fault injections that are currently defined for the connection. Additionally the connection is reset (any currently ongoing transmission and reception are cancelled).
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDelayTXDFasr
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DelayTX_DF( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword delay, dword flags);
description: Description Delay the sending of the appropriate SF, FF or CF.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the SS or FF n>0 : Disturb specific CF (absolute or with SN = n) -1 : Disturb the lastCF -2 : Disturb all DF C-PDUs delay Time [ms] the frame should be delayed. flags 0x01 If set, then interpret the frame no. as an absolutely counted number (else interpret the frame no. as the SN of the CF).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDelayTXFCasr
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DelayTX_FC( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword delay, dword flags);
description: Description Delay the sending of the appropriate Flow Control (FC) or Acknowledge Frame (ACK).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the first FC n>0 : Disturb specific FC (absolutely counted per transmission) -1 : Disturb the ACK -2 : Disturb all FC C-PDUs delay Time [ms] the frame should be delayed. flags Not used yet.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDoubleTXDFasr
