syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DoubleTX_DF( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword delay, dword flags);
description: Description Send the appropriate SF, FF or CF twice. The repeated frame will optionally delayed (delay > 0).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the SS or FF n>0 : Disturb specific CF (absolute or with SN = n) -1 : Disturb the lastCF -2 : Disturb all DF C-PDUs delay Time [ms] the frame should be delayed. flags 0x01 If set, then interpret the frame no. as an absolutely counted number (else interpret the frame no. as the SN of the CF).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDoubleTXFCasr
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DoubleTX_FC( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword delay, dword flags);
description: Description Send the appropriate Flow Control (FC) or Acknowledge Frame (ACK) twice. The repeated frame will optionally delayed (delay > 0).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the first FC n>0 : Disturb specific FC (absolutely counted per transmission) -1 : Disturb the ACK -2 : Disturb all FC C-PDUs delay Time [ms] the frame should be delayed. flags Not used yet.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDropTXDFasr
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DropTX_DF( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword flags);
description: Description Do not send the appropriate SF, FF or CF.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the SS or FF n>0 : Disturb specific CF (absolute or with SN = n) -1 : Disturb the lastCF -2 : Disturb all DF C-PDUs flags 0x01 If set, then interpret the frame no. as an absolutely counted number (else interpret the frame no. as the SN of the CF).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDropTXFCasr
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DropTX_FC( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword flags);
description: Description Do not send the appropriate Flow Control (FC) or Acknowledge Frame (ACK).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the first FC n>0 : Disturb specific FC (absolutely counted per transmission) -1 : Disturb the ACK -2 : Disturb all FC C-PDUs flags Not used yet.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIMissInterpretRXFCasr
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_MissInterpretRX_FC( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword flags);
description: Description Miss interpret the appropriate received Flow Control (FC) or Acknowledge (ACK-RETRY) frame.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the first FC n>0 : Disturb specific FC (absolutely counted per transmission) -1 : Disturb the ACK -2 : Disturb all FC C-PDUs flags 0x01 If bit is set, then interpret the FC frame as Clear to Send (CTS) or an ACK as a positive acknowledge regardless of its real content and meaning.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPGetAckRetryStateASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetAckRetryState();
description: Description Returns the ACK parameter of a FC-ACK_RET C-PDU: 0 : Positive acknowledge 1 : RETRY Only valid for FC-ACK_RET frames.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPGetBlockSizeASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetBlockSize();
description: Description Returns the buffer size (BfS) parameter of a FC-CTS C-PDU. Only valid for FC-CTS frames.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPGetFrameSubTypeASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetFrameSubType();
description: Description Returns the appropriate sub-type of the C-PDU: 0 : SF, FF, CF, FC 1 : ACK_RET Can be called for all valid TP frame types.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPGetLastReceivedAddressesASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetLastReceivedAddresses(long connectionHandle, word receiverAddress[], word senderAddress[]);
description: Description Returns the addresses of the last received incoming frame.
parameters: Parameters connectionHandle Identifier for connection. receiverAddress Array that can hold one value for output. senderAddress Array that can hold one value for output.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPGetStMinOrBCASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetStMinOrBC();
description: Description Returns the bandwidth control (StMin) parameter of a FC-CTS C-PDU. Only valid for FC-CTS frames.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPProvideTxDataASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_ProvideTxData( long connHandle, BYTE data[], dword offsetInData, dword lengthThisSendData);
description: Description The data provided with this function will be sent as soon as the respective Consecutive Frame or Last Frame has to be filled. It is possible that the DLL currently waits for data to send for the connection; in this case a Consecutive Frame (or Last Frame, see below) is sent immediately.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. data Buffer holding the data. offsetInData The first byte to add to the send data does not have to be located at the start of the buffer given. This argument indicates the position of the first byte. lengthThisSendData Number of bytes to copy from the data buffer to the send buffer starting at the offset. Must be > 0 if the total length of the data to send was given for the transfer.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Every time a diagnostic trouble code (DTC) has been created it is added to the send buffer and forwarded to the connection that is currently transmitting. void ProvideNextDTC( WORD DTC, BYTE status) { BYTE sListOfDTC[1000]; // static list of DTC WORD sNextDTC = 0; // static start position sListOfDTC[sNextDTC ] = (BYTE) (DTC >> 8); sListOfDTC[sNextDTC + 1] = (BYTE) DTC; sListOfDTC[sNextDTC + 2] = status; FrTP_ProvideTxData(gResponseHandle, sListOfDTC, sNextDTC, 3); sNextDTC += 3; }

name: FrTPProvideTxDataIndASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP_ProvideTxDataInd( long connHandle, dword nextBytePosToSend);
description: Description This callback is called when the DLL is not able to fill the data part of the next Consecutive Frame it has to send, i.e. the data provided so far does not cover the bytes starting at nextBytePosToSend. It will only be called for transmissions started with FrTp_SmallBufferSend . If data is provided often enough with FrTp_ProvideTxData , this callback function is never called.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The CANoe sender node introduces a delay in the sequence of Consecutive Frames by providing additional transmission data only after 100 ms have passed. void FrTp_ProvideTxDataInd( long connHandle, dword nextBytePosToSend) { gNextBytePosToSend = nextBytePosToSend; setTimer( tDelayTx, 100); } on timer tDelayTx { // Provide some additional data to continue sending FrTp_ProvideTxData( gConnHandle, gData , gNextBytePosToSend, 50); }

name: FrTPRxBufferFullIndASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP_RxBufferFullInd( long connHandle, dword posFirstByte, BYTE buffer[]);
description: Description This CAPL callback function is called by the DLL when the receiver buffer is full. As default the receiver buffer is not limited, therefore FrTP_SetRxBufferSize() has to reduce the buffer size for the connection (cf. FrTP_SetRxBufferSize ).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. posFirstByte The position of the first byte in the receiver buffer. buffer Received data buffer.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example In a gateway, data received on the source bus is forwarded to the target bus. In case of the first segment of data, a new data transfer with incomplete data is started. Otherwise the data is forwarded to the ongoing transmission. void FrTp_RxBufferFullInd( long connHandle, dword posFirstByte, BYTE segment[]) { SetBusContext( gTargetBus); // activate target bus if( posFirstByte == 0) FrTP_SmallBufferSend( gTargetHandle , gTotalDataLength // set in FirstFrameInd , segment, elcount( segment)); else FrTP_ProvideTxData( gTargetHandle , segment, 0, elcount( segment)); }

name: FrTPSetAbortReceptionOnNewStartASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetAbortReceptionOnNewStart( long connHandle, long abortOnSF, long abortOnFF);
description: Description Enables or disables the abort of an ongoing segmented reception while receiving a new Single Frame or First Frame.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. abortOnSF 0 disables and 1 enables the abort on receiving an unexpected Single Frame; Default: enabled abortOnFF 0 disables and 1 enables the abort on receiving an unexpected First Frame; Default: enabled.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPSetConfigurationDoneASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetConfigurationDone( long connHandle, dword flags);
description: Description When all parameters are defined, then the connection has to initialize internally (e.g. the Tx PDU pool must be announced to the PDU scheduler). Calling this function will trigger the connection's internal initialization process.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPSetConnMode1ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetConnMode( long connHandle, dword connMode);
description: Description This function allows to change the mode of a connection, where connMode may have these values: 1 : Un-acknowledged unicast (one-to-one) connection. 2 : Acknowledged unicast (one-to-one) connection. 3 : Acknowledged unicast (one-to-one) connection without retry. 10 : Multicast (one-to-many) connection – Flow Control and Acknowledging is not enabled. 11 : Segmented multicast (not implemented yet).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. connMode Sets the mode of the connection.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Change the connection mode to multicast. FrTP_SetConnMode( gConnHandle, 10);

name: FrTPSetConnMode2ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetConnMode( long connHandle, dword aTxConnMode, dword aRxConnMode);
description: Description This function allows to change the mode of a connection for both directions separately, where the Mode may have these values: 1 : Un-acknowledged unicast (one-to-one) connection. 2 : Acknowledged unicast (one-to-one) connection. 3 : Acknowledged unicast (one-to-one) connection without retry. 10 : Multicast (one-to-many) connection – Flow Control and Acknowledging is not enabled. 11 : Segmented multicast (not implemented yet).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. aTxConnMode Sets the mode of the connection's Tx direction. aRxConnMode Sets the mode of the connection's Rx direction.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Change the connection mode to multicast. FrTP_SetConnMode( gConnHandle, 10);

name: FrTPSetDirectionModeASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetDirectionMode( long connHandle, dword directionMode);
description: Description Per default a connection is bi-directional (Tx and Rx). With this function the operational direction of the connection can be set to Tx only, Rx only or both. 0 : Rx only 1 : Tx only 2 : Tx and Rx
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPSetExclusiveTXSlotUsageASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetExclusiveTXSlotUsage( long connHandle, long enable, dword flags);
description: Description Sets the exclusive Tx slot usage for the connection. If enabled, then this connection exclusively transmits its C-PDUs on its Tx slots (although the slots are possibly shared with other connections). Thus, other connections will possibly be blocked for sending.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. enable 0 disables and 1 enables the exclusive usage of the assigned Tx slots; Default: disabled flags Mask : 0x0001 Mode : If the bit is set, then reset the exclusive usage after one transmission request has been completed or has been aborted due to an error.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPSetLengthMode1ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetLengthMode( long connHandle, dword lengthMode);
description: Description This function allows to change the maximum message length for a a connection, where lengthMode may have these values: Mode Short Name Short Description 1 FRTP_ISO Up to 4095 bytes, do not use extended TP frame types. 2 FRTP_ISO6 Like FRTP_ISO, but payload length is limited to 6 bytes per C-PDU. 3 FRTP_L16M Up to 2 24 –1 byte, TP frame type FF-E4 is not used. 4 FRTP_L4G Up to 2 32 –1 byte, all TP frame types can be used.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. lengthMode Sets the length mode of the connection.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set the length mode of a connection to FRTP_L16M. FrTP_SetLengthMode( gConnHandle, 3);

name: FrTPSetLengthMode2ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetLengthMode( long connHandle, dword aTxLengthMode, dword aRxLengthMode);
description: Description This function allows to change the maximum message length for a a connection, where lengthMode may have these values: Mode Short Name Short Description 1 FRTP_ISO Up to 4095 bytes, do not use extended TP frame types. 2 FRTP_ISO6 Like FRTP_ISO, but payload length is limited to 6 bytes per C-PDU. 3 FRTP_L16M Up to 2 24 –1 byte, TP frame type FF-E4 is not used. 4 FRTP_L4G Up to 2 32 –1 byte, all TP frame types can be used.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. aTxLengthMode Sets the length mode of the connection's Tx direction. aRxLengthMode Sets the length mode of the connection's Rx direction.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set the length mode of a connection to FRTP_L16M. FrTP_SetLengthMode( gConnHandle, 3);

name: FrTPSetProtocolVersionASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetProtocolVersion( long connHandle, dword AsrMajorVersionNo, dword AsrMinorVersionNo, dword AsrRevisionNo);
description: Description This DLL supports three protocol variants from the corresponding AUTOSAR version: TP Variant AUTOSAR Version 2.x 2.0.0 – 2.1.13 3.x 2.1.14 – 3.2.1 3.2.2 3.2.2 – x.y.z
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. AsrMajorVersionNo The major version of the desired AUTOSAR version. AsrMinorVersionNo The minor version of the desired AUTOSAR version. AsrRevisionNo The revision of the desired AUTOSAR version.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPSetRxBufferSizeASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetRxBufferSize( long connHandle, dword receiverBufferSize);
description: Description This function limits the receive buffer for the connection to the given number of bytes. Every time the buffer is full, the callback function FrTP_RxBufferFullInd is called. Setting a value of 0 will give the buffer an unlimited size, i.e. the callback function will never be called (this is the default).
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Limit the receive buffer to 200 bytes. FrTP_SetRxBufferSize( connHandle, 200);

name: FrTPSetStMinUsASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetStMinUs( long connHandle, dword STminUs);
description: Description Configures the minimum time between the sending of consecutive frames. The DLL will use the next possible value higher or equal. Defaults to 10 ms, maximum value is 127 ms. Note that due to the FlexRay cycle system, values lower than one cycle time cannot be realized.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. STminUs . The minimum time in µs
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set the block size to 0 (if DLL receives data, it will not send further flow control frames after the first one) and send consecutive frames as fast as possible. FrTP_SetSTminUs( gConnHandle, 0);

name: FrTPSetTXMaxPrioPDUsASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetTXMaxPrioPDUs( long connHandle, dword maxTxPrioPDUs);
description: Description Set the connection's maximum number of Tx priority PDUs. The connection can use up to this number of Tx PDUs in the first scheduling round ("guaranteed bandwidth per FlexRay cycle"), before the PDUs of the Tx pool are used by connections with less or none Tx priority PDUs. This is a specific feature of the AUTOSAR protocol version 3.2.2.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPSetTXPriorityASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetTXPriority( long connHandle, long priority);
description: Description Set the connection's Tx priority. Thus, if Tx slots are shared among multiple connections, then the C-PDUs of this connection will possibly be prioritized for sending.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPSmallBufferSendASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SmallBufferSend( long connHandle, dword totalDataLength, BYTE startData[], dword startDataLength);
description: Description This function is provided as a replacement for FrTP_DataRequest in case small buffer handling is to be used. FrTP_DataRequest always expects the complete TP data in advance.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. totalDataLength Length of this data transfer. May be 0 to indicate that the total length is not known in advance. Please consider that the standard limits the length to a maximum of 65535 byte. Note that the First Frame indication provides this number. startData Buffer holding the first bytes for transfer in the First Frame. startDataLength Initial amount of data available for sending, must be > 0.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Start sending data while only a part of it is already set. FillFirstBytes( globalData, 10); // set first 10 bytes of data FrTP_SmallBufferSend( gConnHandle, elcount(globalData) , globalData, 10);

name: GetDLLInfoASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _GetDLLInfo( dword selector);
description: Description Retrieves additional information about DLL and settings. Possible values for selector are: 0 : Return DLL version as 6 digit BCD number, e.g. 0x000909. 1 : Return DLL major version number, e.g. 0. 2 : Return DLL minor version number, e.g. 9. 3 : Return DLL build version number, e.g. 9.
parameters: Parameters selector
returnValues: Return Values See function description.
example: Example Indicate the name of the node and the version of the DLL loaded in the Write Window. char nodeName[50]; FrTP _GetNodeName( nodeName, elcount( nodeName)); write( "This is node <%s>, using DLL V%d.%d.%d", nodeName , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(1) , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(2) , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(3));

name: GetFlowStatusASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _GetFlowStatus();
description: Description Returns the flow status parameter (type of flow control frame) of a FC C-PDU: 3 : Continue To Send (CTS) 4 : Acknowledge/Retry (ACK_RET) 5 : Wait 6 : Abort 7 : Overflow Only valid for FC frames.
parameters: Parameters ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: GetNodeNameASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _GetNodeName( char bufferOut[], dword bufferSize);
description: Description Retrieve this node's name.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the name of the node and the version of the DLL loaded in the Write Window. char nodeName[50]; FrTP _GetNodeName( nodeName, elcount( nodeName)); write( "This is node <%s>, using DLL V%d.%d.%d", nodeName , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(1) , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(2) , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(3));

name: GetPCIOffsetASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _GetPCIOffset();
description: Description Returns the offset of the first byte after the C_PCI. Valid for STF, CF, FC and LF C-PDUs.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: GetSequenceNumberASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _GetSequenceNumber();
description: Description Returns the sequence number (SN) parameter of a CF C-PDU or the message length (ML) parameter of a FF C -PDU or the byte position (BP) of a FC-ACK_RET C-PDU. Valid for FF , CF, FC and FC frames.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: ReceptionIndASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _ReceptionInd( long connHandle, BYTE data[]);
description: Description This function is called when the node has received data from its peer. Since the node may use several connections, the handle indicates which one has received the data. The number of data is available as length of the byte array.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example On reception of data, its length and first byte are written to the Write Window. void FrTP _ReceptionInd( long connHandle, byte data[]) { write( "Received %d byte: [%02x] ..." , elcount( data), data[0]); }

name: ReserveReceiveSlotASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( dword slotId, long startCycle, long cycleRepetition);
description: Description The node reserves one slot in a subset of cycles for reception. The slot can be assigned to connections later, and this assignment can be changed.
parameters: Parameters slotId The slot ID. startCycle The offset in the cycle multiplexing [0 – cycleRepetition]. cycleRepetition The repetion factor of the cycle multiplexing (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Reserve slot 125 in every other cycle, starting with the second cycle. FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( 125, 1, 2);

name: ReserveReceiveSlotsASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlots( dword startSlotId, long count);
description: Description The node reserves a sequence of consecutive slots for reception. The slots can be assigned to connections later, and this assignment can be changed.
parameters: Parameters startSlotId The first slot ID that is to be reserved. count The number of consecutive slots that are to be reserved.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Reserve slot 125 in every other cycle, starting with the second cycle. FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlots( 125, 1, 2);

name: ReserveSendSlot1ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _ReserveSendSlot( dword startSlotId, long startCycle, long cycleRepetition);
description: Description The node reserves one slot in a subset of cycles for sending. The slot can be assigned to connections later, and this assignment can be changed.
parameters: Parameters slotId The slot ID. startCycle The offset in the cycle multiplexing [0 – cycleRepetition]. cycleRepetition The repetion factor of the cycle multiplexing (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Reservation of the slot 130 to 139 for sending of up to 10 PDUs in the same cycle. FrTP _ReserveSendSlot( 130, 10);

name: ReserveSendSlot2ASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _ReserveSendSlot( long connHandle, dword flags, char aTXFrameOrPDUName[]);
description: Description Reserve one Tx frame or PDU to allow later assignment to sending connections. (Cf. function groups Creation of Connections on PDUs or Frames and Slot Assignment ).
parameters: Parameters flags The bit mask control the behavior of this function: Mask : 0x0001 Mode : If bit is set then a frame is specified; otherwise a PDU or frame (depending on database type). aTXFrameOrPDUName The optional symbolic name of a Tx frame or PDU in the communication database.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Reservation of the slot 130 to 139 for sending of up to 10 PDUs in the same cycle. FrTP _ReserveSendSlot( 130, 10);

name: ReserveSendSlotsASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( dword startSlotId, long count);
description: Description The node reserves a sequence of consecutive slots for sending. The slots can be assigned to connections later, and this assignment can be changed.
parameters: Parameters startSlotId The first slot ID that is to be reserved. count The number of consecutive slots that are to be reserved.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Reservation of the slots 130 to 139 for sending of up to 10 PDUs in the same cycle. FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( 130, 10);

name: SetAddressesASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetAddresses( long connHandle, long addrType, dword localAddress, dword remoteAddress);
description: Description Set addresses (C_AI) used in the C- PDUs to the values given .
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. addrType Indicates one byte (1) or two byte (2) address length. localAddress The local address ([0x0000 – 0xFFFF] 16 Bit). remoteAddress The remote address ([0x0000 – 0xFFFF] 16 Bit).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set one byte addressing to 27 and 29 FrTP _SetAddresses( gConnHandle, 1, 27, 29);

name: SetBlockSizeASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetBlockSize( long connHandle, dword blockSize);
description: Description Configures the number of consecutive frames the sender transmits before expecting a flow control frame from the receiver. 0 indicates that no further flow control frames should be expected. Default: 8 frames
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. blockSize The maximum buffer size for receiving data.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set the block size to 0 (if DLL receives data, it will not send further flow control frames after the first one) and send consecutive frames as fast as possible. FrTP_SetBlockSize( gConnHandle, 0);

name: SetExpertParamASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _SetExpertParam( long connHandle, dword option, long value);
description: Description The expert parameter function should only be used on instruction by Vector Informatik GmbH .
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. option The specific parameter option that should be set. value The value for the parameter.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: SetFillByteASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetFillByte( long connHandle, long fillByte);
description: Description Defines the fill byte for padding un-used bytes in the C- PDUs. If the function is not called, then 0 is used.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. fillByte The filler byte.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set the maximum PDU length to 32 and the fill byte to $E5. FrTP _SetFillByte( gConnHandle, 0xE5);

name: SetMaxPDULengthASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetMaxPDULength( long connHandle, dword maxPDUlen);
description: Description The transport protocol will send data in slots with the specified maximum C- PDU length filling undefined bytes with the fill byte specified by FrTP_SetFillByte( ) . If the function is not called, then a default of 254 byte is used.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. maxPDUlen The maximum size of a C-PDU in bytes.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set the maximum PDU length to 32 and the fill byte to $E5. FrTP _SetMaxPDULength( gConnHandle, 32);

name: SetReceiverStatusASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetReceiverStatus( long connHandle, dword status, long argument);
description: Description Allows to set the status of the receiver in order to send a FC-CTS, FC-ACK_RET, FC-WT, FC-ABT or FC-OVFLW next.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. status The flow status to be set in the next FC C-PDU that will be sent. Flow Status Argument Description 0 — Continue To Send (CTS) 1 MaxWaitFrames (max. Number of Wait Frames) Wait 2 — Overflow 3 — NACK
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: SetTimingParamsASR
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetTimingParams( long connHandle, dword timeoutAS, dword timeoutAR, dword timeoutBS, dword timeBR, dword timeCS, dword timeoutCR);
description: Description Defines some timing parameters that handle the distance between two C-PDUs for transmission or reception (timeout).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. timeoutAS Maximum tolerated time [ms] between the request to send a FlexRay frame and its transmission (Tx acknowledge from bus) on the sender side (Default: 125 ms). timeoutAR Maximum tolerated time [ms] between the request to send a FlexRay frame and its transmission (Tx acknowledge from bus) on the receiver side (Default: 125 ms). timeoutBS Timeout [ms] for the reception of a flow control frame on the sender side (Default : 150 ms). timeBR T
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: SetVerbosityASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _SetVerbosity( dword level);
description: Description The function sets the amount of debug information written to the Write Window (the higher the level, the more is printed).
parameters: Parameters level
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TxConfirmationIndASR
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _TxConfirmationInd( long connHandle);
description: Description This function allows the CAPL program to determine the end of a data transmission.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the events in the Write Window. void FrTP _TxConfirmationInd( long connHandle) { write( "Data transmission on connection %d successful" , connHandle); }

name: AddConnSlotsFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _AddConnSlots( long connHandle, dword localSlotId, long countSend, dword remoteSlotId, long countReceive);
description: Description Assign already reserved slots to an existing connection. It is possible to set one of the count values to 0 (the slot id is ignored in that case) to indicate that no slot for that direction should be added.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. localSlotId The slot ID that is used for sending. countSend The number of consecutive slots that are used for sending. remoteSlotId The slot ID that is used for receiving. countReceive The number of consecutive slots that are used for receiving.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Create a connection that sends frames in the slots 141, 143 and 147, assuming that the slots have been reserved before. connHandle = FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( 141, 150); FrTP _AddConnSlots( connHandle, 143, 1, -1, 0); FrTP _AddConnSlots( connHandle, 147, 1, -1, 0);

name: CreateConnUnicast1FRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( dword localSlotId, WORD remoteSlotId);
description: Description This function will create a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the specified slots and return its connection handle. Local slots are used by this node to send its TP frames, and the node listens to TP frames received on the remote slots.
parameters: Parameters localSlotId The slot ID that is used for sending. remoteSlotId The slot ID that is used for receiving.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Example 1 Creation of a unicast connection that sends PDUs in slot 123 and receives PDUs in slot 234. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { gConnHandle = FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( 123, 234); } Example 2 Assign slots reserved in on Start to a connection created later. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( 130, 10); FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( 140, 0, 1); } On key '1' { gConnHandle = FrTP_CreateConnUnicast( 130, 5, 140, 1); }

name: CreateConnUnicast2FRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( dword localSlotId, long localStartCycle, long localCycleRepetition, dword remoteSlotId, long remoteStartCycle, long remoteCycleRepetition);
description: Description This function will create a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the specified slots and return its connection handle. Local slots are used by this node to send its TP frames, and the node listens to TP frames received on the remote slots.
parameters: Parameters localSlotId The slot ID that is used for sending. ocalStartCycle The offset in the cycle multiplexing [0 – localCycleRepetition]. localCycleRepetition The repetion factor of the cycle multiplexing (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64). remoteSlotId The slot ID that is used for receiving. remoteStartCycle The offset in the cycle multiplexing [0 – remoteCycleRepetition]. remoteCycleRepetition The repetion factor of the cycle multiplexing (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64).
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Example 1 Creation of a unicast connection that sends PDUs in slot 123 and receives PDUs in slot 234. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { gConnHandle = FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( 123, 234); } Example 2 Assign slots reserved in on Start to a connection created later. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( 130, 10); FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( 140, 0, 1); } On key '1' { gConnHandle = FrTP_CreateConnUnicast( 130, 5, 140, 1); }

name: CreateConnUnicast3FRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( dword localSlotId, long countSend, dword remoteSlotId, long countReceive);
description: Description This function will create a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the specified slots and return its connection handle. Local slots are used by this node to send its TP frames, and the node listens to TP frames received on the remote slots.
parameters: Parameters localSlotId The slot ID that is used for sending. countSend The number of consecutive slots that are used for sending. remoteSlotId The slot ID that is used for receiving. countReceive The number of consecutive slots that are used for receiving.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Example 1 Creation of a unicast connection that sends PDUs in slot 123 and receives PDUs in slot 234. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { gConnHandle = FrTP _CreateConnUnicast( 123, 234); } Example 2 Assign slots reserved in on Start to a connection created later. Variables { long gConnHandle; } On Prestart { FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( 130, 10); FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( 140, 0, 1); } On key '1' { gConnHandle = FrTP_CreateConnUnicast( 130, 5, 140, 1); }

name: DataRequestFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _DataRequest( long connHandle, byte data[], dword dataLength);
description: Description Send data to the peer. Attention: After return of the function the sending might still be in progress! The return value will indicate if the request could be processed correctly (0), or if an error occurred.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. data Byte array that contains the data. dataLength Length of the data to send and minimum size of the data array.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Send the content of a byte array to the peer. Byte data[20]; // Fill data! if( 0 != FrTP _DataRequest( gConnHandle, data, elcount(data))) { write( "Request to send data failed!"); }

name: DeleteConnFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _DeleteConn( long connHandle);
description: Description Destroy a connection to free its slots or PDUs . They can be assigned to other connections later, though the direction (sending or receiving) cannot be changed.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: ErrorIndFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _ErrorInd( long connHandle, dword error);
description: Description An error occurred for the connection indicated. Please refer to Error Codes for details.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example A warning is written to the Write Window when an error occurs. void FrTP _ErrorInd( long connHandle, dword error) { write( "Error %d for connection %d", error, connHandle); }

name: FirstFrameIndFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _FirstFrameInd( long connHandle, dword length);
description: Description This function allows the CAPL program to determine the start of a segmented data reception.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the events in the Write Window. void FrTP _FirstFrameInd( long connHandle, dword length) { write( "Peer has started transfer of %d byte to this node" , length); }

name: FrameRxIndFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP_FrameRxInd( long connHandle, long typeOfCPDU, BYTE buffer[]);
description: Description This CAPL callback function is called by the DLL whenever a C-PDU is received on its observed slots. During this callback additional information of the frame can be retrieved by calling functions from the group Retrieve Additional Information .
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. typeOfCPDU The type (interpretation) of the received PDU: -1 : Unknown (unexpected) 0 : Un-segmented STF (Single Frame) 1 : Segmented STF (First Frame) 2 : CF ( Continuous Frame) or LF (last Frame) 3 : FC/ACK_RET (Flow Control or Acknowledge/Retry Frame) buffer Received payload of the PDU.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: FrTPAddConnReceiveSlotFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_AddConnReceiveSlot( long connHandle, dword flags, char aRXFrameOrPDUName[]);
description: Description This function adds one Rx frame or PDU to an existing connection(cf. function group Creation of Connections on PDUs or Frames ).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. flags The bit mask control the behavior of this function. Mask Mode 0x0001 If bit is set then a frame is specified; otherwise a PDU or frame (depending on database type). 0x0002 If bit is set then delete all existing PDUs/frames per one direction depending on function, before adding the specified one.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPAddConnSendSlotFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_AddConnSendSlot( long connHandle, dword flags, char aTXFrameOrPDUName[]);
description: Description This function adds one Tx frame or PDU to an existing connection (cf. function group Creation of Connections on PDUs or Frames ). The frame or PDU possibly needs to be reserved before (cf. function group Reservation of Slots ).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. flags The bit mask control the behavior of this function. Mask Mode 0x0001 If bit is set then a frame is specified; otherwise a PDU or frame (depending on database type). 0x0002 If bit is set then delete all existing PDUs/frames per one direction depending on function, before adding the specified one.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPCopyDataReceivedFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_CopyDataReceived( long connHandle, dword posFirstByte, BYTE dataOut[], dword nrOfBytesToCopy);
description: Description While data is received on a connection, it is possible to copy it into a buffer at any time.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. posFirstByte Location of the first byte to copy in the received data. If no bytes are available at this position, an error is returned. dataOut Buffer the bytes are copied into. nrOfBytesToCopy Maximum number of bytes to copy, i.e. if fewer bytes are available, only those will be copied.
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This are the number of bytes that are copied into the buffer. <0 An error occurred, e.g. no data available at the given position. (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example During the indication of a First Frame, it is possible to access the first bytes of data. void FrTP_FirstFrameInd(long connectionHdl, dword dataLength) { BYTE data[100]; FrTP_CopyDataReceived(connectionHdl, 0, data, elcount(data)); // process first data bytes ... }

name: FrTPCreatePDUConnUnicast1FRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_CreateConnUnicast( dword flags, char aTXFrameOrPDUName[], char aRXFrameOrPDUName[]);
description: Description This function creates a unicast (one-to-one) connection with optional one Tx and one Rx frame or PDU or frame of any type. Further frames or PDUs can be added. (Cf. function group Slot Assignment ).
parameters: Parameters flags The bit mask control the behavior of this function: Mask : 0x0001 Mode : If bit is set then a frame is specified; otherwise a PDU or frame (depending on database type). aTXFrameOrPDUName The optional name of a Tx frame or PDU in the communication database. aRXFrameOrPDUName The optional name of a Rx frame or PDU in the communication database.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Given a set of PDU definitions in a FIBEX database, CAPL can assign PDUs to connections. Sample Definitions Sender Node PDU name Length in byte Gateway TPL_PhysReq0 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq1 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq2 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq3 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq4 10 Gateway TPL_FuncReq0 15 Gateway TPL_FuncReq1 15 ABC TPL_PhysRespABC0 10 ABC TPL_PhysRespABC1 10 DEF TPL_PhysRespDEF0 10 DEF TPL_PhysRespDEF1 10 GHI TPL_PhysRespGHI0 10 GHI TPL_PhysRespGHI1 10

name: FrTPCreatePDUConnUnicast2FRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_CreatePDUConnUnicast( dword sendPDUlen, dword maxSendPDU);
description: Description This function creates a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the next maxSendPDU TPL or DIAGNOSTICS PDUs for sending, that have a length of sendPDUlen byte. Note that it is possible to set the number of send PDUs to 0, e.g. when receiving multicast data.
parameters: Parameters sendPDUlen The PDU will be identified by its payload length that must match this value. maxSendPDU The number of PDUs to search for.
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Given a set of PDU definitions in a FIBEX database, CAPL can assign PDUs to connections. Sample Definitions Sender Node PDU name Length in byte Gateway TPL_PhysReq0 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq1 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq2 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq3 10 Gateway TPL_PhysReq4 10 Gateway TPL_FuncReq0 15 Gateway TPL_FuncReq1 15 ABC TPL_PhysRespABC0 10 ABC TPL_PhysRespABC1 10 DEF TPL_PhysRespDEF0 10 DEF TPL_PhysRespDEF1 10 GHI TPL_PhysRespGHI0 10 GHI TPL_PhysRespGHI1 10

name: FrTPCreatePDUConnUnicast3FRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_CreatePDUConnUnicast( char sendPDUNameArray[][], dword nameLength, dword numPDUs);
description: Description This function creates a unicast (one-to-one) connection that uses the TPL or DIAGNOSTICS PDUs with the given names for sending.
parameters: Parameters sendPDUNameArray A two dimensional array containing the names of the PDUs that are used for sending. nameLength The maximum name length in the array (size of first dimension). numPDUs The number of PDUs that are defined in the array (size of second dimension).
returnValues: Return Values >0 The handle for the created connection. ≤0 Error code (Cf. Error Codes ).
example: Example Given a set of PDU definitions in a FIBEX database, CAPL can assign symbolically PDUs for sending to connections. // Reserve PDU TPL_FuncReq1 for sending const dword cPDUNameLen = 257; const dword cNumSendPDUs = 1; // This is the PDU that is used for sending on the connection: FrPDU TPL_FuncReq1 diagPDU1; long gDiagConnHandle = -1; long sendPDULen; char sendPDUNames[cNumSendPDUs][cPDUNameLen]; strncpy(sendPDUNames[0], diagPDU1.name, lcount(diagPDU1.name)); sendPDULen = diagPDU1.FR_PDULength; gDiagConnHandle = FrTP_CreatePDUConnUnicast(sendPDUNames, cPDUNameLen, cNumSendPDUs); FrTP_SetMaxPDULength(gDiagConnHandle, sendPDULen);

name: FrTPFIClearAllfrTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_ClearAll( long connHandle);
description: Description Deletes all fault injections that are currently defined for the connection. Additionally the connection is reset (any currently ongoing transmission and reception are cancelled).
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDelayTXDFfrTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DelayTX_DF( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword delay, dword flags);
description: Description Delay the sending of the appropriate STF, LF or CF.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the STF n>0 : Disturb specific CF (absolute or with SN = n) -1 : Disturb the LF -2 : Disturb all DF C-PDUs delay Time [ms] the frame should be delayed. flags 0x01 If set, then interpret the frame no. as an absolutely counted number (else interpret the frame no. as the SN of the CF).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDelayTXFCfrTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DelayTX_FC( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword delay, dword flags);
description: Description Delay the sending of the appropriate Flow Control (FC) or Acknowledge Frame (ACK).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the first FC n>0 : Disturb specific FC (absolutely counted per transmission) -1 : Disturb the ACK -2 : Disturb all FC C-PDUs delay Time [ms] the frame should be delayed. flags Not used yet.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDoubleTXDFfrTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DoubleTX_DF( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword delay, dword flags);
description: Description Send the appropriate STF, LF or CF twice. The repeated frame will optionally delayed (delay > 0).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the STF n>0 : Disturb specific CF (absolute or with SN = n) -1 : Disturb the LF -2 : Disturb all DF C-PDUs delay Time [ms] the frame should be delayed. flags 0x01 If set, then interpret the frame no. as an absolutely counted number (else interpret the frame no. as the SN of the CF).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDoubleTXFCfrTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DoubleTX_FC( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword delay, dword flags);
description: Description Send the appropriate Flow Control (FC) or Acknowledge Frame (ACK) twice. The repeated frame will optionally delayed (delay > 0).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the first FC n>0 : Disturb specific FC (absolutely counted per transmission) -1 : Disturb the ACK -2 : Disturb all FC C-PDUs delay Time [ms] the frame should be delayed. flags Not used yet.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDropTXDFfrTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DropTX_DF( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword flags);
description: Description Do not send the appropriate STF, LF or CF.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the STF n>0 : Disturb specific CF (absolute or with SN = n) -1 : Disturb the LF -2 : Disturb all DF C-PDUs flags 0x01 If set, then interpret the frame no. as an absolutely counted number (else interpret the frame no. as the SN of the CF).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIDropTXFCfrTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_DropTX_FC( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword flags);
description: Description Do not send the appropriate Flow Control (FC) or Acknowledge Frame (ACK).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the first FC n>0 : Disturb specific FC (absolutely counted per transmission) -1 : Disturb the ACK -2 : Disturb all FC C-PDUs flags Not used yet.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPFIMissInterpretRXFCfrTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTPFI_MissInterpretRX_FC( long connHandle, long frameNo, dword flags);
description: Description Miss interpret the appropriate received Flow Control (FC) or Acknowledge (ACK-RETRY) frame.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. frameNo Number of the frame to be disturbed: 0 : Disturb the first FC n>0 : Disturb specific FC (absolutely counted per transmission) -1 : Disturb the ACK -2 : Disturb all FC C-PDUs flags 0x01 If bit is set, then interpret the FC frame as Clear to Send (CTS) or an ACK as a positive acknowledge regardless of its real content and meaning.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPGetAckRetryStateFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetAckRetryState();
description: Description Returns the ACK parameter of a FC-ACK_RET C-PDU: 0 : Positive acknowledge 1 : RETRY Only valid for FC-ACK_RET frames.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPGetBlockSizeFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetBlockSize();
description: Description Returns the buffer size (BfS) parameter of a FC-CTS C-PDU. Only valid for FC-CTS frames.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPGetFrameSubTypeFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetFrameSubType();
description: Description Returns the appropriate sub-type of the C-PDU: 0 : STF, CF, FC 1 : ACK_RET , CF1 2 : CF2 3 : CF_EOB 4 : Not used 5 : LF Can be called for all valid TP frame types.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPGetLastReceivedAddressesFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetLastReceivedAddresses(long connectionHandle, word receiverAddress[], word senderAddress[]);
description: Description Returns the addresses of the last received incoming frame.
parameters: Parameters connectionHandle Identifier for connection. receiverAddress Array that can hold one value for output. senderAddress Array that can hold one value for output.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: FrTPGetStMinOrBCFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_GetStMinOrBC();
description: Description Returns the bandwidth control (BC) parameter of a FC-CTS C-PDU. Only valid for FC-CTS frames.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: FrTPProvideTxDataFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_ProvideTxData( long connHandle, BYTE data[], dword offsetInData, dword lengthThisSendData);
description: Description The data provided with this function will be sent as soon as the respective Consecutive Frame or Last Frame has to be filled. It is possible that the DLL currently waits for data to send for the connection; in this case a Consecutive Frame (or Last Frame, see below) is sent immediately.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. data Buffer holding the data. offsetInData The first byte to add to the send data does not have to be located at the start of the buffer given. This argument indicates the position of the first byte. lengthThisSendData Number of bytes to copy from the data buffer to the send buffer starting at the offset. Must be > 0 if the total length of the data to send was given for the transfer.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Every time a diagnostic trouble code (DTC) has been created it is added to the send buffer and forwarded to the connection that is currently transmitting. void ProvideNextDTC( WORD DTC, BYTE status) { BYTE sListOfDTC[1000]; // static list of DTC WORD sNextDTC = 0; // static start position sListOfDTC[sNextDTC ] = (BYTE) (DTC >> 8); sListOfDTC[sNextDTC + 1] = (BYTE) DTC; sListOfDTC[sNextDTC + 2] = status; FrTP_ProvideTxData(gResponseHandle, sListOfDTC, sNextDTC, 3); sNextDTC += 3; }

name: FrTPProvideTxDataIndFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP_ProvideTxDataInd( long connHandle, dword nextBytePosToSend);
description: Description This callback is called when the DLL is not able to fill the data part of the next Consecutive Frame it has to send, i.e. the data provided so far does not cover the bytes starting at nextBytePosToSend. It will only be called for transmissions started with FrTp_SmallBufferSend . If data is provided often enough with FrTp_ProvideTxData , this callback function is never called.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The CANoe sender node introduces a delay in the sequence of Consecutive Frames by providing additional transmission data only after 100 ms have passed. void FrTp_ProvideTxDataInd( long connHandle, dword nextBytePosToSend) { gNextBytePosToSend = nextBytePosToSend; setTimer( tDelayTx, 100); } on timer tDelayTx { // Provide some additional data to continue sending FrTp_ProvideTxData( gConnHandle, gData , gNextBytePosToSend, 50); }

name: FrTPRxBufferFullIndFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP_RxBufferFullInd( long connHandle, dword posFirstByte, BYTE buffer[]);
description: Description This CAPL callback function is called by the DLL when the receiver buffer is full. As default the receiver buffer is not limited, therefore FrTP_SetRxBufferSize() has to reduce the buffer size for the connection (cf. FrTP_SetRxBufferSize ).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. posFirstByte The position of the first byte in the receiver buffer. buffer Received data buffer.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example In a gateway, data received on the source bus is forwarded to the target bus. In case of the first segment of data, a new data transfer with incomplete data is started. Otherwise the data is forwarded to the ongoing transmission. void FrTp_RxBufferFullInd( long connHandle, dword posFirstByte, BYTE segment[]) { SetBusContext( gTargetBus); // activate target bus if( posFirstByte == 0) FrTP_SmallBufferSend( gTargetHandle , gTotalDataLength // set in FirstFrameInd , segment, elcount( segment)); else FrTP_ProvideTxData( gTargetHandle , segment, 0, elcount( segment)); }

name: FrTPSet2ndAddressesFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_Set2ndAddresses( long connHandle, dword flags, dword a2ndLocalAddress, dword a2ndRemoteAddress);
description: Description Sets optionally 2nd ECU addresses that are used for incoming or outgoing connection data. This allows e.g. in the context of the Tester to receive responses from the ECU from 2 different (remote) TP addresses. In the context of the ECU (simulation) this allows to accept/receive requests that are directed to 2 different (local) TP addresses. In normal case either the local address or the remote address should be valid according to the current application context (Tester or ECU simulat
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. flags Controls which addresses should be used, and whether responding flow control frames should use the second address. a2ndLocalAddress The 2nd local address ([0x0000 – 0xFFFF] 16 Bit). a2ndRemoteAddress The 2nd remote address ([0x0000 – 0xFFFF] 16 Bit).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set additionally 2nd ECU address from CANoe /CDD parameter settings. const int cIsTester = 0; // default: we are an ECU simulation long localAddress; long localAddress2; long useAddress2; long remoteAddress; long remoteAddress2; long addressType; long gDiagConnHandle = -1; ... localAddress = DiagGetCommParameter("FlexRay.LocalAddress"); remoteAddress = DiagGetCommParameter("FlexRay.RemoteAddress"); addressType = 2; localAddress &= 0xFFFF; remoteAddress &= 0xFFFF; useAddress2 = DiagGetCommParameter("FlexRay.Has2ndECUAddress"); if (useAddress2 == 1) { if (cIsTester) { remoteAddress2 = DiagGetCommParameter("FlexRay.RemoteAddress2"); writeDbgLevel( 1, "%s: Setting %d byte addresses (0x%04X, 0x%04X)", gECU, addressType, localAddress, remoteAddress); FrTP_SetAddresses(gDiagConnHandle, localAddress, remoteAddress); writeDbgLevel( 1, "%s: Setting 2nd ECU address for reception 0x%04X", gECU, localAddress2); FrTP_Set2ndAddresses(gDiagConnHandle, 0x01, 0, remoteAddress2); } else { localAddress2 = DiagGetCommParameter("FlexRay.LocalAddress2"); writeDbgLevel( 1, "%s: Setting %d byte addresses (0x%04X, 0x%04X)", gECU, addressType, localAddress2, remoteAddress); FrTP_SetAddresses(gDiagCon

name: FrTPSetBufferSizeFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetBufferSize( long connHandle, dword bufferSize);
description: Description Configures the way a sender should send data to this node. The sender should await a Flow Control frame after sending this amount of data in one connection. 0 indicates that no further flow control frames should be expected. Default: 0
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set the buffer size to 0 (if DLL receives data, it will not send further flow control frames after the first one ). FrTP_SetBufferSize( gConnHandle, 0 );

name: FrTPSetConnMode1FRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetConnMode( long connHandle, dword connMode);
description: Description This function allows to change the mode of a connection, where connMode may have these values: 1 : Un-acknowledged unicast (one-to-one) connection. 2 : Acknowledged unicast (one-to-one) connection. 3 : Acknowledged unicast (one-to-one) connection without retry. 10 : Multicast (one-to-many) connection – Flow Control and Acknowledging is not enabled. 11 : Segmented multicast (not implemented yet).
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. connMode Sets the mode of the connection.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Change the connection mode to multicast. FrTP_SetConnMode( gConnHandle, 10);

name: FrTPSetRxBufferSizeFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetRxBufferSize( long connHandle, dword receiverBufferSize);
description: Description This function limits the receive buffer for the connection to the given number of bytes. Every time the buffer is full, the callback function FrTP_RxBufferFullInd is called. Setting a value of 0 will give the buffer an unlimited size, i.e. the callback function will never be called (this is the default).
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Limit the receive buffer to 200 bytes. FrTP_SetRxBufferSize( connHandle, 200);

name: FrTPSmallBufferSendFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SmallBufferSend( long connHandle, dword totalDataLength, BYTE startData[], dword startDataLength);
description: Description This function is provided as a replacement for FrTP_DataRequest in case small buffer handling is to be used. FrTP_DataRequest always expects the complete TP data in advance.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. totalDataLength Length of this data transfer. May be 0 to indicate that the total length is not known in advance. Please consider that the standard limits the length to a maximum of 65535 byte. Note that the First Frame indication provides this number. startData Buffer holding the first bytes for transfer in the First Frame. startDataLength Initial amount of data available for sending, must be > 0.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Start sending data while only a part of it is already set. FillFirstBytes( globalData, 10); // set first 10 bytes of data FrTP_SmallBufferSend( gConnHandle, elcount(globalData) , globalData, 10);

name: GetDLLInfoFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _GetDLLInfo( dword selector);
description: Description Retrieves additional information about DLL and settings. Possible values for selector are: 0 : Return DLL version as 6 digit BCD number, e.g. 0x000909. 1 : Return DLL major version number, e.g. 0. 2 : Return DLL minor version number, e.g. 9. 3 : Return DLL build version number, e.g. 9.
parameters: Parameters selector
returnValues: Return Values See function description.
example: Example Indicate the name of the node and the version of the DLL loaded in the Write Window. char nodeName[50]; FrTP _GetNodeName( nodeName, elcount( nodeName)); write( "This is node <%s>, using DLL V%d.%d.%d", nodeName , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(1) , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(2) , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(3));

name: GetFlowStatusFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _GetFlowStatus();
description: Description Returns the flow status parameter (type of flow control frame) of a FC C-PDU: 3 : Continue To Send (CTS) 4 : Acknowledge/Retry (ACK_RET) 5 : Wait 6 : Abort 7 : Overflow Only valid for FC frames.
parameters: Parameters ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: GetNodeNameFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _GetNodeName( char bufferOut[], dword bufferSize);
description: Description Retrieve this node's name.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the name of the node and the version of the DLL loaded in the Write Window. char nodeName[50]; FrTP _GetNodeName( nodeName, elcount( nodeName)); write( "This is node <%s>, using DLL V%d.%d.%d", nodeName , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(1) , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(2) , FrTP _GetDLLInfo(3));

name: GetPCIOffsetFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _GetPCIOffset();
description: Description Returns the offset of the first byte after the C_PCI. Valid for STF, CF, FC and LF C-PDUs.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: GetSequenceNumberFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _GetSequenceNumber();
description: Description Returns the sequence number (SN) parameter of a CF C-PDU or the message length (ML) parameter of a STF or LF -PDU or the byte position (BP) of a FC-ACK_RET C-PDU. Valid for STF , CF, FC and LF frames.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 This is the appropriate requested value. <0 An error occurred. (Cf. Error Codes ).

name: ReceptionIndFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _ReceptionInd( long connHandle, BYTE data[]);
description: Description This function is called when the node has received data from its peer. Since the node may use several connections, the handle indicates which one has received the data. The number of data is available as length of the byte array.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example On reception of data, its length and first byte are written to the Write Window. void FrTP _ReceptionInd( long connHandle, byte data[]) { write( "Received %d byte: [%02x] ..." , elcount( data), data[0]); }

name: ReserveReceiveSlotFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( dword slotId, long startCycle, long cycleRepetition);
description: Description The node reserves one slot in a subset of cycles for reception. The slot can be assigned to connections later, and this assignment can be changed.
parameters: Parameters slotId The slot ID. startCycle The offset in the cycle multiplexing [0 – cycleRepetition]. cycleRepetition The repetion factor of the cycle multiplexing (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Reserve slot 125 in every other cycle, starting with the second cycle. FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlot( 125, 1, 2);

name: ReserveReceiveSlotsFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlots( dword startSlotId, long count);
description: Description The node reserves a sequence of consecutive slots for reception. The slots can be assigned to connections later, and this assignment can be changed.
parameters: Parameters startSlotId The first slot ID that is to be reserved. count The number of consecutive slots that are to be reserved.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Reserve slot 125 in every other cycle, starting with the second cycle. FrTP _ReserveReceiveSlots( 125, 1, 2);

name: ReserveSendSlot1FRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _ReserveSendSlot( dword startSlotId, long startCycle, long cycleRepetition);
description: Description The node reserves one slot in a subset of cycles for sending. The slot can be assigned to connections later, and this assignment can be changed.
parameters: Parameters slotId The slot ID. startCycle The offset in the cycle multiplexing [0 – cycleRepetition]. cycleRepetition The repetion factor of the cycle multiplexing (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Reservation of the slot 130 to 139 for sending of up to 10 PDUs in the same cycle. FrTP _ReserveSendSlot( 130, 10);

name: ReserveSendSlotsFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( dword startSlotId, long count);
description: Description The node reserves a sequence of consecutive slots for sending. The slots can be assigned to connections later, and this assignment can be changed.
parameters: Parameters startSlotId The first slot ID that is to be reserved. count The number of consecutive slots that are to be reserved.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Reservation of the slots 130 to 139 for sending of up to 10 PDUs in the same cycle. FrTP _ReserveSendSlots( 130, 10);

name: SetAddressesFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetAddresses( long connHandle, dword localAddress, dword remoteAddress);
description: Description Set addresses (C_AI) used in the C- PDUs to the values given .
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. localAddress The local address ([0x0000 – 0xFFFF] 16 Bit). remoteAddress The remote address ([0x0000 – 0xFFFF] 16 Bit).
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set addresses to 27 and 29. FrTP_SetAddresses( gConnHandle, 27, 29);

name: SetBandwidthControlFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP_SetBandwidthControl( long connHandle, long separationCycles, long maxPDUsPerCycle );
description: Description Configures the way a sender should send data to this node. Please be aware that additional parameters influence the actual behavior of a sender: If fewer slots than allowed are configured, the sender will not be able to send the specified number of PDUs per cycle. The same is true for the receiver buffer size, since a full buffer at the receiver requires the sender to wait for a Flow Control frame. This parameter (BC) is transmitted in the first FC after the STF of an segmented trans
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. separationCycles The number of separation cycles must be smaller than 128. maxPDUsPerCycle Up to 31 C-PDUs per cycle are possible.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example The sender should send up to 7 PDUs in the same cycle, then pausing for 2 cycles. FrTP_SetBandwidthControl( gConnHandle, 2, 7);

name: SetExpertParamFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _SetExpertParam( long connHandle, dword option, long value);
description: Description The expert parameter function should only be used on instruction by Vector Informatik GmbH .
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. option The specific parameter option that should be set. value The value for the parameter.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: SetFillByteFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetFillByte( long connHandle, long fillByte);
description: Description Defines the fill byte for padding un-used bytes in the C- PDUs. If the function is not called, then 0 is used.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. fillByte The filler byte.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set the maximum PDU length to 32 and the fill byte to $E5. FrTP _SetFillByte( gConnHandle, 0xE5);

name: SetMaxPDULengthFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetMaxPDULength( long connHandle, dword maxPDUlen);
description: Description The transport protocol will send data in slots with the specified maximum C- PDU length filling undefined bytes with the fill byte specified by FrTP_SetFillByte( ) . If the function is not called, then a default of 254 byte is used.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. maxPDUlen The maximum size of a C-PDU in bytes.
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes
example: Example Set the maximum PDU length to 32 and the fill byte to $E5. FrTP _SetMaxPDULength( gConnHandle, 32);

name: SetReceiverStatusFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetReceiverStatus( long connHandle, dword status, long argument);
description: Description Allows to set the status of the receiver in order to send a FC-CTS, FC-ACK_RET, FC-WT, FC-ABT or FC-OVFLW next.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. status The flow status to be set in the next FC C-PDU that will be sent. Flow Status Argument Description 3 BfS Continue To Send (CTS) 4 BP Retry (ACK_RET with ACK = 1) 5 — Wait 6 — Abort 7 — Overflow
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: SetTimingParamsFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax long FrTP _SetTimingParams( long connHandle, dword timeoutAS, dword timeoutAR, dword timeoutBS, dword timeBR, dword timeCS, dword timeoutCR);
description: Description Defines some timing parameters that handle the distance between two C-PDUs for transmission or reception (timeout). If the function is not called, then 0 is used.
parameters: Parameters connHandle The handle of the connection. timeoutAS Maximum tolerated time [ms] between the request to send a FlexRay frame and its transmission (Tx acknowledge from bus) on the sender side (Default: 125 ms). timeoutAR Maximum tolerated time [ms] between the request to send a FlexRay frame and its transmission (Tx acknowledge from bus) on the receiver side (Default: 125 ms). timeoutBS Timeout [ms] for the reception of a flow control frame on the sender side (Default : 150 ms). timeBR T
returnValues: Return Values see Error Codes

name: SetVerbosityFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _SetVerbosity( dword level);
description: Description The function sets the amount of debug information written to the Write Window (the higher the level, the more is printed).
parameters: Parameters level
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TxConfirmationIndFRTPiso
syntax: Function Syntax void FrTP _TxConfirmationInd( long connHandle);
description: Description This function allows the CAPL program to determine the end of a data transmission.
parameters: Parameters connHandle
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the events in the Write Window. void FrTP _TxConfirmationInd( long connHandle) { write( "Data transmission on connection %d successful" , connHandle); }

name: apIndLastAwakeSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void apIndLastAwake( )
description: Description This function is called when the node is the last active node on the bus that does not signal sleep readiness. Therefore the bus would go into sleep mode if this node also signaled sleep readiness. The condition is checked on the reception of every NM message where the sleep readiness bit is set, therefore this function can be called repeatedly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ApIndPreSendSTD
syntax: Function Syntax long apIndPreSend (byte dlc[], byte data[])
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function.
parameters: Parameters data length code The DLC of the message to be sent (alterable). data The message data to be sent (alterable).
returnValues: Return Values <=0 The NM message will not be sent on the bus. >0 The (potentially altered) NM message will be sent on the bus.

name: BusSleepSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void ap BusSleep( );
description: Description The SleepAck signal was received/sent, and time T_BUSSLEEP has elapsed. The CAN transceiver can now be transferred from standby to sleep mode. The function is called during the transition to sleep mode directly after the ap CanSleep function call.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CanNormalSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void ap CanNormal( );
description: Description This switches the CAN transceiver to active state. This function is called if the GotoMode_Awake function is called while the bus is asleep.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CanOffSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void ap CanOff( );
description: Description This function is called during the transition into sleep mode. It tells the CAPL node not to send any more messages. From CANoe version 2.2, there is a function that prevents the sending of messages from a node: CanOffline (see CANoe online help). This function should be used in CAPL models that are run in CANoe version 2.2 and higher!
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CanOnSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void ap CanOn( );
description: Description Network management is reactivated and CAN output is released. From CANoe version 2.2, there is a function that reactivates the sending of messages from a node after CanOffline has been called: CanOnline (see CANoe online help). This function should be used in CAPL models that are run in CANoe version 2.2 and higher!
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CanSleepSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void ap CanSleep( );
description: Description The SleepAck signal was received/sent, and time T_BUSSLEEP has elapsed. The CAN transceiver can now be transferred from active to standby. The function is called during the transition to sleep mode directly before the ap BusSleep function call.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CanWakeUpSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void ap CanWakeUp( );
description: Description This function is called when the network management modeling library receives a message in sleep mode. In this case the GotoMode_Awake function needs to be called in order to activate network management! If network management is to remain ready to sleep, the GotoMode_BusSleep function should be called after the Awake call.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CmpConfigSTD
syntax: Function Syntax dword CmpConfig( );
description: Description Checks whether all ECUs in the target configuration are participating in network management, taking the mask settings into consideration.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 if all required ECUs are participating 0 if one or more are missing

name: GetConfigSTD
syntax: Function Syntax dword GetConfig( );
description: Description The current configuration shows which ECUs are actively participating in network management. The bit with the highest value is ECU 31 (31 — 0).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Returns a bit vector of currently active nodes.
example: Example /*@@var:*/ variables{ dword ActualConfig=0; // current net configuration. } /*@@end*/ /*@@key:'i':*/ on key 'i' { // Get the current network stations participate on NWM-Ring. ActualConfig = GetConfig(); Write("WIND-Config is %lX",AktualConfig); } /*@@end*/—

name: GotoModeAwakeSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void GotoMode_Awake( );
description: Description This function can be used while network management is running to reverse the ready-to-sleep status of the application. If the application is already asleep, this function should be called in order to initialize network management. The function is then generally called from the ap CanWakeUp function.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GotoModeBusSleepSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void GotoMode_BusSleep( );
description: Description This function indicates to network management that the application is ready to sleep.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: IndRingDataSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void ap IndRingData( );
description: Description This function is called when the node receives the token and the ring is stable. The node can then read the ring data and send its own data. The application can get the latest data received by this NM-message.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NMCtrlSimulationOffSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void NMCtrlSimulationOff ( );
description: Description This function deactivates the NM simulation completely. The function NMCrtlSimulationOn has to be called to reactivate the NM simulation.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NMCtrlSimulationOnSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void NMCtrlSimulationOn ( );
description: Description This function reactivates the NM simulation after a previous call of NMCtrlSimulationOff .
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NMGetAwakeConfigSTD
syntax: Function Syntax long NMGetAwakeConfig(dword arraySize, byte flags[], dword startIndex = 0);
description: Description This function is used to query the number of awake nodes known to network management: If flags [i] == 1 , then node (i + startIndex ) is known to be awake.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values This function returns the total number of awake notes, even if flagsOut is not large enough to store the state of all the nodes.

name: NMGetConfigSTD
syntax: Function Syntax long NMGetConfig(dword arraySize, byte flags[], dword startIndex = 0)
description: Description The elements in the field flags are set to 1 , if the node corresponding to the element index participates in the NM, to 0 otherwise.
parameters: Parameters startIndex denotes the node index corresponding to field element 0 , i.e. flags[n] is set to the state of node (n + startIndex). can be omitted and 0 is assumed
returnValues: Return Values The number of active nodes is returned by this function, therefore it is possible to query the number of active nodes with the following construct: byte dummy[1]; count = NMGetConfig( 1, dummy);

name: NMGetStatusSTD
syntax: Function Syntax dword NMGetStatus( );
description: Description This function returns the network management status.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values The return value has to be interpreted bitwise: 0 Config stable 1 CAN Drv Active 2 — 3 — 4 NM in BusSleep 5 NM in limphome 6 NM on 7 Ready to Sleep On

name: NMSetAutoStartParamSTD
syntax: Function Syntax void NMSetAutoStartParam (dword autoStartParam);
description: Description Define the behavior of NM at measurement start. If used at all, the function must be called within the on preStart event handler of a CAPL Program.
parameters: Parameters autoStartParam Determines the start up behavior: 0 : No automatic network request at measurement start by NM (network requests via CAPL or from the interaction layer might still lead to transmitting NM messages at measurement start). 1 : Automatic network request at measurement start.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NMSetConfigMask1STD
