description: Description Finds out the name of the first assigned database.
parameters: Parameters buffer Buffer in which the database name is written. size Size of the buffer in Byte.
returnValues: Return Values If successful unequal 0, otherwise 0.

name: GetMessageAttrInt
syntax: Function Syntax long GetMessageAttrInt(message canMessage, char attributeName[]); long GetMessageAttrInt(pg parameterGroup, char attributeName[]);
description: Description Gets the value of a message attribute from the database. A user-defined attribute with the name specified in the parameter, and of the Integer type, must be defined in the database. If no such attribute is defined, the function returns 0. If no attribute value is assigned to the message in the database, the default value of the attribute definition is returned.
parameters: Parameters canMessage Message variable attributeName Attribute name
returnValues: Return Values Value of the attribute (or default value) from the database.

name: GetMessageID
syntax: Function Syntax dword getMessageID(char messageName[]); // form 1 dword getMessageID(char messageName[], char dbName[]); // form 2
description: Description Finds out the message ID
parameters: Parameters messageName Name of the message. dbName Name of the database, needed if the message name is used in more than one database.
returnValues: Return Values Message ID, or (dword)-1 if the message is not found

name: GetMessageName
syntax: Function Syntax dword getMessageName( dword id, dword context, char buffer[], dword size)
description: Description Finds out the message name.
parameters: Parameters id Id of the message for which the message name should be found. context Context of assigned databases. The low word of context contains the channel number. The high word of context contains the bus system. Available bus systems: CAN 1 LIN 5 FlexRay 7 J1708 9 buffer Buffer in which the message name is written. size Size of the buffer in Byte.
returnValues: Return Values If successful unequal 0, otherwise 0.

name: GetNextCANdbFileName
syntax: Function Syntax dword getNextCANdbFilename( dword pos, char buffer[], dword size);
description: Description Finds out the filenames of the other assigned databases with pos .
parameters: Parameters pos Position number of the database to be found. buffer Buffer in which the database filename is written. size Size of the buffer in Byte.
returnValues: Return Values If successful unequal 0, otherwise 0.

name: GetNextCANdbName
syntax: Function Syntax dword getNextCANdbName( dword pos, char buffer[], dword size)
description: Description Finds out the names of the other assigned databases with pos .
parameters: Parameters pos Position number of the database to be found. buffer Buffer in which the database name is written. size Size of the buffer in Byte.
returnValues: Return Values If successful unequal 0, otherwise 0.

name: GetPayloadData
syntax: Function Syntax getPayloadData(errorframe errFrame, byte[] payload, dword payloadSize;
description: Description Returns the valid payload of a frame that was interrupted during transmission. Depending on error position valid bits are written to the payload parameter. If there is no payload data, nothing is written into the payload parameter.
parameters: Parameters errFrame Error frame which includes the payload. payload Array for writing the transmitted payload of the error frame. payloadSize Size of the payload parameter.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No valid payload. >0 Count of valid bits within the payload array.

name: GetSetCanBittimingConfiguration
syntax: Function Syntax int GetCanBittimingConfiguration(dword channel, BitTiming timing); int GetCanBittimingConfiguration(dword channel, BitTiming[] timings, dword countOfBittimings);   int SetCanBittimingConfiguration(dword channel, BitTiming timing); int SetCanBittimingConfiguration(dword channel, BitTiming[] timings, dword countOfBittimings);
description: Description The CAN, CAN FD or CAN-XL controller parameters can be set or read. A automatic reset of the controller is performed.
parameters: Parameters Channel The CAN channel. BitTiming The bit timing for the defined phase. BitTimings The bit timings for more than one defined phase. countofBittimings Number of bit timings contained in the array.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful -1 Invalid bit timing phase -2 Invalid parameter -3 No bit timings available for defined phase -4 No bit timings available for defined channel -255 Unknown error

name: GetSignalName
syntax: Function Syntax char[] getSignalName(caplCanMessage* msg, dword startBit); // form 1 char[] getSignalName(caplAutosarPDU* pdu, dword startBit); // form 2
description: Description Finds out the signal name at a specified start index inside a CAN message (form 1). Finds out the signal name at a specified start index inside a PDU (form 2).
parameters: Parameters msg CAN message. pdu AUTOSAR PDU. startBit First bit of the signal of the AUTOSAR PDU or CAN message.
returnValues: Return Values Returns the signal name at a given start index inside a CAN message. Start index is defined in DBC Editor (form 1). Returns signal name at a given start index inside a PDU. Start index is defined with AUTOSAR Explorer (form 2).

name: IsStdLd
syntax: Function Syntax long isStdId(dword id); // form 1 long isStdId(message m); // form 2 long isExtId(dword id); // form 3 long isExtId(message m); // form 4
description: Description Checks parameter for extended identifier (29 bit) or standard identifier (11 Bit).
parameters: Parameters message Variable of type message id Id part of a message
returnValues: Return Values 1 if check was successful, else 0

name: MkExtID
syntax: Function Syntax dword mkExtId(dword id);
description: Description Returns an extended id.
parameters: Parameters id Id part of a message.
returnValues: Return Values Extended identifier

name: Output
syntax: Function Syntax void output(message msg); // form 1 void output(errorFrame); // form 2
description: Description Outputs a message (form 1) or an Error Frame (form 2) from the program block.
parameters: Parameters msg Variable of type message. errorFrame Variable of type errorFrame.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ResetCan
syntax: Function Syntax void resetCan();
description: Description Resets the CAN controller. Can be used to reset the CAN controller after a BUSOFF or to activate configuration changes. Since execution of the function takes some time and the CAN controller is disconnected from the bus briefly, messages can be lost when this is performed. Note With this function you can reset CAN1 and CAN2. If only one specific CAN channel is used, resetCan stops with an error and the CAN channels keep offline. In this case the ResetCanEx function has to be used. Th
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ResetCanEx
syntax: Function Syntax void ResetCanEx(long channel);
description: Description Resets the CAN controller for one specific CAN channel. Can be used to reset the CAN controller after a BUSOFF or to activate configuration changes. Since execution of the function takes a certain amount of time and the CAN controller is disconnected from the bus for a brief period messages may be lost. Note The Rx queue of the CAN channel will not be flushed by default.
parameters: Parameters channel CAN channel
returnValues: Return Values —

name: RxChipErrorCount
syntax: Function Syntax long RxChipErrorCount ()
description: Description Returns the current Rx error count in the receiver of channel x . Valid x values: 1…32
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Current error count in the receiver of channel x .

name: ScanBaudrateActive
syntax: Function Syntax long ScanBaudrateActive( dword channel, dword messageID, double firstBaudrate, double lastBaudrate, dword timeout);
description: Description The function determines the baudrate for the given channel. Result of the function is written into the Write Window. The baudrate scanner checks different baudrates and tries to send a message through the given channel. The function is finished if the message was sent successfully and the baudrate was determined. If a wrong baudrate is present, the other power supply takers cannot receive the message. CANoe as the transmitter does not receive an acknowledge and sends an Error Frame.
parameters: Parameters channel Channel number. (1,..,32) messageID ID of the message that the scanner will send to detect the baudrate. The DLC of the message is always 8. firstBaudrate / lastBaudrate Baudrate range to scan. If both values are set to zero the scanner checks the most commonly used baudrates: 33.333, 50.0, 83.333, 100.0, 125.0, 250.0, 500.0, 1000.0 [kBaud] If both values are the same but not zero the scanner multiplies the baudrate with a given factor (Value range 0.25-5.0). The factor is cha
returnValues: Return Values Returns 0 if the scan function was successfully started. Otherwise the return value is non-zero.

name: ScanBaudratePassive
syntax: Function Syntax long ScanBaudratePassive( dword channel, dword messageID, double firstBaudrate, double lastBaudrate, dword timeout, dword bAcknowledge);
description: Description Baudrate scanner checks different baudrates and tries to receive a message on the channel. Function starts the scan and detects the baudrate on the given channel. Result of the function is written into the Write Window. If a wrong baudrate is present, CANoe cannot receive messages and sends an Error Frame, which can be put on the bus using the parameter bAcknowledge . Note CAN.INI file settings for the baudrate scanner: [BaudrateScanner] Dlc=8 DisplayBaudrateList=0 Dlc: DLC of the me
parameters: Parameters channel Channel number. (1,..,32) messageID ID of the message that the scanner will receive to detect the baudrate. If this value is 0xffff the scanner will receive all the messages on the channel. firstBaudrate / lastBaudrate Baudrate range to scan. If both values are set to zero the scanner checks the most commonly used baudrates: 33.333, 50.0, 83.333, 100.0, 125.0, 250.0, 500.0, 1000.0 [kBaud] If both values are the same but not zero the scanner multiplies the baudrate with a given
returnValues: Return Values Returns 0 if the scan function was successfully started. Otherwise the return value is non-zero.

name: SetBtr
syntax: Function Syntax long setBtr(long channel, byte btr0, byte btr1);
description: Description Sets another baudrate. The values do not become active until the next call of the function resetCan . It should be noted that these values depend on the CAN controller used.
parameters: Parameters CAN channel 0 All controllers 1 - 32 channel 1 - 32 BTR0 Value of Bit Timing Register 0. BTR1 Value of Bit Timing Register 1.
returnValues: Return Values Always 1

name: SetOcr
syntax: Function Syntax setOcr(long channel, byte ocr);
description: Description Sets the Output Control Register. The values do not become active until the next call of the function resetCan() . It should be noted that these values depend on the CAN platform used.
parameters: Parameters CAN channel 0 all channels > 0 only the given channel OCR Value of the Output Control Register
returnValues: Return Values 1 success 0 error

name: SetSignalStartValues
syntax: Function Syntax setSignalStartValues(message msg); // form 1 setSignalStartValues(multiplexed_message msg); // form 2 setSignalStartValues(frFrame frame); // form 3 setSignalStartValues(frFrame frame, byte uninitializedData); // form 4 setSignalStartValues(frPDU pdu); // form 5 setSignalStartValues(frPDU pdu, byte uninitializedData); // form 6 setSignalStartValues(pg paramGroup); // form 7 setSignalStartValues(j1587Param param); // form 8 setSignalStartValues(linFrame msg); // form 9
description: Description Sets the values of the signals in the parameter to the start values defined in the database.
parameters: Parameters msg, frame, pdu, paramGroup, parameter Objects where the signals shall be set. uninitializedData (Form 4, Form 6) Value to which the bytes in the frame / PDU shall be set which are not used by signals.
returnValues: Return Values 0 function was successful 1 message / frame / PDU / paramGroup wasn't found in the database 2 at least one signal start value didn't fit into the signal in the message

name: StandardFrameRate
syntax: Function Syntax long StandardFrameRate ()
description: Description Returns the current rate of standard CAN messages on channel x . Valid x values: 1…32
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Current rate of standard CAN frames on channel x in messages per second.

name: StandardRemoteFrameRate
syntax: Function Syntax long StandardRemoteFrameRate ()
description: Description Returns the current rate of standard remote CAN frames of channel x . Valid x values: 1…32
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Current rate of standard remote CAN frames of channel x in messages per second.

name: TxChipErrorCount
syntax: Function Syntax long TxChipErrorCount ()
description: Description Returns the current number of Tx errors in the CAN receiver of channel x . Valid x values: 1…32
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Current number of errors in the CAN receiver of channel x .

name: ValOfId
syntax: Function Syntax long valOfId(dword id); long valOfId(message m);
description: Description Returns the value of a message identifier independent of its type. Identifier as long value.
parameters: Parameters message Variable of the type message. id Id portion of a message.
returnValues: Return Values Identifier as long value.

name: CanDisturbanceTriggerEnable
syntax: Function Syntax long canDisturbanceTriggerEnable(long deviceID, canDisturbanceFrameTrigger trigger, canDisturbanceSequence sequence); // form 1 long canDisturbanceTriggerEnable(long deviceID, canDisturbanceFrameTrigger trigger, canDisturbanceSequence sequence, canDisturbanceTriggerRepetitions repetitions); // form 2 long canDisturbanceTriggerEnable(long deviceID, canDisturbanceFrameTrigger trigger, canDisturbanceSequence sequence, canDisturbanceTriggerRepetitions repetitions, dword flags); // fo
description: Description Configures a frame trigger and a user-defined output sequence on a CAN Disturbance Interface. When the trigger condition is fulfilled, the CAN Disturbance Interface starts sending the given sequence on the bus. The starting point of the sequence can be configured by the canDisturbanceFrameTrigger parameter. A typical use case for this function is to send a sequence within a CAN frame, to disturb a single bit or a range of bits. Constraints If the level of the trigger bit must be a sp
parameters: Parameters deviceID The unique device ID that is configured via the Network Hardware Configuration dialog for a CAN channel. trigger The frame trigger condition for the CAN Disturbance Interface to output the sequence. sequence The user-defined sequence that is sent if the trigger condition is fulfilled. repetitions The trigger repetitions. If the trigger repetitions are not defined, the CAN Disturbance Interface uses default values. flags The flags can be used to set a digital input line as an
returnValues: Return Values 1 Success 0 Error -1 Device ID was not assigned to CAN Disturbance Interface -2 Erroneous trigger definition -3 Erroneous repetition definition -4 Erroneous trigger enabling flag combination -5 Erroneous frame based sequence

name: ApplILTxPending
syntax: Function Syntax dword applILTxPending(long aId, dword aDlc, byte data[]);
description: Description This callback is optionally being called before the IL sends a message to the bus. In this callback it is possible to prevent the sending of the message or to change the data of the message.
parameters: Parameters aId With the ID you can identify the message. aDLC The DLC of the message to be sent and the length of the data bytes array. data Data bytes array with the bytes to be sent. The bytes can optionally be changed.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Sending of the message with the supplied aId is prevented. 1 Sending of the message with the supplied aId is done.

name: ApplILTxRequestConsumed
syntax: Function Syntax void applILTxRequestConsumed ( );
description: Description After the IL clamp 15 state (for ASR PDU IL Ignition state) has been enabled by ILActivateClamp15 (for ASR PDU IL ARILSetIgnitionState ) or any wakeup-allowed signal is transmitted, then optionally this functions is called. Within this function the bus NM sleep timer should be restarted.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ApplILTxRequestPending
syntax: Function Syntax void applILTxRequestPending ( );
description: Description If the IL clamp 15 state (for ASR PDU IL Ignition state) is being enabled by ILActivateClamp15 (for ASR PDU IL ARILSetIgnitionState ) or any wakeup-allowed signal is changed, then optionally this functions is called. Within this function the bus should be requested by the network management DLL.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ILActivateClamp15
syntax: Function Syntax long ILActivateClamp15(); long ILDeactivateClamp15();
description: Description
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -1 The IL is not initialized properly. The function is ignored.

name: ILCalculateChecksum
syntax: Function Syntax long ILCalculateChecksum (char pduNamme[], long type, BYTE payload[], long payloadLength, BYTE crc [])
description: Description Calculates the corresponding CRC checksum based on the payload. The correct offset is calculated from the database using the PDU name.
parameters: Parameters pduName Name of the PDU. type type = 0 : CRC type = 1 : CHK payload Payload for which the checksum is to be calculated. payloadLength Number of data bytes in payload. crc The calculated checksum.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 General error.

name: ILConfigureNMNotifications
syntax: Function Syntax dword ILConfigureNMNotifications (dword direction, dword mode);
description: Description The function allows manipulating the coupling between IL and NM module. The function can be called at any time.
parameters: Parameters direction 0: IL->NM 1: NM -> IL Other values are reserved. mode 0: notifications are off 1: notifications are done always (regardless of existing callback functions) 2 and 3: notifications are done only in absence of certain callback functions (default) Other values are reserved.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error Others Error in module

name: ILControlInit
syntax: Function Syntax long ILControlInit ()
description: Description Initialization of CANoe IL. This function may only be used in on preStart to prevent the IL autostart function.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog.

name: ILControlMsg
syntax: Function Syntax ILControlMsg(dbMsg msg, dword control, dword flags, dword param1, dword param2)
description: Description Allows to manipulate the sending behavior of a message.
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should be influenced. control Specifies the manner of influencing the message. control = 1: Enables sending of the message (comparable to function ILEnableMsg ). Parameter param1 , param2 have no meaning. Parameter flags is evaluated bitwise: 0x1: One message is sent immediately if the message would be sent cyclically under the current conditions. 0x2: One message is sent immediately independently from current conditions. This is also true for noncyclic messages. 0x4:
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. 1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. 50 Node layer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog.

name: ILControlResume
syntax: Function Syntax long ILControlResume ()
description: Description Cyclical sending restarts. This function may only be used after ILControlWait .
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. -50 Node layer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog.

name: ILControlSimulationOff
syntax: Function Syntax long ILControlSimulationOff()
description: Description The simulation of the IL is stopped. After that no other function to control the IL has an effect to the IL. To restart the simulation of the IL use ILControlSimulationOn .
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error.

name: ILControlSimulationOn
syntax: Function Syntax long ILControlSimulationOn()
description: Description Start the simulation of the IL. The IL is in the same state as it was before stopping it by the function ILControlSimulationOff .
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query.

name: ILControlStart
syntax: Function Syntax long ILControlStart ()
description: Description Cyclical sending starts; setting signals is now possible. Signal changes that occurred while the interaction layer was switched off (by ILControlStop or ILControlSimulationOff ) are not considered on its activation.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog.

name: ILControlStop
syntax: Function Syntax long ILControlStop()
description: Description Sending is completely stopped. In this state the interaction layer is inoperative. Neither function calls, nor signal changes are considered during its inactivity and on its activation (by ILControlStart ).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog.

name: ILControlWait
syntax: Function Syntax long ILControlWait()
description: Description Cyclical sending is stopped; setting signals is possible. Cyclical sending is continued only after an ILControlResume .
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. 1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. 50 Node layer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog.

name: ILDisableMsg
syntax: Function Syntax long ILDisableMsg (char msgName[])
description: Description Disables the sending of the message except by calling the function ILSetMsgEvent .
parameters: Parameters msgName Message that should be disabled.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function.

name: ILEnableMsg
syntax: Function Syntax long ILEnableMsg (char msgName[])
description: Description Enables the sending of the message.
parameters: Parameters msgName Message that should be re-enabled after having disabled it with ILFaultInjectionDisableMsg / ILDisableMsg .
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function.

name: ILEnableTimingCyclic
syntax: Function Syntax long ILEnableTimingCyclic(char pduName[], int enable)
description: Description Controls the cyclic timing of PDUs. The cyclic timing can be enabled/disabled. This function influences a simulation node with an assigned CANoe interaction layer.
parameters: Parameters aPDUName Name of the PDU that should be modified. enable 0 = disable 1 = enable
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 General error.

name: ILEnableTimingEvtTrg
syntax: Function Syntax long ILEnableTimingEvtTrg(char pduName[], int enable);
description: Description Controls the event triggered timing of PDUs. The event triggered timing can be enabled/disabled. This function influences a simulation node with an assigned CANoe interaction layer.
parameters: Parameters aPDUName Name of the PDU that should be modified. enable 0 = disable 1 = enable
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 General error.

name: ILEnableTimingImmed
syntax: Function Syntax long ILEnableTimingImmed (char pduName[], int enable);
description: Description Controls the immediate timing of PDUs. The immediate timing can be enabled/disabled. This function influences a simulation node with an assigned CANoe interaction layer.
parameters: Parameters aPDUName Name of the PDU that should be modified. enable 0 = disable 1 = enable
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 General error.

name: ILErrno
syntax: Function Syntax long ILErrno ()
description: Description Returns the last CANoe IL error code .
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Error Code of the last call. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog.

name: ILFaultInjectionDisableMsg
syntax: Function Syntax long ILFaultInjectionDisableMsg (dbMsg msg)
description: Description Disables the sending of the message except by calling the function ILSetMsgEvent .
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should be disabled.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node.

name: ILFaultInjectionEnableMsg
syntax: Function Syntax long ILFaultInjectionEnableMsg (dbMsg msg)
description: Description Enables the sending of the message.
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should be re-enabled after having disabled it with ILFaultInjectionDisableMsg .
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node.

name: ILFaultInjectionResetAllFaultInjections
syntax: Function Syntax long ILFaultInjectionResetAllFaultInjections()
description: Description Resets the fault injection settings for all messages of the node.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog.

name: ILFaultInjectionResetMsgCycleTime
syntax: Function Syntax long ILFaultInjectionResetMsgCycleTime(dbMsg msg)
description: Description Resets the cycle time of the message to the database cycle time, after having manipulated the cycle time with ILFaultInjectionSetMsgCycleTime .
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should get the database cycle time.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node.

name: ILFaultInjectionResetMsgDlc
syntax: Function Syntax long ILFaultInjectionResetMsgDlc (dbMsg msg)
description: Description Resets the DLC of the message to the database DLC, after having manipulated the DLC with ILFaultInjectionSetMsgDlc .
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should get the database DLC.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node.

name: ILFaultInjectionResetMsgLength
syntax: Function Syntax long ILFaultInjectionResetMsgLength (dbMsg msg)
description: Description Resets the message length (in bytes) of the message to the database, after having manipulated the message legth with ILFaultInjectionSetMsgLength .
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should get the database message length.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node.

name: ILFaultInjectionSetMsgCycleTime
syntax: Function Syntax long ILFaultInjectionSetMsgCycleTime (dbMsg msg, double value)
description: Description Assigns a new cycle time to the message. To set the cycle time back to its original value, use ILFaultInjectionResetMsgCycleTime .
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should get a new cycle time. value New cycle time of the message in milliseconds.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node.

name: ILFaultInjectionSetMsgDlc
syntax: Function Syntax long ILFaultInjectionSetMsgDlc (dbMsg msg, dword dlc)
description: Description Assigns a new DLC to the message. To set the DLC back to its original value, use ILFaultInjectionResetMsgDlc .
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should get a new DLC. dlc New DLC of the message.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node.

name: ILFaultInjectionSetMsgLength
syntax: Function Syntax ILFaultInjectionSetMsgLength(dbMsg msg, dword msgLength)
description: Description Assigns a new message length (in bytes) to the message. To set the message length back to its original value, use ILFaultInjectionResetMsgLength .
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should get a new message length. msgLength New message length (in bytes) of the message.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this function. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node.

name: ILILSetAutoStartParam
syntax: Function Syntax long ILSetAutoStartParam(dword autoStartParam)
description: Description Defines the behavior of the interaction layer at the start of measurement. If used at all, the function must be called within the on preStart event handler of a CAPL program.
parameters: Parameters autoStartParam State after measurement start (other values are reserved): 0: Waiting 1: Stopped 2: Starting Note The default behavior (i.e. when ILSetAutoStartParam is omitted) is analogous to ILSetAutoStartParam(2) .
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -1 General error

name: ILNodeControlMsg
syntax: Function Syntax ILNodeControlMsg(dbMsg msg, dword control, dword flags, dword param1, dword param2); // form 1 ILNodeControlMsg(char messageName[], dword control, dword flags, dword param1, dword param2); // form 2 ILNodeControlMsg(dword msgId, dword control, dword flags, dword param1, dword param2); // form 3
description: Description This generic function influences the sending of a certain message of a simulation node with an assigned CANoe Interaction Layer. The meaning of the parameters flags, param1 and param2 depends on the parameter control.
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should be influenced. messageName Name of the message that should be influenced. msgId CAN identifier of the message that should be influenced. control Specifies the manner of influencing the message. control = 1: Enables sending of the message (comparable to function ILEnableMsg ) Parameter param1 , param2 have no meaning. Parameter flags is evaluated bitwise: 0x1: One message is sent immediately if the message would be sent cyclically under the current conditions. 0
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeControlResume
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeControlResume(char aNodeName[]); // form 1 long ILNodeControlResume(char aNodeName[],dword flags); // form 2
description: Description Restarts the interaction layer of the specified simulation node. Cyclical sending of messages is restarted. The specified node must be in the current bus context. In all other cases the function has no effect and will return with an error.
parameters: Parameters aNodeName Name of the node of which the interaction layer should be started. flags 0: Signals/PDUs are sent with the last current values (of the latest updated buffer content). 1: Signals/PDUs are sent with Init value, corresponds to form 1.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeControlSimulationOff
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeControlSimulationOff(char aNodeName[])
description: Description Stops the simulation of the interaction layer of the specified simulation node. In this state the interaction layer does not react on any function besides turning the simulation on again. The specified node must be in the current bus context. In all other cases the function has no effect and will return with an error.
parameters: Parameters aNodeName Name of the node of which the interaction layer should be turned off.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeControlSimulationOn
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeControlSimulationOn(char aNodeName[])
description: Description Start the simulation of the interaction layer of the specified simulation node. The IL is in the same state as it was before stopping. The specified node must be in the current bus context. In all other cases the function has no effect and will return with an error.
parameters: Parameters aNodeName Name of the node of which the interaction layer should be turned on.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeControlStart
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeControlStart(char aNodeName[])
description: Description Restarts the interaction layer of the specified simulation node. Cyclical sending of messages is restarted and setting of signals is possible again. The specified node must be in the current bus context. In all other cases the function has no effect and will return with an error.
parameters: Parameters aNodeName Name of the node of which the interaction layer should be started.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeControlStop
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeControlStop(char aNodeName[])
description: Description Stops the interaction layer of the specified simulation node. Cyclical sending of messages is stopped and setting of signals is not possible anymore. The specified node must be in the current bus context. In all other cases the function has no effect and will return with an error.
parameters: Parameters aNodeName Name of the node of which the interaction layer should be stopped.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeControlWait
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeControlWait(char aNodeName[]); // form 1 long ILNodeControlWait(char aNodeName[],dword flags); // form 2
description: Description Stops the interaction layer of the specified simulation node. Cyclical sending of messages stops but setting of signals is still possible. The specified node must be in the current bus context. In all other cases the function has no effect and will return with an error.
parameters: Parameters aNodeName Name of the node of which the interaction layer should be stopped. flags 0: data update allowed 1: data update ignored, corresponds to form 1
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeDisturbAllNodesUpdateBits
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeDisturbAllNodesUpdateBits(long flags, long disturbanceMode, long disturbanceCount, long disturbanceValue);
description: Description Modifies all update bits of signals/signal groups of all nodes in the current bus context. Different fault injections are possible. This function influences a simulation node with an assigned CANoe interaction layer.
parameters: Parameters flags The lowest two bits of this parameter are evaluated: 0 Update bits of signals and signal groups are disturbed. 1 Only signal update bits are disturbed. 2 Only signal group update bits are disturbed. disturbanceMode Identifies the disturbance mode: 0 Value The disturbance uses the value in disturbanceValue as update bit. 1 Freeze The current update bit value is transmitted. 2 Random A random value is transmitted as update bit. disturbanceCount -1 Infinite. 0 Stops the disturbance
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeResetPDUAsrTxMode
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeResetPDUAsrTxMode (dbMsg dbMessage)
description: Description Resets the current valid transmission mode to the condition that is defined by or-ing all data filters of all signals.
parameters: Parameters dbMessage The symbolic name of a PDU in the database.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeResetPDUTimingCyclic
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeResetPDUTimingCyclic (dbMsg dbMessage, long TrueOrFalse)
description: Description Resets the cyclic-timing to the values from the database. Possibly two timings ( False and True ) exist in parallel and are selected upon the current transmission mode of the PDU.
parameters: Parameters dbMessage The symbolic name of a PDU in the database. TrueOrFalse 0: denotes the False timing 1: denotes the True timing 3: denotes the True and the False timing
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeResetPDUTimingEvent
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeResetPDUTimingEvent (dbMsg dbMessage, long TrueOrFalse)
description: Description Resets the event-timing to the values from the database. Possibly two timings ( False and True ) exist in parallel and are selected upon the current transmission mode of the PDU.
parameters: Parameters dbMessage The symbolic name of a PDU in the database. TrueOrFalse 0: denotes the False timing 1: denotes the True timing 3: denotes the True and the False timing
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeSetAllNodesOperationMode
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeSetAllNodesOperationMode(long mode, long param)
description: Description Sets specific operation mode in the interaction layer of all nodes in the current bus context.
parameters: Parameters mode mode - 0: influences update bits; other values have currently no effect and are reserved. param The effect of param depends on the parameter mode : Mode Value Param Value 0 0: all update bits will be set or reset depending on the signal updates 1: all update bits will always be sent with value = 1
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeSetOperationMode
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeSetOperationMode(char aNodeName[], long mode, long param); // form 1 long ILNodeSetOperationMode(dbMsg message, char signalGroupName[], long mode, long param); //(2) long ILNodeSetOperationMode(dbSignal signal, long mode, long param); // form 3
description: Description Form 1: Sets specific operation mode in the interaction layer of the specified simulation node. The specified node must be in the current bus context. In all other cases the function has no effect and will return with an error. Form 2: Sets specific operation mode in the interaction layer for the specified signal group of the specified message. Form 3: Sets specific operation mode in the interaction layer for the specified signal.
parameters: Parameters aNodeName Name of the node of which the specific operation mode should be set. Supported qualification patterns for form 1: [DBName::]aNodeName signalGroupName Name of the signal group. message Message of which the specific operation mode should be set. signal Name of the update bit signal of which the specific operation mode should be set. mode mode - 0: influences update bits; other values have currently no effect and are reserved. param The effect of param depends on the parameter
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeSetPDUAsrTxMode
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeSetPDUAsrTxMode (dbMsg dbMessage, long mode, long disturbanceCount, long flags)
description: Description Overrides the current valid transmission mode of the PDU. The transmission mode can be False or True . The current transmission mode is defined by or-ing the current data filter condition value of all signal values of the PDU.
parameters: Parameters dbMessage The symbolic name of a PDU in the database. mode Sets the True timing ( 1 ) or the False timing ( 0 ) to be active (regardless of the signal values). disturbanceCount Reserved/unused; should be set to -1 ("infinite"). flags Reserved; should be set to 0 .
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeSetPDUTimingCyclic
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeSetPDUTimingCyclic (dbMsg dbMessage, long TrueOrFalse, long offset, long period, long disturbanceCount, long flags)
description: Description Overrides the defined cyclic-timing from the database. Possibly two timings ( False and True ) exist in parallel and are selected upon the current transmission mode of the PDU.
parameters: Parameters dbMessage The symbolic name of a PDU in the database. TrueOrFalse 0: denotes the False timing 1: denotes the True timing 3: denotes the True and the False timing offset Defines the delay in [ms] from now when the first cyclic transmission will start. period Defines the period in [ms] for the cyclic transmission. disturbanceCount Reserved/unused; should be set to -1 (infinite) flags Reserved; should be set to 0 .
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILNodeSetPDUTimingEvent
syntax: Function Syntax long ILNodeSetPDUTimingEvent (dbMsg dbMessage, long TrueOrFalse, long enable, long repetitionPeriod, long repetitionCount, long debounceDelay, long disturbanceCount, long flags)
description: Description Overrides the defined event timing from the database. Possibly two timings ( False and True ) exist in parallel and are selected upon the current transmission mode of the PDU. Only when an enabled event timing exists, the PDU can be transmitted regardless of any cyclic timing if any transfer property of a signal or signal group is firing/triggering.
parameters: Parameters dbMessage The symbolic name of a PDU in the database. TrueOrFalse 0: denotes the False timing 1: denotes the True timing 3: denotes the True and the False timing enable Enables ( 1 ) or disables ( 0 ) the appropriate event timing. repetitionPeriod Defines the period in [ms] for the possible repeated transmissions (only valid and used, when repetitionCount > 0 ). repetitionCount Defines the amount of repeated transmissions after the event occurred ( 0 means only one transmission withou
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -10000 Unspecific error -10001 Node or module not found -10002 No suitable module available -10003 Function is not supported by module -10004 Module returns illegal value other Error in module

name: ILResetAllCANFDParam
syntax: Function Syntax long ILResetAllCANFDParam()
description: Description The function resets CAN FD parameters of all messages to database values.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 ok -53 CAN FD not supported by CANoe version

name: ILSetCANFDParam
syntax: Function Syntax long ILSetCANFDParam(dword ID, int FDF, int BRS, int DLC) long ILSetCANFDParam(dbMsg msg, int FDF, int BRS, int DLC)
description: Description The function allows setting of CAN FD parameters for a specific message.
parameters: Parameters ID CAN-ID of message that should be modified. msg message that should be modified. FDF -2: reset FDF setting of message according to database -1: do not change FDF setting of message 0: FDF off 1: FDF on This parameter has highest priority and will overrule settings for BRS and DLC, i.e. BRS = 0 and max DLC = 8 in case that FDF off. BRS -2: reset BRS settings of message according to database -1: do not change BRS setting of message 0: BRS off 1: BRS on DLC -2: reset DLC settings of me
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error -53 CAN FD not supported by CANoe version -100 message/message ID not found, because not handled by IL

name: ILSetEvent
syntax: Function Syntax long ILSetEvent (dbSignal sig)
description: Description Sends the transferred signal directly to the bus if the network is active. The send model is ignored.
parameters: Parameters sig Signal that should be sent. Specify as follows 'Message::Signal'.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. 1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node -104 General error for invalid calls.

name: ILSetMsgEvent
syntax: Function Syntax long ILSetMsgEvent (dbMsg msg)
description: Description Sends the transferred message directly to the bus if the network is active. The send model is ignored.
parameters: Parameters msg Message that should be sent.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node. -104 General error for invalid calls.

name: ILSetResultString
syntax: Function Syntax long ILSetResultString (VILResult aResult, char *aText, long aLenText)
description: Description Converts the transferred error code to text.
parameters: Parameters aResult Return value of a CANoe IL API function. aText Char array. aLenText Length of the char array.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -103 Invalid value was passed.

name: ILSetSignalRawField
syntax: Function Syntax long ILSetSignalRawField (dbSignal sig, const char *pData, long aDataLen)
description: Description Sets the transferred signal to the provided value. Use for integer signals > 32 bit.
parameters: Parameters sig Signal that should be set. Specify as follows 'Message::Signal'. pData Byte array, which contains the raw data. aDataLen Length of the byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error. -1 Momentary state of the IL does not permit this query. -50 Nodelayer is inactive — possibly deactivated in the node's configuration dialog. -100 Signal or message was not found in the database or is not mapped to this node. -101 The maximum possible value range was exceeded (no sending on the bus). -103 Invalid value was passed.

name: sCANopenDomainServerOpenFileRead

returnValues: Return Values The return value is the file handle that must be used for read operations. If an error occurs, the return value is 0 .

name: sCANopenDomainServerSetFileContents

Value range 1..65.535. subIndex Subindex of the object. Value range 0..255. filename Name of the file which will be used for the domain data entry. errCode 0: Ok. 1: No file selected for the domain data object in the CANopen Setup window. 2: Object entry does not exist. 3: The file does not exist or cannot be opened.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: sCANopenEmergency

name: sCANopenInternalNMTCommand

parameters: Parameters nodeID ID of the simulated CANopen node. NMTCommand Value of the NMT command to be executed: 0x01: Start node 0x02: Stop node 0x80: Enter pre-operational 0x81: Reset node 0x82: Reset communication
returnValues: Return Values The data as qword.

name: sCANopenLssActivateBitTimingParameters
syntax: Function Syntax dword CANopenLssActivateBitTimingParameters(dword switchDelay);
description: Description The LSS master activates the bit timing of a LSS slave. The bit timing can be configured using the function CANopenLssConfigureBitTimingParameters .
parameters: Parameters switchDelay Delay in ms until the bit timing is active.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful 1 Invalid channel (not CAN or inactive)

name: sCANopenLssConfigNodeId
syntax: Function Syntax dword CANopenLssConfigNodeId (byte nodeId);
description: Description The LSS master configures the node ID of a LSS slave.
parameters: Parameters nodeId New node ID of the LSS slave.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful 1 Invalid channel (not CAN or inactive) 2 Invalid node ID

name: sCANopenLssConfigureBitTimingParameters
syntax: Function Syntax dword CANopenLssConfigureBitTimingParameters (byte tableSelector, byte tableIndex);
description: Description The LSS master configures the bit timing of a LSS slave.
parameters: Parameters tableSelector 0 : bit timing table according to CiA 301 0x80..0xFE : manufacturer-specific bit timing table tableIndex Index of the bit timing table to be used.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful 1 Invalid channel (not CAN or inactive)

name: sCANopenLssFastScan
syntax: Function Syntax dword CANopenLssFastScan (dword vendorID, dword productCode, dword revisionNumber, dword timeout);
description: Description The LSS master starts a LSS fast scan to identify an unconfigured LSS slave. The callback is called if an unconfigured slave is found during the scan.
parameters: Parameters channelID Number of the CAN channel. vendorID Vendor-ID of the device. productCode Product code of the device. revisionNumber Revision number of the device. serialNumber Serial number of the device. timeout Distance between each LSS message of the fast scan in ms.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful 1 Invalid channel (not CAN or inactive)

name: sCANopenLssIdentNonConfigSlave
syntax: Function Syntax dword CANopenLssIdentNonConfigSlave ();
description: Description The LSS master commands all LSS slaves without configured node-ID to identify themselves.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful 1 Invalid channel (not CAN or inactive)

name: sCANopenLssIdentRemoteSlave
syntax: Function Syntax dword CANopenLssIdentRemoteSlave (dword vendorId, dword productCode, dword revisionNoLow, dword revisionNoHigh, dword serialNoLow, dword serialNoHigh);
description: Description The LSS master commands all LSS slaves whose LSS address matches the transmitted LSS address parameters to identify themselves.
parameters: Parameters vendorID Vendor-ID of the device. productCode Product code of the device. revisionNoLow Lower limit of the revision number. revisionNoHigh Upper limit of the revision number. serialNoLow Lower limit of the serial number. serialNoHigh Upper limit of the serial number.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful 1 Invalid channel (not CAN or inactive) 4 Invalid high limit of the serial number 5 Invalid high limit of the revision number

name: sCANopenLssSetUnconfiguredNodeId
syntax: Function Syntax dword CANopenLssSetUnconfiguredNodeId (); // form 1 dword CANopenLssSetUnconfiguredNodeId (byte nodeID); // form 2
description: Description CANopen simulation nodes boot up with the node ID configured in the CANopen Configuration Window. If the option Responds as LSS Server is active, the simulation can be put in an unconfigured state which means that the node does not boot up with the configured node ID. Instead, it will stay in a state before PreOperational and wait until an LSS master assigns a node ID. When the function is called during a running simulation, the node will immediately switch into this state, i.e. it w
parameters: Parameters nodeID Specifies the node which should be switched to unconfigured.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful 1 Node with the specified NodeID not found 2 LSS server not active for this simulation node

name: sCANopenUpload
syntax: Function Syntax void CANopenUpload( dword id, dword index, dword subIndex, dword blockMode, dword errCode[] ); // form 1 void CANopenUpload( dword clientCOBID, dword serverCOBID, dword index, dword subIndex, dword blockMode, dword errCode[] ); // form 2 void CANopenUpload( char[] namespace, char[] variable, dword blockMode, dword errCode[] ); // form 3 void CANopenUpload( SysVarName, dword blockMode, dword errCode[] ); // form 4
description: Description Reads an entry from the object dictionary of another node using the SDO protocol. When the node responds with the requested data, the callback OnCANopenUploadResponse is called. If the data transfer is aborted, the callback OnCANopenAbort is called.
parameters: Parameters nodeID CANopen node ID of the SDO server, i.e. the node that contains the object dictionary with the entry to be read. Value range 1..127 id nodeId for form 1, serverCOBID for form 2 clientCOBID CAN ID of the SDO client serverCOBID CAN ID of the SDO server index Index of the object, value range 1..65.535 subIndex Subindex of the object, value range 0..255 size Size of the data in bytes blockMode 0: Use expedited or segmented data transfer 1: Use block data transfer errCode Error code
returnValues: Return Values —

name: sCANopenUploadAll
syntax: Function Syntax void CANopenUploadAll (dword serverNodeID, dword blockMode, dword[] errCode);
description: Description Reads all objects from the object dictinary of another node. The callback OnCANopenUploadAllResponse is called after all entries are read and the corresponding system variables are updated. The callbacks OnCANopenUploadResponse and OnCANopenAbort are called for each object in the object dictionary similar to the function CANopenUpload .
parameters: Parameters serverNodeID Node ID of the SDO server. blockMode 0 : Do not use SDO block transfer. 1: Use SDO block transfer. errCode Error code buffer (is entered in index 0 of the field): 0 : Operation can be started. 1 : Operation already running. 5 : No CANopen license. 6 : No CAN channel.
returnValues: Return Values —

description: Description Reads an entry of type DOMAIN from the object dictionary of another node... form 1: ... by given node ID. form 2, 3: ... by given system variable. form 4: ... by given COB-ID.
parameters: Parameters nodeID Node ID of the SDO server, i.e. the node that contains the object dictionary with the entry to be read. Value range 1..127 clientCOBID CAN ID of the SDO client serverCOBID CAN ID of the SDO server index Index of the object, value range 1..65.535 id nodeID for form 1-3, serverCOBID for form 4 subIndex Subindex of the object, value range 0..255 domainDataFile Path to a file into which the domain data will be written. If empty, the callbacks CANopenDomainUploadBegin and CANopenDom
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CoTfsActivateGuardingReqMonitor
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsActivateGuardingReqMonitor ( dword nodeID, dword guardTime, dword tolerance );
description: Description When this function is called, it is checked whether the DUT Device Under Test makes available the remote frame for the guarding within the defined times. For each correct occurrence, the callback void coTfsOnGuardingRTRMsg(dword nodeId) is called. If the time is not adhered to, then the callback void coTfsOnGuardingRTRFail(dword nodeId, dword cause) is called once. After an error has occurred, the callback system is automatically disabled. The reason for the error is specified in the
parameters: Parameters nodeID Node-ID of the DUT. guardTime Guarding time in us. tolerance Permitted time deviation of the target device in us. It is recommended that you use an even value. The tolerated time frame within which a message is still accepted is: x - (delta/2) <= x <= x + (delta/2)
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsActivateHeartbeatMonitor
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsActivateHeartbeatMonitor ( dword nodeID, dword producerTime, dword tolerance );
description: Description This function checks whether the DUT Device Under Test as heartbeat producer makes the heartbeat message available within the defined times. For each correct occurrence, the callback void coTfsOnHeartbeatMsg (dword nodeId) is called. If the time is not adhered to, then the callback void coTfsOnHeartbeatFail(dword nodeId, dword cause) is called. After an error has occurred, the callback system is automatically disabled. The reason for the error is specified in the parameter cause : 1
parameters: Parameters nodeID Node-ID of the DUT. producerTime Heartbeat time in us. tolerance Permitted time deviation of the target device in us. It is recommended that you use an even value. The tolerated time frame within which a message is still accepted is: x - (delta/2) <= x <= x + (delta/2)
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsActivateSyncMonitor
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsActivateSyncMonitor ( dword canId, dword producerTime, dword tolerance, dword windowLength, dword maxCounter );
description: Description This function checks whether the DUT Device Under Test as sync producer makes the sync available within the defined times. For each correct occurrence, the callback void coTfsOnSyncMsg (dword nodeId) is called. If the time is not adhered to, then the callback void coTfsOnSyncFail(dword nodeId, dword cause) is called. After an error has occurred, the callback system is automatically disabled. The reason for the error is specified in the parameter cause : 1 = Message distance too small
parameters: Parameters canID CANopen® CAN-ID of the DUT. producerTime Sync time in micro seconds. tolerance Permitted time deviation of the target device in micro seconds. It is recommended that you use an even value. The tolerated time frame within a message is still accepted is: x - (delta/2) <= x <= x + (delta/2) windowLength Synchronous PDO window length in micro seconds. maxCounter 1 < maxCounter < 241
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsActivateSyncPdoMonitor
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsActivateSyncPdoMonitor ( dword syncCanId, dword pdoCanId, dword transmissionType );
description: Description This function checks if the DUT Device Under Test sends a synchronous PDO with the given CAN-ID. The check is only available if the sync check was activated with coTfsActivateSyncMonitor . For each correct occurrence, the callback void coTfsOnSyncPdoMsg(dword canId) is called. The synchronous PDO must occur in the defined time slot. If the time is not adhered to, then the callback void coTfsOnSyncPdoFail(dword canId) is called once. After an error has occurred, the callback system is
parameters: Parameters syncCanId Sync CAN-ID to be used. pdoCanId CAN-ID of the PDO to be monitored. transmissionType PDO transmission type, range 1..240.
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsAtoxD
syntax: Function Syntax dword coTfsatoxD ( char inValueBuf[], dword valueBufSize );

parameters: Parameters inValueBuf[] Buffer containing the string. valueBufSize Buffer size in byte of inValueBuf .
returnValues: Return Values Converted string as dword value.

name: CoTfsAtoxL
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsatoxL ( char inValueBuf[], dword valueBufSize );

parameters: Parameters inValueBuf[] Buffer containing the string. valueBufSize Buffer size in byte of inValueBuf .
returnValues: Return Values Converted string as long value.

name: CoTfsCheckAndCompareGenericMonitorMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsCheckAndCompareGenericMonitorMessage ( dword canId, dword dlc, qword msgData, qword msgMask, dword order );
description: Description This function checks if the expected message data are received. After monitoring is started with coTfsConfigureGenericMonitor the received messages can be checked with this function. The parameter order defines to which message the comparison is executed. The check is successfully passed if the message with the expected message number and the expected data is received. The function can be used during a monitoring started with coTfsConfigureGenericMonitor as well as after coTfsDeactiv
parameters: Parameters canId CAN-ID of the monitored message. dlc Message length in byte, [0..8]. msgData Expected message data. msgMask Message data mask, set bits are compared and the message is presumed to be valid if the bits match with the data. order 0: Any receive message must contain the data specified in msgData / msgMask 1..0xFFFFFFFE: The numeric specified message must contain the data specified in msgData / msgMask , 1 is the oldest message 0xFFFFFFFF: The last received message must contain the
returnValues: Return Values Error code

parameters: Parameters nodeId Node-ID of the relevant node or 0 if all nodes are used.

name: CoTfsConfigureGenericMonitor
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsConfigureGenericMonitor ( dword canId, dword isRTR, dword dlc, dword msgData, qword msgMask, dword minOcc, dword maxOcc, char comment[], dword varContent );
description: Description This function configures and activates a generic message monitor. With the monitor you can check the occurrence count of a defined CAN message in the period between configuration and coTfsDeactivateGenericMonitor . The evaluation is done in the coTfsDeactivateGenericMonitor function. Between the two functions any other test functions can be used. The monitored message needn't sent and received cyclically, no time check is executed. Each received message with the correct CAN-ID and DL
parameters: Parameters canId CAN-ID of the monitored message. isRTR 0: Message is a data frame 1: Message is a remote frame dlc Message length in byte, [0..8]. msgData If data frame: expected message data. msgMask If data frame: message data mask; set bits are compared and the message is presumed to be valid if the bits match with the data. minOcc Defines how often the expected message must be received at least. maxOcc Defines how often the expected message is allowed to be received at a maximum. comme
returnValues: Return Values Error code

description: Description This function stops monitoring and evaluates the monitoring results.

returnValues: Return Values Error code

description: Description The function converts a dword value into a byte array.

returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsEmcyGetErrorCode
syntax: Function Syntax dword coTfsEmcyGetErrorCode ( dword nodeID, dword outCanId[1], dword outTimeStamp[1], dword outEmcyCode[1], dword outErrorReg[1], byte outMsCodeBuf[5], dword msCodeBufSize );
description: Description This function transmits as return value the number of received emergency messages for the CANopen® node labeled with nodeID . Each emergency message can only be called up once. The return value contains the number of received messages including the emergency message returned with the function call. All data pointers are only valid if there is at least one message present. It is possible to omit the nodeID parameter. In this case, the internally-stored value set with coTfsSetNodeId is
parameters: Parameters nodeID The function transmits emergency messages from the selected node-ID. If node-ID is set to 0, the messages of all nodes are transmitted. outCanId CAN-ID data field. outTimeStamp Data field on the time stamp of the transmitted emergency message in ms. outEmcyCode Emergency code data field. outErrorReg Error register data field. outMsCodeBuf[] Manufacturer-specific error code (data field). msCodeBufSize Size of buffer in byte of outMsCodeBuf .
returnValues: Return Values Number of available emergency messages.

name: CoTfsEmcyResetList
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsEmcyResetList ( void ); long coTfsEmcyResetList ( dword nodeID );
description: Description This function deletes the internal list on which occurring emergency messages are stored. It is possible to omit the nodeID parameter. In this case, the internally-stored value set with coTfsSetNodeId is used.
parameters: Parameters nodeID All messages of the associated node-ID are deleted; for nodeID=0 , all emergency messages of all nodes are deleted.
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsGuarding
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsGuarding ( void );
description: Description This test checks the life guarding and node guarding functionality of the node. The life guarding tests are executed with the following parameters: guard time: 1s, 500ms, 500ms retry factor: 3, 5, 3 permissible tolerance: 10%, 10%, 5% After that, the node guarding tests with decreasing time tolerances are executed to check the response time. The permissible deviations are 500 ms, 10 ms, 50 ms and 26 ms. To pass the test, all individual test cases must be run through successfully.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsGuardingRequest
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsGuardingRequest ( dword guardTime, dword retryFactor, dword tolerance, dword guardReqNumber, dword waitForEmcy );
description: Description The test sets the guard time and retry factor objects in the DUT Device Under Test . After that, guardReqNumber remote guarding frames are sent to the target device. The target device must respond to each individual query within the tolerance delta. The test run is sequential. After that, the sending of the guarding remote frames is stopped. Depending on the waitForEmcy setting, it is waited for an emergency message ( coTfsSetTimeoutValue ) before guard time and retry factor are rese
parameters: Parameters guardTime Guard time in milliseconds. retryFactor Retry factor tolerance Permitted time deviation of the target device in milliseconds. It is recommended that you use an even value. The tolerated time-frame within which a message is still accepted is: x - (delta/2) <= x <= x + (delta/2) guardReqNumber Number of the remote frames to be sent. waitForEmcy If this value is set to 1, after setting the sending of the remote frames, an emergency message with the following content is awaited:
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsHeartBeat
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsHeartbeat ( dword virtProducerId );
description: Description This function tests the heartbeat consumer and heartbeat producer of the DUT Device Under Test with two different time settings. First, the heartbeat producer is tested for regularity. The test duration is 10 seconds in each case. The heartbeat time is set to 1sec and after that, 200 ms.  The tolerance is 10 or 20%. After that, the heartbeat consumer is tested with a cycle time of 1sec and a tolerance of 10 % and 5%. The test uses internally the functions coTfsHeartbeatConsumer
parameters: Parameters virtProducerId Virtual heartbeat producer, this node may not exist in the test setup.
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsHeartbeatConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsHeartbeatConsumer ( dword virtProducerId, dword consumerTime, dword tolerance );
description: Description A heartbeat consumer is configured on the DUT Device Under Test (object 0x1016, subindex 1). The tester then sends a single heartbeat message as heartbeat producer. Thus a heartbeat event should be triggered for the heartbeat consumer. The tester now waits for an emergency message with the error code 0x8130. The DUT is now reset with a NMT reset command. The test waits for the coming boot-up message. After this test, the device is in the pre-operational state.
parameters: Parameters virtProducerId This "virtual" node emulates the necessary heartbeat producer, this node number may not be assigned in the system to be tested. consumerTime Heartbeat consumer time in ms. tolerance Permissible tolerance (x - (tolerance/2) <= x <= x + (tolerance/2)) for the receipt of the emergency message.
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsHeartbeatProducer
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsHeartbeatProducer ( dword duration, dword producerTime, dword tolerance );
description: Description This function activates the heartbeat producer of the DUT Device Under Test . For this the object 0x1017 with producerTime is described via a SDO write access.  After this, the regularity of the heartbeat message is checked and then the heartbeat producer is switched off again (0x1017 = 0). The test duration must be greater than the permitted time deviation. At least two heartbeat messages of the DUT are awaited, even if the test duration is set shorter.
parameters: Parameters duration Test duration in ms, how long the regularity of the heartbeat producer should be tested. producerTime Heartbeat producer time in ms. tolerance Permitted time deviation of the DUT in ms. It is recommended that you use an even value. The tolerated time-frame within which a message is still accepted is: x - (tolerance/2) <= x <= x + (tolerance/2)
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsHeartbeatProducerCheckIfActive
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsHeartbeatProducerCheckIfActive ( dword duration, dword producerTime, dword tolerance );
description: Description This function tests whether the heartbeat producer on the selected DUT Device Under Test is active. If heartbeat messages are detected by the DUT, then the regularity is tested across a predefined time. The test fails if the heartbeat producer is not active or works outside of the set tolerance. This test works passively, no SDO accesses are executed on the DUT.
parameters: Parameters duration Test duration in milliseconds, how long the regularity of the heartbeat producer should be tested. producerTime Heartbeat producer time in milliseconds. tolerance Permitted time deviation of the target device in milliseconds. It is recommended that you use an even value. The tolerated time-frame within which a message is still accepted is: x - (tolerance/2) <= x <= x + (tolerance/2)
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsLifeGuarding
syntax: Function Syntax long coTfsLifeGuarding ( dword guardTime, dword retryFactor, dword tolerance );
description: Description The test sets the guard time and retry factor objects in the DUT Device Under Test . After that, 20 guarding remote frames are sent to the target device which must respond to all queries within the guardTime+tolerance . Afterwards the sending of the remote frames is stopped. It is waited for the corresponding emergency message (EMCY code = 0x8130, Error Register = 0x11) before the values of the guard time and retry factor objects are reset.
parameters: Parameters guardTime Guard time in milliseconds. retryFactor Retry factor tolerance Permitted time deviation of the target device in milliseconds. It is recommended that you use an even value. The tolerated time frame within which a message is still accepted is: x - (delta/2) <= x <= x + (delta/2)
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: CoTfsLoadDeviceDescription

description: Description This function waits for one of the LSS Master requests which have been configured before. Depending on the request, an appropriate response is sent. If several requests occur during the wait process, all requests are handled. After a single event the function waits for a further event (standard timeout) if at least one wait condition is available. So the whole wait time of the function can exceed the standard timeout. To configure LSS Master requests, you can use the following functi
