name: C2xGetScenarioHandle
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetScenarioHandle(long index)
description: Description Get the scenario handle.
parameters: Parameters index Index of the scenario
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 Handle of the scenario -1 Error occurred

name: C2xGetStationAttributeDouble
syntax: Function Syntax float C2xGetStationAttributeDouble(char[] attributeName); // form 1 float C2xGetStationAttributeDouble(char[] stationName, char[] attributeName); // form 2
description: Description This function returns the actual value of the floating point typed scenario attribute. Form 1 of the function returns values of an attribute of the calling (current) station. Form 2 allows to get values of all attributes of any station in the scenario, even if the scenario station does not have an assigned database node.
parameters: Parameters stationName Scenario station name attributeName Name of the scenario attribute which belongs to the scenario station specified by stationName parameter.
returnValues: Return Values The floating point value of the attribute at the time of function call. -1 Error, specified station and/or attribute name do not exist in the scenario.

name: C2xGetStationAttributeInt64
syntax: Function Syntax int64 C2xGetStationAttributeInt64(char[] attributeName); // form 1 int64 C2xGetStationAttributeInt64(char[] stationName, char[] attributeName); // form 2
description: Description This function returns the actual value of the integer-typed scenario attribute. Form 1 of the function returns values of an attribute of the calling (current) station. Form 2 allows to get values of all attributes of any station in the scenario, even if the scenario station does not have an assigned database node.
parameters: Parameters stationName Scenario station name. attributeName Name of the scenario attribute which belongs to the scenario station specified by stationName parameter.
returnValues: Return Values The integer value of the attribute at the time of function call. -1 Error, specified station and/or attribute name do not exist in the scenario.

name: C2xGetStationColor
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetStationColor(long stationHandle); long C2xGetStationColor(char dbNodeName[]);
description: Description Retrieve the color associated with the ITS Station in the Station Manager. The ITS Station can be identified by its handle or by the name of the associated node in the database.
parameters: Parameters stationHandle Handle of the ITS station. dbNodeName Name of the database node associated with the station. Note : CAPL Macro %NODE_NAME% can be used as value for the dbNodeName parameter to get the station color for a calling ECU CAPL node.
returnValues: Return Values RGB Value (0xBBGGRR) The station color encoded as RGB Value (0xBBGGRR). -1 Error, specified station and/or attribute name do not exist in the scenario.

name: C2xGetStationHandle
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetStationHandle(long packetHandle); long C2xGetStationHandle(char *dbNodeName);
description: Description Retrieve an ITS Station handle associated with a received packet or a database node. The handle can be used as input for the other functions of the Station API.
parameters: Parameters packetHandle Handle of the received packet. This function is only usable in a CAPL callback that had been registered with C2xReceivePacket or with C2xRegisterCallback for Receive Indications. dbNodeName Name of a database node. Returns the handle of the station which is assigned to the database node in the StationManager .
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not available or failure ≠0 Station handle

name: C2xGetStationHandleByStationName
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetStationHandle(long packetHandle); long C2xGetStationHandle(char *dbNodeName);
description: Description Retrieves an ITS station handle by the name of the ITS station. The handle can be used as input for the other functions of the Station API, for instance C2xAssignNodeToStation .
parameters: Parameters stationName Name of the station as displayed in the ITS Station Manager.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Station handle for parameter stationName not available ≠0 Station handle

name: C2xGetStationName
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetStationName(long stationHandle, long length, char name[]);
description: Description Get the name assigned to the ITS Station in the StationManager .
parameters: Parameters stationHandle Handle of the ITS station. length Maximum number of bytes to be copied. name Buffer in which the name is copied.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xGetStationPosition
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetStationPosition(out float latitude, out float longitude, out float elevation, out float heading); // form 1 long C2xGetStationPosition(char* stationName, out float latitude, out float longitude, out float elevation, out float heading); //form 2
description: Description This function returns the position and heading of a station as out parameters.
parameters: Parameters stationName Name of the station defined in a scenario. latitude Latitude of the station as float value longitude Longitude of the station as float value elevation Elevation of the station as float value heading Heading of the station as float value (degrees)
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Error

name: C2xGetStationPropertyDouble
syntax: Function Syntax float C2xGetStationPropertyDouble(char* propertyName); // form 1 float C2xGetStationPropertyDouble(char* stationName, char* propertyName); //form 2
description: Description This function returns the value of a station property of type float that was assigned in a V2x scenario.
parameters: Parameters stationName Name of the station propertyName Name of the station's property Possible property values are: Latitude Longitude Elevation Heading Acceleration YawRate Length Width Height Distance
returnValues: Return Values Value Value of type double -1 Error

name: C2xGetStationPropertyInt64
syntax: Function Syntax int64 C2xGetStationPropertyInt64(char* propertyName); // form 1 int64 C2xGetStationPropertyInt64(char* stationName, char* propertyName); // form 2
description: Description This function returns the value of a station property of type int64 that was assigned in a V2x scenario.
parameters: Parameters stationName Name of the station. propertyName Name of the station's property. Possible property values are: StartOffset ID
returnValues: Return Values Value Value of type int64 -1 Error

name: C2xGetStationSubAttributeDouble
syntax: Function Syntax float C2xGetStationSubAttributeDouble(char[] attributeName, char[] subAttributeName); // form 1 float C2xGetStationSubAttributeDouble(char[] stationName, char[] attributeName, char[] subAttributeName); // form 2
description: Description This function returns the actual value of the floating point typed scenario sub-attribute. Form1 of the function returns values of an attribute of the calling (current) station. Form2 allows to get values of all sub-attributes of any station in the scenario, even if the scenario station does not have an assigned databasis node.
parameters: Parameters stationName Scenario station name attributeName Name of the scenario attribute which belongs to the scenario station specified by stationName parameter subAttributeName Name of the scenario sub-attribute which belongs to the scenario station attribute specified by attributeName parameter
returnValues: Return Values The floating point value of the attribute at the time of function call. -1 Error, specified station and/or attribute name do not exist in the scenario.

name: C2xGetStationSubAttributeInt64
syntax: Function Syntax float C2xGetStationSubAttributeInt64(char[] attributeName, char[] subAttributeName); // form 1 float C2xGetStationSubAttributeInt64(char[] stationName, char[] attributeName, char[] subAttributeName); // form 2
description: Description This function returns the actual value of the integer-typed scenario sub-attribute. Form 1 of the function returns values of a sub-attribute of the calling (current) station. Form 2 allows to get values of all sub-attributes of any station in the scenario, even if the scenario station does not have an assigned database node.
parameters: Parameters stationName Scenario station name attributeName Name of the scenario attribute which belongs to the scenario station specified by stationName parameter subAttributeName Name of the scenario sub-attribute which belongs to the scenario station attribute specified by attributeName parameter
returnValues: Return Values The integer value of the attribute at the time of function call. -1 Error, specified station and/or attribute name do not exist in the scenario.

name: C2xGetTokenBitOfBitString
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetTokenBitOfBitString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], char namedBit[] ); // form 1 long C2xGetTokenBitOfBitString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long bitPosition ); // form 2
description: Description This function gets the value of a bit in a bit string that is specified either by its name (form 1) or by its position (form 2).
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. geo_cnh tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. data . Tokens in the security protocols can be accessed with the path to the token and the prefix messageTree , e.g. messageTree.content.signedData.tbsData.headerInfo.requestedCertificate.toBeSigned.certIssuePermissions.0.eeType . Tokens in the security protocols are read only and can only be accessed on the RX side. namedBit
returnValues: Return Values Value of the bit.

Tokens in the security protocols can be accessed with the path to the token and the prefix messageTree , e.g. messageTree.content.signedData.tbsData.headerInfo.requestedCertificate.toBeSigned.certIssuePermissions.0.eeType . Tokens in the security protocols are read only and can only be accessed on the R
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

Tokens in the security protocols are read only and can only be accessed on the RX side. byteOffset Offset from the beginning of the to
returnValues: Return Values With C2xGetLastError you can check if the function has been processed successfully. 0 — ≠0 Number of copied bytes

name: C2xGetTokenInt
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetTokenInt ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[] ); // form 1 long C2xGetTokenInt ( long packet, char protocolDesignatorl[], char tokenDesignator[], long byteOffset, long length, long networkByteOrder ); // form 2

Tokens in the security protocols can be accessed with the path to the token and the prefix messageTree , e.g. messageTree.content.signedData.tbsData.headerInfo.generationTime . name: C2xGetTokenLengthBit
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetTokenLengthBit ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[] );
description: Description This function returns the length of a token in bit.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. geo_cnh tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. header . Tokens in the security protocols can be accessed with the path to the token and the prefix messageTree , e.g. messageTree.content.signedData.signer.certificate.0.toBeSigned.appPermissions.0.ssp.bitmapSsp . Tokens in the security protocols are read only and can only be accessed on the RX side.
returnValues: Return Values Length of the token in bit With C2xGetLastError you can check if the function has been processed successfully.

name: C2xGetTokenOidElement
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetTokenOidElement(long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long index);
description: Description This function gets an element of the ASN.1 datatype OID (Object Identifier or Relative OID) that is specified by its index to a new value.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet. protocolDesignator Name of the protocol. tokenDesignator Name of the token. index Zero based index of the element.
returnValues: Return Values Value of the element. With C2xGetLastError you can check if the function has been processed successfully.

name: C2xGetTokenPhys
syntax: Function Syntax double C2xGetTokenPhys(long packet, char[] protocolDesignator, char[] tokenDesignator)
description: Description This function returns the physical value of the existing ASN.1 token in the packet. This function succeeds only if following requirements apply: the token path specified by the tokenDesignator parameter exists and belongs to ASN.1 part of the message, setting the physical value for lower protocols like GeoNet or WSMP is not supported the ASN.1 token specified by the tokenDesignator parameter has a raw/physical value conversion factor defined in the database (column Format in the Car2
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket or was provided as callback function parameter protocolDesignator Name of the protocol, e.g. CAM tokenDesignator Name of the ASN.1 token, e.g. cam.camParameters.basicContainer.referencePosition.latitude
returnValues: Return Values With C2xGetLastError you can check if the function has been processed successfully. 0 — ≠0 Physical value of the token Example

name: C2xGetTokenString
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetTokenString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long bufferSize, char buffer[] );
description: Description This function copies characters from the token and adds a terminating \0 .
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. geo_cnh tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. data . Tokens in the security protocols can be accessed with the path to the token and the prefix messageTree , e.g. messageTree.content . Tokens in the security protocols are read only and can only be accessed on the RX side. bufferSize Size of buffer in byte This function adds a terminating \0 . Thus the maximum number of c
returnValues: Return Values With C2xGetLastError you can check if the function has been processed successfully. 0 — ≠0 Number of copied characters

name: C2xGetTokenSubString
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xGetTokenSubString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long byteOffset, long length, char buffer[] );
description: Description This function copies a specified number of characters from a given position inside the token and adds a terminating \0 .
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. geo_cnh tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. data byteOffset Offset from the beginning of the token in byte length Number of characters to be copied buffer Buffer in which the characters are copied This function adds a terminating \0 . Thus, the size of the buffer must be at least one byte larger than length .
returnValues: Return Values With C2xGetLastError you can check if the function has been processed successfully. 0 — ≠0 Number of copied characters

name: C2xInitPacket
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xInitPacket ( void ); long C2xInitPacket ( char protocolDesignator[] ); long C2xInitPacket ( char protocolDesignator[], char packetTypeDesignator[] ); long C2xInitPacket ( long packetToCopy ); long C2xInitPacket ( long rawDataLength, byte rawData[] ); long C2xInitPacket ( long rawDataLength, char rawData[] ); long C2xInitPacket ( long rawDataLength, struct * rawDataStruct );
description: Description This function creates a new WLAN packet. Other functions can access to the newly created packet with the returned handle. Protocol fields that are marked as InitProtocol in the protocol description are initialized.
parameters: Parameters protocolDesignator Designator of the protocol , which should be used for initialization packetTypeDesignator Designator of the packet type packetToCopy Handle of a packet which was created with C2xInitPacket before or handle of a packet which has been received within a callback (< OnC2xPacket >) The header and the data of this packet are copied to the new created packet. rawDataLength Length of rawData in byte rawData/rawDataStruct Raw data of a WLAN packet that is used to initialized
returnValues: Return Values With C2xGetLastError you can check if the function has been processed successfully. 0 — ≠0 Handle of the created packet.

name: C2xInitProtocol
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xInitProtocol ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[] ); long C2xInitProtocol ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char packetTypeDesignator[] );
description: Description This function initializes the protocol for a packet. If necessary further needed lower protocols are initialized, e.g. IPv4. Already initialized higher protocols are deleted. Protocol fields that are marked as InitProtocol in the protocol overview are initialized.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket protocolDesignator Name of the protocol, taken from the protocol overview packetTypeDesignator Type of the packet, taken from the protocol overview
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xIsMsgEnabled
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xIsMsgEnabled (char* dbMessage); //form 1 long C2xIsMsgEnabled (char* dbMessage, char* stationName); //form 2 long C2xIsMsgEnabled (long packetHandle); // form 3 long C2xIsMsgEnabled (char stationName[], long packetHandle); // form 4
description: Description Check if a message is enabled for cyclic sending.
parameters: Parameters dbMessage Name of the message to check. stationName Name of the sending station. packetHandle Packet handle of the message to check.
returnValues: Return Values 1 True 0 False -1 Error

name: C2xIsNodeStarted
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xIsNodeStarted ();
description: Description Check if the node is started.
returnValues: Return Values 1 True 0 False

name: C2xIsPacketValid
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xIsPacketValid ( long packet );
description: Description This function checks, if a received packet has a protocol error. Packets with a protocol error are marked with an error icon in the Trace Window.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that should be checked.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Packet is valid. !0 Packet has protocol errors.

name: C2xIsScenarioStarted
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xIsScenarioStarted();// form 1 long C2xIsScenarioStarted(long scenarioHandle); // form 2
description: Description Form 1 This function checks, if the last loaded scenario in the CANoe configuration is in the running state. The scenario can be started: manually in Scenario Manager Window ; as a result of calling C2xStartScenario() CAPL function. Form 2 This function checks, if the last scenario specified by scenarioHandle in the running state. The scenario can be started by calling C2xStartScenario (scenarioHandle) CAPL function.
parameters: Parameters scenarioHandle (form 2) Handle of the scenario loaded using C2xLoadScenario .
returnValues: Return Values 1 Scenario was started (is running now). 0 Scenario is not running now. -1 Error

name: C2xIsTokenAvailable
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xIsTokenAvailable ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[] );
description: Description This function checks, if the specified token is part of the packet. Note For ASN.1 based protocols, tokens that are not set are not part of the packet between the functions calls of C2xInitPacket and C2xCompletePacket . For other protocols the non-optional tokens are always part of the packet.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket . protocolDesignator Name of the protocol . tokenDesignator Name of the token.
returnValues: Return Values 1 Token is part of the packet. 0 Token is not part of the packet.

name: C2xLoadRoute
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xLoadRoute(char* routeFilePath, char* routeName, int loopFlag) // form 1 long C2xLoadRoute(char* routeFilePath, char* routeName, int loopFlag, out long routeHandle) // form 2
description: Description This function loads the route with the name routeName from the file specified by routeFilePath parameter and adds it to the currently loaded scenario. For form 2, the handle of the loaded route is returned. Note Available file formats are nmea, gpx and kml. The route names in the file depend on the file format: .nmea: name is the same as filename .gpx: name is same as the trk-name given in file .kml: name is same as the Placemark-name given in file
parameters: Parameters routeFilePath The path to the route file, e.g., c:\SomeFolder\TestRoute.gpx. The relative file paths are also accepted with relation to CANoe configuration folder.
returnValues: Return Values 1 Success -1 Route loading error or invalid routeFilePath parameter. -2 (form 1) Route cannot be loaded as route with the same name already exists.

name: C2xLoadScenario
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xLoadScenario(char* scenarioFilePath); // form 1 long C2xLoadScenario(char* scenarioFilePath, out long scenarioHandle); // form 2
description: Description Form 1 This function loads the scenario from the file specified by scenarioFilePath parameter. If the current scenario is running, it must be stopped via C2xStopScenario() function call or by stopping scenario in Scenario Manager window before calling C2xLoadScenario function. This loading call clears all previously loaded scenarios. Form 2 This function loads the scenario from the file specified by scenarioFilePath parameter and returns the scenarioHandle as an output parameter. If
parameters: Parameters scenarioFilePath The path to the scenario file, e.g. c:\SomeFolder\NewScenario.scn. The relative file paths are also accepted, with the relation to the configuration folder of your CANoe . scenarioHandle (form 2) The handle of the loaded scenario.
returnValues: Return Values 1 Scenario load request successfully accepted. The scenario is now loaded in the background. Once the scenario load is completed, the OnScenarioStateChanged will be called with the parameter 0 (scenario load succeded). In the case, when scenario load fails, the OnScenarioStateChanged callback function will not be called. -1 Scenario loading error or invalid scenarioFilePath parameter. -2 Scenario cannot be loaded because current scenario is still running. (form 1)

name: C2xMoveRouteRelativeToRefPoint
syntax: Function Syntax C2xMoveRouteRelativeToRefPoint(char* routeName, char* refRouteName, char* refPointName) //form 1 C2xMoveRouteRelativeToRefPoint (char* routeName, char* refRouteName, int refPointID) //form 2 C2xMoveRouteRelativeToRefPoint(char* routeName, char* refRouteName, char* refPointName, char* stationName) //form 3 C2xMoveRouteRelativeToRefPoint (char* routeName, char* refRouteName, int refPointID, char* stationName) //form 4
description: Description If the current scenario is running, it must be stopped via C2xStopScenario() function call or by stopping scenario in Scenario Manager window. Form 1 and 2: This function sets the reference point defined by refPointName on the route specified by refRouteName . The position is determined by the starting point of the route and the offset of the reference point. The route defined by routeName is translated and rotated such that it is located relative to the reference point in the same a
parameters: Parameters routeName Name of the route that should be moved refRouteName Name of the route where the scenario reference point should be set on refPointName (form 1,3) Name of the scenario reference point refPointID (form 2,4) ID of the scenario reference point stationName Name of the station
returnValues: Return Values 1 Success -1 Specified route(s), reference point and/or station do not exist in the scenario -2 Route cannot be moved because current scenario is still running -3 The reference point cannot be set on the route specified by refRouteName, as the route is too short

name: C2xOutputPacket
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xOutputPacket ( long packet) // form 1 long C2xOutputPacket ( long packet, long txPower ) // form 2
description: Description This function sends a WLAN packet. (form 1) This function sends a WLAN packet with a specific txPower. (form 2) This method extends the existing OutputPacket function and allows to send a frame with a dedicated Tx-power. It can be used in combination with the existing (old) OutputPacket function, which still uses the default-txPower defined in the Setup-Dialog or by SetSendParameters . Note Be aware that this method belongs only to the raw Output-method, the SendMsg-method for period
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of the packet to send txPower Transmission power in dBm
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xParsePacket
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xParsePacket(long packet)
description: Description Reinterprets the packet after externally defined binary payload data has been assigned to the packet. When parts of the packet are already available as byte array from external sources, for example gateways or traffic intersection geometry design tools (for MAP packet), these binary byte arrays can be applied to the packet by using C2xSetTokenData function. Here mentioned are byte arrays representing the whole header payload parts like wsmp_t/data, dsmp/data or btp/data and not the d
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket or was obtained via callback function parameter.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xProtocolAnalyzerAddRule
syntax: Function Syntax C2xProtocolAnalyzerAddRule(char[] scope, long ruleId, char[] ruleName, char[] ruleDescription, char[] helpText, long severity) C2xProtocolAnalyzerAddRule(char[] scope, long ruleId, char[] ruleName, char[] ruleDescription, char[] helpText, long severity, long verboseFindingReporting)
description: Description Add a user defined rule declaration to the Protocol Analyzer. The rule will appear in the rule tree of Protocol Analyzer before any rule violation appears. This allows the user to see all available rules in case no rule violation occurs.
parameters: Parameters scope Text value indicating the position of the rule in the Protocol Analyzer rule tree, each level is separated by the pipe character "|" and corresponds to a separate folder in the tree. E.g. My rules|EU|CAM|Custom Rules . ruleId Integer number which identify the rule, please use numbers above 50000 to avoid interfering with your CANoe  build-in rules. ruleName Short rule name, will be shown in the Protocol Analyzer rule tree. ruleDescription Detailed description of the be
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success not 0 No custom rule was created.

name: C2xProtocolAnalyzerGetRuleList
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xProtocolAnalyzerGetRuleList(long bufferSize, char[] buffer)
description: Description This function copies the multiline text representation of the Car2x Protocol Analyzer rules to the variable specified by buffer function parameter. The [x] and [ ] symbols before each single rule or rule group name represents the active status. The [o] symbol before a rule group means partially active , at least one rule in this rule group was deactivated.
parameters: Parameters bufferSize Maximum number of bytes to be copied. buffer Buffer in which the the multiline text representation of the Car2x Protocol Analyzer rules is copied.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success <0 Error, target buffer to small or error occurred during copying the Protocol Analyzer rules.

name: C2xReceivePacket
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xReceivePacket ( char *onPacketCallback );
description: Description Use this function to register a CAPL callback to receive WLAN packets. The callback has a packet handle as parameter and the functions to access the tokens can be used. The C2xGetThis-functions can be used to access the payload of the highest interpretable protocol. The callback must have the following signature: void <OnC2xPacket> ( long channel, long dir, long radioChannel, long signalStrength, long signalQuality, long packet )
parameters: Parameters onPacketCallback Name of CAPL callback
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xRegisterCallback
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xRegisterCallback(long callbackType, char* callbackFunction); long C2xRegisterCallback(long callbackType, char* callbackFunction, char* dbMessage);
description: Description Register a callback function. The callback can be registered for all or for a specific message. If the dbMessage parameter is present, the function can be called earliest in the On Start event handler. With multiple calls of this function it is possible to register several callback functions for the same message or to register the same callback function for several messages.
parameters: Parameters callbackType 0: Register a Receive Indication callback 1: Register a Pre Transmit Indication callback dbMessage Name of a database message for which the callback is invoked.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xReleasePacket
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xReleasePacket ( long packet );
description: Description This function deletes a packet created with C2xInitPacket . The handle can not be used any longer.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of the packet to delete
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xRemoveNodeAssignment
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xRemoveNodeAssignment(long stationHandle);
description: Description Removes an assigned database node from an ITS station.
parameters: Parameters stationHandle The station handle for which the node assignment should be removed.
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Internal error 1 No station found for parameter stationHandle 2 No error but station had no node assigned to remove

name: C2xRemoveToken
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xRemoveToken ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[] );
description: Description This function removes a token from a protocol header.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket protocolDesignator Name of the protocol tokenDesignator Name of the token
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xReportProtocolViolation
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xReportProtocolViolation (long stationHandle, long severity, char[] scope, long ruleId, char[] ruleName, char[] violationDescription, char[] ruleDescription)// form 1 long C2xReportProtocolViolation(long stationHandle, long severity, char[] scope, long ruleId, char[] ruleName, char[] violationDescription, char[] ruleDescription, int64 timeStamp) // form 2 long C2xReportProtocolViolation(long stationHandle, long severity, char[] scope, long ruleId, char[] ruleName, char[] v
description: Description This function creates a new user defined Car2x Protocol Analyzer protocol violation finding for the specified Car2x packet and shows it in Protocol Analyzer Window.
parameters: Parameters stationHandle Handle of the station. Can be obtained with the CAPL function C2xGetStationHandle . severity 0 – the protocol violation finding will be shown as error. 1 – the protocol violation finding will be shown as warning. scope Text value indicating the position of the rule in the Protocol Analyzer rule tree, each level is separated by the pipe character "|" and corresponds to a separate folder in the tree. For example "My rules|EU|CAM|Custom Rules". ruleId Integer number which i
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success <0 Error, the protocol violation was not created

name: C2xResetPathHistory
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xResetPathHistory() // form 1 long C2xResetPathHistory(long routeId) // form 2
description: Description This function clears path history data for the current station which was previously stored by periodic C2xAddGeoPos calls. Call this function when: station gets a new geographical position, more than 0.15° degree latitude or longitude away from the current position. Otherwise, the (relative) latitude or longitude calculated for the path history points cannot be applied to the message due to violation of the maximal range value. a new identity or use case is set for this station, so t
parameters: Parameters routeId Unique integer number which identify the path history position data storage from where the position data shall be deleted. This concrete routeId value must be previously used in periodic calls to the CAPL Function C2xAddGeoPos . Valid value range: routeId > 0. If routeId parameter is not used (form 1), all previously known path history data will be deleted.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xResetScenario
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xResetScenario()
description: Description If the current scenario is running, it must be stopped via C2xStopScenario() function call or by stopping scenario in Scenario Manager window before calling C2xLoadScenario function. This function resets all scenario objects to their original state as defined in the loaded scenario.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error

name: C2xResizeToken
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xResizeToken ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long newBitLength );
description: Description This function sets the length of a token. In case the token length is increased by full bytes, the additional bytes are initialized with 0.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. wlan tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. data newBitLength New length of the token in bits
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSecCertificateCreate
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecCertificateCreate(long originalCertificateHandle, dword validityInSeconds); long C2xSecCertificateCreate(long originalCertificateHandle, dword validityInSeconds, long signerCertificateHandle);
description: Description Generate a new certificate based on another certificate. The certificate is only valid while the measurement is running and it is automatically discarded afterwards. The certificate is only known to the realtime part of your CANoe . The analysis area only gets knowledge of it when it is transmitted in a message.
parameters: Parameters originalCertificateHandle Handle of a certificate which is used as a template. The new certificate inherits all properties of the original certificate except the key pair and those properties specified by the other parameters below. validityInSeconds The new certificate is at least valid for the specified seconds past the current CANoe simulation time. The validity period of the new certificate starts at midnight of the current simulation day. signerCertificateHandle Handle to a certi
returnValues: Return Values 0 Failure ≠0 Handle of the new certificate

name: C2xSecCertificateGetHandle
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecCertificateGetHandle ( char name[] ); long C2xSecCertificateGetHandle ( byte hashedId8[] );
description: Description Gets a certificate handle from a certificate listed in the Car2x Certificate Manager .
parameters: Parameters name Name of the certificate as it is given in the Car2x Certificate Manager. hashedId8 hashedId8 digest of the certificate (e.g. included in the security header of a received frame), size must be 8 bytes. In some protocols this parameter is called certId8.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Failure ≠0 Handle of the certificate

name: C2xSecCertificateGetHashedId8
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecCertificateGetHashedId8 ( long certificateHandle, byte hashedId8[] );
description: Description Gets HashedId8 digest of a certificate.
parameters: Parameters certificateHandle Handle of the certificate hashedId8 Buffer in which the digest is copied, size must be 8 bytes: In some protocols this parameter is called certId8.
returnValues: Return Values Any number Number of bytes copied. 8 Success 0 Failure

name: C2xSecCertificateGetName
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecCertificateGetName ( long certificateHandle, long length, char name[] );
description: Description Gets a certificate name from Car2x Certificate Manager .
parameters: Parameters certificateHandle Handle of the certificate. length Maximum number of bytes to be copied; make sure size of destination is equal or larger than length of name[] . name Buffer in which the name is copied.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSecCertificateGetSignerHandle
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecCertificateGetSignerHandle ( long certificateHandle );
description: Description Gets the signer (parent) certificate of a certificate.
parameters: Parameters certificateHandle Handle of the certificate
returnValues: Return Values 0 — ≠0 Handle of the signer (parent) certificate

name: C2xSecCertificateGetSignerHashedId8
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecCertificateGetSignerHashedId8 ( long certificateHandle, byte signerHashedId8[] );
description: Description Gets the signer (parent) certificate's HashedId8 digest. In some protocols the digest is called CertId8.
parameters: Parameters certificateHandle Handle of the certificate. signerHashedId8 Buffer in which the digest is copied, size must be 8 byte.
returnValues: Return Values Any number Number of bytes copied. 8 Success 0 Failure

name: C2xSecCertificateGetStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecCertificateGetStatus ( long certificateHandle );
description: Description Gets validity status of a certificate.
parameters: Parameters certificateHandle Handle of the certificate
returnValues: Return Values The validity status consists of 3 bit fields which are combined using a binary OR operation. The overall status is masked with 0xf000 and has the meaning: 0x0000 Internal error. 0x1000 At least one signer in trust chain is unknown. 0x2000 At least one signature in trust chain is invalid. 0x3000 All signatures in the chain are valid and end in a certificate which is marked as trusted in the Certificate Manager . The relevant subject in the trust chain is identified by the mask

name: C2xSecCertificateValidAppSspBitmap
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecCertificateValidAppSspBitmap(long certHdl, dword aid, dword sspLen, byte ssp[], byte msk[], dword checkChain)
description: Description Validates the Service Specific Permission (SSP) bitmask of a certificate.
parameters: Parameters certHdl Handle of the certificate to be tested. aid Application ID / PSID. sspLen Length of the byte arrays ssp and msk (mask). ssp Byte Array The SSP to be tested. msk Byte Array Mask of the bits in the SSP array to be compared. checkChain If set to 1, the whole certificate chain is tested (recommended).
returnValues: Return Values kValidOk 0x00, kValidError 0x01, // unknown / wrong parameter kValidChainIncomplete 0x02, kValidChainInvalid 0x03, // signature wrong or root not trustworthy kValidConditionNotMet 0x04, // country-id or ssp/msk not permitted

name: C2xSecCertificateValidIdentifiedRegion
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecCertificateValidIdentifiedRegion(long certHdl, dword countryId, dword checkChain);
description: Description Validates the validity of a certificate region.
parameters: Parameters certHdl Handle of the certificate to be tested. countryId Country code checkChain If set to 1, the whole certificate chain is tested (recommended).
returnValues: Return Values kValidOk 0x00, kValidError 0x01, // unknown / wrong parameter kValidChainIncomplete 0x02, kValidChainInvalid 0x03, // wrong signature or root not reliable kValidConditionNotMet 0x04, // country-id or ssp/msk not allowed

name: C2xSecPacketGetSignerHandle
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecPacketGetSignerHandle ( long packetHandle );
description: Description Gets the handle of the certificate used by the signer of the packet.
parameters: Parameters packetHandle Handle of the packet
returnValues: Return Values 0 Failure ≠0 Handle of the used certificate.

name: C2xSecPacketGetSignerHashedId8
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecPacketGetSignerHashedId8( long packetHandle, byte signerHashedId8[] );
description: Description Gets the HashedId8 digest of the certificate which was used to sign the packet.
parameters: Parameters packetHandle Handle of the packet. signerHashedId8 Buffer in which the digest is copied, size must be 8 byte.
returnValues: Return Values Any number Number of bytes copied. 8 Success 0 Failure

name: C2xSecPacketGetSignerType
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecPacketGetSignerType(long packetHandle);
description: Description Retrieves how the message signer information is included in the message. The information can be transmitted as digest (HashedId8), certificate or certificate chain.
parameters: Parameters packetHandle Handle of the signed packet.
returnValues: Return Values 0 unsecured 1 digest 2 certificate 3 certificate chain

name: C2xSecPacketGetStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecPacketGetStatus ( long packetHandle );
description: Description Gets validity status of a packet. This is interesting for received packets only and indicates, if the signature of the security header of the packet is valid. In case the signature is not valid, an appropriate error code is returned.
parameters: Parameters packetHandle Handle of the packet.
returnValues: Return Values The validity status consists of 3 bit fields which are combined using a binary OR operation. The overall status is masked with 0xf000 and has the meaning: 0x0000 Internal error. 0x1000 At least one signer in trust chain is unknown. 0x2000 At least one signature in trust chain is invalid. 0x3000 All signatures in the chain are valid and end in a certificate which is marked as trusted in the Certificate Manager . The relevant subject in the trust chain is identified by the mask

name: C2xSecPacketIsSecured
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecPacketIsSecured (long packetHandle);
description: Description Checks if the packet is secured by a security layer ( Security Header or WAVE Security Services ).
parameters: Parameters packetHandle Handle of the packet.
returnValues: Return Values 1 The packet is secured. 0 The packet is not secured.

name: C2xSecPacketSetSignerHandle
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecPacketSetSignerHandle ( long packetHandle, long certificateHandle );
description: Description Assigns a certificate to a Tx packet. The assigned certificate is used to sign the Tx packet when C2xCompletePacket(packetHandle) is called. The protocol type and version of the packet must match the protocol type and version of the certificate. E.g. an ETSI TS 103 097 encoded message cannot be signed using an IEEE 1609.2 encoded certificate.
parameters: Parameters packetHandle Handle of the packet. certificateHandle Handle of the certificate.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSecPacketSetSignerType
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSecPacketSetSignerType(long packetHandle, long signerType);
description: Description Specifies how the message signer information is included in the message. It is possible to add only a digest (HashedId8), to transmit the signer's certificate or to transmit the complete certificate chain.
parameters: Parameters packetHandle Handle of the packet signerType Signer information type 0: unsecured 1: digest 2: certificate 3: certificate chain
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSendMsg
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSendMsg(char* dbMessage); //form 1 long C2xSendMsg(char* dbMessage, char* stationName); //form 2 long C2xSendMsg (long packetHandle); // form 3 long C2xSendMsg (char stationName[], long packetHandle); // form 4
description: Description Send the message with the specified message name.
parameters: Parameters dbMessage Name of the message to send. stationName Name of the sending station. packetHandle Packet handle of the message to check.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSetCycleTime
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetCycleTime(char* dbMessage, int64 milliseconds); //form 1 long C2xSetCycleTime(char* dbMessage, int64 milliseconds, char* stationName); //form 2 long C2xDisableMsg(long packetHandle, int64 milliseconds); // form 3 long C2xDisableMsg(char stationName[], long packetHandle, int64 milliseconds); // form 4
description: Description Sets the cycle time for a message.
parameters: Parameters dbMessage Name of the message. milliseconds New cycle time in milliseconds. stationName Name of the sending station. packetHandle Packet handle of the message to check.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSetRouteStartPoint
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetRouteStartPoint(char* routeName, double latitude, double longitude, double heading) // form 1 long C2xSetRouteStartPoint(char* routeName, char* stationName, double latitude, double longitude, double heading) // form 2 long C2xSetRouteStartPoint(long routeHandle, char* stationName, double latitude, double longitude, double heading) // form 3
description: Description If the current scenario is running, it must be stopped via C2xStopScenario() function call or by stopping scenario in Scenario Manager window. Form 1 All stations positioned on the route specified by routeName are moved such that the position of StartOffset = 0 is located at the projection of the geographical position given in latitude and longitude parameters on the route. The following condition must be met: 1) There is a part of the route which direction does not differ more than
parameters: Parameters routeName (form 1-2) Name of the route routeHandle (form 3) Handle of the route stationName (form 2-3) Name of the scenario station latitude Actual geographical latitude of the station longitude Actual geographical longitude of the station heading Actual geographical heading of the station
returnValues: Return Values 1 Success -1 Speficied route and/or station name do not exist in the scenario. -2 Station(s) cannot be moved because current scenario is still running. -3 Start point of route cannot be changed, because the given position is too far away or the offset of the station(s) is larger than the remaining route length.

name: C2xSetSendParameters
syntax: Function Syntax C2xSetSendParameters(LONG appChannel, LONG txPower, LONG antenna, LONG dataRate, LONG expiry)
description: Description Set transmission relevant send parameters. This method allows to redefine some or all transmission related parameters on the fly; for all following transmissions on a specific VN4610 channel those new default values will be used. In fact the method overwrites the corresponding internal configuration settings provided by the setup-dialog. To set just a subset of the parameters the value <-1> can be used for antenna, dataRate and expiry to leave the corresponding parameters of the conf
parameters: Parameters appChannel Channel to apply settings to (1 .. n), n = available hardware channels (1=Ath1, 2=Ath2,…) txPower Transmission power in dBm; use -100 to keep the original setting. antenna Antenna used for Tx: 1: Ant1 2: Ant2 3: both -1: keep the original setting dataRate Data rate [Mbps] to be used (use 4 to set 4.5Mbps); Allowed values: 3,4,6,9,12,18,24,27 -1: keep the original setting expiry MAC expiry time [µs] 0: never -1: keep the original setting
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code as in C2xConfigureChannel .

name: C2xSetSignal
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetSignal( char *signalPath, int64 value); //form 1 long C2xSetSignal( char *signalPath, int64 value, char* stationName); //form 2
description: Description Sets a signal in a database defined packet.
parameters: Parameters signalPath Path to the signal value to be set in the packet. value New signal value. stationName Name of the sending station.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSetStationAttributeDouble
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetStationAttributeDouble(char stationName[], char attributeName[], long keypointIndex, double value) // form 1 long C2xSetStationAttributeDouble(char attributeName[], long keypointIndex, double value) // form 2 Int64 C2xSetStationAttributeDouble(char* attributeName, float value); // form 3 Int64 C2xSetStationAttributeDouble(char* stationName, char* attributeName, float value); // form 4
description: Description Form 1-2: Sets an attribute value for a scenario station and a particular keypoint as double. Form 3-4: Sets the floating point value for a station's attribute. Internally a keypoint is created at the current point in time.
parameters: Parameters Form 1-2 stationName Name of the station. attributeName Name of the attribute in the station's timeline. keypointIndex The Keypoint index of the attribute (starts at 0). value The value to be set. Form 3-4 stationName Name of the station. attributeName Name of the attribute in the station's timeline. value The value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values Form 1-2 0 Success ≠0 Error occurred Form 3-4 0 OK -1 Error

name: C2xSetStationAttributeInt64
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetStationAttributeInt64(char stationName[], char attributeName[], long keypointIndex, int64 value) // form 1 long C2xSetStationAttributeInt64(char attributeName[], long keypointIndex, int64 value) // form 2 Int64 C2xSetStationAttributeInt64(char* attributeName, Int64 value); // form 3 Int64 C2xSetStationAttributeInt64(char* stationName, char* attributeName, Int64 value); // form 4
description: Description Form 1-2: Sets an attribute value for a scenario station and a particular keypoint as Int64. Form 3-4: Sets the 64-bit integer value for a station's attribute. Internally a keypoint is created at the current point in time.
parameters: Parameters Form 1-2 stationName Name of the station. attributeName Name of the attribute in the station's timeline. keypointIndex The Keypoint index of the attribute (starts at 0). value The value to be set. Form 3-4 stationName Name of the station. attributeName Name of the attribute in the station's timeline. value The value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values Form 1-2 0 Success ≠0 Error occurred Form 3-4 0 OK -1 Error

name: C2xSetStationBaseAttributes
syntax: Function Syntax Int64 C2xSetStationBaseAttributes(char* stationName, float latitude, float longitude, float elevation, float direction, float speed);
description: Description Assigns the data latitude, longitude, direction and speed to a dynamically added station of a scenario.
parameters: Parameters stationName Name of the station. Latitude Latitude of the station (multiplier 10000000.0). Longitude Longitude of the station (multiplier 10000000.0). Elevation Elevation of the station. Direction Heading in euler degrees. Speed Speed in m/s.
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Error

name: C2xSetStationOnRoute
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetStationOnRoute(char* stationName, char* targetRouteName*) // form 1 long C2xSetStationOnRoute(char* stationName, long targetRouteHandle) // form 2
description: Description If the current scenario is running, it must be stopped via C2xStopScenario() function call or by stopping scenario in Scenario Manager window. Form 1 This function sets the station specified by stationName on the route specified by targetRouteName . Form 2 This function sets the station specified by stationName on the route specified by targetRouteHandle .
parameters: Parameters stationName Name of the specific station that should be set on targetRouteName. targetRouteName (form1) Name of the route that the station should be set on. targetRouteHandle (form2) Handle of the route that the station should be set on.
returnValues: Return Values 1 Success -1 Routes and/or station do not exist in the loaded scenario. -2 Station cannot be set on the given route, because current scenario is still running. -3 Station cannot be set on the given route, because the offset of the station is larger than the route length.

name: C2xSetStationPropertyInt64
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetStationPropertyInt64(char* propertyName, int64 propertyValue); // form 1 long C2xSetStationPropertyInt64(char* stationName, char* propertyName, int64 propertyValue); // form 2
description: Description Sets the value of a station property of type int64 in a V2x scenario.
parameters: Parameters stationName Name of the station. propertyName Name of the station’s property. Possible property values are: StationType (only if scenario is not running). propertyValue The value to set for the specified property.
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Error

name: C2xSetStationsOnRoute
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetStationsOnRoute(char* sourceRouteName, char* targetRouteName)// form 1 long C2xSetStationsOnRoute(long sourceRouteHandle, long targetRouteHandle) // form 2
description: Description If the current scenario is running, it must be stopped via C2xStopScenario() function call or by stopping scenario in Scenario Manager window. This function sets all stations currently positioned on the route specified by sourceRouteName on the route specified by targetRouteName .
parameters: Parameters sourceRouteName Name of the route that stations should be moved from targetRouteName Name of the route that stations should be set on
returnValues: Return Values 1 Success -1 Route(s) and/or station do not exist in the loaded scenario. -2 Station(s) cannot be set on the given route, because current scenario is still running -3 Station(s) cannot be set on the given route, because the offset of the station(s) is larger than the route length

name: C2xSetStationSpeed
syntax: Function Syntax Int64 C2xSetStationSpeed(char* stationName, double speed, double acceleration);
description: Description With this function the speed of a station can be changed. The parameter speed defines the speed that has to be reached with a given acceleration (2nd parameter). Note This function does not work for dynamically added stations because their speed is defined by the frequency their positions are provided.
parameters: Parameters stationName Name of the station. The name must match the name of the node in the database. speed The new speed of the station in km/h. acceleration The used acceleration in m/s2 to reach the new speed (in case of deceleration, this will be calculated automatically).
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Error

name: C2xSetStationStartOffset
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetStationStartOffset (char* stationName, double startOffset)
description: Description If the current scenario is running, it must be stopped via C2xStopScenario() function call or by stopping scenario in Scenario Manager window before calling C2xLoadScenario function. This function sets the StartOffset of the station defined by stationName to the given value.
parameters: Parameters stationName Name of the scenario station startOffset StartOffset to be set for the station
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Cannot set the offset because scenario is running or station not available.

name: C2xSetTokenBitOfBitString
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetTokenBitOfBitString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], char namedBit[], long value ); // form 1 long C2xSetTokenBitOfBitString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long bitPosition, long value ); // form 2
description: Description This function sets a bit of a bit string that is specified either by its name (form 1) or by its position (form 2) to a new value. If the bit string does not yet contain the specified bit the bit string is resized up to the specified bit. On resizing the new bits before the specified bit are set to 0.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket . protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. geo_cnh . tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. data . namedBit Name of the bit. bitPosition Zero based index of the bit. value New value of the bit.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSetTokenBitString
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetTokenBitString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long bufferSize, byte buffer[] ); // form 1 long C2xSetTokenBitString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long bufferSize, char buffer[] ); // form 2
description: Description This function sets the bit string of a token.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket . protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. userDefinedPayload . tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. bitString . bufferSize Size of buffer in bits. buffer Buffer in which the data is copied.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSetTokenData
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long length, char data[] ); long C2xSetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long length, byte data[] ); long C2xSetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long length, struct dataStruct ); long C2xSetTokenData ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long byteOffset, long length, char data[] ); l
description: Description This function sets the data or a part of data of a token. It does not resize the token. Use C2xResizeToken to change the length.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket . protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. eth . tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. source . byteOffset Offset from the beginning of the token in byte. length Number of bytes to be copied. data Data that are copied to the token. dataStruct Struct containing the data.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSetTokenInt
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetTokenInt ( long packet, char protocolDesignatorl[], char tokenDesignator[], long value ); // form 1 long C2xSetTokenInt ( long packet, char protocolDesignatorl[], char tokenDesignator[], long byteOffset, long length, long networkByteOrder, long value ); // form 2
description: Description This function sets the integer value of a token. Form 2 with byte offset sets a part of the token data as integer.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket . protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. geo_cnh . tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. lpvSpeed . byteOffset Offset from the beginning of the token in byte. length Length of the integer value, must be 1, 2, 3 or 4 byte. networkByteOrder 0: INTEL (little-endian) 1: MOTOROLA (big-endian) value New integer value.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSetTokenInt64
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetTokenInt64 ( long packet, char protocolDesignatorl[], char tokenDesignator[], int64 value ); // form 1 long C2xSetTokenInt64 ( long packet, char protocolDesignatorl[], char tokenDesignator[], long byteOffset, int64 length, long networkByteOrder, int64value ); // form 2
description: Description This function sets the integer value of a token. Form 2 with byte offset sets a part of the token data as integer.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket . protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. geo_cnh . tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. lpvSpeed . byteOffset Offset from the beginning of the token in byte. length Length of the integer value, up to 8 byte. networkByteOrder 0: INTEL (little-endian) 1: MOTOROLA (big-endian) value New integer value.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSetTokenOidElement
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetTokenOidElement(long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long index, long value);
description: Description This function sets an element of the ASN.1 datatype OID (Object Identifier or Relative OID) that is specified by its index to a new value.
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet. protocolDesignator Name of the protocol. tokenDesignator Name of the token. index Zero based index of the element. value New value of the element.
returnValues: Return Values

name: C2xSetTokenPhys
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetTokenPhys(long packet, char[] protocolDesignator, char[] tokenDesignator, double value)
description: Description This function sets the physical value of the token in the packet. This function succeeds only if following requirements apply: the token path specified by the tokenDesignator parameter belongs to ASN.1 part of the message, setting the physical value for lower protocols like GeoNet or WSMP is not supported the ASN.1 token specified by the tokenDesignator parameter has a raw/physical value conversion factor defined in the database (column "Format" in the Car2x Network Explorer must not
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket or was provided as callback function parameter. protocolDesignator Name of the protocol, e.g. CAM. tokenDesignator Name of the ASN.1 token, e.g. cam.camParameters.basicContainer.referencePosition.latitude. value The physical value of the token.
returnValues: Return Values

name: C2xSetTokenString
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetTokenString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], char data[] ); long C2xSetTokenString ( long packet, char protocolDesignator[], char tokenDesignator[], long byteOffset, char data[] );
description: Description This function copies the string value to the token without terminating \0 .
parameters: Parameters packet Handle of a packet that has been created with C2xInitPacket . protocolDesignator Name of the protocol , e.g. geo_cnh . tokenDesignator Name of the token, e.g. data . byteOffset Offset from the beginning of the token in byte. data Data as string.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xSetTxPower
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetTxPower(long channel, long txPower)
description: Description Sets the txPower for the device mapped to an application channel.
parameters: Parameters channel Channel number of the mapped device txPower txPower to set (dBm)
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -2 Channel number out of range 11 Function call is not valid (e.g. if in simulated mode)

name: C2xSetVerbosity
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xSetVerbosity ( long verbosity );
description: Description This function sets the verbosity level of the Car2x IL. The default setting is that only error messages are written to the Write Window of your CANoe .
parameters: Parameters verbosity Verbosity level 0: do not write messages to the Write Window 1: write only error messages (default) 2: write warning and error messages 3: write information, warning and error messages
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success ≠0 Error code

name: C2xStartNode
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xStartNode(); //form 1 long C2xStartNode(char* nodeName); //form 2
description: Description
parameters: Parameters nodeName Name of the node that starts the transmission
returnValues: Return Values 1 — ≠1 Error code

name: C2xStartScenario
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xStartScenario(); // form 1 long C2xStartScenario(char* stationName, double latitude, double longitude, double heading); // form 2 long C2xStartScenario(char* stationName); // form 3 long C2xStartScenario(long routeHandle, char* stationName, double latitude, double longitude, double heading); // form 4 long C2xStartScenario(long scenarioHandle); // form 5 long C2xStartScenario(long scenarioHandle, long routeHandle, char* stationName, double latitude, double longitude, doub
description: Description Form 1 This function starts the scenario assigned to the current CANoe configuration or the last scenario that was loaded in CAPL using the C2xLoadScenario function immediately. Form 2-3 Form 2: Starts the current scenario at specified geographical position (latitude, longitude) in specified direction (heading parameter). Form 3: Starts the current scenario at the real GNSS of the given station, if corresponding node is configured with Use GNSS-Sysvars in the Network Explorer. The or
parameters: Parameters stationName (form 2, 3, 4, 6) Name of the scenario station. latitude (form 2, 4, 6) Actual geographical latitude of the station. longitude (form 2, 4, 6) Actual geographical longitude of the station. heading (form 2, 4, 6) Actual heading of the station relative to the north. routeHandle (form 4, 6) Handle of the route loaded using C2xLoadRoute . scenarioHandle (form 5, 6) Handle of the scenario loaded using C2xLoadScenario .
returnValues: Return Values 1 Success -1 Error, scenario start was not successful. -2 Error, invalid or out of range parameter specified.

name: C2xStopNode
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xStopNode(); //form 1 long C2xStopNode(char* nodeName); //form 2
description: Description The function stops the transmission of messages for a specified node.
parameters: Parameters nodeName Name of the node that stops the transmission.
returnValues: Return Values 1 — ≠1 Error code

name: C2xStopScenario
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xStopScenario(); // form 1 long C2xStopScenario(long scenarioHandle); // form 2
description: Description Form 1 This function stops the execution of the last loaded scenario immediately. The values of scenario attributes remain constant after the scenario stop. The scenario can be started again by calling C2xStartScenario() CAPL function or manually in Scenario Manager window , if the scenario was loaded in the GUI. Form 2 This function stops the execution of the scenario specified by scenarioHandle immediately. The values of scenario attributes remain constant after the scenario stop.
parameters: Parameters scenarioHandle (form 2) Handle of the scenario loaded using C2xLoadScenario
returnValues: Return Values 1 Scenario was successfully stopped. 0 Scenario stop failed. -1 Error

name: C2xTestGetWaitForMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xTestGetWaitForMessage(long &packethandle) // form 1 long C2xTestGetWaitForMessage(long index, long &packethandle) // form 2
description: Description If a valid Car2x message is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the packet's handle can be called up with form 1. Form 2 can only be used for joined events . The number of the joined event (return value of C2xTestJoin… ) is here being used as an index.
parameters: Parameters packetHandle A packet handle for the awaited Car2x message. Index Number of the joined event corresponds with the return value of C2xTestJoin… .
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Data access could not be executed, the last event was not an Car2x event.

name: C2xTestJoinMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xTestJoinMessage(char* AppMsg) //form 1 long C2xTestJoinMessage(char* AppMsg, char* stationName) //form 2
description: Description Completes the current set of joined events with the transmitted event. This function does not wait.
parameters: Parameters appMsg Name of application message of the Car2x message that should be awaited. stationName Name of the sender station of the Car2x message that should be awaited.
returnValues: Return Values -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. >0 Number of the joined event.

name: C2xTestJoinSignalInRange
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xTestJoinSignalInRange (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, int64 minTokenValue, int64 maxTokenValue) // form 1 long C2xTestJoinSignalInRange (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, int64 minTokenValue, int64 maxTokenValue, char* stationName) // form 2
description: Description Completes the current set of joined events with the transmitted event. This function does not wait.
parameters: Parameters protocolDesignator Protocol designator of the Car2x message that should be awaited. tokenDesignator Token designator of the Car2x message that should be awaited. minTokenValue Lower limit of the value. maxTokenValue Lower limit of the value. stationName Name of the sender station of the Car2x message that should be awaited.
returnValues: Return Values -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. >0 Number of the joined event.

name: C2xTestJoinSignalMatch
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xTestJoinSignalMatch (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, int64 tokenValue) // form 1 long C2xTestJoinSignalMatch (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, int64 tokenValue, char* stationName) // form 2 long C2xTestJoinSignalMatch (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, long dataSize, byte* data) // form 3 long C2xTestJoinSignalMatch (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, long dataSize, byte* data, char* stationName) // form 4 long
description: Description Completes the current set of joined events with the transmitted event. This function does not wait.
parameters: Parameters protocolDesignator Protocol designator of the Car2x message that should be awaited. tokenDesignator Token designator of the Car2x message that should be awaited. tokenValue Token value that should be awaited. dataSize Length of the token data that should be awaited in bytes. data Token data that should be awaited. namedBit Name of a bit in a bitstring thar should be awaited. bitPosition Position of a bit in a bitstring thar should be awaited. The first bit has the bitposition 0. stati
returnValues: Return Values -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. >0 Number of the joined event.

name: C2xTestWaitForMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xTestWaitForMessage(char* appMsg, long aTimeout) // form 1 long C2xTestWaitForMessage(char* appMsg, char* stationName, long aTimeout) // form 2
description: Description Waits for the occurrence of the first Car2x message matching the conditions passed as arguments. Should the message not occur before the expiration of the time aTimeout, the wait condition is resolved nevertheless.
parameters: Parameters appMsg Name of application message of the Car2x message that should be awaited. stationName Name of the sender station of the Car2x message that should be awaited. aTimeout Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling
returnValues: Return Values -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. 0 Resume due to timeout. 1 Resume due to event occurred.

name: C2xTestWaitForSignalInRange
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xTestWaitForSignalInRange (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, int64 minTokenValue, int64 maxTokenValue, long timeout) // form 1 long C2xTestWaitForSignalInRange (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, int64 minTokenValue, int64 maxTokenValue, char* stationName, long timeout) // form 2
description: Description Waits for the occurrence of the first Car2x message with a signal that is inside or equals the given limit. Should the message not occur before the expiration of the time aTimeout, the wait condition is resolved nevertheless.
parameters: Parameters protocolDesignator Protocol designator of the Car2x message that should be awaited. tokenDesignator Token designator of the Car2x message that should be awaited. minTokenValue Lower limit of the value. maxTokenValue Upper limit of the value. stationName Name of the sender station of the Car2x message that should be awaited. aTimeout Maximum time that should be waited [ms]. Transmission of 0: no timeout controlling.
returnValues: Return Values 1 Resume due to event occurred.

name: C2xTestWaitForSignalMatch
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xTestWaitForSignalMatch (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, int64 tokenValue, long timeout) // form 1 long C2xTestWaitForSignalMatch (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, int64 tokenValue, char* stationName, long timeout) // form 2 long C2xTestWaitForSignalMatch (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, long dataSize, byte* data, long timeout) // form 3 long C2xTestWaitForSignalMatch (char* protocolDesignator, char* tokenDesignator, long d
description: Description Waits for the occurrence of the first Car2x message with a signal that matches the given value. Should the message not occur before the expiration of the time aTimeout, the wait condition is resolved nevertheless.
parameters: Parameters protocolDesignator Protocol designator of the Car2x message that should be awaited. tokenDesignator Token designator of the Car2x message that should be awaited. tokenValue Token value that should be awaited. dataSize Length of the token data that should be awaited in bytes. data Token data that should be awaited. namedBit Name of a bit in a bitstring thar should be awaited. bitPosition Position of a bit in a bitstring thar should be awaited. The first bit has the bitposition 0. stati
returnValues: Return Values -1 General error, for example, functionality is not available. 0 Resume due to timeout. 1 Resume due to event occurred.

name: C2xUnloadScenario
syntax: Function Syntax long C2xUnloadScenario(long scenarioHandle);
description: Description Remove the scenario specified by scenarioHandle from the Scenario Manager by unloading it. After this function call, the scenario with handle scenarioHandle is no longer available.
parameters: Parameters scenarioHandle Handle of the scenario
returnValues: Return Values 1 Success ≠1 Error occurred

name: CarMakerBeginFinishWrite
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_BeginWrite( ); long CarMaker_FinishWrite( );
description: Description Groups several write operations to a single atomic write operation. All write operations succeeding CarMaker_BeginWrite are buffered until the call to CarMaker_FinishWrite .
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerConnect
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_Connect (char host[], char user[]);
description: Description Creates a connection to the CarMaker instance running on the selected host by the given user. If the user name is an empty string, only the host name is used to select the CarMaker instance. An IPv4 address is also accepted as host name.
parameters: Parameters host Host name or IP address of the CarMaker host computer. user Name of the user running the CarMaker instance. If this value is an empty string and multiple CarMaker instances are running on the specified host, any instance is connected. If the special name localuser is given the current user of the CANoe RT kernel is resolved and used for the connection.
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerDisconnect
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_Disconnect ( );
description: Description Terminates the connection to CarMaker.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerGetNamedValue
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_GetNamedValue (char name[], char buffer[], long bufferSize);
description: Description Copies the current value with the given name into the buffer provided by the caller. If the size of the buffer is insufficient the value will be truncated and zero terminated.
parameters: Parameters name Name of the named value. buffer Output buffer for the current value. bufferSize Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerInvokeCommand
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_InvokeCommand (char command[], char result[], long resultSize);
description: Description Synchronously calls a generic command and stores the result.
parameters: Parameters command CarMaker script command including parameters. result Output buffer for the result text. resultSize Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerLoadTestRun
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_LoadTestRun (char testRun[]);
description: Description Loads a test run with the given name within CarMaker.
parameters: Parameters testRun Hierarchical name of the test run.
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerLoadTestSeries
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_LoadTestSeries (char testSeries[]);
description: Description Loads a test series with the given name within CarMaker. Although CarMaker_LoadTestRun can also be used to load test series, this function should be preferred. This is because CarMaker may display a warning about unsaved test series that will otherwise interfere with the test.
parameters: Parameters testSeries Hierarchical name of the test series.
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerPoll
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_Poll (char host[], char user[]);
description: Description Manages the internal state and updates the CANoe system variables. If the polling cycle is set to zero in the configuration dialog, automatic polling is disabled, and this function must be called regularly. If the polling cycle is set in the configuration dialog, the automatic polling is enabled and calling this function is not required. The function can also be used to get the current APO return code.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerSetNamedValue
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_SetNamedValue (char name[], char value[]);
description: Description Defines or changes a named value in CarMaker.
parameters: Parameters name Name of the named value. value The new value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerStartCommand
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_StartCommand (char host[char command[]);
description: Description Asynchronously calls a generic command and ignores the result.
parameters: Parameters command CarMaker script command including parameters.
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerStartSim
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_StartSim ( );
description: Description Starts the simulation in CarMaker.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerStartTestSeries
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_StartTestSeries ( );
description: Description Starts test series asynchronously in CarMaker.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_Status ( );
description: Description Retrieves the status of the CarMaker connection.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values No connection 0x10 ApoConnDown 0x11 ApoConnError 0x12 ApoConnTimedout 0x13 ApoConnRejected 0x14 ApoConnDisconnected Connection is being built up 0x20 ApoConnPending Connection established 0x40 ApoConnUp 0x41 ApoConnUnsubscribed 0x42 ApoConnSubscribing 0x43 ApoConnReady 0x44 ApoConnDictChange

name: CarMakerStatusText
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_StatusText (char buffer[], long bufferSize);
description: Description Retrieves a textual representation of the current state.
parameters: Parameters buffer Buffer that takes the status text. bufferSize Size of the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerStopSim
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_StopSim ( );
description: Description Stops the simulation in CarMaker.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerStopTestSeries
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_StopTestSeries ( );
description: Description Stops test series immediately in CarMaker.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values APO return code

name: CarMakerSubscribe
syntax: Function Syntax long CarMaker_Subscribe (char group[], double freq_hz);
