description: Description Allows to activate or deactivate DoIP routing activation retries. Retries are only attempted for specific negative ACK codes that can be given in a list of codes. There is a default list that is used when nackCodesCount is 0 .
parameters: Parameters numberOfRetries Routing activation will be retried this many times. The feature can be deactivated with a value of 0 (default). retryPeriod_ms Time to wait between the failure of the routing activation and the next try. Must be > 0 if numberOfRetries is > 0 . nackCodes Array of negative ACK codes that will lead to a retry. nackCodesCount Number of negative ACK codes in the array. If this value is 0 , a default will be activated that consists of the values 0x04 and 0x11 .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPConfigureVehicleAnnouncement
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_ConfigureVehicleAnnouncement(dword toInitial_ms, dword messageCount, dword toInterval_ms);
description: Description Configures when the Vehicle Announcement Messages are sent.
parameters: Parameters toInitial_ms Initial timeout before sending the first announcement message after measurement start. Note This parameter is ignored, if the function DoIP_AnnounceVehicle is used for sending Vehicle Announcement Messages. messageCount Number of announcement messages sent when the measurement starts, or DoIP_AnnounceVehicle is called. toInterval_ms Time between the announcement messages when more than 1 is configured.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPConnectionClosedInd
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_ConnectionClosedInd( long reason);
description: Description The TCP connection to the peer has been closed, i.e. another exchange of diagnostic messages will require a connection setup. This callback is also called in a vehicle simulation.
parameters: Parameters reason 0: The connection was closed by the node itself 10054: The peer closed the connection Others: Reserved
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPConnectToVehicle
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_ConnectToVehicle();
description: Description Tries to connect to the configured vehicle. A vehicle identification phase may be started to retrieve the vehicle IP address.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPCreatePDUCombination
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_CreatePDUCombination();
description: Description Initialize the combination of several PDUs for sending as one block of data on the TCP connection. If PDUs were already stored they will be discarded.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success Other Reserved

name: DoIPDataCon
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_DataCon( dword count);
description: Description This callback is called from the DoIP/TP layer when the data to be sent was successfully handed over to the DoIP/TP layer. Note : The physical transmission of the data might not have been completed by the DoIP/TP layer (maybe not even been started) when this callback is called.
parameters: Parameters count Size of the sent data in bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPDataInd
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_DataInd( byte buffer[], dword count, dword ecuAddress, dword testerAddress);
description: Description This callback is called from the DoIP layer when new data is received.
parameters: Parameters buffer Buffer containing the received data. count Size of the received data in bytes. ecuAddress Logical DoIP address of the ECU the data was sent to testerAddress Logical DoIP address of the tester that sent/received the data. ( Note : before 8.2 SP4 the value of testerAddress is 0 always.)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPDataReq
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_DataReq( byte buffer[], dword count, dword vehicleAddress, dword testerAddress);
description: Description Request the transfer of the given data to the DoIP peer.
parameters: Parameters buffer Buffer containing the data to be transmitted. count Size of data in bytes. vehicleAddress Logical DoIP address of the ECU, i.e. the target in a tester and the source in an ECU simulation. testerAddress An ECU simulation may specify the logical address of the tester to send to by giving a value other than 0 here. Otherwise and in tester nodes, 0 should be given. This will determine the tester's address automatically, i.e. the configured value in a tester, or the sender address o
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPDisableDiagnosticAckMessages
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_DisableDiagnosticAckMessages(long disabled);
description: Description If called for a tester, the tester will not expect diagnostic acknowledgments from the ECU. Not receiving an acknowledgment is therefore expected and does not raise an error. Unexpected (negative) acknowledgments are ignored and do not cause errors. If called in an ECU simulation, the ECU will not send diagnostic acknowledgments to the tester, even in cases where it would normally send a negative acknowledgment.
parameters: Parameters disabled 0: enabled: send diagnostic acknowledgments (default) 1: disabled: do not send acknowledgments
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPEnableTLS
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_EnableTLS(dword mode, char name[]);
description: Description Enables secure communication for DoIP tester or ECU simulation. If enabled, DoIP communication is carried out via TLS (Transport Layer Security). Note TLS must be enabled before DoIP communication starts. It is recommended to call the function on preStart or on start before configuring TP parameters.
parameters: Parameters mode 0: disable TLS, no secure communication. 1: enable TLS by routing activation response code (RARC) 0x07. 2: (tester only) enable TLS. Only secure connection is allowed. If ECU responds with RARC 0x10 on the first unsecure routing activation, the tester stops communication. 3: (tester only) enable TLS. Tester connects to ECU directly via TLS without establishing connection on unsecure port (e.g. 13400). name (ECU simulation) name of the server certificate, which must be used for TL
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPEntityStatusResponse
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_EntityStatusResponse( char IPaddress[], WORD port, BYTE nodeType, BYTE maxConcurrentTCPsockets, BYTE currentlyOpenTCPsockets, DWORD maxDataSize);
description: Description A response for an entity status request was received.
parameters: Parameters IPaddress Address in text form, e.g. "169.254.32.1" (IPv4) or "2001::1" (IPv6). port IP source port of the packet at the sender. nodeType Type of the node. Currently 0 for gateway and 1 for node are defined. maxConcurrentTCPsockets Maximum number of TCP connections the entity can handle simultaneously. currentlyOpenTCPsockets Number of TCP sockets currently open and in use. maxDataSize Maximum size of a diagnostics request this entity can process. Note : A value of 0 indicates that th
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPErrorInd
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_ErrorInd( int error);
description: Description This callback is called when an error occurred in the DoIP communication.
parameters: Parameters error Type of error that occurred: 0: No vehicle responded to the Vehicle Identification request within the expected timeframe. 1: Connection to vehicle failed. 2: Routing Activation failed. 3: A negative ACK Code was received for a Diagnostic Message. 4: No Ack for diagnostics message received. 5: A "Generic DoIP header negative acknowledge" was received. Other: reserved Note For error types 2, 3 and 5 the response code received from the vehicle is available with DoIP_GetLastResponse
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPForceLocalTCPSendPort
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_ForceLocalTCPSendPort(dword forcedPort);
description: Description Forces local TCP send port to a specific value. According to ISO13400, the local send port for outgoing TCP connections should be dynamically selected. This function allows forcing the local TCP send port to a specific value, typically to 13400 to indicate DoIP. Note that specifying a well-known or system port (1..1023) might not work on most systems.
parameters: Parameters forcedPort 0: use a dynamically assigned value (default) 1..65535: send from this local TCP port other: reserved
returnValues: Return Values 0 success -10 invalid parameter Others reserved

name: DoIPForceLocalUDPSendPort
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_ForceLocalUDPSendPort(dword forcedPort);
description: Description According to ISO 13400, the local send port for UDP messages should be dynamically selected. This function allows forcing the local UDP send port to a specific value, typically to 13400 to indicate DoIP. Note that specifying a "well-known" or system port (1..1023) might not work on most systems.
parameters: Parameters forcedPort 0: use a dynamically assigned value (default) 1..65535: bind to this local UDP port other: reserved.
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: DoIPGetAnnounceInterval
syntax: Function Syntax dword DoIP_GetAnnounceInterval();
description: Description Gets the time between the Vehicle Announcement Messages sent by a vehicle simulation (in milliseconds).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Time between the sent vehicle announcement messages (configured in the DoIP.INI or via DoIP_ConfigureVehicleAnnouncement ).

name: DoIPGetAnnounceMessageCount
syntax: Function Syntax dword DoIP_GetAnnounceMessageCount();
description: Description Gets the number of configured Vehicle Announcement Messages to be sent by a vehicle simulation.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values The number of configured vehicle announcement messages (configured in the DoIP.INI or via DoIP_ConfigureVehicleAnnouncement ).

name: DoIPGetAnnounceWaitTime
syntax: Function Syntax dword DoIP_GetAnnounceWaitTime();
description: Description Gets the delay before the sending of Vehicle Announcement Messages starts in a vehicle simulation (in milliseconds).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values The delay before the sending of vehicle announcement messages starts in a vehicle simulation (configured in the DoIP.INI or via DoIP_ConfigureVehicleAnnouncement ).

name: DoIPGetLastResponseCode
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_GetLastResponseCode();
description: Description Returns the last negative response code received from the peer.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 No response code received yet , or this error does not have a specific response code ≥ 0 Response code, depending on protocol and error type. Please refer to ISO13400 and HSFZ specifications for details. Others reserved

name: DoIPGetSetAliveCheckTimeout
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetAliveCheckTimeout(dword toAliveCheck_ms); dword DoIP_GetAliveCheckTimeout();
description: Description Sets or returns Alive Check Timeout (T_TCP_Alive_Check)) in milliseconds. This function must be called in on preStart .
parameters: Parameters toAliveCheck_ms The timeout parameter in milliseconds.
returnValues: Return Values Form 1 — Form 2 The timeout parameter in milliseconds

name: DoIPGetSetControlTimeout
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetControlTimeout(dword toControl_ms); dword DoIP_GetControlTimeout();
description: Description Sets or returns the timeout for Vehicle Identification Requests, connect and routing activation (in milliseconds).
parameters: Parameters toControl_ms Timeout for Vehicle Identification Requests and connect [ms].
returnValues: Return Values Form 1 — Form 2 Timeout for Vehicle Identification Requests and connect [ms].

name: DoIPGetSetDiagnosticMessageTimeout
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetDiagnosticMessageTimeout(dword toDiagnosticMessage); dword DoIP_GetDiagnosticMessageTimeout();
description: Description Sets or returns the diagnostic/session layer timeout in a tester waiting for the confirmation that the ECU received a diagnostic request. The timer starts after the diagnostic request was handed over to the DoIP/TP layer of the tester and the confirmation is given after a diagnostic message positive or negative acknowledgment was entirely received by the diagnostic/session layer of the tester. Note : As the timer already starts when physical transmission of the request has not been c
parameters: Parameters toDiagnosticMessage An ACK is expected within this amount of milliseconds [ms].
returnValues: Return Values Form 1 — Form 2 Timeout waiting for a diagnostic message positive or negative acknowledgment.

name: DoIPGetSetGenericTimeout
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetGenericTimeout( dword toGeneric_ms); // form 1 dword DoIP_GetGenericTimeout(); // form 2
description: Description Sets or returns the generic timeout. The generic timeout specifies the maximum time of inactivity of the TCP connection before the connection is automatically closed.
parameters: Parameters toGeneric_ms The timeout parameter in milliseconds.
returnValues: Return Values Form 1 — Form 2 Timeout waiting for a diagnostic message positive or negative acknowledgment.

name: DoIPGetSetInitialTimeout
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetInitialTimeout( dword toInitial_ms); // form 1 dword DoIP_GetInitialTimeout(); // form 2
description: Description Sets or returns the initial timeout, started when a tester connects to the ECU (in milliseconds). A route activation request must be received in this interval, otherwise the TCP connection is closed. Note If routing activation requests are switched off in the DoIP.INI via DisableRouteActivation , this timeout is not checked.
parameters: Parameters toInitial_ms The timeout parameter in milliseconds.
returnValues: Return Values Form 1 — Form 2 Initial timeout

name: DoIPGetSetReconnectDelay
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetReconnectDelay(dword reconnectDelay_ms); dword DoIP_GetReconnectDelay();
description: Description Sets or returns the delay started by the tester when the ECU closes the TCP connection. After the configured time the tester attempts at first to re-open the TCP connection and afterwards to re-establish the routing activation. If re-opening the TCP connection fails, the number of further attempts and their interval can be configured with DoIP_SetReconnectInterval .
parameters: Parameters reconnectDelay_ms Delay in milliseconds between TCP connection close and attempt to reactivate it. A value of 0 will deactivate this feature, i.e. the tester does not try to reactivate the route.
returnValues: Return Values Form 1 — Form 2 TCP reconnection delay

name: DoIPGetSetReconnectInterval
syntax: Function Syntax dword DoIP_GetReconnectInterval(); // form 1 void DoIP_SetReconnectInterval(dword reconnectInterval_ms, dword maxAttempts); // form 2
description: Description Get the distance between reconnect attempts by the tester, or configure the way the tester repeats reconnect attempts to the vehicle. When the vehicle closes the connection the tester will wait for a time configured with DoIP_SetReconnectDelay before trying to connect to the vehicle again. It will then send up to maxAttempts TCP connection requests to the ECU, waiting reconnectInterval_ms between attempts.
parameters: Parameters reconnectInterval_ms >0 After sending a TCP connection request the tester will wait this long for the vehicle to accept it. If it does not respond, the tester will send another request, unless the maximum number of attempts is reached. maxAttempts Maximum number of reconnection attempts the tester will perform.
returnValues: Return Values Form 1 dword reconnectInterval_ms Form 2 void

name: DoIPGetSetRoutingActivationTimeout
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetRoutingActivationTimeout(dword toRoutingActiv_ms); dword DoIP_GetRoutingActivationTimeout ();
description: Description Sets or returns the timeout for routing activation (in milliseconds).
parameters: Parameters toRoutingActiv_ms Timeout for routing activation [ms].
returnValues: Return Values Value1 Timeout for routing activation [ms].

name: DoIPGetSetVehicleDiscoveryTimeout
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetVehicleDiscoveryTimeout(dword toVehicleDiscovery); // form 1 dword DoIP_GetVehicleDiscoveryTimeout(); // form 2
description: Description Sets or returns the timeout waiting for Vehicle Identification Response Messages after a Vehicle Identification Request was sent (in milliseconds).
parameters: Parameters toVehicleDiscovery Time in milliseconds [ms]
returnValues: Return Values Form 1 — Form 2 Timeout waiting for Vehicle Identification Response Messages

name: DoIPIdentificationRequest
syntax: Function Syntax long _DoIP_IdentificationRequest(long type, BYTE VINorEID[]);
description: Description A vehicle simulation has received a Vehicle Identification Request Message. The value returned will determine the reaction of the DoIP.DLL. Note that requests not targeted at the ECU will be ignored.
parameters: Parameters type 1: No argument, VINorEID is empty 2: Argument contains the EID (6 byte) 3: Argument contains the VIN (17 byte/characters) VINorEID The argument from the request message.
returnValues: Return Values -2 Ignore Vehicle Identification Request, i.e. do nothing -1 Send a vehicle identification response message after A_DoIP_Announce_Wait ms (default: 500 ms, can be configured in DoIP.INI) ≥ 0 Send a vehicle identification response message after this many ms

name: DoIPInitAsTester
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_InitAsTester();
description: Description Per default the DoIP.DLL will operate as ECU simulation. In order to use this DLL as tester, e.g. in a test module, the operation mode has to be changed by calling this function in on preStart . It is NOT necessary to call this function when the built-in diagnostics channel is used, i.e. if the DoIP.DLL is not configured in the tester.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPMessageAcknowledgeInd
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_MessageAcknowledgeInd( WORD payloadType, long ackCode, WORD testerAddress, WORD ecuAddress); void _DoIP_MessageAcknowledgeInd( WORD payloadType, long code, WORD testerAddress, WORD ecuAddress, BYTE previousDiagnosticMessageData [], DWORD previousDiagnosticMessageSize);
description: Description A positive or negative acknowledgment was received in the tester. The code may provide additional information on the reason for a negative acknowledge. Use only one signature for the MessageAcknowledgeInd. If your CAPL code contains both functions only the first function available CAPL file is called.
parameters: Parameters payloadType ISO13400 0x8002: Diagnostic message positive acknowledgment 0x8003: Diagnostic message negative acknowledgment HSFZ 0x02: positive acknowledgment 0x40..0xFF: negative acknowledgment (different reasons) other reserved ackCode Response code transported in the acknowledgment, or -1 for HSFZ. -1: not available, i.e. HSFZ does not send a code 0: OK (ISO 13400 positive acknowledgment) 2..8: Not OK (ISO 13400 negative acknowledgment) other reserved testerAddress Logical address o
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPPeriodicDataInd
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_PeriodicDataInd( byte buffer[], dword count, dword sourceAddress, dword targetAddress, byte timeBaseStatus, dword nanosecondsPartOfTime, dword secondsPartOfTime, dword remainingDataRecords);
description: Description This callback is called from the DoIP layer when perdiodic data according to ISO 13400-2:2019/Amd1 is received. Note that the callback may be called several times for one DoIP periodic response message PDU of payload type 0x8004, depending on how many periodic data identfiers are transmitted in the DoIP PDU.
parameters: Parameters buffer Buffer containing the received data. count Size of the received data in bytes. sourceAddress Logical DoIP address of the sender. targetAddress Logical DoIP address of the receiver. timeBaseStatus Synchronization state of the ECU local time base with the vehicle's global time base. nanosecondsPartOfTime Time stamp according ISO 13400-2:2019/Amd1. secondsPartOfTime Time stamp according ISO 13400-2:2019/Amd1. remainingDataRecords Expected further data records. Indicates the number
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPPowerModeInformationResponse
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_PowerModeInformationResponse(char IPaddress[], BYTE value);
description: Description The tester received a response for a Power Mode Information Request. The vehicle's IP address is given as first argument, in text form.
parameters: Parameters IPaddress Address in text form, e.g. "169.254.32.1" (IPv4) or "2001::1" (IPv6). value Power mode information returned by the vehicle.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPRemoveLocalIPaddress
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_RemoveLocalIPaddress(char ipAddress[]);
description: Description Removes an IP address the vehicle will no longer listen at.
parameters: Parameters ipAddress Textual representation of the numeric IP address, e.g. 192.168.123.45 for an IPv4 address, or 2001::7654 for an IPv6 address.
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK < 0 < 0: Error Others reserved

name: DoIPRoutingActivationRequest
syntax: Function Syntax long _DoIP_RoutingActivationRequest(dword sourceAddress, dword activationType, dword reserved, dword OEM, BYTE sendResponse[], BYTE responseCodeOut[]);
description: Description A request for routing activation was received in a vehicle simulation. It is possible to ignore, accept or deny the request, and to specify if a response message is sent.
parameters: Parameters sourceAddress Logical address of the tester that sent the request. activationType The tester has requested this type of connection, e.g. WWH-OBD or default. reserved The value of the reserved field in the request message. OEM The value of the OEM field in the request, or 0, if field is not present. sendResponse Output parameter to indicate whether the simulation shall send a routing activation response message: 0: Do not send a response message 1: Send a response message other: reserv
returnValues: Return Values 0 Process request and send a response as if callback was not present (default). 1 The activation has failed, i.e. routing is not activated in the simulation. 2 The activation was successful, i.e. routing is activated.

name: DoIPRoutingActivationResponseInd
syntax: Function Syntax long _DoIP_RoutingActivationResponseInd(byte isSecureConnection, dword testerLogAddr, dword senderLogAddr, dword responseCode, dword reserved, dword oem);
description: Description A response to the routing activation request was received in a tester implementation. It is possible to continue with the connection or deny it. The callback can also define special treatment for secure connections.
parameters: Parameters isSecureConnection True if the connection is "secure" i.e., uses TLS. testerLogAddr Logical address of the of target of the message i.e., the tester. senderLogAddr Logical address of the DoIP entity which sent the response. responseCode Routing activation response code. reserved The value of the reserved field in the response message. oem The value of the OEM field in the response , or 0, if field is not used.
returnValues: Return Values -1 Treat the response as "denied" and abort connection attempt with an error. 0 Continue with the "normal" interpretation of the response code. 1 Treat the response as "success" and continue without TLS. 2 Treat the response as "success" and continue with TLS. other reserved for future use, currently treated as 0.

name: DoIPSelectVehicle
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_SelectVehicle(byte EID[6]); long DoIP_SelectVehicle(char VIN[]);
description: Description Specify which vehicle the tester shall target, i.e. indicate in the Vehicle Identification Request Message, or select one of the responding vehicles for communication. If this function is called before a Vehicle Identification Request (VIR) Message is sent, it will set the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) or Entity ID (EID) in the VIR messages. If this function is called during a vehicle identification phase, a matching vehicle will be selected if its Vehicle Identification Respon
parameters: Parameters EID (6 bytes) entity ID, typically an Ethernet MAC address. VIN The vehicle identification number (17 characters). If an empty string is given, VIR messages without parameter are sent.
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: DoIPSendEntityStatusRequest
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SendEntityStatusRequest(); void DoIP_SendEntityStatusRequest(char IPaddress[]);
description: Description Sends an entity status request as limited broadcast or to the given address.
parameters: Parameters IPaddress Address to send to, i.e. not a limited broadcast. May be a directed broadcast address.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSendPDUCombination
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_SendPDUCombination();
description: Description Send the combined PDUs on the open TCP connection in one block. Typically, the PDUs will be sent in one IP packet, depending on the PDU size of the transporting medium.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -11 Sending data on the TCP connection failed -6 No PDUs have been combined yet -4 The TCP connection is not established 0 Success Other Reserved

name: DoIPSendPowerModeInformationRequest
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SendPowerModeInformationRequest(); void DoIP_SendPowerModeInformationRequest(char IPaddress[]);
description: Description Sends power mode information request as limited broadcast or to the given address.
parameters: Parameters IPaddress Address to send to, i.e. not a limited broadcast. May be a directed broadcast address.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSendRoutingActivationRequest
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SendRoutingActivationRequest();
description: Description Sends routing activation request to connected vehicle. Note This is done automatically when a connection is established.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetBroadcastIPaddress
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetBroadcastIPaddress(char broadcastAddress[]);
description: Description Sets target IP address for broadcast messages sent from this node.
parameters: Parameters broadcastAddress 255.255.255.255: send a limited broadcast (default if IPv4 is active) "" (empty string): send a directed broadcast, deriving address from local IPv4 address other: if this is a valid string representation of an IP address, send to this address, e.g. a broadcast directed to a different network, or an IPv6 multicast address
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetEcuSoAdId
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetEcuSoAdId (dword soadId);
description: Description Sets the SoAd ID for the direction to the ECU. Messages physically addressed to the ECU will contain this value as the 4 bytes SoAd ID within the message header.
parameters: Parameters soadId Address used (0x00000000 to 0xFFFFFFFE)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetEID
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetEID(byte EID[]); void DoIP_SetGroupIdentification(byte GID[], dword furtherActionRequired, dword VINGIDsyncStatus); void DoIP_SetVIN(byte VIN[]); void DoIP_SetVIN(char VIN[]);
description: Description Configures the Vehicle Announcement/Vehicle Identification Response Message sent by a vehicle simulation. Note If these functions are used in combination with DiagInitEcuSimulation , the functions must be called in on prestart . Otherwise the functions have no effect. Reason: The DoIP.DLL is initialized in a ECU simulation after on prestart but before on start.
parameters: Parameters EID Send this entity ID in the message, e.g. a MAC address. GID Send this group identity in the message, e.g. a MAC address. furtherActionRequired 0: No further action is required (default) VINGIDsyncStatus 0: VIN and GID are synchronized (default) 0x10: VIN and GID are NOT synchronized; the tester should repeat the Vehicle Identification Request. VIN Vehicle Identification Number sent in the message.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetEntityStatusInformation
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetEntityStatusInformation(long nodeType, dword maxRequestSize);
description: Description Configures the response sent for an entity status request.
parameters: Parameters nodeType -1: do not send a response for the request (default) 0: gateway 1: node others: reserved maxRequestSize 0: do not send this optional parameter in a response 0xFFFFFFFF: send a value that is supported by this implementation. Currently 16777216 is sent (i.e. 16 MiB) other: value sent in the response
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetGatewayLogicalAddress
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetGatewayLogicalAddress(dword logicalAddress);
description: Description Sets the logical address of the gateway that responds to a vehicle announcement request.
parameters: Parameters logicalAddress 0: there is no gateway or gateway's and target's logical address are identical 1..65535: the gateway has the given logical address other: reserved
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetIdentificationRequestEIDVIN
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetIdentificationRequestEID(byte EID[]); void DoIP_SetIdentificationRequestVIN(char VIN[]);
description: Description Configures the Vehicle Identification Request Message sent by a tester.
parameters: Parameters EID Send an entity ID in the message, e.g. a MAC address. VIN Send a Vehicle Identification Number in the message. If this is an empty string, the message will be sent without indicating a specific vehicle.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetLocalIPaddress
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_SetLocalIPaddress (char ipAddress[]);
description: Description Set local IP address to bind to. In an ECU simulation, Vehicle Announcement Messages (VAM) will be sent from this IP address, in a tester node the Vehicle Identification Request (VIR) messages. If the tester node sets IP address in on prestart, it can receive VAMs even before it sends VIR messages itself. The target IP address is derived from this IP address: for an IPv4 address the broad-cast address is determined using the network mask, for an IPv6 address the multicast address is
parameters: Parameters ipAddress Textual representation of the IP address to bind to, e.g. 192.169.2.22 for an IPv4 address, or 2001::1234 for an IPv6 address. An empty string may be given to reset the stored address, e.g. if the address of an adapter shall be retrieved in a later step.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success -10 Invalid address string given

name: DoIPSetLocalIPaddressVersion
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetLocalIPaddressVersion(dword addressIPVersion);
description: Description When no local IP address is given explicitly, select the local address according to the priority set with this function. For example, this will allow the selection of a specific address type when only the network adapter is configured.
parameters: Parameters addressIPVersion 0: Use IPv6 address if no IPv4 address is available (default) 4: Use only IPv4 address 6: Use only IPv6 address other: reserved
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: DoIPSetMulticastScopeId
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetMulticastScopeId(dword scopeId);
description: Description Sets an IPv6 address scope ID for multicast requests, sent by a tester. The scope ID is equal to an adapter index of the tester node. See ipGetAdapter functions for more information. The function allows to select an appropriate adapter for IPv6 multicast address, to be able, for example, to send multicast requests with a given VLAN tag. Note DoIP_SetMulticastScopeId must be called before DoIP_SetLocalIPaddress in order to use the scope ID with the specified local IP address.
parameters: Parameters scopeId Scope ID for selection of a network adapter for IPv6 multicast requests.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetNextDiagnosticMessageNACKCode
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetNextDiagnosticMessageNACKCode(long NACKCode);
description: Description Sets the next return code sent in the acknowledgment for a diagnostic message received. The code is reset to 0 (positive) after usage. Note Values other than 0 let the peer assume that transmission failed, i.e. error handling is necessary by the peer.
parameters: Parameters NACKCode ISO 13400 defines these values (amongst others): 0: Positive (default) 2: Invalid source address 3: Unknown target address
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetPowerModeInformation
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetPowerModeInformation(long powerMode);
description: Description Sets the value returned automatically for a Power Mode Information Request.
parameters: Parameters powerMode -1: do not send a response for the request 0: not ready 1: ready 2: not supported (default) Others: reserved
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetProtocol
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetProtocol (dword protocolID);
description: Description Sets protocol version used in DoIP PDUs.
parameters: Parameters protocolID 1: Draft version (2010) 2: First released ISO standard 2012 (ISO 13400-2 first edition 2012-06-01) 1000: HSFZ protocol Others reserved
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetRoutingActivationOEMSpecific
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetRoutingActivationOEMSpecific(long sendOEMSpecific, dword OEMspecific);
description: Description Sets the OEM-specific parameters in the Routing Activation Request Message.
parameters: Parameters sendOEMSpecific 0: Do not send the OEM specific field (default) 1: Send the given value in the OEM specific field OEMspecific Value to send if the OEM specific field is activated.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetRoutingActivationType
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_SetRoutingActivationType(char ecuQualifier[], dword activationType); long DoIP_SetRoutingActivationType(dword activationType);
description: Description Configures the value of the ISO 13400-2 parameter routing activation request activation type sent by the tester the next time it starts to communicate with a vehicle on the built-in channel. If no ECU qualifier is given, the channel selected with DiagSetTarget is used.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Set the activation type on the channel to the given target. activationType Send this activation type value, e.g. 0 for default, 0xE1-0xFF for OEM-specific.
returnValues: Return Values Error Code

name: DoIPSetSoAdEcuIPAddress
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetSoAdEcuIPAddress (char address[]);
description: Description Sets the IP address to be used by the DoSoAd layer.
parameters: Parameters address IP address of the SoAd (SocketAdaptor protocol based) Ecu. For IPv4 address like '192.168.1.17' and for IPv6 address format like '2001:db8::ff00:0:42:8328'.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetTesterAdapter
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetTesterAdapter(char adapter[]);
description: Description Sets the network interface to be used by the DoIP layer. This function must be called in on preStart .
parameters: Parameters adapter The name of the network adapter.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetTesterLogicalAddress
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetTesterLogicalAddress(dword logicalAddress);
description: Description Sets logical DoIP address of the tester used for sending. Note that ECU simulations will typically send responses to the tester address received in requests.
parameters: Parameters logicalAddress Address used (0x0000 to 0xFFFF)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetTesterSoAdId
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetTesterSoAdId (dword soadId);
description: Description Sets the SoAd ID for the direction from the Ecu. Messages addressed from the Ecu will contain this value as the 4 bytes SoAd ID within the message header.
parameters: Parameters soadId Address used (0x00000000 to 0xFFFFFFFE)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetTesterUdpPort
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetTesterUdpPort( dword port);
description: Description Sets the UDP port (UDP_TEST_EQUIPMENT_LISTEN) to be used by the DoIP layer. This function must be called in on preStart .
parameters: Parameters port The UDP_TEST_EQUIPMENT_LISTEN port of the test equipment.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetVehicleAdapter
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetVehicleAdapter( char adapter[])
description: Description Sets the network interface to be used by the DoIP layer. This function must be called in on preStart .
parameters: Parameters adapter The name of the network adapter.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetVehicleAddress
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetVehicleAddress( char address[]);
description: Description Sets the address to be used by the DoIP layer. If given, the address is used to access the DoIP entity from the tester equipment, i.e. in case an IP address is used, a Vehicle Identification Request is omitted. In a vehicle simulation it will determine the adapter used for communication; in this case this function must be called in on preStart .
parameters: Parameters address The address of the DoIP layer. Can be a VIN, IPv4 address, IPv6 address or EID ( Entity ID , typically a MAC address). Note : If an IPv4/IPv6 address is given as address , the Vehicle Identification Sequence is omitted.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetVehicleLogicalAddress
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetVehicleLogicalAddress(dword logicalAddress);
description: Description Sets vehicle logical DoIP address sent in DoIP PDUs.
parameters: Parameters logicalAddress Address used (0x0000 to 0xFFFF)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetVehicleTcpPort
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetVehicleTcpPort( dword port);
description: Description Sets the TCP port (TCP_DATA) to be used by the DoIP layer. This function must be called in on preStart .
parameters: Parameters port The TCP_DATA port of the DoIP layer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetVehicleUdpPort
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetVehicleUdpPort( dword port);
description: Description Sets the UDP port (UDP_VEHICLE_LOCAL) to be used by the DoIP layer. This function must be called in on preStart .
parameters: Parameters port The UDP_VEHICLE_LOCAL port of the DoIP layer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPSetWriteLevel
syntax: Function Syntax void DoIP_SetWriteLevel(long level);
description: Description Sets the amount of messages written to the CANoe Write Window by the DoIP implementation.
parameters: Parameters Level 0: no output 1: only errors 2: also warnings Higher values are only relevant during development.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPTCPDataInd
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_TCPDataInd( BYTE DoIPVersion, WORD payloadType, BYTE pay-load[]);
description: Description A DoIP PDU was received from the connected peer that cannot be interpreted by this implementation.
parameters: Parameters DoIPVersion Protocol version as sent in the header. payloadType Value from the header. payload The payload.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPTCPPreSend
syntax: Function Syntax long _DoIP_TCPPreSend( BYTE DoIPVersion[], WORD payloadType[], DWORD payloadLen[], BYTE payload[]);
description: Description The DoIP implementation is about to send a TCP DoIP PDU with the given content. It is possible to change the values in header and data, to drop the PDU, and to reduce the length of the payload. Only DoIP PDUs with valid layout are sent. Note PDUs sent with DoIP_TCPSend are NOT indicated here since it is possible to provide any data using that call anyway. If the length of the PDU shall be increased, it is necessary to drop this PDU by returning -1, and send a longer PDU with DoIP_TCP
parameters: Parameters DoIPVersion Protocol version value to send, defaults to configured value. payloadType Type of the PDU sent. The value is converted from and to network byte order automatically. payloadLen Length of the payload as indicated in the header and actually sent. The value is converted from and to network byte order automatically. payload Payload itself. Elcount( payload) indicates the maximum number of bytes that can be accessed in the callback, and the maximum value payloadLen can be set to
returnValues: Return Values -1 Drop this PDU. This might violate the DoIP protocol. 0 Send the PDU with the data given. Others Reserved

name: DoIPTCPSend
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_TCPSend(dword payloadType, byte payload[], dword payloadLen); long DoIP_TCPSend(dword protocolID, dword payloadType, byte payload[], dword payloadLen);
description: Description Sends a DoIP PDU with valid layout on the open TCP connection.
parameters: Parameters protocolD If given, this will replace the configured protocol version in the DoIP PDU. [0; 255] payloadType Type to send, i.e. this can be any value. [0; 0xFFFF] payload The data following the generic DoIP header. payloadLen Length of the data following the header.
returnValues: Return Values Error code, e.g. connection not established yet.

name: DoIPTlsHandshakeCompleted
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_TlsHandshakeCompleted (dword handshakeResult);
description: Description The callback function is called when a TLS handshake is completed for the DoIP connection.
parameters: Parameters handshakeResult TLS handshake result. If the operation completed successfully the value is zero. Otherwise, the value is an error code.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPTransportLayerStatusInd
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_TransportLayerStatusInd(byte tpStatusType, byte tpStatusCode, byte applicationLayerService, dword sourceAddress, dword targetAddress);
description: Description This callback is called from the DoIP layer when transport layer status message according ISO 13400-2:2019/Amd1 payload type 0x9001 is received.
parameters: Parameters tpStatusType The transport layer status type. tpStatusCode The transport layer status code related to any error information associated with the transport protocol status type. applicationLayerService The application layer service that the transport layer status message corresponds to. sourceAddress Logical DoIP address of the sender. targetAddress Logical DoIP address of the receiver.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPUDPDataInd
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_UDPDataInd( char IPaddress[], BYTE data[]);
description: Description An UDP packet was received that cannot be interpreted by this DoIP implementation. The senders IP address is given as first argument, in text form. Note Since the DoIP implementation is extended over time, the number of unknown packet types will be reduced. To process all UDP packets use the callback function _DoIP_UDPInd . It is called for every UDP packet received before any processing is done.
parameters: Parameters IPaddress Address in text form, e.g. "169.254.32.1" (IPv4) or "2001::1" (IPv6). data The raw data received, including complete header, if present.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPUDPInd
syntax: Function Syntax long _DoIP_UDPInd( char IPaddress[], WORD port, BYTE data[]);
description: Description An UDP packet was received. All packets are forwarded to this callback function, and it can decide whether processing of the packet stops, or is continued. The sender IP address and source port is given as first arguments. Note This callback is called for every packet before any other processing is per-formed. If only packets unknown to the DoIP implementation shall be processed, use _DoIP_UDPDataInd .
parameters: Parameters IPaddress Address in text form, e.g. "169.254.32.1" (IPv4) or "2001::1" (IPv6). port IP source port of the packet at the sender. data The raw data received, including complete header, if present.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Continue normal processing of the packet. -1 Ignore the packet, i.e. all processing stops other reserved.

name: DoIPUDPPreSend
syntax: Function Syntax long _DoIP_UDPPreSend( BYTE DoIPVersion[], BYTE inverseVersion[], WORD payloadType[], DWORD payloadLen[], BYTE payload[]);
description: Description The DoIP implementation is about to send an UDP DoIP packet with the given content. It is possible to change the values in header and data, and to change the total length of the UDP packet. Note Note that packets sent by DoIP_UDPSend are NOT indicated here since it is possi-ble to provide any data using these calls anyway.
parameters: Parameters DoIPVersion Protocol version value to send, defaults to configured value. inverseVersion Inverse protocol version value to send. payloadType Type of the PDU sent. The value is converted from and to network byte order automatically. payloadLen Length of the payload as indicated in the header. The value is converted from and to network byte order automatically. payload Payload itself. Elcount( payload) indicates the maximum number of bytes that can be accessed in the callback which migh
returnValues: Return Values -1 Drop this UDP packet, i.e. it is NOT sent. This may cause errors in the protocol state machine. 0 The total length of the UDP packet is computed from the payload length value. > 0 Send a UDP packet with this length. For example, values < 8 will cut the DoIP header, i.e. the announced payload is not actually sent. Others Reserved

name: DoIPUDPSend
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_UDPSend(byte data[], dword length); long DoIP_UDPSend(char IPaddress[], byte data[], dword length);
description: Description Sends given raw data to peer(s) as UDP frame.
parameters: Parameters IPaddress If given, do not send to the selected peer, but to this address. data The UDP payload, i.e. it must contain a DoIP header if the peer shall recognize it as such. length Payload length.
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: DoIPUDPSendPort
syntax: Function Syntax long DoIP_UDPSendPort(char IPaddress[], word port, byte data[], dword length);
description: Description Send given raw data to peer(s) as UDP frame.
parameters: Parameters IPaddress Send to this address, may be a broadcast address. port Destination port for the UDP packet. data The UDP payload, i.e. it must contain a DoIP header if the peer shall recognize it as such. length Payload length
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: DoIPVehicleAnnouncementInd
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_VehicleAnnouncementInd(char VIN[], WORD logAddr, BYTE entityIdent[], BYTE groupIdent[], BYTE furtherActionRequired, BYTE VINGIDsyncStatus)
description: Description A Vehicle Announcement Message (VAM) has been received, possibly as a Vehicle Identification Response for a Vehicle Identification Request (VIR) Message. The responding vehicle indicates its presence. If the responding vehicle's VIN or EID has been selected as VIR parameter, this vehicle will be selected for communication.
parameters: Parameters VIN Vehicle Identification Number (typically 17 characters). It is possible that a responding vehicle does not have a VIN yet. logAddr Logical DoIP address of the responding entity, e.g. the gateway. entityIdent (6 byte) Entity ID of the vehicle, often the Ethernet MAC address of the network interface controller transmitting the response. groupIdent (6 byte) Group ID of the vehicle. furtherActionRequired Indicates if a special routing activation method is required. VINGIDsyncStatus In
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPVehicleConnectedIndn
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_VehicleConnectedInd();
description: Description The TCP communications channel to the vehicle has been established successfully, i.e. diagnostic messages can be exchanged without further delays.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DoIPVehicleIdentificationCompleteInd
syntax: Function Syntax void _DoIP_VehicleIdentificationCompleteInd();
description: Description The vehicle identification phase has completed, i.e. the tester is about to select the vehicle to communicate with. This is called when the timeout A_DoIP_Ctrl (standard: 2 s) expires after a Vehicle Identification Request (VIR) Message has been sent. It is also called when the vehicle responds that is targeted by the VIR message, i.e. before communication with this vehicle is initiated.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAnyFctCalled
syntax: Function Syntax on any_fct_called <provided or internal interface method>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a provided method of a distributed object derived from the given interface is called. At this time, the automatic answering feature has already set the out parameters and return value to their default values if it is configured.
parameters: Parameters <provided or internal interface method> Designates the interface method for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnAnyFctCalling
syntax: Function Syntax on any_fct_calling <provided or internal interface method>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a method of a distributed object derived from the given interface is about to be called. At this time, the automatic answering feature has not yet set out parameters or delay, so you can still use the ParamDefaults or DefaultResult values to configure it.
parameters: Parameters <provided or internal interface method> Designates the provided or internal interface method for which the handler shall react.

name: OnAnyFctRetured
syntax: Function Syntax on any_fct_returned <consumed or internal interface method>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a method of a distributed object derived from the given interface has returned at the consumer.
parameters: Parameters <consumed or internal interface method> Designates the consumed or internal interface method for which the handler shall react.

name: OnAnyFctReturning
syntax: Function Syntax on any_fct_returning <provided or internal interface method>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a method of a distributed object derived from the given interface is about to return its answer at the provider. At this time, the automatic answering feature has already set the out parameters and return value to their default values if it is configured and the default answer time has elapsed. The handler is particularly useful if the answer time is determined by another component.
parameters: Parameters <provided or internal interface method> Designates the provided or internal interface method for which the handler shall react.

name: OnAnyMonitoredValueUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax on any_monitored_value_update <value set>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a value of the given set is updated at a monitoring object. The sets are defined by the DO interface members (e.g. SomeInterface.SomeData , SomeInterface.SomeData.SubscriptionState ). Each value at a derived monitoring object is part of such a set.

name: OnAnyValueChange
syntax: Function Syntax on any_value_change <value set>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a value of the given set changes. The sets are defined by the DO interface members (e.g. SomeInterface.SomeData , SomeInterface.SomeData.SubscriptionState ). Each value at a derived object is part of such a set.
parameters: Parameters <value set> Designates the value set for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnAnyValueUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax on any_value_update <value set>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a value of the given set is updated. The sets are defined by the DO interface members (e.g. SomeInterface.SomeData , SomeInterface.SomeData.SubscriptionState ). Each value at a derived object is part of such a set.
parameters: Parameters <value set> Designates the value set for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnfctCalled
syntax: Function Syntax on fct_called <function>;
description: Description The event procedure is called when a service function is called at a provider. At this time, the automatic answering feature has already set the out parameters and return value to their default values if it is configured.
parameters: Parameters <function> Designates the function on which the event procedure shall react. This must be a combination of endpoints on provider side, but the consumer endpoint can also be replaced by all to designate that the handler shall react on all consumer calls.

name: OnfctCalling
syntax: Function Syntax on fct_calling <function>;
description: Description The event procedure is called when a service function is about to be called at a provider. At this time, the automatic answering feature has not yet set out parameters or delay, so you can still use the ParamDefaults or DefaultResult values to configure it.
parameters: Parameters <function> Designates the function on which the event procedure shall react. This must be a combination of endpoints on provider side, but the consumer endpoint can also be replaced by all to designate that the handler shall react on all consumer calls.

name: OnfctReturned
syntax: Function Syntax on fct_returned <function>;
description: Description The event procedure is called at the consumer when a service function answer is returned to the consumer.
parameters: Parameters <function> Designates the function on which the event procedure shall react. This must be a combination of endpoints on consumer side.

name: OnfctReturning
syntax: Function Syntax on fct_returning <function>;
description: Description The event procedure is called when a service function is about to return its answer at a provider. At this time, the automatic answering feature has already set the out parameters and return value to their default values if it is configured and the default answer time has elapsed. The handler is particularly useful if the answer time is determined by another component.
parameters: Parameters <function> Designates the function on which the event procedure shall react. This must be a combination of endpoints on provider side, but the consumer endpoint can also be replaced by all to designate that the handler shall react on all consumer calls.

name: OnTransmitAnnounce
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_announce <Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a provided data, event, or field member transmits an announce via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted.
parameters: Parameters <Member> Provided data, event or field member.

name: OnTransmitAnyAnnounce
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_any_announce <Interface Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever an instance of a provided data, event, or field member transmits an announce via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted. Inside the handler the this expression refers to the parent object of the member.
parameters: Parameters <Interface Member> Designates the provided data, event, or field interface member for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnTransmitAnyCall
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_any_call <Interface Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever an instance of a consumed method member transmits a value via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted.
parameters: Parameters <Interface Member> Designates the consumed interface method for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnTransmitAnyReturn
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_any_return <Interface Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever an instance of a provided method member transmits a return via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted.
parameters: Parameters <Interface Member> Designates the provided interface method for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnTransmitAnySubscribe
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_any_subscribe <Interface Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever an instance of a consumed data, event, or field member transmits a subscribe via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted. Inside the handler the this expression refers to the parent object of the member.
parameters: Parameters <Interface Member> Designates the consumed data, event, or field interface member for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnTransmitAnyUnannounce
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_any_unannounce <Interface Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever an instance of a provided data, event, or field member transmits an unannounce via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted. Inside the handler the this expression refers to the parent object of the member.
parameters: Parameters <Interface Member> Designates the provided data, event, or field interface member for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnTransmitAnyUnsubscribe
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_any_unsubscribe <Interface Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever an instance of a consumed data, event, or field member transmits an unsubscribe via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted. Inside the handler the this expression refers to the parent object of the member.
parameters: Parameters <Interface Member> Designates the consumed data, event, or field interface member for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnTransmitAnyValue
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_any_value <Interface Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever an instance of a provided data, event, or field interface member transmits a value via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted.
parameters: Parameters <Interface Member> Designates the provided data, event, or field interface member for which the handler shall be called.

name: OnTransmitCall
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_call <Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a consumed method member transmits a value via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted. Inside the handler the this expression refers to the call context of the call (i.e. it is of type callContext <Member>).
parameters: Parameters <Member> Consumed method member.

name: OnTransmitReturn
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_return <Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a provided method member transmits a return via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted. Inside the handler the this expression refers to the call context of the call (i.e. it is of type callContext <Member>).
parameters: Parameters <Member> Provided method member.

name: OnTransmitSubscribe
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_subscribe <Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a consumed data, event, or field member transmits a subscribe via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted.
parameters: Parameters <Member> Consumed data, event or field member.

name: OnTransmitUnannounce
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_unannounce <Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a provided data, event, or field member transmits an unannounce via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted.
parameters: Parameters <Member> Provided data, event or field member.

name: OnTransmitUnsubscribe
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_unsubscribe <Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a consumed data, event, or field member transmits an unsubscribe via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted.
parameters: Parameters <Member> Consumed data, event or field member.

name: OnTransmitValue
syntax: Function Syntax on transmit_value <Member>;
description: Description This handler is called whenever a provided data, event, or field member transmits a value via the CAPL binding . If the member does not use the CAPL binding, the handler is ignored. If there are multiple handlers for the same member, the measurement will be aborted. Inside the handler the this expression refers to the value of the member (i.e. it is of type valueHandle <Member type>).
parameters: Parameters <Member> Provided data, event or field member.

name: OnValueChange
syntax: Function Syntax on value_change <value>;
description: Description The event procedure is called whenever the specified value changes. It's not called when the value is updated, but does not change. You can use the event procedure for all values of communication objects, including e.g. signal and event values, LatestCall or LatestReturn of methods, service states, etc.
parameters: Parameters <value> Designates the value on which the procedure shall be called.

name: OnValueUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax on value_update <value>;
description: Description The event procedure is called whenever the specified value is updated, regardless of whether the value has changed. You can use the event procedure for all values of communication objects, including e.g. signal and event values, LatestCall or LatestReturn of methods, service states, etc. Notes A value which has a struct or array data type is always updated automatically. The event procedures for all its members are called even if just one member has changed. Instead of using several
parameters: Parameters <value> Designates the value on which the procedure shall be called.

name: GetAttribute
syntax: Function Syntax getAttribute(attributable object, attribute int32 attribute, long& value); // form 1 getAttribute(attributable object, attribute uint32 attribute, dword& value); // form 2 getAttribute(attributable object, attribute int64 attribute, int64& value); // form 3 getAttribute(attributable object, attribute uint64 attribute, qword& value); // form 4 getAttribute(attributable object, attribute double attribute, double& value); // form 5 getAttribute(attributable object, attribute string
description: Description Reads an attribute value from an attributable object.
parameters: Parameters object Attributable object at which the attribute value is read. attribute Attribute or attribute name for which the value is read. value Out-parameter for the attribute value.
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK 1 Attribute not allowed for object -1 Invalid object -2 Invalid attribute -4 Value has wrong data type -5 String buffer too small -6 String conversion error

name: LookupDistObj
syntax: Function Syntax distObjRef * lookupDistObj(char path[]);
description: Description Obtains an object by using a string. A downcast can be used to get a more concrete type. This function does not return monitoring objects . For those, use lookupMonitoringDistObj .
parameters: Parameters path Fully qualified name of the object.
returnValues: Return Values Object with the given path. Invalid object if nothing was found.

name: LookupDistObjContainer
syntax: Function Syntax distObjContainerRef * lookupDistObjContainer(char path[]);
description: Description Obtains a container by using a string. A downcast can be used to get a more concrete type.
parameters: Parameters path Fully qualified name of the container.
returnValues: Return Values Container with the given path. Invalid container if nothing was found.

name: LookupDistObjReference
syntax: Function Syntax distObjReferenceRef * lookupDistObjReference(char path[]);
description: Description Obtains a reference by using a string. A downcast can be used to get a more concrete type.
parameters: Parameters path Fully qualified name of the reference.
returnValues: Return Values Reference with the given path. Invalid reference if nothing was found.

name: LookupMonitoringDistObj
syntax: Function Syntax monitoringDistObjRef * lookupMonitoringDistObj(char path[]);
description: Description Obtains a monitoring object by using a string. A downcast can be used to get a more concrete type.
parameters: Parameters path Fully qualified name of the object.
returnValues: Return Values Object with the given path. Invalid object if nothing was found.

name: LookupVirtNet
syntax: Function Syntax virtNet lookupVirtNet(char path[]);
description: Description Obtains a virtual network by using a string.
parameters: Parameters path Fully qualified name of the virtual network.
returnValues: Return Values Virtual network with the given path. Invalid virtual network if nothing was found.

name: ResetDistObjValues
syntax: Function Syntax void resetDistObjValues(distObjRef * distObj) // form 1 void resetDistObjValues(distObjContainerRef* distObjContainer) // form 2 void resetDistObjValues(distObjMember* distObjMember) // form 3 void resetDistObjValues(char target[]) // form 4
description: Description Resets the values of distributed objects to their initial values. The values can be selected through a single member, a distributed object, a distributed object container or a namespace.
parameters: Parameters distObj A distributed object. All values of the members of the distributed object are reset. distObjContainer An array or list of distributed objects. All values of all members of all distributed objects in the container are reset. distObjMember A member of a distributed object. The values of the member are reset. target A string designating either a distributed object, or a distributed object container, or a distributed object member, or a namespace. The string must contain all names
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetAttribute
syntax: Function Syntax setAttribute(attributable object, attribute int32 attribute, long value); // form 1 setAttribute(attributable object, attribute uint32 attribute, dword value); // form 2 setAttribute(attributable object, attribute int64 attribute, int64 value); // form 3 setAttribute(attributable object, attribute uint64 attribute, qword value); // form 4 setAttribute(attributable object, attribute double attribute, double value); // form 5 setAttribute(attributable object, attribute string attri
description: Description Writes an attribute value at an attributable object.
parameters: Parameters object Attributable object at which the attribute value is written. attribute Attribute or attribute name for which the value is written. value Attribute value.
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK 1 Attribute not allowed for object 2 Value has wrong data type 6 Attribute not runtime changeable 8 Attribute changes in offline mode disallowed -1 Invalid object -2 Invalid attribute

name: Attributable
syntax: Function Syntax attributable
description: Description The type of objects that can have attributes. This type is a supertype of distObjRef *, distObjMemberRef *, distObjInterface *, distObjInterfaceMember *, and virtNet. The type is not available for user declarations.
parameters: Parameters —

name: Attribute
syntax: Function Syntax attribute * attribute int32 attribute uint32 attribute int64 attribute uint64 attribute double attribute string
description: Description The types of attributes for distributed objects. These types are not available for user declarations.
parameters: Parameters int32 32-Bit signed integer attribute. Access requires type long in CAPL. uint32 32-Bit unsigned integer attribute. Access requires type dword in CAPL. int64 64-Bit signed integer attribute. Access requires type int64 in CAPL. uint64 64-Bit unsigned integer attribute. Access requires type qword in CAPL. double 64-Bit floating point attribute. Access requires type double in CAPL. string String attribute. Access requires a large enough char array in CAPL.

name: Binding
syntax: Function Syntax Binding
description: Description This type provides static methods which are used for implementing a CAPL binding . The type is not available for user declarations.
parameters: Parameters —

name: BindingError
syntax: Function Syntax bindingError
description: Description The bindingError expression refers to a singleton object that can be used to programmatically detect errors in the binding of distributed objects . The object has a valueHandle type, which means that it has to be dereferenced to access its value.
parameters: Parameters —

name: Bytes
syntax: Function Syntax bytes
description: Description The corresponding CAPL type for the bytes type in vCDL . Reading and writing is done via memcpy overloads. The length of a bytes value can be determined with the elCount operator. This type is not available for user declarations.
parameters: Parameters —

name: CallContext
syntax: Function Syntax callContext * <var>; // form 1 callContext <Method> <var>; // form 2 callContext <Prototype> <var>; // form 3
description: Description Contains data of a function call, in particular the parameter values.
parameters: Parameters Method Method of a service, determining the data type of the call Prototype Function prototype (signature), determining the data type of the call

name: distObjBlueprint
syntax: Function Syntax distObjBlueprint * distObjBlueprint <Interface>
description: Description A blueprint type for dynamically creating distributed objects. Variables are initialized with an invalid blueprint. The blueprints have a copy semantic when they are assigned (assignment operator, call-by-value parameter, function return value). Stack allocated variables and temporary values are automatically destroyed when they go out of scope. Blueprint types cannot be used for copy-restore parameters.
parameters: Parameters Interface The optionally qualified name of a distributed object interface. It is also possible to take the reverse of in interface by using the reverse<…> operator.

name: distObjContainerRef
syntax: Function Syntax distObjContainerRef * distObjContainerRef <Typedef name>
description: Description References a container of distributed objects or references. The types provide an overload for the subscript operator to access the elements which have a distObjRef , distObjReferenceRef , or distObjContainerRef type. The wildcard container type ( distObjContainerRef * ) cannot be used with the subscript operator but it is possible to downcast it with a runtime check to a more specialized type. The leaf elements of containers can be iterated over with a for loop (same syntax as C++ r
parameters: Parameters Typedef name The fully qualified name of a distributed object or reference container.

name: distObjDataRef
syntax: Function Syntax distObjDataRef <Member>
description: Description References a distributed object data member. This type cannot be used in declarations. This is a subtype of distObjMemberRef <Member> and valueHandle <DataType> . The referenced value is immutable for consumed members and mutable otherwise.
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a distributed object data member.

name: distObjEventRef
syntax: Function Syntax distObjEventRef <Member>
description: Description References a distributed object event member. This type cannot be used in declarations. This is a subtype of distObjMemberRef <Member> and valueHandle <DataType> . The referenced value is immutable for consumed members and mutable otherwise.
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a distributed object event member.

name: distObjFieldRef
syntax: Function Syntax distObjFieldRef <Member>
description: Description References a distributed object field member. This type cannot be used in declarations. This is a subtype of distObjMemberRef <Member> and valueHandle <DataType> . The referenced value is immutable for consumed members and mutable otherwise.
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a distributed object field member.

name: distObjInterface
syntax: Function Syntax distObjInterface * distObjInterface <Interface>
description: Description A type for creating blueprints and dynamic distributed objects. All distObjInterface types are singleton types. The value of each type is bound to the name of the corresponding interface. The types are not available for user declarations.
parameters: Parameters Interface The optionally qualified name of a distributed object interface. It is also possible to take the reverse of in interface by using the reverse<…> operator.

name: distObjInterfaceMember
syntax: Function Syntax distObjInterfaceMember * distObjInterfaceMember <Interface Member>
description: Description The types of distributed object interface members. They are only useful for specifying value sets at type handlers (i.e. any_value_update , …) and for accessing attributes at distributed object interface members. All distObjInterfaceMember types are singleton types. The types are not available for user declarations.
parameters: Parameters <Interface Member> The path of the interface member.

name: distObjMemberBlueprint
syntax: Function Syntax distObjMemberBlueprint
description: Description A type for configuring the attributes and virtual network of an object blueprint member. This type cannot be used in declarations.
parameters: Parameters —

name: distObjMemberRef
syntax: Function Syntax distObjMemberRef * distObjMemberRef <Member>
description: Description References a distributed object member. This type cannot be used in declarations.
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a distributed object member.

name: distObjMethodRef
syntax: Function Syntax distObjMethodRef <Member>
description: Description References a distributed object method member. This type cannot be used in declarations. This is a subtype of distObjMemberRef <Member> .
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a distributed object method member.

name: distObjRef
syntax: Function Syntax distObjRef * distObjRef <Interface> distObjRef reverse<<Interface>>
description: Description References a distributed object that is derived from the denoted interface.
parameters: Parameters Interface The optionally qualified name of a distributed object interface. reverse<Interface> The reverse of a distributed object interface.

name: distObjReferenceRef
syntax: Function Syntax distObjReferenceRef * distObjReferenceRef <Interface>
description: Description References a reference to a distributed object that is derived from the given interface.
parameters: Parameters Interface The optionally qualified name of a distributed object interface. It is also possible to take the reverse of in interface by using the reverse<…> operator.

name: MonitoringDistObjContainerRef
syntax: Function Syntax monitoringDistObjContainerRef * monitoringDistObjContainerRef <Typedef name>
description: Description References a container of monitoring distributed objects. The types provide an overload for the subscript operator to access the elements which have a distObjRef or distObjContainerRef type. The wildcard container type ( monitoringDistObjContainerRef * ) cannot be used with the subscript operator but it is possible to downcast it with a runtime check to a more specialized type. The leaf elements of containers can be iterated over with a for loop (same syntax as C++ range-based for lo
parameters: Parameters Typedef name The fully qualified name of a monitoring distributed object container.

name: MonitoringDistObjDataRef
syntax: Function Syntax monitoringDistObjDataRef <Member>
description: Description References a monitoring distributed object data member. This type cannot be used in declarations. This is a subtype of monitoringDistObjMemberRef <Member> and valueHandle <DataType> . The referenced value is immutable.
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a monitoring distributed object data member.

name: MonitoringDistObjEventRef
syntax: Function Syntax monitoringDistObjEventRef <Member>
description: Description References a monitoring distributed object event member. This type cannot be used in declarations. This is a subtype of monitoringDistObjMemberRef <Member> and valueHandle <DataType> . The referenced value is immutable.
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a monitoring distributed object event member.

name: MonitoringDistObjFieldRef
syntax: Function Syntax monitoringDistObjFieldRef <Member>
description: Description References a monitoring distributed object field member. This type cannot be used in declarations. This is a subtype of monitoringDistObjMemberRef <Member> and valueHandle <DataType> . The referenced value is immutable.
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a monitoring distributed object field member.

name: MonitoringDistObjMemberRef
syntax: Function Syntax monitoringDistObjMemberRef * monitoringDistObjMemberRef <Member>
description: Description References a monitoring distributed object member. This type cannot be used in declarations.
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a monitoring distributed object member.

name: MonitoringDistObjMethodRef
syntax: Function Syntax monitoringDistObjMethodRef <Member>
description: Description References a monitoring distributed object method member. This type cannot be used in declarations. This is a subtype of monitoringDistObjMemberRef <Member> .
parameters: Parameters Member Path of a monitoring distributed object method member.

name: MonitoringDistObjRef
syntax: Function Syntax monitoringDistObjRef * monitoringDistObjRef <Interface> monitoringDistObjRef reverse<<Interface>>
description: Description References a monitoring distributed object that is derived from the denoted interface. The interface must support monitoring.
parameters: Parameters Interface The optionally qualified name of a distributed object interface. reverse<Interface> The reverse of a distributed object interface.

name: virtNet
syntax: Function Syntax virtNet
description: Description References a virtual network.
parameters: Parameters —

name: OnDtiSendCallback
syntax: Function Syntax void onDtiSendCallback(dword pipeHandle, long result);
description: Description The function is called when a pipe that was previously full is now able to accept packets again.
parameters: Parameters pipeHandle Handle to the pipe that calls this function, thus signaling that it is ready to accept packets again. result Indicates whether the pending package has been successfully transferred to the network interface hardware.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DtiClose
syntax: Function Syntax long DtiClose(dword pipeHandle);
description: Description The function closes the specified pipe.
parameters: Parameters pipeHandle A handle to an open pipe.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The pipe was closed successfully. -1 Failed to close the pipe.

name: DtiIsValid
syntax: Function Syntax —
description: Description The function returns true if the specified pipe is valid and ready to be used. If you are using the function syntax, check the value returned by DtiOpen . If the value is equal to 0 , the pipe is invalid.
parameters: Parameters —
