name: AREthSetVerbosity
syntax: Function Syntax long AREthSetVerbosity ( long verbosity);
description: Description Sets the verbosity level of AUTOSAR Eth IL messages in the Write Window.
parameters: Parameters verbosity Verbosity level: 0: Silent - do not write messages to the Write Window 1: Error - write only error messages (default) 2: Warning - write warning and error messages 3: Information - write information, warning and error messages
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed >0 Error code

name: AREthTCPListen
syntax: Function Syntax long AREthTCPListen(dword aepHandle);
description: Description Listens on this TCP application endpoint.
parameters: Parameters aepHandle An application endpoint
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed

name: AREthTlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration
syntax: Function Syntax AREthTlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration (dword connectionHandle,char configuration[])
description: Description Starts the (D) TLS authentication handshake as client. Certificate common names and Pre Shared Key names have to match the entries in the Vector Security Manager TLS configuration. It is possible to copy the TLS configuration name from the security profile.
parameters: Parameters connectionHandle Handle of the TLS socket to be used. configuration Name of the TLS configuration.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code

name: AREthTlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration
syntax: Function Syntax AREthTlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration (dword connectionHandle,char configuration[])
description: Description Starts the (D) TLS authentication handshake as server. Certificate common names and Pre Shared Key names have to match the entries in the Vector Security Manager TLS configuration. It is possible to copy the TLS configuration name from the security profile.
parameters: Parameters connectionHandle Handle of the TLS socket to be used. configuration Name of the TLS configuration.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code

name: AREthTriggerEvent
syntax: Function Syntax long AREthTriggerEvent ( dword pevHandle );
description: Description This function triggers sending of an Event. Afterwards, the CAPL callback function is called in <OnAREthPrepareEvent> , and the application has the opportunity to change or update the values of the SOME/IP Event before it is sent to its subscriber and by Multicast.
parameters: Parameters pevHandle Handle of the Event that is to be sent. The handle must have been created beforehand with AREthAddEvent .
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed >0 Error code

name: OnAREthClientAepConnected
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthClientAepConnected (dword aepHandle, IP_Endpoint localIPEndpoint, IP_Endpoint remoteIPEndpoint);
description: Description This callback gets called when a client-side SOME/IP Application Endpoint gets connected to a remote peer.
parameters: Parameters aepHandle Handle of the local Application Endpoint that may e.g. be (D)TLS secured, see AREthTlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration and AREthTlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration . localIPEndpoint IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the local endpoint. remoteIPEndpoint IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the remote endpoint.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthClosedIPv6TCPConnection
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthClosedIPv6TCPConnection(byte localIPv6Address[], dword localPort, byte remoteIPv6Address[], dword remotePort); // form 1 void OnAREthClosedIPv4TCPConnection(dword localIPv4Address, dword localPort, dword remoteIPv4Address, dword remotePort); // form 2 void OnAREthClosedTCPConnection(IP_Endpoint localIPEndpoint, IP_Endpoint remoteIPEndpoint); // form 3
description: Description This callback will be called after a IL's TCP connection has been closed.
parameters: Parameters localIPv6Address Local IPv6 address. remoteIPv6Address Remote IPv6 address. localIPv4Address Local IPv4 address. remoteIPv4Address Remote IPv4 address. localPort Local TCP port number. remotePort Remote TCP port number. localIPEndpoint Object of type IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the local endpoint. remoteIPEndpoint Object of type IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the remote endpoint.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed >0 Error code

name: OnAREthClosedTLSConnection
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthClosedTLSConnection(IP_Endpoint locaIPEndpoint, IP_Endpoint remoteIPEndpoint, long closedByPeer);
description: Description This callback will be called after a (D)TLS connection between the local endpoint and remote endpoint has been closed and it indicates whether the connection was closed by peer.
parameters: Parameters localIPEndpoint Object of type IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the local endpoint. remoteIPEndpoint Object of type IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the remote endpoint. closedByPeer Boolean value indicates whether the connection was closed by peer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthEstablishedIPv6TCPConnection
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthEstablishedIPv6TCPConnection(byte localIPv6Address[], dword localPort, byte remoteIPv6Address[], dword remotePort); // form 1 void OnAREthEstablishedIPv4TCPConnection(dword localIPv4Address, dword localPort, dword remoteIPv4Address, dword remotePort); // form 2 void OnAREthEstablishedTCPConnection(IP_Endpoint localIPEndpoint, IP_Endpoint remoteIPEndpoint); // form 3
description: Description This callback will be called after an incoming or outgoing TCP connection has been established for an IL's application endpoint
parameters: Parameters localIPv6Address Local IPv6 address. remoteIPv6Address Remote IPv6 address. localIPv4Address Local IPv4 address. remoteIPv4Address Remote IPv4 address. localPort Local TCP port number. remotePort Remote TCP port number. localIPEndpoint Object of type IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the local endpoint. remoteIPEndpoint Object of type IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the remote endpoint.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed >0 Error code

name: OnAREthEstablishedTLSConnection
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthEstablishedTLSConnection(IP_Endpoint locaIPEndpoint, IP_Endpoint remoteIPEndpoint);
description: Description This callback will be called after a (D)TLS connection between the local endpoint and remote endpoint has been established.
parameters: Parameters localIPEndpoint Object of type IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the local endpoint. remoteIPEndpoint Object of type IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the remote endpoint.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthEventReceived
syntax: Function Syntax void <OnAREthEventReceived> ( dword cevHandle, dword messageHandle );
description: Description This callback function is called by AUTOSAR Eth IL when a notification message has been received for the Event specified in the cevHandle parameter. A callback function with this signature must be passed to the CAPL function AREthCreateEventConsumer .
parameters: Parameters cevHandle Handle of the Event that triggered the callback, see AREthCreateEventConsumer . messageHandle Message handle of the SOME/IP Event.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthFieldNotification
syntax: Function Syntax void <OnAREthFieldNotification> ( dword cfHandle, dword messageHandle );
description: Description This callback function is called by AUTOSAR Eth IL when a notification message has been received for the field specified in the pfHandle parameter. A callback function with this signature must be passed to the CAPL function AREthCreateFieldConsumer .
parameters: Parameters cfHandle Handle of the field that triggered the callback. messageHandle Message handle of the SOME/IP field notification.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthMessage
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthMessage ( dword messageHandle );
description: Description This callback function can be implemented in the CAPL program to be notified when a SOME/IP message is received. The function is called for all those SOME/IP messages that are received on the local Application Endpoint of the IL, see AREthOpenLocalApplicationEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters messageHandle Handle of the received SOME/IP message. The handle is needed for the functions that read out message data.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthMethodError
syntax: Function Syntax void <OnAREthMethodError> (dword methodCallHandle, dword messageErrorHandle);
description: Description A callback function with this signature must be passed to the CAPL function AREthCreateMethodCall .
parameters: Parameters methodCallHandle Handle of the Event that triggered the callback, see AREthAddMethod . messageErrorHandle Message handle of the SOME/IP error.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthMethodRequest
syntax: Function Syntax void <OnAREthMethodRequest> ( dword methodHandle, dword messageHandle, dword messageResponseHandle ); // form 1 void <OnAREthMethodRequest>( dword methodHandle, dword messageHandle ); // form 2 long <OnAREthMethodRequest>( dword methodHandle, dword messageHandle, dword messageResponseHandle ); // form 3
description: Description A callback function with this signature must be passed to the CAPL function AREthAddMethod . Note The syntax of the callback function specified in form 2 must be used if the fireAndForget parameter was used when the method was created by the provider (see also AREthAddMethod ). This callback is called when a consumer has called the method created with AREthAddMethod .
parameters: Parameters methodHandle Handle of the Event that triggered the callback, see AREthAddMethod . messageHandle Message handle of the SOME/IP request. The message contains the parameter values specified by the consumer in the method call. Note If the getter method of a field was overwritten (see also AREthAddField ), the SOME/IP message payload of the request is empty. If the setter method of a field was overwritten (see also AREthAddField ), the SOME/IP message contains the current field content. m
returnValues: Return Values Form 1, 2 — Form 3 The response will be send if the callback returns 1. Otherwise the response has been filtered by the callback.

name: OnAREthMethodResponse
syntax: Function Syntax void <OnAREthMethodResponse> (dword methodCallHandle, dword messageResponseHandle );
description: Description A callback function with this signature must be passed to the CAPL function AREthCreateMethodCall .
parameters: Parameters methodCallHandle Handle of the Event that triggered the callback, see AREthAddMethod . messageResponseHandle Message handle of the SOME/IP response.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthNewServerAep
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthNewServerAep (dword aepHandle, IP_Endpoint localIPEndpoint);
description: Description This callback gets called when a server-side SOME/IP Application Endpoint gets opened. Note In case of UDP, the callback gets called for both, server and client Application Endpoints. The information provided by the localIPEndpoint parameter such as IP address and port number may be used to differentiate Application Endpoints.
parameters: Parameters aepHandle Handle of the local Application Endpoint that may e.g. be (D)TLS secured, see AREthTlsAuthenticateAsClientWithConfiguration and AREthTlsAuthenticateAsServerWithConfiguration . localIPEndpoint IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the local endpoint.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthPrepareEvent
syntax: Function Syntax void <OnAREthPrepareEvent> ( dword pevHandle, dword messageHandle );
description: Description A callback function with this signature must be passed to the CAPL function AREthAddEvent . The callback is called before sending. The values of the SOME/IP message can be changed or updated before sending.
parameters: Parameters pevHandle Handle of the Event that triggered the callback. messageHandle Message handle of the SOME/IP request.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthProcessRxMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long OnAREthProcessRxMessage (dword messageHandle, long rxChannel);
description: Description This callback function is called whenever the IL has received a SOME/IP message. The return value of this callback determines if the message should be processed by the IL or if it should be ignored. Furthermore the message may be modified before the IL processes the message.
parameters: Parameters messageHandle Handle of the received SOME/IP message that triggered the callback. rxChannel The channel of the application endpoint that received the message.
returnValues: Return Values The message will be processed if the callback returns 1. Otherwise the message has been ignored by the callback.

name: OnAREthProcessTxARPDU
syntax: Function Syntax long OnAREthProcessTxARPDU (dword sourceAddress, dword sourcePort, dword destinationAddress, dword destinationPort, dword length, char buffer[], dword headerID ); // form 1 is deprecated , use form 3 long OnAREthProcessTxARPDU (dword sourceAddress, dword sourcePort, dword destinationAddress, dword destinationPort, dword length, byte buffer[], dword headerID ); // form 2 is deprecated , use form 4 long OnAREthProcessTxARPDU(IP_Endpoint sourceEndpoint, IP_Endpoint destinationEndpoi
description: Description This callback is called before the interaction layer wants to send an AUTOSAR PDU. For PDUs with headers, form 3 or form 4 must be used, for headerless PDUs form 5 or form 6. This makes it possible to suppress the transmission.
parameters: Parameters sourceAddress IPv4 address of the local application endpoint. sourcePort UDP or TCP port of the local application endpoint. sourceEndpoint Endpoint of the local application endpoint. destinationAddress IPv4 address of the target. destinationPort UDP or TCP port of the target. destinationEndpoint Endpoint of the target. length Length of the data. buffer Data to transmit. headerId ID which is to be written in the header before the AUTOSAR PDU.
returnValues: Return Values The AUTOSAR PDU is sent by the interaction layer, if the callback sends back 1 . Otherwise the transmission is suppressed.

name: OnAREthProcessTxMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long OnAREthProcessTxMessage (dword messageHandle, long txChannel);
description: Description This callback function is called whenever the IL wants to send a SOME/IP message. The return value of this callback determines if the message should be send or if it should be blocked. Furthermore the message may be modified before the IL sends the message.
parameters: Parameters messageHandle Handle of the to be sent message that triggered the callback. txChannel The channel of the application endpoint that will be used to send the message.
returnValues: Return Values The message will be send if the callback returns 1. Otherwise the message has been filtered by the callback.

name: OnAREthSDClientEventGroupStatusChanged
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthSDClientEventGroupStatusChanged ( dword serviceId, dword instanceId, dword eventGroupId, long status );
description: Description This callback function can be implemented in the CAPL program if an Event Consumer (Client) wants to be notified of status changes to Event Groups. The function is called whenever the status of an Event Group changes. The callback function can only be called for status changes related to an Event Group that is consumed by this node.
parameters: Parameters serviceId ID of the service whose status has changed. instanceId Instance ID eventGroupId ID of the Event Group status 0: not subscribed 1: subscribed
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthSDClientServiceStatusChanged
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthSDClientServiceStatusChanged ( dword serviceId, dword instanceId, LONG status );
description: Description This callback function can be implemented in the CAPL program, if the Client wants to be notified of status changes to the services. The function is called when the status of a service changes. The callback is called regardless of whether or not the service is consumed by AREthCreateConsumedServiceInstance .
parameters: Parameters serviceId ID of the service whose status has changed. instanceId Instance ID status 0: down 1: up
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthSDServerEventGroupStatusChanged
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthSDServerEventGroupStatusChanged ( dword serviceId, dword instanceId, dword eventGroupId, long status, dword newIpAddress, dword newPort ); // form 1 void OnAREthSDServerEventGroupStatusChanged ( dword serviceId, dword majorVersion, dword instanceId, dword eventGroupId, long status, dword newIpAddress, dword newPort ); // form 2
description: Description This callback function can be implemented in the CAPL program if a Service Server wants to be notified whenever a Subscriber is added. This function is called when a Client executes a subscribe eventgroup command.
parameters: Parameters serviceId ID of the service whose status has changed. instanceId Instance ID eventGroupId ID of the Event Group. status 0: not subscribed 1: subscribed newIpAddress IPv4 address via which the subscription was received. newPort Source port via which the subscription was received. majorVersion Service interface major version.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAREthSDServerEventGroupStatusChangedIPv6
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAREthSDServerEventGroupStatusChangedIPv6 ( dword serviceId, dword majorVersion, dword instanceId, dword eventGroupId, long status, byte newIpAddress[], dword newPort );
description: Description This callback function can be implemented in the CAPL program if a Service Server wants to be notified whenever a Subscriber is added. This function is called when a Client executes a subscribe eventgroup command.
parameters: Parameters serviceId ID of the service whose status has changed. instanceId Instance ID eventGroupId ID of the Event Group. status 0: not subscribed 1: subscribed newIpAddress IPv6 address via which the subscription was received. newPort Source port via which the subscription was received. majorVersion Service interface major version.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: AvbAccept
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbAccept (dword listenerHandle);
description: Description The function accepts an incoming connection request on the specified Listener resulting in a new Listener. It may only be called inside an OnAvbListen callback. If the operation fails, the function will return 0.
parameters: Parameters listenerHandle The handle of the Listener that received the new connection request, i.e., the handle that was given to OnAvbListen .
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function failed. Call AvbGetLastError to get a more specific error code. Any other value New Listener handle.

name: AvbCloseListener
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbCloseListener (dword listenerHandle);
description: Description The function closes the Listener references by the given handle. Upon successful completion the supplied Listener handle is no longer valid. This function should be called in the on prestop event handler to shutdown the simulation gracefully. However, this is not strictly necessary as Talkers, Listeners, and Media Types are released automatically when the simulation is stopped.
parameters: Parameters listenerHandle The handle of the Listener to be closed.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. >0 Error code

name: AvbCloseTalker
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbCloseTalker (dword talkerHandle);
description: Description The function closes the Talker references by the given handle. Upon successful completion the supplied Talker handle is no longer valid. This function should be called in the on prestop event handler to shutdown the simulation gracefully. However, this is not strictly necessary as Talkers, Listeners, and Media Types are released automatically when the simulation is stopped.
parameters: Parameters talkerHandle The handle of the Talker to be closed.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. >0 Error code

name: AvbConnect
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbConnect (dword talkerHandle, byte remoteMacAddress[], char onConnectCallback[]); // form 1 dword AvbConnect (dword talkerHandle, dword remoteIpV4Address, dword port, char onConnectCallback []); // form 2 dword AvbConnect (dword talkerHandle, byte remoteIpV6Address[], dword port, char onConnectCallback []); // form 3 dword AvbConnect (dword talkerHandle, onConnectCallback []); // form 4
description: Description The function establishes a connection with the specified location. If the connect operation does not complete immediately the operation is performed asynchronously and the function will return 460609. In this case the passed CAPL callback OnAvbConnect will be called on completion (successful or not). Used transport protocols: AVTP for form 1 RTP via UDP for form 2 and 3 RTSP via TCP for form 4 Note that not all transport protocols are connection-oriented. For instance, AVTP is not co
parameters: Parameters talkerHandle The Talker handle. remoteMacAddress The (6 byte) destination MAC address (usually a multicast address). remoteIpV4Address The (4 byte) destination IPv4 address (usually a multicast address). remoteIpV6Address The (16 byte) destination IPv6 address (usually a multicast address). onConnectCallback The name of the CAPL callback function (see OnAvbConnect ).
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. 460609 The operation is pending and will be completed later. >0 Other error code .

name: AvbGetLastError
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbGetLastError ( );
description: Description This function can be used to check whether the most recent call to a function of the AVB IL has been successfully executed. The call of this function does neither reset the saved error, nor the saved error text returned by AvbGetLastErrorText . In case of an error during the execution of the previously called function, a detailed error description can be obtained using the AvbGetLastErrorText function.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 The last called function was successfully executed. >0 Error code

name: AvbGetLastErrorText
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbGetLastErrorText (dword bufferLength, char buffer[]);
description: Description The functions returns a description of the error that occurred during the execution of the most recent call to a function of the AVB IL. A call of the AvbGetLastErrorText function does not reset the saved error text.
parameters: Parameters bufferLength Size of the buffer, i.e., the maximum number of characters to be copied. buffer Buffer receiving the error text.
returnValues: Return Values Number of copied characters.

name: AvbGetMediaType
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbGetMediaType (dword listenerOrTalkerHandle);
description: Description Gets the media type handle for a Talker or a Listener. The retrieved handle can be used to get or set properties of the media type. Note that changes on the media type do not affect other objects created by the Media API automatically.
parameters: Parameters talkerOrListenerHandle Handle of a Talker or Listener as returned by AvbOpenTalker or AvbOpenListener .
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function failed. Call AvbGetLastError to get a more specific error code. Any other value The media type handle

name: AvbGetStreamId
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbGetStreamId (dword listenerOrTalkerHandle, byte retStreamId[]);
description: Description The function retrieves the Stream Identifier (ID) of the Listener or Talker. The Stream ID constitutes of a source MAC representing the Talker of the stream and the Unique ID of the stream.
parameters: Parameters listenerOrTalkerHandle The handle of a Listener or Talker. retStreamId Contains the 64 bitstream Identifier (ID) upon successful return of the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. >0 Error code

name: AvbGetStreamSourceAddress
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbGetStreamSourceAddress (dword listenerOrTalkerHandle, byte retStreamSourceAddress[]);
description: Description The function retrieves the stream's Source Address of the Listener or Talker. This usually is a 48 bit MAC address defining the sourcing system of the stream and is part of the Stream Identifier (ID). Note This address is not the source MAC address of the Ethernet layer.
parameters: Parameters listenerOrTalkerHandle The handle of the Listener or Talker. retStreamSourceAddress The 48 bit bitstream Source Address upon successful return of the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. >0 Error code

name: AvbGetStreamUniqueId
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbGetStreamUniqueId (dword listenerOrTalkerHandle, dword& retStreamUniqueId);
description: Description The function retrieves the stream's Unique Identifier (ID) of the Listener or Talker as part of the Stream Identifier (ID).
parameters: Parameters listenerOrTalkerHandle The handle of the Listener or Talker. retStreamUniqueId The 16 bitstream Unique Identifier (ID) upon successful return of the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. >0 Error code

name: AvbILControlInit
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbILControlInit ();
description: Description Initialization of the AVB Interaction Layer for the calling CAPL node. Initializes, but prevents the AVB IL, i.e. the simulated time aware end station (or PTP clock instance according to IEEE 802.1AS-2020), from starting automatically. This means that no gPTP frames are sent automatically and that no Talkers or Listeners can be opened. If this function is used, the AVB IL must be started with the AvbILControlStart function before Talkers or Listeners can be opened. This function may
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code

name: AvbILControlResume
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbILControlResume ();
description: Description Resumes AVB/TSN automatic message sending behavior after a previous suspension by AvbILControlWait . Previously opened Talkers and Listeners do not have to be opened again.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code

name: AvbILControlStart
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbILControlStart ();
description: Description Starts the AVB Interaction Layer. Enables sending and receiving of AVB/TSN related messages, e.g., automatically generated gPTP frames. After the start of the AVB IL, the time aware end station (PTP clock instance) starts up and Talkers and/or Listeners can be created. The AVB IL can be stopped using the AvbILControlStop function.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code

name: AvbILControlStop
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbILControlStop ();
description: Description Stops the AVB Interaction Layer. Disables sending and receiving of AVB/TSN related messages, e.g., automatically generated gPTP frames. Opened Talkers and Listeners are closed, and the associated handles become invalid. Handles obtained via the Media API stay valid. The AVB IL can be started again using the AvbILControlStart function.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code

name: AvbILControlWait
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbILControlWait ();
description: Description Stops sending automatically generated AVTP messages by the calling CAPL node. Currently, only the heartbeat messages of ACF are affected by this function. Alternatively, you can use AvbSetProperty and set the property StartTimeOffset of a Talker to a high value to archive the same effect. Open Talkers and Listeners are not closed. The time-aware end station (or PTP clock instance according to IEEE 802.1AS-2020) continues to run, i.e., gPTP frames are still sent. Stream content contin
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code

name: AvbListen
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbListen (dword listenerHandle, char onListenCallback[]);
description: Description The function causes the Listener to listen for incoming connection requests, which will be provided in the CAPL callback OnAvbListen passed to this function. The function simultaneously listens for incoming connection requests for streams propagated via the following transport protocols: AVTP RTP via UDP (*) RTSP via TCP (**) (only if property RtspAddress is set) Incoming connection requests can be accepted with AvbAccept from inside the provided callback function OnAvbListen . Note
parameters: Parameters listenerHandle The Listener handle. onListenCallback The name of the CAPL callback function (see OnAvbListen ).
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. >0 Error code

name: AvbOpenListener
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbOpenListener ( ); // form 1 dword AvbOpenListener (dword streamUniqueId); // form 2 dword AvbOpenListener (byte streamSourceAddress[]); // from 3 dword AvbOpenListener (byte streamSourceAddress[], dword streamUniqueId); // form4
description: Description The function creates a Listener for use in connection-based, message-oriented communications. The created Listener should be closed with AvbCloseListener when it is not needed anymore to release the allocated resources.
parameters: Parameters streamUniqueId The stream's unique identifier. streamSourceAddress The stream's source MAC address (6 bytes).
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function failed. Call AvbGetLastError to get a more specific error code. Any other value A valid Listener handle identifying the created Listener.

name: AvbOpenTalker
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbOpenTalker ( ); // form 1 dword AvbOpenTalker (dword streamUniqueId); // from 2 dword AvbOpenTalker (byte streamSourceAddress[]); // form 3 dword AvbOpenTalker (byte streamSourceAddress[], dword streamUniqueId); // form 4
description: Description The function creates a Talker for use in connection-based, message-oriented communications. The created Talker should be closed with AvbCloseTalker when it is not needed anymore to release the allocated resources.
parameters: Parameters streamUniqueId The stream's unique identifier. streamSourceAddress The stream's source MAC address (6 byte).
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function failed. Call AvbGetLastError to get a more specific error code. Any other value A valid Talker handle identifying the created Listener.

name: AvbReceive
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, int buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 1 dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, long buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 2 dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, byte buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 3 dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, message * buffer[], dword& length, char onReceiveCallback[]); // form 4 dword AvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, linFrame * b
description: Description The function receives data into the specified buffer. If the receive operation completes immediately the function returns the number of received elements in the length parameter and the passed CAPL callback OnAvbReceive will not be called. If the receive operation does not complete immediately the operation is performed asynchronously and the function will return 460609. In this case the CAPL callback OnAvbReceive will be called on completion (successful or not). The type of the buff
parameters: Parameters listenerHandle The Listener handle. buffer The buffer used to store the incoming data. length The length of the data buffer. Note that a reference must be supplied as this is an output parameter. onReceiveCallback The name of the CAPL callback function (see OnAvbReceive).
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. 460609 The operation is pending and will be completed later. Any other value Other Error code

name: AvbSend
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, int buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 1 dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, long buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 2 dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, qword buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 3 dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, byte buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallback[]); // form 4 dword AvbSend (dword talkerHandle, message * buffer[], dword& length, char onSendCallbac
description: Description The function sends the data passed in the buffer. If the send operation completes immediately, the function returns the number of sent elements in the length parameter and the passed CAPL callback OnAvbSend will not be called. If the send operation does not complete immediately the operation is performed asynchronously and the function will return 460609. In this case the CAPL callback OnAvbSend will be called on completion (successful or not). No data should be written to the buffer
parameters: Parameters talkerHandle The Talker handle. buffer The buffer containing the data to be sent. length The length of the data buffer. Note that a reference must be supplied as this is an output parameter. onSendCallback The name of the CAPL callback function (see OnAvbSend ). The type of the buffer parameter of the supplied callback function must match with the type of the buffer parameter of the called overload of AvbSend . Otherwise, the callback will not be invoked..
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. 460609 The operation is pending and will be completed later. Any other value Other Error code

name: AvbSetMediaType
syntax: Function Syntax long AvbSetMediaType (dword talkerHandle, dword mediaTypeHandle);
description: Description Sets the media type for a Talker. Setting the media type means the Talker can expect data that conforms to that media type passed to its AvbSend function.
parameters: Parameters talkerHandle Handle of the Talker for which a media type should be set. mediaTypeHandle The media type handle.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. >0 Error code

name: AvbSetProperty
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbSetProperty (char propertyName[], int64 value); //form 1 dword AvbSetProperty (dword objHandle, char propertyName[], int64 value); // form 2 dword AvbSetProperty (dword objHandle, char propertyName[], dword length, byte value[]); // form 3
description: Description The behavior of the AVB IL can be configured using properties. Properties can be set for the current bus context (form 1) as well as on an object-specific basis for talkers (form 2, 3). The default settings for the properties can be obtained from the table below.
parameters: Parameters propertyName Name of the property to be set. Name Default Value Overload Description General Properties StreamReservation 0 Form 1 Stream Reservation is currently not supported. Set this property to 0. VlanId 2 (Talkers) / Not set (Listeners) Form 2 The VLAN ID to be used for sending or receiving AVB packets. If not set, all AVTPDUs are delivered to a Listener, regardless of the VLAN ID. If set to 0xFFFF, only frames without VLAN tag are delivered to a Listener. RtpPort 1024 (Listener
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function was successfully executed. >0 Error code

name: AvbSetVerbosity
syntax: Function Syntax dword AvbSetVerbosity (long verbosity);
description: Description Sets the verbosity level of AVB IL messages in the Write Window.
parameters: Parameters verbosity Verbosity level: 0: Silent - do not write messages to the Write Window 1: Error - write only error messages (default) 2: Warning - write warning and error messages 3: Information - write information, warning and error messages
returnValues: Return Values 0 Setting the verbosity was successful. 460603 Invalid parameter supplied to the parameter verbosity . 460604 Function was called in another bus context than Ethernet.

name: GlobalTimeSetParameter
syntax: Function Syntax dword GlobalTimeSetParameter ( char parameterName[], int64 value); // form 1 dword GlobalTimeSetParameter ( char parameterName[], char value[]); // form 2 dword GlobalTimeSetParameter ( char parameterName[], byte value[]); // form 3 dword GlobalTimeSetParameter ( byte clockIdentity[], char parameterName[], int64 value); // form 4 dword GlobalTimeSetParameter ( byte clockIdentity[], char parameterName[], char value[]); // form 5 dword GlobalTimeSetParameter ( byte clockIdentity[],
description: Description The AUTOSAR Global Time specification introduces various configuration parameters that can be used to control and customize the behavior of an AUTOSAR Global Time instance. The configuration parameters that are relevant for end instances can be set with this function. Note Properties that are not specific to Global Time are set using PtpSetProperty .
parameters: Parameters parameterName The name of the property to be changed. Case-sensitive . value The new value of the property. clockIdentity The clock identity for which a property should be set. Only relevant for error detection. The length must be exactly 8!
returnValues: Return Values 0 Setting the property was successful. 1 The given configuration parameter does not exist for the used type of value. 2 The given value is invalid for the given configuration parameter.

name: OnAvbConnect
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAvbConnect (dword talkerHandle, dword result);
description: Description Signature of a user-defined callback supplied to AvbConnect . The callback is dispatched when an asynchronous connection operation completes. Note that a different name can be used, but the remaining signature must be exactly as given above.
parameters: Parameters talkerHandle The Talker handle. result The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is zero. Otherwise the value is non-zero. See Error Codes for a list of possible error codes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAvbListen
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAvbListen (dword listenerHandle, dword result);
description: Description Signature of a user-defined callback supplied to AvbListen . The callback is dispatched when a connection request for the specified Listener is received. Note that a different name can be used, but the remaining signature must be exactly as given above.
parameters: Parameters listenerHandle The Listener handle. result The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is zero. Otherwise the value is non-zero. See Error Codes for a list of possible error codes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAvbReceive
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, int buffer[], dword length); // form 1 void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, long buffer[], dword length); // form 2 void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, byte buffer[], dword length); // form 3 void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, message * buffer[], dword length); // form 4 void OnAvbReceive (dword listenerHandle, dword result, linFrame * buffer[], dword length); // form 5
description: Description Signature of a user-defined callback supplied to AvbReceive . The callback is dispatched when an asynchronous receive operation completes. Note that a different name can be used, but the remaining signature must be exactly as given above. The type of the buffer parameter of the supplied callback must match the type of the buffer parameter of the corresponding AvbReceive call. An OnAvbReceive callback is invoked only once per call to AvbReceive . This means that AvbReceive must be cal
parameters: Parameters listenerHandle The Listener handle. result The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is zero. Otherwise the value is non-zero. buffer The buffer into which the data was stored. length The length of the received data buffer, i.e., the number of elements of the respective type.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAvbSend
syntax: Function Syntax void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, int buffer[], dword length); // form 1 void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, long buffer[], dword length); // form 2 void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, qword buffer[], dword length); // form 3 void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, byte buffer[], dword length); // form 4 void OnAvbSend (dword talkerHandle, dword result, message * buffer[], dword length); // form 5 void OnAvbSend (dword talkerH
description: Description This callback is dispatched when an asynchronous send operation on a Talker completes. The type of the buffer parameter must match with the type of the buffer parameter of the corresponding call to AvbSend .
parameters: Parameters talkerHandle The Talker handle. result The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is zero. Otherwise the value is non-zero. buffer The buffer provided with the send operation. length The number of elements sent.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnAvbSendPrepare
syntax: Function Syntax long OnAvbSendPrepare ( dword talkerHandle, ethernetPacket * packet);
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called right before a packet will be sent by the AVB stack. Currently only packets of an AVTP stream are supported. The name and the signature must match exactly with the signature given above. The sending of the packet on the network is blocked by returning 0. Return 1 if the packet should be sent. The callback can be used to inject faults in the AVTP stream by modifying a packet (including its header) or by simulating packet loss.
parameters: Parameters talkerHandle The Talker handle. packet The Ethernet packet which will be send.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Block the Ethernet packet. 1 Send the Ethernet packet.

name: OnGlobalTimePrepareIcv
syntax: Function Syntax void OnGlobalTimePrepareIcv ( ethernetPacket * packet, dword offset );
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback, it is called before a Follow_Up message with a Time Authenticated Sub-TLV is sent. It is called before the OnGlobalTimeSendPrepare callback. Note The signature (including the name of the callback function, the return type, and the parameter number and types) must match exactly with the signature above.
parameters: Parameters packet The Ethernet packet which will be send. offset The byte offset of the first byte of the first Time Authenticated Sub-TLV.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnGlobalTimeSendPrepare
syntax: Function Syntax long OnGlobalTimeSendPrepare ( ethernetPacket * packet );
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback, it is called right before a packet will be sent by the Global Time stack. The sending of the packet on the network is blocked by returning 0. Return 1 if the packet should be sent. The callback can be used to inject faults in the time synchronization via Global Time by modifying a packet (including its header) or by simulating packet loss.
parameters: Parameters packet The Ethernet packet which will be send.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Block the Ethernet packet. 1 Send the Ethernet packet.

name: OnGlobalTimeVerifyIcv
syntax: Function Syntax dword OnGlobalTimeVerifyIcv ( ethernetPacket * packet, dword offset );
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback, it is called after receiving a Follow_Up message with a Time Authenticated Sub-TLV. It is called before the OnGlobalTimeSendPrepare callback. Note The signature (including the name of the callback function, the return type, and the parameter number and types) must match exactly with the signature above.
parameters: Parameters packet The Ethernet packet which will be send. offset The byte offset of the first byte of the first Time Authenticated Sub-TLV.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The verification failed and the message should be dropped. Any other value The verification succeeded and the message content should be used to derive the synchronized time.

name: OnPtpSendPrepare
syntax: Function Syntax long OnPtpSendPrepare( ethernetPacket * packet );
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called right before a packet will be sent by the gPTP stack. The sending of the packet on the network is blocked by returning 0. Return 1 if the packet should be sent. The callback can be used to inject faults in the time synchronization via gPTP by modifying a packet (including its header) or by simulating packet loss.
parameters: Parameters packet The Ethernet packet which will be send.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Block the Ethernet packet. 1 Send the Ethernet packet.

name: PtpSetProperty
syntax: Function Syntax long PtpSetProperty (char propertyName[], int64 value); // form 1 long PtpSetProperty (char propertyName[], dword length, byte value[]); // form 2
description: Description The behavior of the PTP layer can be configured using properties. For instance, you can configure the port role of a simulated node. Use this function in the on preStart event handler to set properties before the first gPTP frame is constructed. For example you can set the PortRole property to SlavePort (2) for a node in its on prestart CAPL event handler.
parameters: Parameters propertyName Name of the property to be set. Name Overload Description PortRole Form 1 Values (Integer): 0 = Auto select (Role is selected by BMCA) (Default) 1 = MasterPort (Send Sync, Follow_Up, Announce, and PDelay_Req messages), 2 = SlavePort (Send PDelay_Req messages), 3 = PassivePort (Send PDelay_Req messages), 4 = DisabledPort (do not send any PTP messages) Values > 0: By choosing a static PortRole the node will skip BMCA. The clock of such a node can therefore either be synchro
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function succeeded. >0 Error code

name: PtpSimulationTimespanFromPtpTimespan
syntax: Function Syntax int64 PtpSimulationTimespanFromPtpTimespan(qword ptpTimespan);
description: Description Returns the simulation timespan equivalent to the given PTP timespan. A timespan is the time passed between two points in time.
parameters: Parameters ptpTimespan PTP timespan in ns.
returnValues: Return Values Simulation timespan corresponding tot he given PTP timespan in ns.

name: PtpTimeFromSimulationTime
syntax: Function Syntax qword PtpTimeFromSimulationTime (int64 simulationTime);
description: Description Returns the global PTP time corresponding to the simulation time.
parameters: Parameters simulationTime Simulation time (ns).
returnValues: Return Values Global PTP time (ns).

name: PtpTimeNow
syntax: Function Syntax qword PtpTimeNow ( );
description: Description Returns the current global PTP time.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values The current global PTP time (ns).

name: OnEthernet10BASET1SStatus
syntax: Function Syntax on ethernet10BASET1SStatus *; // form 1 on ethernet10BASET1SStatus ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>; // form 2
description: Description The event procedure is called on the change of the status of the 10Base link. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on ethernet10BASET1SStatus procedure, to access the information of the link status.
parameters: Parameters ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName> Ethernet port qualification.

name: OnEthernetErrorPacket
syntax: Function Syntax on ethernetErrorPacket *; // form 1 on ethernetErrorPacket msgChannel<X>.*; // form 2, not recommended for network-based access. Use form 3 instead. on ethernetErrorPacket ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>.*; // form 3
description: Description The event procedure is called on the receipt of an invalid Ethernet packet with checksum or length error. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on ethernetErrorPacket procedure, to access the data of the packet that has just been received. CAPL programs are by default not transparent to bus events. This means that a CAPL node in the evaluation branch of the measurement configuration will block the data flow to its right s
parameters: Parameters msgChannel<X> Ethernet channel number, range 1..32. ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName> Ethernet port qualification.

name: OnethernetMacsecStatus
syntax: Function Syntax on ethernetMacsecStatus *; // form 1 on ethernetMacsecStatus ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>; // form 2
description: Description This event procedure is called when the Ethernet MACsec connection state of a physical port has changed. To access the control information, use the selectors. The keyword this is available in this procedure to access the status information.
parameters: Parameters ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName> Ethernet port qualification.

name: OnEthernetPacket
syntax: Function Syntax on ethernetPacket *; // form 1 on ethernetPacket msgChannel<X>.*; // form 2, not recommended for network-based access. Use form 3 instead. on ethernetPacket ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>.*; // form 3
description: Description The event procedure is called on the receipt of a valid Ethernet packet. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on ethernetPacket procedure, to access the data of the packet that has just been received. CAPL programs are by default not transparent to bus events. This means that a CAPL node in the evaluation branch of the measurement configuration will block the data flow to its right side. You must explicitly program the p
parameters: Parameters msgChannel<X> Ethernet channel number, range 1..32. ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName> Ethernet port qualification.

name: OnEthernetPacketForwarded
syntax: Function Syntax on ethernetPacketForwarded *; on ethernetPacketForwarded msgChannel<X>.*;
description: Description The event procedure is called when the Ethernet network interface has send a received Ethernet packet on one or more other hardware channels, i.e. with VN5640 which is configured as Ethernet switch. It can only be used with network interfaces which supported forwarding of Ethernet packets. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on ethernetPacketForwarded procedure, to access the data of the packet that has just been receiv
parameters: Parameters msgChannel<X> Ethernet channel number, range 1..32.

name: OnEthernetPhyState
syntax: Function Syntax on ethernetPhyState *; // form 1 on ethernetPhyState ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName>; // form 2
description: Description The event procedure is called on the change of the state of an Ethernet PHY or if a relevant PHY activity has been triggered. To access the control information you would use selectors. The key word this is available within an on ethernetPhyState procedure, to access the information of the PHY state and event type.
parameters: Parameters ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName> Ethernet port qualification.

name: OnEthernetStatus
syntax: Function Syntax on ethernetStatus *; // form 1 on ethernetStatus msgChannel<X>.*; // form 2, not recommended for network-based access. Use form 3 instead. on ethernetStatus ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>; // form 3
description: Description The event procedure is called on the change of the status of the Ethernet link. To access the control information you would use selectors . The key word this is available within an on ethernetStatus procedure, to access the information of the link status.
parameters: Parameters msgChannel<X> Ethernet channel number, range 1..32. ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName> Ethernet port qualification.

name: OnPtpSyncPoint
syntax: Function Syntax on ptpSyncPoint *; on ptpSyncPoint <ethernet port>;
description: Description Define a CAPL handler which gets invoked when a PTP instance's time or rate ratio changes in the hardware.
parameters: Parameters —

name: OnServiceSignal
syntax: Function Syntax on serviceSignal <signal>;
description: Description This function is called when the signal value is changed and the associated SOME/IP message has been received. Within the callback function you have direct access to the signal value via keyword this : Key Word Description Data Type this Access to the physical value of the Service Signal FLOAT this.raw Access to the raw value of the Service Signal FLOAT this.raw64 Access to the raw value of the Service Signal QWORD
parameters: Parameters —

name: OnServiceSignalUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax on serviceSignal_update <signal>;
description: Description This function is called when the associated SOME/IP message has been received. It is immaterial whether or not the signal value was changed. Within the callback function you have direct access to the signal value via keyword this : Key Word Description Data Type this Access to the physical value of the Service Signal FLOAT this.raw Access to the raw value of the Service Signal FLOAT this.raw64 Access to the raw value of the Service Signal QWORD
parameters: Parameters —

name: OnSomeIpMessage
syntax: Function Syntax void OnSomeIpMessage ( dword messageHandle );
description: Description This callback function can be implemented in the CAPL program to be notified when a SOME/IP message is received. The function is called for all those SOME/IP messages that are received on the local Application Endpoint of the IL, see SomeIpOpenLocalApplicationEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters messageHandle Handle of the received SOME/IP message. The handle is needed for the functions that read out message data.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnSomeIpSD
syntax: Function Syntax on someipSD *; // form 1 on someipSD ethernetPort::<Network>::<PortName>.*; // form 2 on someipSD <service-id>[::major-version[::instance-id]]; // form 3 on someipSD [<network-name|database>::][<packag-name::]<service-interface-name>[::major-version[::instance-id]]; // form 4 on someipSD [<network-name|database>::][<package-name::][<service-interface-name|id>[::major-version[::instance-id]]] [::<OfferService|StopOfferService|FindService|StopFindService|Subscribe|StopSubscribe|Sub
description: Description The event procedure is called on the receipt of a valid SOME/IP Service Discovery Entry. A Service Discovery message (Service ID FFFFh, Method ID 8100h) can contain more than one Service Discovery entry. To access the control information, you would use selectors and methods. The key word this is available within an on someipSD procedure, to access the data of the Service Discovery entry that has just been received. Note CAPL programs are by default not transparent to bus events. This
parameters: Parameters ethernetPort::<NetworkName>::<PortName> Ethernet port qualification. <service-id[::major-version[::instance-id]]> Service qualification by ID. <service-name[::major-version[::instance-id]]> Service qualification by database qualifier. [::<OfferService|StopOfferService|FindService|StopFindService|Subscribe|StopSubscribe|SubscribeACK|SubscribeNACK>] Service Discovery entry type. Can be appended to other qualifications.

name: EthClearMacAddressTable
syntax: Function Syntax long ethClearMacAddressTable ( long channel);
description: Description Clears the MAC address table of the Ethernet network interface. In channel-based mode the function can only be used with Vector network interface (i.e. VN5640 ) in operation mode which manages a MAC address table, i.e. Ethernet switch. In port-based mode the function resets the tables of all switches within the network referenced by the channel number.
parameters: Parameters channel Ethernet channel number. Value range: 1..32
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success 1 Not supported by network interface. 2 Failed, check settings of network interface. 3 Not supported in channel-based simulation mode.

name: EthDisableStatisticsOfAllNetworks
syntax: Function Syntax void ethDisableStatisticsOfAllNetworks( byte disable);
description: Description Disables or enables the processing of the RT statistic values for all ports on all Ethernet networks. It will not influence the Statistic Window or the system variables.
parameters: Parameters disable 0 : Statistic values are not processed 1 : Statistic values are processed and the current values are reseted
returnValues: Return Values —

name: EthDisableStatisticsOfNetwork
syntax: Function Syntax void ethDisableStatisticsOfNetwork( byte disable, ethernetPort portOnNetwork);
description: Description Disables or enables the processing of the RT statistic values for all ports of the Ethernet network on which the passed port exists. It will not influence the Statistic Window or the system variables.
parameters: Parameters disable 0 : Statistic values are not processed 1 : Statistic values are processed and the current values are reseted portOnNetwork A port of the addressed network.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: EthDisableStatisticsOfPort
syntax: Function Syntax void ethDisableStatisticsOfPort( byte disable, ethernetPort port);
description: Description Disables or enables the processing of the RT statistic values for one port. It will not influence the Statistic Window or the system variables.
parameters: Parameters disable 0 : Statistic values are not processed 1 : Statistic values are processed and the current values are reseted port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: EthGet10BASET1SCorruptedTxCount
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGet10BASET1SCorruptedTxCount(ethernetPort port);
description: Description Returns the number of corrupted packets of the specified port.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values Number of corrupted packets, 0 if no valid value currently exists or the port is not a 10BASE-T1S port.

name: EthGet10BASET1SRemoteJabberCount
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGet10BASET1SRemoteJabberCount(ethernetPort port);
description: Description Returns the number of remote Jabber of the specified port.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values Number of corrupted packets, 0 if no valid value currently exists or the port is not a 10BASE-T1S port.

name: ethGetEthernetPort
syntax: Function Syntax ethernetPort ethGetEthernetPort(long Channel);
description: Description Gets the Ethernet port for the current simulation node. The channel needs to be specified and can be used for gateway nodes to differentiate between the ports on different networks.
parameters: Parameters Channel Application channel, i.e. Eth 1.
returnValues: Return Values The requested port in case of success or an invalid port in case of failure.

name: EthGetEthernetPortInfos
syntax: Function Syntax long ethGetEthernetPortInfos (ethernetportinfo portInfos[]);
description: Description Retrieves port related information of all connected network-based Ethernet devices. You must provide an array of ethernetPortInfo elements of sufficient size for all available hardware ports.
parameters: Parameters portInfos Array of type <ethernetPortInfo> , which contains the retrieved hardware info as a struct with the following members: char PortName[32] char NetworkName[32] char SegmentName[32] uint32_t IsPhysical
returnValues: Return Values Number of valid ethernetPortInfo elements (0 in case of any error).

name: EthGetLinkSpeed
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetLinkSpeed (ethernetPort port, ValueSelector valueSelector);
description: Description Returns the link speed of the specified port. The parameter valueSelector controls whether the minimum value, the maximum value, the average value or the current value has to be returned.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port. valueSelector Controls which value to return. The enumerator supports these values: eMinValue : Returns the minimum value eMaxValue : Returns the maximum value eAvgValue : Returns the average value eCurrValue : Return the current values
returnValues: Return Values Link speed in [MBit/sec], 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetLinkStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long ethGetLinkStatus ( long channel ); // form 1 long ethGetLinkStatus ( long channel, long hwChannel ); // form 2 long ethGetLinkStatus ( ethernetPort port ); // form 3
description: Description Returns the link status of the channel.
parameters: Parameters channel Ethernet channel number. Value range: 1..32 hwChannel Hardware channel number. Vector network interface must support more than 1 hardware channel to use. Value range: 1..16 port Ethernet port, described by network name and port name.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Link down 1 Link up -1 Read link status failed

name: EthGetMacAddressAsNumber
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetMacAddressAsNumber ( char macAddrStr[] );
description: Description Converts a MAC address string, i.e. "02:00:00:00:00:01", to a qword-number, which can be used with ethernetPacket .source and ethernetPacket .destination.
parameters: Parameters macAddrStr MAC address as string, i.e. "02:00:00:00:00:01".
returnValues: Return Values The MAC address as qword or 0, if failed

name: EthGetMacAddressAsString
syntax: Function Syntax long ethGetMacAddressAsString ( qword macAddr, char buffer[], dword bufferLength );
description: Description Converts a MAC address from qword to string. The function is helpful with ethernetPacket .source and ethernetPacket .destination.
parameters: Parameters macAddr MAC address as qword, i.e. from ethernetPacket.source. buffer Buffer where the MAC address string is copied to. bufferLength Length of buffer
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. 8 The address buffer was insufficient.

name: EthGetPhyConnector
syntax: Function Syntax byte ethGetPhyConnector (ethernetPort port, EthernetPhyConnector& phyConnector);
description: Description Gets the PHY connector. Only supported for network-based configurations and physical measurement ports.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port. phyConnector 0: kEthernetPhyConnectorDefault (the only available connector on this channel) 1: kEthernetPhyConnectorRJ45 2: kEthernetPhyConnectorDSub
returnValues: Return Values 0 Failure 1 Success

name: EthGetPhyMedium
syntax: Function Syntax byte ethGetPhyMedium (ethernetPort port, EthernetPhyMedium& phyMedium);
description: Description Gets the PHY medium. Only supported for network-based configurations and physical measurement ports.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port. phyMedium 0: kEthernetPhyMediumUnknown 1: kEthernetPhyMediumIEEE_802_3 2: kEthernetPhyMedium100BASE_T1 3: kEthernetPhyMedium1GB_COAX 4: kEthernetPhyMedium1000BASE_T1 5: kEthernetPhyMedium2500BASE_T1 6: kEthernetPhyMedium5000BASE_T1 7: kEthernetPhyMedium10000BASE_T1 8: kEthernetPhyMedium10BASE_T1S
returnValues: Return Values 0 Failure 1 Success

name: EthGetPhyMode
syntax: Function Syntax byte ethGetPhyMode (ethernetPort port, EthernetPhyMode& phyMode);
description: Description Gets the PHY mode. Only supported for network-based configurations and physical measurement ports.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port. phyMode 0: kEthernetPhyModeUnknown 1: kEthernetPhyModeMaster 2: kEthernetPhyModeSlave
returnValues: Return Values 0 Failure 1 Success

name: EthGetPhyState
syntax: Function Syntax long ethGetPhyState (long channel ); // form 1 long ethGetPhyState (long channel, long hwChannel ); // form 2 long ethGetPhyState (ethernetPort port ); // form 3
description: Description Gets the PHY state. TC10 Sleep/Wakeup states can be retrieved with this function. Note This function can be used only for PHY types supporting this feature like TJA-1101. Form 1 and form 2 is only for channel-based access. Form 3 is only for network-based access.
parameters: Parameters channel Ethernet channel number. Value range: 1..32 hwChannel Hardware channel number. Vector network interface must support more than 1 hardware channel to use. Value range: 1..16 port Ethernet port. Only supported for network-based configurations and physical measurement ports.
returnValues: Return Values PHY state 1: ePhyNormalState 2: ePhySleepState 3: ePhyPowerOffState 4: ePhySleepRequestState 5: ePhySleepLocalState 6: ePhySleepLocalRequestState Note States 5 and 6 can only be get with 10Base-T1S TC10-capable modules.

name: EthGetRxBitRate
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetRxBitRate (ethernetPort port, ValueSelector valueSelector);
description: Description Returns the RX bitrate of the specified port. The parameter valueSelector controls whether the minimum value, the maximum value, the average value or the current value has to be returned.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port. valueSelector Controls which value to return. The enumerator supports these values: eMinValue : Returns the minimum value eMaxValue : Returns the maximum value eAvgValue : Returns the average value eCurrValue : Return the current values
returnValues: Return Values RX bitrate in [Bit/sec], 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetRxBytesTotalCount
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetRxBytesTotalCount (ethernetPort port);
description: Description Returns the number of received bytes of the specified port.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values Number of received bytes, 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetRxErrorPacketsTotalCount
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetRxErrorPacketsTotalCount (ethernetPort port);
description: Description Returns the number of received error packets of the specified port.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values Number of received error packets, 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetRxPacketRate
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetRxPacketRate (ethernetPort port, ValueSelector valueSelector);
description: Description Returns the RX packet rate of the specified port. The parameter valueSelector controls whether the minimum value, the maximum value, the average value or the current value has to be returned.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port. valueSelector Controls which value to return. The enumerator supports these values: eMinValue : Returns the minimum value eMaxValue : Returns the maximum value eAvgValue : Returns the average value eCurrValue : Return the current values
returnValues: Return Values RX packet rate in [Packets/sec], 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetRxPacketsTotalCount
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetRxPacketsTotalCount (ethernetPort port);
description: Description Returns the number of received packets of the specified port.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values Number of received packets, 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetSQI
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetSQI (ethernetPort port, ValueSelector valueSelector);
description: Description Returns the SQI (Signal Quality Indicator) of the specified port. The parameter valueSelector controls whether the minimum value, the maximum value, the average value or the current value has to be returned.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port. valueSelector Controls which value to return. The enumerator supports these values: eMinValue : Returns the minimum value eMaxValue : Returns the maximum value eAvgValue : Returns the average value eCurrValue : Return the current values
returnValues: Return Values SQI, -1 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetTxBitRate
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetTxBitRate (ethernetPort port, ValueSelector valueSelector);
description: Description Returns the TX bitrate of the specified port. The parameter valueSelector controls whether the minimum value, the maximum value, the average value or the current value has to be returned.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port. valueSelector Controls which value to return. The enumerator supports these values: eMinValue : Returns the minimum value eMaxValue : Returns the maximum value eAvgValue : Returns the average value eCurrValue : Return the current values
returnValues: Return Values TX bitrate in [Bit/sec], 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetTxBytesTotalCount
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetTxBytesTotalCount (ethernetPort port);
description: Description Returns the number of transmitted bytes of the specified port.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values Number of transmitted bytes, 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetTxErrorPacketsTotalCount
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetTxErrorPacketsTotalCount (ethernetPort port);
description: Description Returns the number of transmitted error packets of the specified port.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values Number of transmitted error packets, 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetTxPacketRate
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetTxPacketRate (ethernetPort port, ValueSelector valueSelector);
description: Description Returns the TX packet rate of the specified port. The parameter valueSelector controls whether the minimum value, the maximum value, the average value or the current value has to be returned.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port. valueSelector Controls which value to return. The enumerator supports these values: eMinValue : Returns the minimum value eMaxValue : Returns the maximum value eAvgValue : Returns the average value eCurrValue : Return the current values
returnValues: Return Values TX packet rate in [Packets/sec], 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthGetTxPacketsTotalCount
syntax: Function Syntax qword ethGetTxPacketsTotalCount (ethernetPort port);
description: Description Returns the number of transmitted packets of the specified port.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values Number of transmitted packets, 0 if no valid value currently exists.

name: EthInjectPacket
syntax: Function Syntax long ethInjectPacket (ethernetPacket packet, byte direction);
description: Description Transmits an Ethernet packet by a specific ethernetPort defined in <packet> . Allows to output a frame not only on a simulation port like output , but on a physical port as well e.g. for error injection. Note The injection is not supported for measurement ports that are connected to a multidrop segment in the hardware configuration.
parameters: Parameters packet Variable of type ethernetPacket . direction transmit direction from viewpoint of the connected segment: <rx> or <0> to send from a simulated port (into the connected segment) in real mode <tx> or <1> to send from a physical measurement port (away from the connected segment) in real mode in simulated mode use <tx> or <rx> (respectively <0> or <1>) to send from simulated ports either into the connected segment (rx) or away from the connected segment (tx).
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success 1 Invalid Ethernet port 2 packet failure 3 Output not allowed

name: EthResetStatistics
syntax: Function Syntax void ethResetStatistics( long channel );
description: Description Resets Ethernet channel statistics. The Ethernet channel statistics are provided by the Eth <channel> object.
parameters: Parameters channel Ethernet channel number.
returnValues: Return Values Value range 1..32

name: EthResetStatisticsOfAllNetworks
syntax: Function Syntax void ethResetStatisticsOfAllNetworks();
description: Description Resets the RT statistic values of all ports of all Ethernet networks. It will not influence the Statistic Window or the system variables.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: EthResetStatisticsOfNetwork
syntax: Function Syntax void ethResetStatisticsOfNetwork( ethernetPort portOnNetwork);
description: Description Resets the RT statistic values of all ports of the Ethernet network on which the passed port exists. It will not influence the Statistic Window or the system variables.
parameters: Parameters portOnNetwork A port of the addressed network.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: EthResetStatisticsOfPort
syntax: Function Syntax void ethResetStatisticsOfPort( ethernetPort port);
description: Description Resets the RT statistic values of the port. It will not influence the Statistic Window or the system variables.
parameters: Parameters port Ethernet port.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: EthSetLinkStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long ethSetLinkStatus ( long channel, long status ); // form 1 long ethSetLinkStatus ( long channel, long hwChannel, long status ); // form 2 long ethSetLinkStatus ( ethernetPort port, long status ); // form 3
description: Description Configures the channel of the Vector hardware to establish or terminate a link.
parameters: Parameters channel Ethernet channel number. Value range: 1..32 status 0 = link down 1 = link up hwChannel Hardware channel number. Vector network interface must support more than 1 hardware channel to use. Value range: 1..16 port Ethernet port, described by network name and port name. Only supported for network-based configurations and physical measurement ports.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success 1 Invalid channel 2 Read configuration failed 3 Link down failed 4 Link up failed 5 Restore bypass failed 6 Invalid link state

name: EthSetPhyState
syntax: Function Syntax long ethSetPhyState (long channel, long phyState ); // form 1 long ethSetPhyState (long channel, long hwChannel, long phyState ); // form 2 long ethSetPhyState (ethernetPort port, long phyState ); // form 3
description: Description Sets the PHY state. TC10 Sleep/Wakeup events can be sent with this function. Note This function can be used only for PHY types supporting this feature like TJA-1101. Form 1 and form 2 is only for channel-based access. Form 3 is only for network-based access.
parameters: Parameters channel Ethernet channel number. Value range: 1..32 phyState 1: ePhyNormalState 2: ePhySleepState 3: ePhyPowerOffState 5: ePhySleepLocalState Note State 5 can only be set with 10Base-T1S TC10-capable modules.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success 1 Invalid channel or port. 2 Failed

name: EthStartPacketGenerator
syntax: Function Syntax long ethStartPacketGenerator ( ethernetPacket txPacket, dword transmissionRate ); long ethStartPacketGenerator ( ethernetPacket txPacket, dword transmissionRate, dword numberOfFrames ); long ethStartPacketGenerator ( ethernetPacket txPacket, dword transmissionRate, dword numberOfFrames, dword counterByteOffset );
description: Description Starts the Ethernet packet generator that sends continuously the configured packets. It is possible to run the packet generator for one packet per Ethernet channel or port. If you do not specify a number of Frames the generator sends until you call EthStopPacketGenerator . Note In network-based mode the sender port must be defined in the txPacket and reference a physical measurement port. The packet generator operates at the lowest priority; it will not send frames while other frames
parameters: Parameters txPacket The Ethernet packet to send. transmissionRate The rate in frames per second the packet should be transmitted. Range: [1 ... 1.000.000] in channel-based mode. Range: [1 ... 30.000.000] in network-based mode. numberOfFrames The number of Frames that should be transmitted overall. Range: [0x1 ... 0xFFFFFFFE], 0xFFFFFFFF to send until EthStopPacketGenerator. counterByteOffset The position a 4 byte counter is placed inside the Ethernet-payload on a zero based index. Index 0 is the
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success -1 Output on specified Ethernet channel not allowed. -2 Packet generator not supported in simulation mode. -3 Packet generator is only available with Vector Ethernet network hardware. -4 Configuration of packet generator failed. -5 Starting of packet generator failed.

name: EthStopPacketGenerator
syntax: Function Syntax long ethStopPacketGenerator ( ethernetPacket txPacket );
description: Description Stops the Ethernet packet generator, which was started with EthStartPacketGenerator .
parameters: Parameters txPacket The Ethernet packet which was used to start the generator.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success -1 Output on specified Ethernet channel not allowed. -2 Packet generator not supported in simulation mode. -3 Packet generator is only available with Vector Ethernet network hardware. -5 Stopping the packet generator failed.

name: InjectCanFrame
syntax: Function Syntax dword InjectCanFrame(int64 simulationTimestamp, word msgChannel, byte dir, message * msg);
description: Description This function injects a CAN frame received via ACF into the event system of CANoe . From the point of view of CANoe , the injected frame is processed as if it had been observed on the given channel at the given simulation time. This means that it is evaluated in the trace window according to the configured database of the channel and that matching event handlers in CAPL programs are invoked. This includes signals and PDUs that are contained in the frame. The semantics of this functio
parameters: Parameters simulationTimestamp The simulation time of the injected frame. msgChannel The channel on which the frame should be injected. Number between 1 and 32. dir The direction of the frame. msg The CAN frame that will be injected.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The injection was successful. 1 The injection failed because the runtime license check was not successful. 2 The injection failed because the function was not called inside a callback registered by RegisterAcfCanObservation . 3 The injection failed because the given channel does not exist. 4 The injection failed because the frame would have been injected on the same channel on which it was observed previously. Injecting on the same channel is not allowed as this results easi

name: InjectFlexRayFrame
syntax: Function Syntax dword InjectFlexRayFrame(int64 simulationTimestamp, word msgChannel, byte dir, frFrame * msg);
description: Description This function injects a FlexRay frame received via ACF into the event system of CANoe . From the point of view of CANoe , the injected frame is processed as if it had been observed on the given channel at the given simulation time. This means that it is evaluated in the trace window according to the configured database of the channel and that matching event handlers in CAPL programs are invoked. This includes signals and PDUs that are contained in the frame. The semantics of this fun
parameters: Parameters simulationTimestamp The simulation time of the injected frame. msgChannel The channel on which the frame should be injected. Number between 1 and 32. dir The direction of the frame. msg The FlexRay frame that will be injected.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The injection was successful. 1 The injection failed because the runtime license check was not successful. 2 The injection failed because the function was not called inside a callback registered by RegisterAcfFlexRayObservation . 3 The injection failed because the given channel does not exist. 4 The injection failed because the frame would have been injected on the same channel on which it was observed previously. Injecting on the same channel is not allowed as this results
