description: Description This function can be implemented in the CAPL program. The function is called up by the node layer if a process variable has been changed with a value command via the CAN Bus.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU ddi Data dictionary identifier of the changed process variables elementNumber Element number of the changed process variables value New value of the process variables
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Iso11783PDDOnDefaultLogRequest
syntax: Function Syntax void Iso11783PDDOnDefaultLogRequest ( dword ecuHandle );
description: Description This function can be implemented in the CAPL program. The function is called up by the node layer if default logging is requested by the Task Controller. Default logging can then be started with the CAPL function Iso11783PDDSetLogTrigger .
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Iso11783PDDOnError
syntax: Function Syntax void Iso11783PDDOnError ( dword ecuHandle, long errorCode, dword param );
description: Description This function can be implemented in the CAPL program. The function is called up by the node layer when an error occurs.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU errorCode Error code param additional parameter, dependent on the error code
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Iso11783PDDOnIdentifyWorkingSet
syntax: Function Syntax long Iso11783PDDOnIdentifyWorkingSet ( dword handle );
description: Description The function is called by the ISO11783 node layer to determine if the node layer is responsible for sending of the Working Set Master message after the Task Controller starts to send his status messages. If this callback function is not implemented or it returns the value 1, the Working Set Master message is sent by the ISO11783 node layer. The transmitted number of Working Set members is 1. If the return value of the function is 0, the ISO11783 node layer doesn't send the Working Se
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU
returnValues: Return Values 1 Working Set Master message is sent by the ISO11783 node layer with member count 1 0 Working Set Master message is sent by the CAPL application

name: Iso11783PDDOnPDM
syntax: Function Syntax long Iso11783PDDOnPDM ( long command, long parameter, long senderAddress, long pddStatus );
description: Description The callback method is called from the ISO11783 Node Layer if the ISO11783 Node Layer receives the Process Data Message parameter group (PDM pg).
parameters: Parameters command First nibble of the first byte of the received PDM pg; usually represents the received command parameter Second nibble of the first byte of the received PDM pg senderAddress Address of the sender of the received PDM pg pddStatus Current state of the Process Data Dictionary 0: not initialized 1: initialized 2: start up delay (6 seconds) 3: wait for task controller status 4: query information 5: transfer object pool 6: object pool activation 7: online 8: task active
returnValues: Return Values 0 received PDM pg will be ignored by the ISO11783 Node Layer 1 received PDM pg will be processed by the ISO11783 Node Layer

name: Iso11783PDDOnStateChanged
syntax: Function Syntax void Iso11783PDDOnStateChanged ( dword ecuHandle, dword state );
description: Description Is called up when the Node Layer changes its state.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU state Task state: 0: not initialized 1: initialized 2: start up delay (6 seconds) 3: wait for task controller status 4: query information 5: transfer object pool 6: object pool activation 7: online 8: task active
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Iso11783PDDsetdistance
syntax: Function Syntax long Iso11783PDDSetDistance ( dword ecuHandle, dword distance );
description: Description The distance covered is set with this function. The value is needed for the distance trigger and should be updated cyclically.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU. The handle must previously have been created with Iso11783CreateECU . distance Distance covered in [m]
returnValues: Return Values Error code

name: Iso11783PDDSetLogTrigger
syntax: Function Syntax long Iso11783PDDSetLogTrigger ( dword ecuHandle, dword command, ulong ddi, ulong elNum, ulong value );
description: Description A measurement command can be executed with this function. It can be used in the callback function Iso11783PDDOnDefaultLogRequest to activate the default logging.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU, must previously has been created with Iso11783CreateECU . command Measurement command: 4: Time Interval 5: Distance Interval 6: Minimum within Threshold 7: Maximum within Threshold 8: Change Threshold ddi Data Dictionary Identifier, 0x0000..0xFFFF elNum Element number, 0x000..0xFFF value Depends on command command value Time Interval time in ms Distance Interval distance in mm Minimum within Threshold Maximum within Threshold Change Threshold value of the
returnValues: Return Values error code

name: Iso11783PDDSetParameter
syntax: Function Syntax long Iso11783PDDSetParameter ( dword ecuHandle, char paramName[], float paramValue );
description: Description Changes an internal parameter of the PDD API.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU. The handle must be created with Iso11783CreateECU beforehand or ZERO for general parameters. paramName Value Description "debug" activates debug outputs in the Write Window 1: on 0: off "autoUpdateVariable" process variable is updated if value command is received 1: auto update 0: process variable has to be updated manually by Iso11783PDDSetValue e.g. in callback function Iso11783PDDOnDataChanged ) "version" supported version of the ISO11783 specification:
returnValues: Return Values 0 process data dictionary has been created successfully < 0 error code

name: Iso11783PDDsetvalue
syntax: Function Syntax long Iso11783PDDSetValue ( dword ecuHandle, dbSignal signal, dword elementNumber, float alue ); long Iso11783PDDSetValue ( dword ecuHandle, dword ddi, dword elementNumber, float value );
description: Description Sets the value of a process variable.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU. The handle must previously have been created with Iso11783CreateECU . signal Signal from the database. The attribute ddi must have been defined for the signal. The signal must be defined in the same database as the node! ddi Data Dictionary Identifier, 0x0000.0xFFFF elementNumber Element number, 0x000.0xFFF value New value of the process variable
returnValues: Return Values error code

name: Iso11783PDDUpdateDeviceDescription
syntax: Function Syntax long Iso11783PDDUpdateDeviceDescription ( dword ecuHandle, char deviceCfgPath [] );
description: Description The function updates the current device description object pool at runtime. Only the device object and objects of type DeviceValuePresentation of the specified file are transmitted.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle Handle of the ECU. The handle must be created with Iso11783CreateECU beforehand or ZERO for general parameters. deviceCfgPath Filename of the device configuration file. The file must be located in the directory of the CANoe configuration.
returnValues: Return Values error code

name: Iso11783Set
syntax: Function Syntax long Iso11783SetAAC( char[] deviceName, long aac); long Iso11783SetIG( char[] deviceName, long ig); long Iso11783SetVS( char[] deviceName, long vs); long Iso11783SetVSInstance( char[] deviceName, long vsi); long Iso11783SetFct( char[] deviceName, long fct); long Iso11783SetFctInstance( char[] deviceName, long fct ); long Iso11783SetECUInstance( char[] deviceName, long ecui ); long Iso11783SetMC( char[] deviceName, long mc); long Iso11783SetIN( char[] deviceName, long in);
description: Description The function modifies a part of the device name.
parameters: Parameters deviceName Buffer containing the modified part of the device name, size of 8 bytes required
returnValues: Return Values 0 or error code

name: Iso11783SetEnvDbl
syntax: Function Syntax dword Iso11783SetEnvDbl ( char envVar[], dword index, float value );
description: Description This function sets the value of an environment variable.
parameters: Parameters envVar Name of the environment variable index Index of the environment variable value New value
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or error code

name: Iso11783SetEnvInt
syntax: Function Syntax dword Iso11783SetEnvInt ( char envVar[], dword index, dword value );
description: Description This function sets the value of an environment variable.
parameters: Parameters envVar Name of the environment variable index Index of the environment variable value New value
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or error code

name: Iso11783SetTPParam
syntax: Function Syntax dword Iso11783SetTPParam ( dword ecuHandle, dword what, dword value );
description: Description Changing transport protocol settings.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle ECU handle what Index of the parameter Index Description Value Range Initial Value 1 Maximum number of bytes, which can be transferred 8 < value < 117440505 100 MByte 2 Number of packets, which are requested by CTS 1 < value < 255 1 3 Number of packets, which are requested by ECTS (Extended TP) 1 < value < 255 64 8 Number of packets, which is used with RTS 1 < value < 255 255 10 Set the used debug level 0 < value < 3 2 11 Use of network management 0 - deactivate network
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or error code

name: Iso11783SetWSMAddr
syntax: Function Syntax dword Iso11783SetWSMAddr ( dword ecuHandle, dword wsmAddress );
description: Description Use this function to set the address of the Working Set Master, if this ECU is a member of a working set. All parameter groups which are addressed to the Working Set Master are also received by an ECU which calls this function.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle ECU handle wsmAddress Address of the Working Set Master for this ECU
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or error code

name: Iso11783TableTime
syntax: Function Syntax dword Iso11783TableTime ( char busName[] );
description: Description The function returns the time stamp of the last received "Request for Address Claim". Use this function to determine, if the network table is up to data.
parameters: Parameters busName Bus name or "default"
returnValues: Return Values Time stamp of the last received Request for Address Claim in 10µs

name: Iso11783TxAbort
syntax: Function Syntax dword Iso11783TxAbort ( dword ecuHandle, dword pgn );
description: Description Interrupt of a transfer that has previously been started with Iso11783TxReqPG() . Please note in this regard that for technical reasons, it is only possible to interrupt PGs that are fragmented.
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle ECU handle pgn Parameter group handle
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or error code

name: Iso11783TxReqPG
syntax: Function Syntax dword Iso11783TxReqPG ( dword ecuHandle, dword pgn, dword destination, dword priority, dword dlc, char data[] ); dword Iso11783TxReqPG ( dword ecuHandle, dword pgn, dword destination, dword priority, dword dlc, byte data[] );
description: Description Sends any PG. The node layer handles fragmenting automatically. As soon as the PG has been transferred, Iso11783AppTxIndication() is called. This gives the application the possibility of protocol monitoring, especially in the case of fragmented PGs. Note It should be noted with this function that the destination address must be transferred in a separate parameter dest for PDU Format I. The PDUS byte of pgNum must therefore contain "00". The PG is then transferred to a specific destin
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle ECU handle pgn PGN which should be sent destination Destination address or global address (255) priority Priority dlc Number of data bytes data Data
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or error code

name: Iso11783UpdateTable
syntax: Function Syntax dword Iso11783UpdateTable ( char busName[] );
description: Description Updates the network table. The function deletes the content of the current table and initiates the RequestPGN that was used to request Address Claiming. After that, the table is restructured. With busName , you assign a table which is used in a network. A node which was displaced from the network if it has lost the Address Claiming, will be placed in the network table with the NULL address. After calling this function, all contents of the table will deleted and the table will update
parameters: Parameters busName Bus name or "default"
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or error code

name: J1587AppendParameter
syntax: Function Syntax byte J1587AppendParameter (J1587Message msg /*in/out*/, J1587Param param /*in*/)
description: Description Appends a J1587 parameter to a J1708 message.
parameters: Parameters msg J1708 message param returned parameter at the given index
returnValues: Return Values Value Description 0 no error 0x1 A J1587Param of type PCMD not specifying a PPID cannot be appended to a J1587 message with DLC > 0 in any case (a "non PPID" PCMD always has its own message, there are no multiple PCMD messages!). 0x2 The parameter to append is of a different page than the first parameter (if yet any = DLC > 0) of the J1708 message. 0x3 The parameter to append is of a different type (parameter, proprietary parameter, PCMD data) than the first parameter (if yet

name: J1587GetParameter
syntax: Function Syntax byte J1587GetParameter (J1587Message msg /*in*/, J1587Param param /*out*/, word index /*in*/)
description: Description Gets a J1587 parameter inside a J1708 message at a given index.
parameters: Parameters msg J1708 message param returned parameter at the given index index index of parameter to retrieve
returnValues: Return Values 0 or error code

name: J1587GetParameterByPID
syntax: Function Syntax byte J1587GetParameterByPID (J1587Message msg /*in*/, J1587Param param /*out*/, dword dbPID /*in*/)
description: Description Gets a J1587 parameter inside a J1708 message given a specific PID. The function returns an error if a parameter with the specified dbPID is not found inside the J1708 message.
parameters: Parameters msg J1708 message param returned parameter at the given index dbPID database PID
returnValues: Return Values 0 or error code

name: J1587GetParameterCount
syntax: Function Syntax word J1587GetParameterCount (J1587Message msg /*in*/)
description: Description Gets the count of J1587 parameters inside a given J1708 message that can be used to further calls to J1587GetParameter() .
parameters: Parameters msg J1708 message
returnValues: Return Values number of parameters

name: J1587SetJ1587TP
syntax: Function Syntax void setJ1587TP ( j1587Param param, dword mode );
description: Description Selects the setting for the transport protocol to use when sending a J1587 parameter via the output function.
parameters: Parameters param J1587 parameter for which the setting is used mode 0 (default) select the transport protocol by means of the receiver (selector J1587_ReceiverMID) MID 127: Multisection other MID: CMP/CDP 1 disable use of any transport protocol 2 use Multisection 3 use CMP/CDP
returnValues: Return Values —

name: J1939AddContainedPG
syntax: Function Syntax J1939AddContainedPG (message* multiPG, pg* pgToAdd)
description: Description Checks whether the multiPG (previously initialized with J1939InitializeMultiPG ) is large enough to accommodate the pgToAdd as a containedPG . The function should be used in combination. If the DLC of the multiPG is too small for this, the necessary adjustment (if possible) is made, so that the smallest possible CAN FD is created. The pgToAdd is inserted directly after the last existing containedPG . Payload behind the used pgToAdd is covered with the padding service. The length of t
parameters: Parameters multiPG This message is a multiPG and will accommodate the pgToAdd as additional containedPG . pgToAdd This PG is to be integrated into the multiPG as an additional containedPG .
returnValues: Return Values Negative value means that the pgToAdd could not be integrated: 0 pgToAdd is successfully integrated into the multiPG -1 multiPG is NULL -3 Content of multiPg is not compliant with the standard: it is not possible to determine the end of the last C-PG. -6 multiPG is not a CAN FD Frame -7 pgToAdd is NULL -8 pgToAdd is too big to be transmitted using multiPG -9 there is not enough space in the multiPG for the pgToAdd

name: J1939CalcChecksum
syntax: Function Syntax J1939CalcChecksum(pg * msg, char[] calculationMethod, dword & checksum)
description: Description Provides the checksum calculation for all PGNs as described in the J1939 Digital Annex. Possible calculation algorithms and message counter alignment is described in J1939 Functional Safety – Messages With Integrated Checksum and Counter .
parameters: Parameters msg The parameter group to calculate the checksum for. calculationMethod Two letters in the form [A-E][a-f] (e.g. Bc ) that define the calculation rule and position of the checksum and message counter. checksum Out parameter where the checksum is stored.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Checksum is successfully filled. -1 Error: calculation method is invalid.

name: J1939CalculateCrcOfSDM
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939CalculateCrcOfSDM(qword sdmPayload)
description: Description Calculates CRC for payload of a Safety Data Message according to SAE J1939-76
parameters: Parameters sdmPayload Payload of a Safety Data Message.
returnValues: Return Values Calculated CRC

name: J1939CheckMultiPG
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939CheckMultiPG(message multiPG); long J1939CheckMultiPG(message multiPG, char[] errorText);
description: Description Checks whether a J1939-22 Multi-PG and its Contained-PGs (C-PGs) are consistent.
parameters: Parameters multiPG A J1939-22 Multi-PG message. errorText Returns an error text if return value is < 0.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Multi-PG is valid -1 Leading three bytes of Padding C-PG (TOS = 0) must be 0x00 -2 Padding bytes 4-15 shall be set all to 0xAA or all to 0x00 -3 Data length of Multi-PG is unnecessary big for the contained C-PGs -4 Multi-PG is using the D_PDU1 format (destination specific) and therefore must not contain PDU2 C-PGs -5 Not enough space for another C-PG. Expected padding bytes -6 C-PG is a A-PDU1 and therefore PDU Specific (PS) field of PGN must be 0x00 -7 Length of C-PG exceed

name: J1939ConvertMessageToPg
syntax: Function Syntax void J1939ConvertMessageToPg(pg* target, message* source);
description: Description Converts a CAN message to a J1939 parameter group (PG). This function copies all possible selectors as well as data up to 64 bytes.
parameters: Parameters target Target parameter group. source Source CAN frame.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: J1939ConvertPgToMessage
syntax: Function Syntax void J1939ConvertPgToMessage(message* target, pg* source);
description: Description Converts a J1939 parmeter group (PG) to a CAN message. This function copies all possible selectors as well as data up to 64 bytes. Data beyond 64 bytes will be cut off.
parameters: Parameters target Target CAN frame. source Source parameter group.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: J1939FillSHM
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939FillSHM(pg pgSDM, byte sequenceNumber, pg* pgSHM);
description: Description This function uses the PGN, addresses, priority and payload of a Safety Data Message ( SDM ) as well as a sequence number to set the payload, priority and addresses of a Safety Header Message ( SHM ). The payload of the SDM consist of following signals (see SAE J1939-76 ): Inverted SDM Data Page. Inverted SDM Extended Data Page. SDG Sequence Number. Inverted SDM Source Address. Inverted SDM PS Value. Inverted SDM PF Value. SDM Data CRC.
parameters: Parameters pgSDM The data of SDM is used to fill the SHM. DLC of SDM shall be 8. sequenceNumber New sequence number of the SHM. pgSHM SHM message to be filled. DLC of SHM shall be 8.
returnValues: Return Values 0 SHM is successfully filled. -1 DLC of SDM is not 8 bytes.

name: J1939GetAddress
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939GetAddress ( char[] busName, dword componentMask, char eviceName[8] ); dword J1939GetAddress ( long busHandle, dword componentMask, char deviceName[8] );
description: Description This function returns a network address with which a device that is described by the function parameters logged onto the network.
parameters: Parameters busName bus name or "default" busHandle bus handle, see J1939GetBus componentMask  The bitmask specifies the part of the name, which should be used for comparison. To compare the whole name use the value 0x1ff. Combine the values of the following table with "or" to compare only parts of the name. If there are more than one node which fits to name, only the first one is returned. Bit Description 1 Self-configurable 2 Industry group 4 Device class instance 8 Device class 16 Functio
returnValues: Return Values address of the node or Null Address (0xFE) if no device is found or FFFFFFFFh if the functions fails.

name: J1939GetChecksumAndCounter
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939GetChecksumAndCounter(pg * msg, char[] calculationMethod, dword & checksum, dword & counter)
description: Description This function returns the checksum and the message counter of the given parameter group. The available calculation methods are described in J1939 Functional Safety – Messages With Integrated Checksum and Counter and are used to determine the alignment of the checksum and message counter in the given parameter group. After calling this function the parameters checksum and counter contain the values that where transmitted in the message and the return value indicates if an error occurr
parameters: Parameters msg The parameter group to get the checksum and counter from. calculationMethod Two letters in the form [A-E][a-f] (e.g. Bc ) that define the calculation rule and position of the checksum and message counter. checksum The checksum retrieved from the given parameter group. counter The message counter retrieved from the given parameter group.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Checksum successfully filled. -1 Error: calculation method is invalid.

name: J1939GetContainedPG
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939GetContainedPG(message multiPG, byte index, pg cpg);
description: Description Returns a Contained PG (C-PG) at the specified index of a J1939-22 Multi-PG . This function does not return the parameters of Padding C-PG (TOS = 0).
parameters: Parameters multiPG A J1939-22 Multi-PG message. index Index of the C-PG within the Multi-PG. cpg This PG is filled with data of the Contained PG (Parameter Group Number (PGN), type of service, trailer format, payload as well as source and destination address of the Multi-PG and priority of the Multi-PG.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function successfully determined the parameters -1 Message is not a J1939-22 Multi-PG message -2 Index is invalid

name: J1939GetContainedPGAssuranceData
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939GetContainedPGAssuranceData(message multiPG, byte index, qword assuranceData);
description: Description Returns the assurance data of a Contained-PG (C-PG) at the specified index of a J1939-22 Multi-PG .
parameters: Parameters multiPG A J1939-22 Multi-PG message index Index of the C-PG within the Multi-PG assuranceData Returns the assurance data of the C-PG
returnValues: Return Values >0 Length in bytes of assurance data 0 C-PG contains no assurance data -1 Message is no J1939-22 Multi-PG message -2 Index is invalid

name: J1939GetContainedPGData
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939GetContainedPGData(message multiPG, byte index, byte[] data);
description: Description Returns the PG Data of a Contained PG (C-PG) at the specified index of a J1939-22 Multi-PG . The maximum length of PG Data is 60 bytes.
parameters: Parameters multiPG A J1939-22 Multi-PG message index Index of the C-PG within the Multi-PG payload Returns the PG Data of the C-PG
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 Length in bytes of PG Data -1 Message is no J1939-22 Multi-PG message -2 Index is invalid

name: J1939GetDataPage
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939GetDataPage (pg* aPG); // form 1 dword J1939GetDataPage (dword canId); // form 2
description: Description Returns the data page bit of a parameter group or CAN ID.
parameters: Parameters aPG The function returns the data page of this parameter group. canId The function returns the data page of this CAN ID.
returnValues: Return Values 0 or 1 Data page bit.

name: J1939GetDestAddress
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939GetDestAddress (pg* aPG); // form 1 dword J1939GetDestAddress (dword canId); // form 2
description: Description Returns the value of the PDU specific field of a parameter group or CAN ID. For PDU1 messages this is the destination address.
parameters: Parameters aPG The function returns the destination address of this parameter group. canId The function returns the destination address of this CAN ID.
returnValues: Return Values 0..255 Destination address (PDU1 Format) / PDU Specific field (PDU2 Format).

name: J1939GetDeviceName
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939GetDeviceName(byte address, qword& deviceName); // (form 1); long J1939GetDeviceName(byte address, byte[] deviceName); // (form 2) long J1939GetDeviceName(dbNode node, qword& deviceName); // (form 3) long J1939GetDeviceName(dbNode node, byte[] deviceName); // (form 4)
description: Description Function retrieves the J1939 NAME for a J1939 node - as reported on the bus with the PG Address Claimed . However, if the node is offline or did not report it's name, the function retrieves the J1939 NAME from the database if available.
parameters: Parameters address Address of the J1939 node whose J1939 NAME is to be retrieved. node Database node that identifies the J1939 node whose J1939 NAME is to be retrieved. deviceName (forms 1 and 3) Retrieved J1939 NAME as a qword. deviceName (forms 2 and 4) Retrieved J1939 NAME as a byte array with at least 8 bytes length.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success, node is offline. 1 Success, node is online. -1 Device name not available or not seen on the bus. -2 Array length is less than 8 bytes.

name: J1939GetDTC
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939GetDTC(pg pgWithDTC, dword spn, byte fmi, byte oc, dword &dtcValue)
description: Description Function looks through a J1939 diagnostic message and returns the first DTC block that matches the search criteria, i.e. that contains defined SPN/FMI/OC values.
parameters: Parameters pgWithDTC Message to be checked for a specific DTC block. Must be a J1939 Parameter Group. spn Suspect Parameter Number of the specified DTC. 0xFFFFFFFF if spn is to be ignored. fmi Failure Mode Identifier of the specified DTC. 0xFFFF if fmi is to be ignored. oc Occurrence Counter of the specified DTC . 0xFFFF if oc is to be ignored. dtcValue The value of the found DTC block.
returnValues: Return Values ≥ 0 Byte number of the start position of the found DTC block (zero-based). -1 Message does not contain a DTC block matching the search criteria.

name: J1939GetFmiFromDTC
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939GetFmiFromDTC(dword dtc)
description: Description Function extracts FMI from DTC.
parameters: Parameters dtc DTC from which an FMI is to be extracted.
returnValues: Return Values Extracted FMI.

name: J1939GetNumberOfContainedPGs
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939GetNumberOfContainedPGs(message multiPG);
description: Description Returns the number of C-PGs which are contained in a J1939-22 Multi-PG . A Padding C-PG (TOS = 0) is ignored.
parameters: Parameters multiPG A J1939-22 Multi-PG message
returnValues: Return Values ≥0 Number of Contained-PGs which are contained in the message -1 Message is no J1939-22 Multi-PG message

name: J1939GetNumOfDTCs
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939GetNumOfDTCs(pg pgWithDTCs, dword spn, byte fmi, byte oc)
description: Description Function looks through a J1939 diagnostic message and returns the number of DTC blocks that match the search criteria, i.e. that contains defined SPN/FMI/OC values.
parameters: Parameters pgWithDTC Message to be checked for a specific DTC block. Must be a J1939 Parameter Group. spn Suspect Parameter Number of the specified DTC. 0xFFFFFFFF if spn is to be ignored. fmi Failure Mode Identifier of the specified DTC. 0xFFFF if fmi is to be ignored. oc Occurrence Counter of the specified DTC . 0xFFFF if oc is to be ignored.
returnValues: Return Values ≥ 0 Byte number of the start position of the found DTC block (zero-based). -1 Message does not contain a DTC block matching the search criteria.

name: J1939GetOcFromDTC
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939GetOcFromDTC(dword dtc)
description: Description Function extracts OC from DTC.
parameters: Parameters dtc DTC from which an OC is to be extracted.
returnValues: Return Values Extracted OC.

name: J1939GetPGN
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939GetPGN (pg* aPG); // form 1 dword J1939GetPGN (dword canId); // form 2
description: Description Returns the parameter group number (PGN) of a parameter group or CAN ID.
parameters: Parameters aPG The function returns the PGN of this parameter group. canId The function returns the PGN of this CAN ID.
returnValues: Return Values 0..3FFFFh Parmeter group number.

name: J1939GetPrio
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939GetPrio (pg* aPG); // form 1 dword J1939GetPrio (dword canId); // form 2
description: Description Returns the J1939 priority of a parameter group or CAN ID.
parameters: Parameters aPG The function returns the priority of this parameter group. canId The function returns the priority of this CAN ID.
returnValues: Return Values 0..7 Priority

name: J1939GetSourceAddress
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939GetSourceAddress (pg* aPG); // form 1 dword J1939GetSourceAddress (dword canId); // form 2
description: Description Returns the source address of a parameter group or CAN ID.
parameters: Parameters aPG The function returns the source address of this parameter group. canId The function returns the source address of this CAN ID.
returnValues: Return Values 0..255 Source address.

name: J1939GetSpnFromDTC
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939GetSpnFromDTC(dword dtc)
description: Description Function extracts SPN from DTC.
parameters: Parameters dtc DTC from which an SPN is to be extracted.
returnValues: Return Values Extracted SPN.

name: J1939InitializeMultiPG
syntax: Function Syntax J1939InitializeMultiPG(message* multiPG, byte sa); // (form 1) J1939InitializeMultiPG(message* multiPG, byte prio, byte da, byte sa); // (form 2)
description: Description Sets CAN ID of the multiPG (11 bit CAN ID for form 1 resp. 29 bit for form 2). Form 2 sets the PGN to 0x2500. Sets selector FDF of the multiPG to 1 (corresponds to CAN FD). Sets DLC of the multiPG to 4 (minimum length of the multiPG ). The payload of the multiPG is assigned with the padding service: the first three bytes are set to 0x00, the last byte to 0xAA. To add C-PGs to the newly initialized Multi-PG you can use the CAPL function J1939AddContainedPG .
parameters: Parameters multiPG This message object is to be initiated to multiPG . sa Source Address of the multiPG . da Destination Address of the multiPG . prio Priority of the multiPG .
returnValues: Return Values 0 multiPG is successfully initiated < 0 an error occurred

name: J1939InitPGData
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939InitPGData (pg * aPG);
description: Description Sets all data bytes of the parameter group to 255.
parameters: Parameters aPG Parameter group which is initialized.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Initialization succeeded -1 General error

name: J1939IsPDU1
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939IsPDU1 (dword canId);
description: Description Checks if the parameter group number (PGN) contained in the CAN ID has the PDU Format 1 .
parameters: Parameters canId CAN ID to check.
returnValues: Return Values 0 PGN is PDU Format 2. 1 PGN is PDU Format 1.

name: J1939IsPDU2
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939IsPDU2 (dword canId);
description: Description Checks if the parameter group number (PGN) contained in the CAN ID has the PDU Format 2 .
parameters: Parameters canId CAN ID to check.
returnValues: Return Values 0 PGN is PDU Format 1. 1 PGN is PDU Format 2.

name: J1939MakeCanId
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939MakeCanId (byte priority , dword pgn, byte destinationAddress, byte sourceAddress, byte setExtendedBit);
description: Description Assembles a CAN ID of a parameter group (PG) from source/destination address, parameter group number (PGN) and priority. For a PGN of PDU Format 2, the parameter destination address is ignored. The parameter setExtendedBit defines whether the most significant bit of the resulting CAN ID is set (see CAN Extended Identifier ).
parameters: Parameters priority Priority of the PG. pgn Parameter group number (PGN) of the PG. destinationAddress Destination address of the PG. sourceAddress Source address of the PG. setExtendedBit Defines whether the most significant bit of the resulting CAN ID is set.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: J1939MakeDTC
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939MakeDTC(dword spn, byte fmi, byte oc, dword &dtcValue)
description: Description Function generates a DTC from the defined SPN, FMI and OC.
parameters: Parameters spn Suspect Parameter Number of the specified DTC. Valid value range: 0x0 – 0x7FFFF. fmi Failure Mode Identifier of the specified DTC. Valid value range: 0x0 – 0x1F. oc Occurrence Counter of the specified DTC . Valid value range: 0x0 – 0x7F. dtcValue The value of the generated DTC block.
returnValues: Return Values 0 dtcValue is successfully generated. -1 one of the input parameters does not fit into the valid value range.

name: J1939Output
syntax: Function Syntax output(pg pg_variable);
description: Description Outputs a parameter group onto the CAN bus.
parameters: Parameters pg_variable Variable of the type "pg".
returnValues: Return Values —

name: J1939ParseSHM
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939ParseSHM(pg* pgSHM, dword & sdmPgn, byte& sdmSourceAddress, byte& sdmDestinationAddress, byte& sequenceNumber, dword & crc):
description: Description Provides data of the SDM message, which are contained in payload of the SHM message according to SAE J1939-76 .
parameters: Parameters pgSHM SHM to parse. DLC of SHM shall be 8. sdmPgn Returns the PGN of the SDM. sdmSourceAddress Returns the source address of the SDM. sdmDestinationAddress Returns the destination address of the SDM. sequenceNumber Returns the sequence number of the Safety Data Group (SDG). crc Returns the SDM Data CRC.
returnValues: Return Values 0 SHM is successfully parsed. -1 DLC of a PG is not 8 bytes.

name: J1939SetChecksumAndCounter
syntax: Function Syntax long J1939SetChecksumAndCounter(pg * msg, char[] calculationMethod, dword counter)
description: Description This function calculates and sets the checksum of the given parameter group as well as the given counter. The available calculation algorithms are described in J1939 Functional Safety – Messages With Integrated Checksum and Counter .
parameters: Parameters msg The parameter group to calculate and set the checksum for. calculationMethod Two letters in the form [A-E][a-f] (e.g. "Bc") that define the calculation rule and position of the checksum and message counter. counter Current message counter to be set in the given parameter group.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Checksum successfully filled. -1 Error: calculation method is invalid.

name: J1939SetDatapage
syntax: Function Syntax void J1939SetDatapage (pg* aPG, dword dataPage);
description: Description Sets the data page of a parameter group.
parameters: Parameters aPG Data page of this parameter group is set. dataPage New data page. A value bigger than 1 is truncated.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: J1939SetDestAddress
syntax: Function Syntax void J1939SetDestAddress (pg* aPG, dword destinationAddress);
description: Description Sets the PDU specific field of a parameter group or CAN ID. For PDU1 messages this is the destination address.
parameters: Parameters aPG Destination address of this parameter group is set. destinationAddress New destination address (PDU1 Format) or PDU Specific field (PDU2 Format). A value bigger than 255 is truncated.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: J1939SetPrio
syntax: Function Syntax void J1939SetPrio (pg* aPG, dword priority);
description: Description Sets the priority of a parameter group or CAN ID.
parameters: Parameters aPG Priority of this parameter group is set. priority New priority. A value bigger than 7 is truncated.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: J1939SetSourceAddress
syntax: Function Syntax void J1939SetSourceAddress (pg* aPG, dword sourceAddress);
description: Description Sets the source address of a parameter group.
parameters: Parameters aPG Source address of this parameter group is set. sourceAddress New source address. A value bigger than 255 is truncated.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GBT27930ILBlockRxMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long GBT27930IL_BlockRxMessage (dword pgn, dword sourceAddress, qword filterMask, qword filterValue); // form 1 long GBT27930IL_BlockRxMessage (dbNode node, dword pgn, dword sourceAddress, qword filterMask, qword filterValue); // form 2
description: Description Prevents processing of a received message by the interaction layer. The message to block is specified by PGN, destination source address and a part of the first eight data bytes. To revert this command you can use GBT27930IL_ResetBlockedRxMessage or GBT27930IL_ResetAllBlockedTxMessages .
parameters: Parameters pgn The message with this PGN shall be blocked. sourceAddress 0 - 255: The message which is sent to this address shall be blocked. 0xFFFFFFFF (-1): The source address of the message doesn't matter. filterMask Defines the bits of the message which shall be used to identify the message. filterValue Defines the value of the bits of the message which shall be used to identify the message. If the value of the masked bits of a received message is equal to this value then the data of the mes
returnValues: Return Values 0 Manipulation is set successfully -102 An invalid parameter is used

name: GBT27930ILBlockTxMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long GBT27930IL_BlockTxMessage (dword pgn, dword destinationAddress, qword filterMask, qword filterValue); // form 1 long GBT27930IL_BlockTxMessage (dbNode node, dword pgn, dword destinationAddress, qword filterMask, qword filterValue); // form 2
description: Description Prevents transmitting of a message generated and sent by the interaction layer. The message to block is specified by PGN, destination address and a part of the first eight data bytes. To revert this command you can use GBT27930IL_ResetBlockedTxMessage or GBT27930IL_ResetAllBlockedTxMessages .
parameters: Parameters pgn The message with this PGN shall be blocked. destinationAddress 0 - 255: The message which is sent to this address shall be blocked. 0xFFFFFFFF (-1): The destination address of the message doesn't matter. filterMask Defines the bits of the message which shall be used to identify the message. filterValue Defines the value of the bits of the message which shall be used to identify the message. If the value of the masked bits of a sent message is equal to this value then the data of t
returnValues: Return Values 0 Manipulation is set successfully -102 An invalid parameter is used

name: GBT27930ILControlInit
syntax: Function Syntax long GBT27930IL_ControlInit (); // form 1 long GBT27930IL_ControlInit (dbNode node); // form 2
description: Description This function can only be used in on preStart , to suppress the auto-start function of the IL.
parameters: Parameters node Simulation node to apply the function
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or IL Error Code

name: GBT27930ILControlStart

name: GBT27930ILOnRxMessage

description: Description This callback function is called from the GBT27930 IL if the GBT27930 IL receives the parameter group, namely before the parameter group is processed by the GBT27930 IL. The message data can be manipulated in the callback method or handling of the message by the IL can be suppressed. Usage To ignore some incoming messages. To simulate faulty behavior of the node that communicates with simulated node (by manipulating of incoming messages).
parameters: Parameters rxPG message which is received
returnValues: Return Values 0 Received parameter group will be ignored by the J1939 Interaction Layer. 1 Received parameter group will be processed by the J1939 Interaction Layer.

name: GBT27930ILSetMaxAllowedMissingTimeForMsg
syntax: Function Syntax long GBT27930IL_SetMaxAllowedMissingTimeForMsg (dword msgCode, dword maxAllowedMissingTime); // form 1 long GBT27930IL_SetMaxAllowedMissingTimeForMsg (dbNode node, dword msgCode, dword maxAllowedMissingTime); // form 2
description: Description For simulated BMS node : defines the maximum time during which the specified message from the Charger may be missing. After this time the simulated BMS stops charging and starts sending the BEM message. For simulated Charger node : defines the maximum time during which the specified message from the BMS may be missing. After this time the simulated Charger stops charging and starts sending the CEM message.
parameters: Parameters msgCode defines the respective message. The following values are supported Value Meaning for BMS Node Meaning for Charger Node 0 CRM (BMS is not recognized) — 1 CRM (BMS is recognized) BRM 2 CML BCP 3 CRO (not ready for charging) BRO (not ready for charging) 4 CRO (ready for charging) BRO (ready for charging) 5 CCS BCL 6 CST BCS 7 CSD BST 8 CHM BSD
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success < 0 IL Error Code

name: GNSSILActivateDiagnosticsSupport
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_ActivateDiagnosticsSupport (byte enable); // form 1 long GNSSIL_ActivateDiagnosticsSupport (dbNode node, byte enable); // form 2
description: Description This function activates or deactivates the support of J1939 diagnostics by the IL. If diagnostics support is activated the node: sends message DM1 always cyclically sends message DM2 if DM2 is requested clears all previously active DTCs if DM3 is requested clears all active DTCs if DM11 is requested sends configured diagnostics messages if they are requested If diagnostics support is activated then diagnostics message which are in the Tx list of the node are disabled and therefor not
parameters: Parameters enable Enables or disables support of J1939 diagnostics: 0: Disable support of J1939 diagnostics 1: Enable support of J1939 diagnostics node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code

name: GNSSILActivateDTC
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_ActivateDTC (dword spn, byte fmi, dword options); // form 1 long GNSSIL_ActivateDTC (dword spn, byte fmi, byte occurrenceCount, dword options); // form 2 long GNSSIL_ActivateDTC (dbNode node, dword spn, byte fmi, dword options); // form 3 long GNSSIL_ActivateDTC (dbNode node, dword spn, byte fmi, byte occurrenceCount, dword options); // form 4
description: Description This function activates a diagnostics trouble code (DTC) and add it to the list of active DTCs. This list with active DTCs is reported in the message DM1. The occurrence count of the DTC is either determined automatically (form 1, 3) or set to a specific value (form 2, 4). Calling form 1 or 3 of the function increments the occurrence count of the DTC with every call. The DTC is uniquely identified by the combination of SPN and FMI. The activated DTC uses conversion method 4 (SPN repr
parameters: Parameters spn Suspect Parameter Number of the activated DTC, 0..524287. fmi Failure Mode Indicator of the activated DTC, 0.. 31. options Bit 0 = 0: Send message DM1 as soon as possible. Bit 0 = 1: Send message DM1 with next cycle. occurrenceCount Occurrence Count of the activated DTC, 0..127. If a form without this parameter is used, then the occurrence count is determined automatically. node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code -803 Failed to activate DTC because diagnostics support is not activated

name: GNSSILAddBezierToGeoPoint
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_AddBezierToGeoPoint (float latitude, float longitude); // form 1 long GNSSIL_AddBezierToGeoPoint (dbNode node, float latitude, float longitude); // form 2
description: Description The function adds a Bézier curve to the vehicle’s trajectory. It ends at the given geographic point. The Bézier curve adapts to its adjacent trajectory segments (up to two before and two after) to create tangent-continuous and curvature-continuous joints.
parameters: Parameters latitude Latitude (in degrees) of the new end point. Valid range: -85.0 ≤ latitude ≤ 85.0 longitude Longitude (in degrees) of the new end point. Valid range: -180.0 ≤ longitude ≤ 180.0 node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Function was executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code

name: GNSSILAddBezierToXYDiff
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_AddBezierToXYDiff (float xDiff, float yDiff); // form 1 long GNSSIL_AddBezierToXYDiff (dbNode node, float xDiff, float yDiff); // form 2
description: Description The function adds a Bézier curve to the vehicle’s trajectory. The end point is the sum of the previous (cartesian) end point and the given difference parameters. The origin of the cartesian coordinate system is the start point of the trajectory, the positive x-axis points east, the positive y-axis points north. The Bézier curve adapts to its adjacent trajectory segments (up to two before and two after) to create tangent-continuous and curvature-continuous joints.
parameters: Parameters xDiff X difference (in meter) of the new end point. Valid range: the new end point must be within -180.0° ≤ longitude ≤ 180.0°. yDiff Y difference (in meter) of the new end point. Valid range: the new end point must be within -85.0° ≤ latitude ≤ 85.0°. node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Function was executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code

name: GNSSILAddCircleArcCourseStartEnd

description: Description The function adds a circular arc with the given radius to the vehicle’s trajectory. The arc starts at the previous end point with the given start course and ends with the given end course. The new end point is calculated based on these values.
parameters: Parameters radius Radius (in meter) of the circle arc. radius < 0 : curved right radius > 0 : curved left Valid ranges: -10000.0 ≤ radius ≤ -0.1 and 10000.0 ≤ radius ≤ 0.1 courseStart Course (in degrees) at the start point. Valid range: 0.0 ≤ courseStart ≤ 360.0 courseEnd Course (in degrees) at the end point. Valid range: 0.0 ≤ courseStart ≤ 360.0 node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Function was executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code

name: GNSSILAddDTC
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_AddDTC (dword pgn, dword spn, byte fmi, byte occurrenceCount); // form 1 long GNSSIL_AddDTC (dbNode node, dword pgn, dword spn, byte fmi, byte occurrenceCount); // form 2
description: Description This function adds a diagnostics trouble code (DTC) to a diagnostics message. If the diagnostics message with the specified PGN is requested, the response contains the added DTC. The added DTC uses conversion method 4 (SPN represented as Intel format for all 19 bits with the SPN Conversion Method set to 0). You can remove an added DTC with function GNSSIL_RemoveDTC . Note : You can use this function only if support of J1939 Diagnostics is enabled by function GNSSIL_ActivateDiagnostic
parameters: Parameters spn Suspect Parameter Number of the deactivated DTC, 0..524287. fmi Failure Mode Indicator of the deactivated DTC, 0.. 31. State Returns the current state of the DTC. 0: DTC is not active and hasn't been previously active. 1: DTC is active. 2: DTC has been previously active. occurrenceCount Occurrence Count of the activated DTC, 0..127. pgn Parameter Group Number (PGN) of the message which shall contain the DTC. Possible values are: PGN Acronym Message FECAh DM1 Active Diagnostic Trou
returnValues: Return Values 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code -803 Failed to activate DTC because diagnostics support is not activated

name: GNSSILAddMeander
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_AddMeander (float courseStart, float width, float height, float radius, dword direction, dword numOfLoops); // form 1 long GNSSIL_AddMeander (dbNode node, float courseStart, float width, float height, float radius, dword direction, dword numOfLoops); // form 2
description: Description The function adds a meander line to the vehicle’s trajectory. It consists of straight lines and Bézier curves and therefore has tangent-continuous and curvature-continuous inner joints. Geometrically invalid combinations of valid arguments will lead to an error, as well as a too big overall propagation.
parameters: Parameters courseStart Course (in degrees) at the start point. Valid range: 0.0 ≤ courseStart ≤ 360.0 radius Dimension (in meter) of the Bezier curve segments. Valid range: 0.0 ≤ radius ≤ 10000.0 width Distance (in meter) between the parallel lines of the meander. Valid range: width > 0.0 height Overall height (in meter) of the meander. Valid range: height > 0.0 direction Spreading direction of the meander. ==0 : to the right !=0 : to the left numOfLoops Number of loops contained by the meander.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Function was executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code

description: Description This function deactivates a diagnostics trouble code (DTC) and removes it from the list of active DTCs. A deactivated DTC is no longer reported in message DM1 (Active Diagnostic Trouble Codes, PGN FECAh) but it is moved to the list of previously active DTCs and is reported in the requestable message DM2 (Previously Active Diagnostic Trouble Codes, PGN FECBh). parameters: Parameters spn Suspect Parameter Number of the deactivated DTC, 0..524287. fmi Failure Mode Indicator of the deactivated DTC, 0.. 31. options Bit 0 = 0: Send message DM1 as soon as possible. Bit 0 = 1: Send message DM1 with next cycle. node Simulation node to apply the function.

If an address violation is detected, the simulated node sends an Address Claimed message. If diagnostics support is activated (via GNSSIL_ActivateDiagnosticsSupport ) then the node also sends message DM1 with SPN=2000+nodeAddress, FMI=31 and OC=1. This behavior can be inf

If numberOfDM1Transmissions is 0xFFFFFFFF then sending of DM1 is not stopped. node Simulation node to apply the function.

syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_GetDeviceName (qword& deviceName); // form 1 long GNSSIL_GetDeviceName (byte[] deviceName); // form 2 long GNSSIL_GetDeviceName (dbNode node, qword& deviceName); // form 3 long GNSSIL_GetDeviceName (dbNode node, byte[] deviceName); // form 4
description: Description Function returns the current J1939 NAME of the simulation node (as set in the database and possibly changed by GNSSIL_SetNodeProperty ).
parameters: Parameters deviceName (Forms 1 and 3) Retrieved J1939 NAME as a qword. deviceName (Forms 2 and 4) Retrieved J1939 NAME as a byte array with at least 8 bytes length. node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success, node is offline. 1 Success, node is online. -1 No device name available. -2 Byte array is less than 8 bytes.

name: GNSSILGetDistanceEnd
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_GetDistanceEnd (float& distanceEnd); // form 1 long GNSSIL_GetDistanceEnd (dbNode node, float& distanceEnd); // form 2
description: Description The function returns the predicted distance traveled by the vehicle at the end of the predefined trajectory.
parameters: Parameters distanceEnd Distance (in meter) at the end. node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Function was executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred: See IL Error Code and output in the Write window.

name: GNSSILGetDTCStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_GetDTCStatus (dword spn, byte fmi, word& state, word& occurrenceCount); // form 1 long GNSSIL_GetDTCStatus (dbNode node, dword spn, byte fmi, word& state, word& occurrenceCount); // form 2
description: Description This function returns the current occurrence count and state of a diagnostics trouble code (DTC). The function checks the list of active DTCs and the list of previously active DTCs for the specified DTC. Note : You can use this function only if support of <J1939|ISO11783> Diagnostics is enabled by function GNSSIL_ActivateDiagnosticsSupport .
parameters: Parameters spn Suspect Parameter Number of wanted DTC, 0..524287. fmi Failure Mode Indicator of wanted DTC, 0.. 31. state Returns the current state of the DTC. 0: DTC is not active and hasn't been previously active. 1: DTC is active. 2: DTC has been previously active. occurrenceCount Returns the current occurrent count of the DTC. If DTC has never been active the returned occurrence count is 0. node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code -803 Failed to activate DTC because diagnostics support is not activated -804 Failed to get status of DTC because DTC not found

description: Description The function calculates maximum absolute curvature along the whole predefined trajectory of the vehicle (can be positive or negative). In addition to the curvature value, the function calculates the index of the segment (index starts at 0) and the geographic position of the first occurrence.

description: Description The function returns the geographic position after traveling the given distance on the predefined trajectory. The distance is always the distance traveled by the vehicle. You can choose between the position of the simulated vehicle and the position of the simulated GNSS receiver.
parameters: Parameters device Device for which the position is to be calculated. 0 : vehicle 1 : receiver distance Traveled distance (in meter) of the vehicle. latitude Latitude (in degrees) of the position. longitude Longitude (in degrees) of the position. node Simulation node to apply the function.

description: Description The function returns the cartesian position after traveling the given distance on the predefined trajectory. The distance is always the distance traveled by the vehicle. You can choose between the position of the simulated vehicle and the position of the simulated GNSS receiver. The origin of the cartesian coordinate system is the start point of the trajectory, the positive x-axis points east, the positive y-axis points north.
parameters: Parameters device Device for which the position is to be calculated. 0 : vehicle 1 : receiver distance Traveled distance (in meter) of the vehicle. x X-coordinate (in meter) of the position. y Y-coordinate (in meter) of the position. node Simulation node to apply the function.

If the file was created by any other application, the contained waypoints will be connected either by Bézier curves or by straight lines, determined by the parameter defaultShape . The loaded route can be used as the

If the value of the masked bits of a sent message is equal to this value then the data of
returnValues: Return Values 0 Manipulation is set successfully -102 An invalid parameter is used

name: GNSSILOnAddressClaimConflict
syntax: Function Syntax dword GNSSIL_OnAddressClaimConflict (dword addressArbitrationResult, dword nextAddress, dword& suppressAddressClaimed);
description: Description This callback function is called by any interaction layer that detects an address conflict. A conflict occurs if another node sends an Address Claim Message from the same address with or without preceding request. Additionally, the callback is called when an ISOBUS Interaction Layer sends a Request for Address Claim at initialization time and its preferred address is already used, regardless of the priority of the device name. If the IL is Arbitrary Address Capable the parameter next
parameters: Parameters addressArbitrationResult 0: Arbitration won 1: Arbitration lost 2: Address already in use an current ECU won 3: Address already in use and current ECU lost nextAddress The address the IL will claim if not overridden. suppressAddressClaimed Set to 1, if the ACL message shall be suppressed.
returnValues: Return Values 0..254 The new address that shall be claimed.

name: GNSSILOnAddressViolation
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_OnAddressViolation ( pg * txPG );
description: Description This callback function is called from the IL if address violation detection is enabled ( GNSSIL_EnableAddressViolationDetection ) and if the J1939 IL receives the parameter group with the source address of the simulated node that was not sent by the simulated node itself. If the callback returns 1 then the default behavior is performed: the simulated node sends an Address Claimed message and starts the cyclic sending of message DM1 with SPN=2000+nodeAddress, FMI=31 and OC=1. If the c
parameters: Parameters rxPG Message which has caused the address violation.
returnValues: Return Values 0 simulated node ignores the address violation. 1 simulated node performs the default behavior.

name: GNSSILOnRxMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_OnRxMessage ( pg * rxPG )
description: Description This callback function is called from the J1939 IL if the J1939 IL receives the parameter group, namely before the parameter group is processed by the J1939 IL. The message data can be manipulated in the callback method or handling of the message by the IL can be suppressed. Usage To ignore some incoming messages. To simulate faulty behavior of the node that communicates with simulated node (by manipulating of incoming messages).
parameters: Parameters rxPG message which is received
returnValues: Return Values 0 Received parameter group will be ignored by the J1939 Interaction Layer. 1 Received parameter group will be processed by the J1939 Interaction Layer.

name: GNSSILOnTxPrepare

description: Description This callback function is called from the J1939 IL before a parameter group is sent. The signal value can be updated in the callback or sending of the message can be suppressed. Note Suppressing of AddressClaim and CannotClaimAddress Messages. Using this method you can suppress the AddressClaim or CannotClaimAddress messages as well. Please notify that in this case the corresponding node will switch to the next state (Active, Stopped or Initialized) even though the AddressClaim or Ca
parameters: Parameters txPG message which should be sent
returnValues: Return Values 1 send message 0 do not send message

name: GNSSILRemoveDTC
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_RemoveDTC (dword pgn, dword spn, byte fmi); // form 1 long GNSSIL_RemoveDTC (dword pgn, dword spn); // form 2 long GNSSIL_RemoveDTC (dbNode node, dword pgn, dword spn, byte fmi); // form 3 long GNSSIL_RemoveDTC (dbNode node, dword pgn, dword spn); // form 4
description: Description This function removes a diagnostics trouble code (DTC) from a diagnostics message which has been added by GNSSIL_AddDTC . Form 2 and 4 removes all DTCs with the specified SPN (regardless of FMI). A removed DTC is no longer reported by the message if the message is requested. Note: You can use this function only if support of J1939 Diagnostics is enabled by function GNSSIL_ActivateDiagnosticsSupport .
parameters: Parameters pgn Parameter Group Number (PGN) of the message which shall contain the DTC. spn Suspect Parameter Number of the deactivated DTC, 0..524287. fmi Failure Mode Indicator of the deactivated DTC, 0.. 31. node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code -803 Failed to activate DTC because diagnostics support is not activated -804 Failed to activate DTC because DTC not found

name: GNSSILSaveRoute
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_SaveRoute (char[] filepath); // form 1 long GNSSIL_SaveRoute (dbNode node, char[] filepath); // form 2
description: Description Saves the currently defined vehicle route to a GPX file. The function saves the start point and every route segment as a <trkpt> element and uses an <extensions> element within every <trkpt> to store additional, GNSS IL specific parameters. With the function GNSSIL_LoadRoute , it is possible to restore the saved vehicle route without any losses.
parameters: Parameters filepath Path of the target file (*.gpx). node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Function was executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code

name: GNSSILSetAcceleration
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_SetAcceleration (float speedTarget, float acceleration); // form 1 long GNSSIL_SetAcceleration (dbNode node, float speedTarget, float acceleration); // form 2
description: Description The function changes the speed of the simulated vehicle linearly to reach the given speed by applying the given acceleration. If the sign of the difference of the given speed and the current speed is different to the sign of the given acceleration, the call of the function will fail.
parameters: Parameters speedTarget Speed (in meter per second) that is to be reached. Valid range: speedTarget ≥ 0.0 acceleration Acceleration (in meter per second2) at which the target speed is to be reached. acceleration < 0 : deceleration of vehicle acceleration > 0 : acceleration of vehicle Valid range: acceleration != 0.0 node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Function was executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code

name: GNSSILSetCurvature
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_SetCurvature (float curvature); // form 1 long GNSSIL_SetCurvature (dbNode node, float curvature); // form 2
description: Description The functions sets the current curvature of the simulated vehicle’s movement. At the first call of this function, the predefined route is left. It is not possible to go back to the predefined route and to follow it again. The vehicle will now travel on a circle arc or a straight line. As the curvature is the reciprocal of the radius, a smaller absolute curvature value will lead to a bigger absolute radius.
parameters: Parameters curvature Curvature (in 1/meter) to apply. curvature < 0 : curved right curvature = 0 : straight curvature > 0 : curved left node Simulation node to apply the function.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Function was executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code

name: GNSSILSetLampStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_SetLampStatus (dword pgn, word lampStatus); // form 1 long GNSSIL_SetLampStatus (dbNode node, dword pgn, word lampStatus); // form 2
description: Description This function sets the lamp status of a diagnostics message. If the diagnostics message with the specified PGN is requested, the response contains the specified lamp status. The lamp status is reported by the first two bytes of a diagnostics message. Note : The diagnostics message is only sent if support of J1939 Diagnostics is enabled by function GNSSIL_ActivateDiagnosticsSupport .
parameters: Parameters pgn Parameter Group Number (PGN) of the message which shall contain the lamb status. Possible values are: PGN Acronym Message FECAh DM1 Active Diagnostic Trouble Codes FECBh DM2 Previously Active Diagnostic Trouble Codes FECFh DM6 Emission-Related Pending Diagnostic Trouble Codes FED4h DM12 Emission-Related MIL-On Diagnostic Trouble Codes FDB5h DM23 Emission-Related Previously MIL-On Diagnostic Trouble Codes FD82h DM27 All Pending DTCs FD80h DM28 Emission-Related Permanent Diagnostic
returnValues: Return Values 0 success < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code -803 Failed to activate DTC because diagnostics support is not activated

name: GNSSILSetMessageProperty
syntax: Function Syntax long GNSSIL_SetMessageProperty ( dbMsg msg, char propertyName[], long propertyValue); // form 1 long GNSSIL_SetMessageProperty (dbNode node, dbMsg msg, char propertyName[], long propertyValue); // form 2
description: Description This function sets the internal property of a message.
parameters: Parameters msg message name as defined in the database propertyName name of property to be set Value Description "MessageCounterToContinue" Defines the next value of the intern message counter that will be sent. Applicable for the PGNs 0, 1024, 2816, 61469 and 61483 only (see Message Checksum and Message Counter "). Valid values for propertyValue are 0 to 7 (PGN = 0) or 0 to 15 (all other applicable PGNs). propertyValue new value to be set for the property node Simulation node to apply the funct
returnValues: Return Values 0 success (property has been set successfully) < 0 IL Error Code

Property Value Description Value Range Initial Value FP_Interval Defines delay (im ms) when sending each FP packet (for Fast Packet transmitting). 0..3600000 0 ms BAM_DT_Interval Defines the interval time (in ms) between each TP DT packet (for BAM transmitting). 1..3600000 50 ms FD_BAM_Interval CAN FD only: Defines th

parameters: Parameters x NRP_X (in millimeter) Valid range: -20000.0 ≤ x ≤ 20000.0 y NRP_Y (in millimeter) Valid range: -20000.0 ≤ y ≤ 20000.0 z NRP_Z (in millimeter) Valid range: -20000.0 ≤ z ≤ 20000.0 node Simulation node to apply the function.

Valid range: -180.0 ≤ longitude ≤ 180.0 altitude Altitude (in meter) of the whole trajectory. Valid range: -10000.0 ≤ altitude ≤ 10000.0 node Simulation node to apply the function.

description: Description The function sets values of the NMEA2000® PG GNSS Position Data (PGN = 0x1F805) which are not set by the simulation automatically. The parameters hdop , pdop and geoSep have to be given as physical values.

returnValues: Return Values 0 Function was executed successfully. < 0 An error has occurred: IL Error Code

name: GNSSILSetVerbosity
