syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939TxReqPG ( dword ecuHandle, dword pgn, dword destination, dword priority, dword dlc, char data[] ); dword J1939TxReqPG ( dword ecuHandle, dword pgn, dword destination, dword priority, dword dlc, byte data[] );
description: Description Sends any PG. The node layer handles fragmenting automatically. As soon as the PG has been transferred, J1939TxIndication() is called. This gives the application the possibility of protocol monitoring, especially in the case of fragmented PGs. Note It should be noted with this function that the destination address must be transferred in a separate parameter dest for PDU Format I. The PDUS byte of pgNum must therefore contain "00". The PG is then transferred to a specific destination
parameters: Parameters ecuHandle ECU handle pgn PGN which should be sent destination destination address or global address (255) priority priority dlc number of data bytes data data
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or error code

name: J1939UpdateTable
syntax: Function Syntax dword J1939UpdateTable ( char busName[] );
description: Description Updates the network table. The function deletes the content of the current table and initiates the RequestPGN that was used to request Address Claiming. After that, the table is restructured. With busName , you assign a table which is used in a network. A node which was displaced from the network if it has lost the Address Claiming, will be placed in the network table with the NULL address. After calling this function, all contents of the table will deleted and the table will update
parameters: Parameters busName bus name or "default"
returnValues: Return Values 0 on success or error code

name: DiagInitEcuSimulation
syntax: Function Syntax long DiagInitEcuSimulation(char ecuQualifier[])
description: Description Initialize CAPL node to represent a diagnostics ECU simulation. From now on the ECU can interpret and use all diagnostic objects in the CAPL code of this node as defined by the respective diagnostic description. CANoe will initialize the CAPL callback interface for diagnostics during the call. Note This function cannot be used together with DiagSetTarget . It cannot be used in a test module; it must be called from the on preStart handler.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Qualifier of the ECU as set in the diagnostic configuration dialog for the respective diagnostic description.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: DiagPreSend
syntax: Function Syntax _Diag_Presend(BYTE data[], WORD& dataLenInOut, WORD headerLen)
description: Description Is called before a frame is transmitted. Allows manipulating the frame.
parameters: Parameters data Transmitted data buffer of the frame. dataLenInOut Indicates the length of the frame when _Diag_PreSend is called. Determines the length of the frame before the frame is transmitted. headerLen Length of the header of a frame. Not supported yet
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DiagSendRequestPDU
syntax: Function Syntax long DiagSendRequestPDU(BYTE rawPDU[], WORD rawPDULength)
description: Description Sends a raw byte buffer. Allows also to send invalid requests.
parameters: Parameters rawPDULength Length of the raw buffer raw PDU Byte buffer.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: DiagSendResponsePDU
syntax: Function Syntax long DiagSendResponsePDU(BYTE rawPDU[], WORD rawPDULength)
description: Description Sends a raw byte buffer. E.g. this allows sending responses with invalid protocol headers.
parameters: Parameters rawPDU Byte buffer to be transmitted. rawPDULength Length of the byte buffer.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineByteReceptionInd
syntax: Function Syntax _KLine_ByteReceptionInd(BYTE byteIn, int64 endOfByteTimes_ns)
description: Description Is called when a byte has been received.
parameters: Parameters byteIn Received byte endOfByteTimes_ns End of byte time stamp in ns resolution.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: KLineByteTransmissionCon
syntax: Function Syntax _KLine_ByteTransmissionCon(BYTE byteIn, int64 endOfByteTimes_ns)
description: Description Is called when a byte has been transmitted.
parameters: Parameters byteIn Transmitted byte endOfByteTimes_ns End of byte time stamp in ns resolution.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: KLineCreateECURepresentation
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_CreateECURepresentation(dword channel)
description: Description Initialize K-Line ECU communication on given channel. Note Must be called in on preStart handler.
parameters: Parameters channel Number of the hardware channel.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineDataCon
syntax: Function Syntax _KLine_DataCon(long count)
description: Description Called for a transmitted K-Line response. Note This function models a K-Line TP layer which is required for a ECU simulation.
parameters: Parameters count Number of transmitted bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: KLineDataInd
syntax: Function Syntax _KLine_DataInd(byte data[])
description: Description Called for a received K-Line request. Note This function models a K-Line TP layer which is required for a ECU simulation.
parameters: Parameters data Data buffer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: KLineEnableSegmentedResponses
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_EnableSegmentedResponses(long enable)
description: Description Enables receiving segmented responses.
parameters: Parameters enable 1: enable receiving of segmented responses. 0: disable receiving of segmented responses.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineErrorInd
syntax: Function Syntax _KLine_ErrorInd(dword errType)
description: Description Called when a protocol error occurred.
parameters: Parameters dword errType Type of the received error.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: KLineFastInitPatternReceived
syntax: Function Syntax _KLine_FastInitPatternReceived(dword TiniL_us, dword TWup_us, int64 timestampTWup)
description: Description Called when a fast init pattern has been received. Called for the following values: TinL : 25 ms +- 4 ms TWup : 50 ms +- 4 ms
parameters: Parameters TiniL_us TiniL time in us. TWup_us TWup time in us. timestampTWup End of fast init pattern timestamp
returnValues: Return Values —

name: KLineFrameReceptionInd
syntax: Function Syntax _KLine_FrameReceptionInd(BYTE data[], int64 timestamps[])
description: Description Is called when a frame has been received.
parameters: Parameters data Received data buffer of the frame. timestamps End of byte time stamps of the received data bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: KLineFrameTransmissionCon
syntax: Function Syntax _KLine_FrameTransmisionCon(BYTE data[], int64 timestamps[])
description: Description Is called when a frame has been transmitted.
parameters: Parameters data Transmitted data buffer of the frame. timestamps End of byte time stamps of the transmitted data bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: KLineGetMeasuredInitParameter
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_GetMeasuredInitParameter(dword parameterID)
description: Description Retrieves the different parameters of the init patterns.
parameters: Parameters parameterID Value range: 0-10 0: KeyByte1 1: Keybyte2 2: TiniL 3: Twup 4: W4 between key byte 2 and inverted key byte 2 5: W1 6: W2 7: W3 8: W4 9: W4 between inverted keybyte 2 and inverted address byte 10: BaudRate from sync pattern
returnValues: Return Values Parameter value or error code .

name: KLineInit5BaudEcuParams
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_Init5BaudEcuParams(dword 5BaudAddress, dword rate_10thBd, dword W1_us, dword W2_us, dword W3_us, dword W4_us, dword W4Min_us, dword W4Max_us, dword addressNot);
description: Description Initialize K-Line channel for reception of the 5 baud pattern. Note Must be called in on prestart handler after KLine_CreateECURepresentation .
parameters: Parameters 5BaudAddress The 5 Baud address to accept on the active channel. rate_10thBd Rate of the address in 1/10 of baud, i.e. a value of 50 should be used here. W1_us W2_us W3_us W4_us W4Min_us W4Max_us Timing parameters in milliseconds used for the initialization pattern. A detailed description of the parameters is available on the Vector K-Line Protocol Reference Chart e.g. available at vector.com. addressNot The inverted address to be sent by the ECU simulation. A value of 0 will use the
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineResetECUConnection
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_ResetECUConnection ()
description: Description Resets the connection state of the simulated ECU. After calling this function an initialization pattern can be received again.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSendFrame
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SendFrame( byte data[], dword count)
description: Description Send data on the active K-Line communication channel. The K-Line header is generated automatically due to header settings. Note In a tester node, you can achieve this functionality by setting the diagnostics request to the byte sequence directly (with DiagResize and DiagSetPrimitiveData ).
parameters: Parameters data Data buffer dword count Length of data buffer
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSet5BaudAddressTester
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_Set5BaudAddressTester( Byte fiveBaudAddress)
description: Description Sets the 5 baud address for the 5 baud init pattern.
parameters: Parameters fiveBaudAdress Five baud address of the ECU.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetBaudrate
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetBaudrate(dword baudrate)
description: Description Allows changing the baudrate during the measurement.
parameters: Parameters baudrate Value range: 200 baud - 115200 baud.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetECUAddress
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetECUAddress(Byte ecuAddress)
description: Description Sets the address of the ECU.
parameters: Parameters ecuAddress Address of the ECU.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetFunctionalAddress
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetFunctionalAddress(Byte functionalAddress)
description: Description Sets the functional address of the ECU, i.e. it can receive broadcast messages directed at this address.
parameters: Parameters functionalAddress Broadcast address used by the tester to address a group of ECUs.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetHeaderFormat
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetHeaderFormat(long useAddresses, long forceLengthByte)
description: Description Configures the used header format.
parameters: Parameters useAddresses 0: Address bytes are not used. 1: Address bytes are used. forceLengthByte 0: No Length byte will be sent. 1: Length byte will always be sent. 2: Length byte will be sent for frames greater than 63 bytes.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetInitType
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetInitType(long initType)
description: Description Configures the used initialization pattern. Restriction : It is only possible to change the init type between the pre-configured init type an no init pattern. It is not possible to change between fast init and 5 baud init or vice versa.
parameters: Parameters initType Initialization pattern 1 = 5 baud init 2 = fast init 3 = no init pattern
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetP1ECU
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetP1ECU(dword P1_us)
description: Description Sets the interbyte time of a response.
parameters: Parameters P1_us P1 in us Value range: 0 – 650000.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetP2max
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetP2max(dword P2max_us)
description: Description Sets the maximum time between the client (tester) request and the server (ECU) response, or between 2 server (ECU) responses.
parameters: Parameters P2max_us P2max in us Value range: 0 – 100000000 (100 s).
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success < 0 Error code

name: KLineSetP3max
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetP3max(dword P3max_us)
description: Description Sets the maximum time between the end of the server (ECU) response and start of a new client (tester) request. If no request is sent in this interval, the ECU can assume that the tester is no longer present.
parameters: Parameters P3max_us P3max in us Value range: 0 – 10000000 (10 s).
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success < 0 Error code

name: KLineSetP3Tester
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetP3Tester(dword P3_us)
description: Description Sets the P3 time which the tester waits until transmitting a further request.
parameters: Parameters P3_us P3 time in us. Value range: 0 – 10000000
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetP4Tester
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetP4Tester(dword P4_us)
description: Description Sets the interbyte time of a request.
parameters: Parameters P4_us P4 in us Value range: 0 – 650000.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetTesterAddress
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetTesterAddress(Byte testerAddress)
description: Description Sets the address of the tester.
parameters: Parameters testerAddress Address of the tester.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetUARTParameter
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetUARTParameter(dword dataBits, dword parity, dword stopBits);
description: Description Configures the way bytes are sent on K-Line, by setting the parameters the UART (universal asynchronous receiver/transmitter) uses.
parameters: Parameters dataBits Number of bits a byte consists of. Possible values: 7, 8, 9. Note: 7 bits in a byte will not allow standard K-Line communication. parity If a parity bit is used to indicate the number of bits set in the byte 1 means _odd_ and 2 _even_ parity. A value of 0 indicates that no parity bit is used. stopBits The number of stop bits that indicate the end of a byte. 1 or 2 is possible.
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise error code .

name: KLineSetW4Tester
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetW4Tester( dword W4_us)
description: Description Sets the W4 timing of the tester between keybyte2 and keybyte2Not.
parameters: Parameters W4_us W4 time in us. Value range: 0 - 650000
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetW5Tester
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetW5Tester( dword W5_us)
description: Description Sets the W5 timing befor the tester starts to transmit the address byte.
parameters: Parameters W5_us W5 tim in us. Value range: 0 – 10 000 000
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSetWakeUpTimesTester
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SetWakeUpTimesTester(dword TiniL_us, dword Twup_us)
description: Description Sets the timings of the fast init wakeup pattern.
parameters: Parameters TiniL_us Duration of the low phase in us. Value range: 2000 - 650000 Twup_us Duration of the wakeup pattern. Value range: 2000 - 650000
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineSuppressAutomaticStopCommunication
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_SuppressAutomaticStopCommunication(long doSuppress)
description: Description For a fast init ECU, automatic sending of a Stop communication command will be suppressed after closing the channel or a S3 timeout.
parameters: Parameters doSuppress 1: Suppress sending of stop communication command 0: do not suppress sending of stop communication command
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: KLineUseDefaultHeader
syntax: Function Syntax long KLine_UseDefaultHeader()
description: Description The header format specified in the diagnostic description file will be used.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values On success 0, otherwise a value less than 0.

name: LINActivateCollisionResolution
syntax: Function Syntax long linActivateCollisionResolution(long etfId, long activate);
description: Description Activates or deactivates the Master node's automatic collision resolution of an event-triggered frame. Per default the automatic collision resolution is active. If a collision occurs for any event-triggered frame, the Master resolves this collision by sending headers for all associated frames using the event-triggered frame slot. After all associated frames have sent new data, the Master sends the event-triggered frame header until the next collision occurs. If the automatic collisio
parameters: Parameters etfId Identifier of event-triggered frame. Value range: 0-59 activate 0: Disable 1: Enable
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINActivateFlashMode
syntax: Function Syntax dword linActivateFlashMode(byte activate);
description: Description This function activates flash mode on high-speed capable transceivers. Note that in flash mode such a transceiver will use faster rising and falling edges and will disregard the EMC limitations of the LIN network. Note also, that the activation of the flash mode cannot be done while the channel is transmitting or the scheduler is running.
parameters: Parameters activate 1 will activate the flash mode, 0 will deactivated it.
returnValues: Return Values On successful request returns 1, otherwise 0.

name: LINActivateGlobalNetworkManagement
syntax: Function Syntax long linActivateGlobalNetworkManagement(long active);
description: Description Activates/deactivates network management for the entire LIN network (the channel is determined by the CAPL program context). Network management is responsible for the automatic setting and resetting of response error signals in the simulated Slave nodes.
parameters: Parameters active 0: deactivate 1: activate
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINActivateResps
syntax: Function Syntax long linActivateResps(); // form 1 long linActivateResps(char nodeName[]); // form 2
description: Description Reactivates all frame responses published by the calling Slave node according to the LIN database file (LDF), after having been previously deactivated by linDeactiveResps() or linResetSlave() . Per default all frame responses are activated for Slave nodes on measurement start i.e. it is assumed that Slave nodes have nonvolatile memory. LIN2.0 Slave nodes will automatically activate responses for re-configurable frames on receiving valid reconfiguration commands i.e. AssignFrameID. In
parameters: Parameters nodeName Slave node
returnValues: Return Values Number of activated frame responses or -1 if an error occurs.

name: LINActivateSlaveNetworkManagement
syntax: Function Syntax long linActivateSlaveNetworkManagement(long active);
description: Description Activates/deactivates network management for the calling Slave node. Network management is responsible for the automatic setting and resetting of response error signals in the simulated Slave nodes.
parameters: Parameters active 0: deactivate 1: activate
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINActivateSlot
syntax: Function Syntax long linActivateSlot(dword tableIndex, dword slotIndex);
description: Description Reactivates a schedule table slot defined in the LIN database file (LDF), after having been previously deactivated by linDeactivateSlot() . Schedule slots containing MasterRequests are automatically sent only if their data is updated i.e. using output(). Note If the Master node is not simulated or no schedule tables are defined, then this function will have no effect.
parameters: Parameters tableIndex Schedule table index according to the LIN database file (LDF). First table in the LDF has the index 0. slotIndex Slot index. First slot in a table has the index 0.
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINBits2TimeNS
syntax: Function Syntax int64 linBits2Time_ns(dword bitTimes); int64 linBits2Time_ns(dword channel, dword bitTimes);
description: Description Converts specified bit time to an absolute time. The absolute time is calculated using the current baudrate on the channel determined by the CAPL program context. Converts specified bit time to an absolute time. The absolute time is calculated using the current baudrate on the channel determined by the CAPL program context.
parameters: Parameters bitTimes Time in bits. channel Channel number, whose baudrate will be used.
returnValues: Return Values Absolute time in ns.

name: LINBusIsAwake
syntax: Function Syntax long linBusIsAwake();
description: Description Queries the awake state of the LIN bus.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Returns a value unequal to zero if the bus is awake, otherwise zero.

name: LINChangeSchedtable
syntax: Function Syntax long linChangeSchedTable(dword tableIndex); long linChangeSchedTable(dword tableIndex, dword slotIndex); long linChangeSchedTable(dword tableIndex, dword slotIndex, dword onSlotIndex);
description: Description This function switches from the current schedule table to another one. By calling this function in the event procedure on preStart , it is possible to specify in which schedule table the measurement should start. Note If this function is called multiple times before the new schedule table is started, only the most recent call will have an effect. To ensure that the new table has been started, wait until the on linSchedulerModeChange event procedure is called. If the Master node is no
parameters: Parameters tableIndex Index of the schedule table to be changed to. Value range: 0..N-1, where N is a total number of  defined schedule tables slotIndex Index of slot to be started within the new schedule table. Default value: 0 Value range: 0..Y-1, where Y is a total number of slots in the new schedule table onSlotIndex Index of last slot in the current schedule table to be sent before changing to the new schedule table. Default value: -2 - makes change immediately Value range: -2..X-1, wh
returnValues: Return Values Index of the current schedule table or -1 if no active schedule table exists and on failure.

name: LINCheckOEMDataInd
syntax: Function Syntax long linCheckOEMDataInd();
description: Description This function checks the data indication bits of all slave nodes defined in the LIN network (the channel is determined by the CAPL program context).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Function call not allowed or execution failed. 0 At least one slave has a negative indication. >0 All slaves reported positive indication.

name: LINCheckOEMSleepInd
syntax: Function Syntax long linCheckOEMSleepInd()
description: Description This function checks the sleep indication bits of all slave nodes defined in the LIN network (the channel is determined by the CAPL program context).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Function call not allowed or execution failed. 0 At least one slave has a negative indication. >0 All slaves reported positive indication.

name: LINCheckOEMWakeupInd
syntax: Function Syntax long linCheckOEMWakeupInd()
description: Description This function checks the wakeup indication bits of all slave nodes defined in the LIN network (the channel is determined by the CAPL program context).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Function call not allowed or execution failed. 0 At least one slave has a negative indication. >0 All slaves reported positive indication.

name: LINCheckRespError
syntax: Function Syntax long linCheckRespError();
description: Description Queries the response_error flags of all Slave nodes defined in the LIN network.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Function call not allowed or execution failed. 0 None of the slaves has the response_error flag set, or no response_error flag has been sent yet. >0 At least one of the slaves has the response_error flag set. (For J2602:2012 error states 4 to 7 will be considered as an error).

name: LINConfigureResponse
syntax: Function Syntax dword linConfigureResponse (dword frameId, dword dlc, byte[] responseData); // form 1 dword linConfigureResponse (linFrame frame); // form 2
description: Description Configures a response frame of a LIN node if no database is used. Note This function may only be called in the event procedure on preStart .
parameters: Parameters frameId ID of the LIN frame which will be configured as response. Value range: 0..63 dlc Number of data bytes contained in the frame (Data Length Code). Value range: 0..8 responseData An array of bytes which contains the response data to be set. frame The LIN frame which will be configured as response.
returnValues: Return Values Returns 1 if the configuration of the response succeeded, otherwise 0 .

name: LINConfigureResponseRange
syntax: Function Syntax dword linConfigureResponseRange (byte[] frameIds, byte[] dlcs, byte[][8] responseData)
description: Description Configures a range of frames as response of a LIN node if no database is used. Note This function may only be called in the event procedure on preStart . All of the array parameters must contain the same number of elements.
parameters: Parameters frameIds Array which contains the ids of every frame to be configured. Valid frame id range: 0..63 dlcs Array of dlcs (Data Length Codes) which defines the number of data bytes for every frame to be configured. Valid dlc range: 0..8 responseData An array which points to an array of 8 bytes which contains the initial response data to be set for every frame.
returnValues: Return Values Returns 1 if the configuration of all given frames succeeded, otherwise 0.

name: LINDeactivateBitInversion
syntax: Function Syntax long LINDeactivateBitInversion();
description: Description With this function it is possible to cancel a previously activated bits inversion for LIN header or response. This function is useful when after calling linInvertHeaderBit() no header occurred yet on the bus or when after calling linInvertRespBit() no frame occurred yet.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values On success a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINDeactivateResps
syntax: Function Syntax long linDeactivateResps(); // form 1 long linDeactivateResps(char nodeName[]); // form 2
description: Description This function deactivates frame responses for all frames published by the calling Slave node. The frame responses can be deactivated individually using the function linSetRespCounter() . With form 2 you can deactivate the Slave responses of the selected Slave node (nodeName ). If the parameter nodeName is not set or an empty string is given, the Slave responses of the node invoking this function will be deactivated. Note If the CAPL-program calling this function, does not model a Sla
parameters: Parameters nodeName Slave node
returnValues: Return Values Number of deactivated frame responses or -1 on failure.

name: LINDeactivateSlot
syntax: Function Syntax long linDeactivateSlot(dword tableIndex, dword slotIndex);
description: Description This function deactivates the specified slot of schedule table. For example, it can be used to turn slots off in a diagnostic table. By calling this function in the event procedure on preStart , it is possible to configure initial state of schedule table. Note If the Master node is not simulated or no schedule tables are defined, then this function will have no effect.
parameters: Parameters tableIndex Index of the schedule table to be configured. Value range: 0..N-1, where N is a total number of defined schedule tables slotIndex Index of slot to be deactivated within the specified schedule table. Value range: 0..Y-1, where Y is a total number of slots in the specified schedule table
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINDetectMultipleErrors
syntax: Function Syntax long linDetectMultipleErrors(long activate);
description: Description This function can be used to instruct LIN Hardware to enable/disable detection of more than one error per a LIN frame. This function can be useful during tests of the stress functionality. For example, an inversion of bits in a frame response may lead to a receive error and checksum error simultaneously. By default only one error per frame is detected, but with this function the detection of both errors can be activated.
parameters: Parameters activate 0: Disable 1: Enable
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINDisturbHeaderWithBitStream
syntax: Function Syntax dword linDisturbHeaderWithBitStream(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, byte bitStream[], dword numberOfBits, dword timeoutPrevention);
description: Description Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the next header with a bitstream.
parameters: Parameters byteIndex Start disturbance in byte with index <byteIndex>. 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte bitIndex The index of the bit where the interrupting bitstream will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 bitStream The bitstream to be used for the interrup
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINDisturbHeaderWithHeader
syntax: Function Syntax dword linDisturbHeaderWithHeader(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, long disturbingFrameId);
description: Description Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the next header with a new header (id=<disturbanceHeaderID>).
parameters: Parameters byteIndex Starts disturbance in byte with index <byteIndex>. 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte bitIndex Starts disturbance at bit position <bitIndex>. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 disturbanceHeaderID Frame ID to be used for the header disturbance. N
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINDisturbHeaderWithVariableBitStream
syntax: Function Syntax dword linDisturbHeaderWithVariableBitStream(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, byte dataBuffer[], int64 lengthInNS[], dword numberOfBits, dword roundUp, dword timeoutPrevention);
description: Description Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the next header with a variable bitstream.
parameters: Parameters byteIndex Start disturbance in byte with index <byteIndex>. 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte bitIndex The index of the bit where the interrupting bitstream will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit. Higher index values specify the interbyte-space after the indexed data byte. In which case, the user should make sure that the interbyte space is large enough. Value range: 0..255 bitStream The bitstream to be used for the interrup
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINDisturbRespWithBitStream
syntax: Function Syntax dword linDisturbRespWithBitStream(long disturbedFrameId, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, byte bitStream[], dword numberOfBits, dword timeoutPrevention);
description: Description Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the specified response with a bitstream.
parameters: Parameters disturbedFrameID ID of the bus event to be disturbed. Value range: 0..63 byteIndex The index of the byte to be disturbed (use 0 for the first byte). If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be disturbed. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length bitIndex The index of the bit where the interrupting bitstream will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINDisturbRespWithHeader
syntax: Function Syntax dword linDisturbRespWithHeader(long disturbedFrameId, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, long disturbingFrameId);
description: Description Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the specified response with a new header (id=<disturbanceHeaderID>).
parameters: Parameters disturbedFrameID ID of the bus event to be disturbed. Value range: 0..63 byteIndex The index of the byte to be disturbed (use 0 for the first byte). If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be disturbed. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length bitIndex The index of the bit where the interrupting header will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop bit
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINDisturbRespWithVariableBitStream
syntax: Function Syntax dword linDisturbRespWithVariableBitStream(long disturbedFrameId, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, byte bitStream[], int64 lengthInNS[], dword numberOfBits, dword roundUp, dword timeoutPrevention);
description: Description Configures the LIN hardware to disturb the specified response with a variable bitstream.
parameters: Parameters disturbedFrameID ID of the bus event to be disturbed. Value range: 0..63 byteIndex The index of the byte to be disturbed (use 0 for the first byte). If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be disturbed. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length bitIndex The index of the bit where the interrupting bitstream will start. An index in the range 0-7 specifies a data bit, while the index 8 specifies the stop
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINEnableStatistics
syntax: Function Syntax void linEnableStatistics(long channel, long flag); // form 1 void linEnableStatistics(long flag); // form 2
description: Description Enables or disables LIN statistics on a specific (form 1) or all (form 2) LIN channels. Note LIN bus statistics for CAPL is disabled by default to avoid performance issues while measurement is running. However, you can enable it by calling these functions, e.g. in an on start {} handler (see example below).
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1...64) flag 0 = disable; 1 = enable
returnValues: Return Values —

name: LINETFSendOnSignalUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax long linETFSendOnSignalUpdate(long etfId, long active);
description: Description With this function it is possible to activate/deactivate the automatic responses on a certain event-triggered frame request in the case of a signal update on its associated frame(s). This concerns all Slave tasks publishing the responses to the specified event-triggered frame.
parameters: Parameters etfId Identifier of event-triggered frame. Value range: 0-59 active 1: Activate 0: Deactivate
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINETFSetDirtyFlag
syntax: Function Syntax long linETFSetDirtyFlag(long assocId, long dirty);
description: Description With this function it is possible to set/clear the dirty flag of an associated frame. If the dirty flag of an associated frame is set when the corresponding event-triggered frame is being requested, then the LIN hardware will try to transmit the associated frame's data. The dirty flag gets reset automatically when the associated frame has been sent successfully – either via the event-triggered frame or unconditionally.
parameters: Parameters assocId Identifier of unconditional frame LIN frame identifier of associated (unconditional) frame whose dirty flag should be set or cleared. Value range: 0 .. 63 dirty 1: Set the dirty flag. 0: Clear the dirty flag.
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINGetBaudRateDevMaster
syntax: Function Syntax double linGetBaudRateDevMaster(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the measured baudrate deviation of the master node on a LIN channel in percent. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Baudrate deviation of the master node in percent, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetBaudRateMaster
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetBaudRateMaster(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the measured baudrate of the master node on a LIN channel in bits per second [bit/s]. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Baudrate of the master node in bits per second, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetBusErrorCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetBusErrorCount(long channel)
description: Description Gets the total count of bus errors of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of bus errors, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetBusIdleTimeout
syntax: Function Syntax dword linGetBusIdleTimeout();
description: Description This function returns the currently set bus idle timeout. Depending on the protocol version, the timeout will be specified in bit times (LIN 1.x and Cooling) or in milliseconds (all others).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values On success, returns the bus idle timeout, otherwise zero.

name: LINGetBusLoad
syntax: Function Syntax double linGetBusLoad(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the busload of a LIN channel in percent. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Busload in percent, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetChecksum
syntax: Function Syntax byte linGetChecksum(linFrame linFrame); form 1 byte linGetChecksum(linCSError linCsError); // form 2
description: Description Returns the checksum of a LIN frame or LIN checksum error. Checksum model (classic/enhanced) is determined automatically. Returns the checksum of a LIN frame or LIN checksum error. Checksum model (classic/enhanced) is determined automatically.
parameters: Parameters linFrame LIN frame object linCsError LIN checksum error object
returnValues: Return Values Calculated checksum

name: LINGetDetectedRespErrorCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetDetectedRespErrorCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of response errors detected by a response error bit on a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of response errors detected by response error bit, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetDiagNoRespCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetDiagNoRespCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of unanswered Slave responses following a Master request of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of unanswered Slave responses, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetDLC
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetDlc(long frameID);
description: Description Queries the Data Length Code (i.e. length in bytes) of a LIN frame. Per default the DLC of LIN frames is initialized according to the LIN Description File (LDF). If no LDF is used, this function returns the DLC initialized in on preStart for LIN frames sent by simulated LIN nodes. For LIN frames whose DLC has not been initialized, this function will return the default DLC for the selected ID as defined in the LIN1.1 protocol specification. For LIN frames sent by external LIN nodes, t
parameters: Parameters frameID LIN frame identifier in the range 0 .. 63.
returnValues: Return Values If successful the frame length [in bytes] or -1 on failure.

name: LINGetDominantTimeout
syntax: Function Syntax int64 linGetDominantTimeout();
description: Description Returns the dominant timeout of the current channel's transceiver in nanoseconds. If zero, the transceiver does not have any dominant timeout.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Returns the dominant timeout of the LINpiggy or zero, if the transceiver does not have any dominant timeout.

name: LINGetDurationHeader
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetDurationHeader(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the duration of the frame header of a LIN channel in microseconds. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Duration of the frame header in microseconds, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetDurationResp
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetDurationResp(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the duration of the frame response of a LIN channel in microseconds. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Duration of the frame response in microseconds, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetETFCollisionCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetETFCollisionCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of Event Triggered Frame (ETF) collisions of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of ETF collisions, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetETFHeaderCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetETFHeaderCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of Event Triggered Frame (ETF) headers of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of ETF headers, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetETFInvalidRespCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetETFInvalidRespCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of invalid Event Triggered Frame (ETF) responses of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of invalid ETF responses, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetETFResolutionCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetETFResolutionCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of Event Triggered Frame (ETF) collision resolutions of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of ETF collision resolutions, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetETFRespCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetETFRespCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of Event Triggered Frame (ETF) responses of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of ETF responses, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte
syntax: Function Syntax dword linGetFallingEdgesOfDisturbedByte(int64 fallingEdges[]);
description: Description With this function it is possible to retrieve time stamps of all falling edges in the disturbed byte or in the pseudo-byte caused by the last bit inversion. Note, that prior to calling this function the measurement of the falling edges has to be activated and the bit inversion has to be executed (see linInvertRespBit , linInvertHeaderBit )
parameters: Parameters fallingEdges An array which will receive the measured time stamps [in nanoseconds]. The array size is 6. Unused time stamps will be set to 0 (i.e. in case of less than 6 falling edges).
returnValues: Return Values On successful request returns 1, otherwise 0.

name: LINGetFrameCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetFrameCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of frames of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of frames, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetFrameCycleTime
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetFrameCycleTime(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the cycle time of valid unconditional frames of a LIN channel in milliseconds. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Cycle time in milliseconds, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetFrameRate
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetFrameRate(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the frame rate of a LIN channel in frames per second [fr/s]. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Frame rate in frames per second, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetHWReceiveAccuracy
syntax: Function Syntax dword linGetHWReceiveAccuracy();
description: Description This function can be used to query the receive resolution of the LIN hardware in units of 1 Hz.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Returns the receive resolution of the LIN hardware.

name: LINGetHWTransmitAccuracy
syntax: Function Syntax dword linGetHWTransmitAccuracy();
description: Description This function can be used to query the transmit resolution of the LIN hardware in units of 1 Hz.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Returns the transmit resolution of the LIN hardware.

name: LINGetInitTimeMaster
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetInitTimeMaster(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the time between the end of a wakeup request and the start of the following sync break with valid sync field (= init time of the master node) of a LIN channel in milliseconds. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Init time of the master node in milliseconds, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetMasterNode
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetMasterNode();
description: Description This function returns a unique internal ID for the current LIN master node of the channel given by the current bus context. See SetBusContext for how to change the current bus context. The return value may be used as input parameter to a call to linSetMasterNode(long nodeId) . The return value must not be used between measurements, as these will be newly generated each time the measurement is started.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Node ID of the current master node. If the return value is 0 (zero), the function failed, or there is currently no LIN master node assigned.

name: LINGetMasterResistorState
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetMasterResistorState();
description: Description Returns the internal master resistor's current state of the LIN interface hardware. Note This function only works with LIN core network interfaces.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values State of the master resistor 1: Resistor is on 0: Resistor is off

name: LINGetMeasBaudrate
syntax: Function Syntax float linGetMeasBaudrate();
description: Description With this function it is possible to retrieve results of a last baudrate measurement, which is done by calling linMeasHeaderBaudrate() or linMeasRespBaudrate() functions.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Returns the last measured baudrate [in bit/sec] or -1 on failure.

name: LINGetMeasByteBitRates
syntax: Function Syntax dword linGetMeasByteBitRates(dword arrSize, float byteBitRates[]);
description: Description This function calculates the bitrates for every byte which has been selected for edge time measurement. It uses the received data as well as the measured edge differences. Therefore, the function linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs must be called in advance. Note that for each measured byte a bitrate will be returned, although it might be 0 (this happens if there had been only one falling edge in the measured byte).
parameters: Parameters arrSize The size of the byteBitRates array. byteBitRates The array which will receive the measured byte bitrates. The unit of the measured bitrates is bits/second.
returnValues: Return Values Returns the number of bitrates copied into the byteBitRates-array.

name: LINGetMeasEdgeTimeDiffs
syntax: Function Syntax dword linGetMeasEdgeTimeDiffs(dword arrSize, float timeDiffs[]);
description: Description This function retrieves the result of a falling edge difference measurement which has been started with linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs . Note that for each byte measured four time differences will be returned, although all of them might be 0 (if there had been only one falling edge in the measured byte). This means that time differences 0 to 3 contain the values for the first measured byte, time differences 4 to 7 contain the values for the second measured byte, etc. The results are sorted in
parameters: Parameters arrSize The size of the timeDiffs array. timeDiffs The array which will receive the measured time differences. The unit of the measured times is nanoseconds. The first time difference of a byte always relates to the falling edge of the start bit.
returnValues: Return Values Returns the number of time differences copied into the timeDiffs-array.

name: LINGetOEMDataInd
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetOEMDataInd(); // form 1 long linGetOEMDataInd(char slaveName[]); // form 2
description: Description With this function it's possible to query the data indication bit of a Slave node. Without parameter, the data indication bit of the calling slave node is returned or zero if the caller is not a slave node.
parameters: Parameters slaveName Name of the queried Slave node as specified in the database.
returnValues: Return Values -1 Function call not allowed, execution failed or invalid slave name given. 0 Queried data indication bit is not set. >0 Queried data indication bit is set.

name: LINGetOEMSleepInd
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetOEMSleepInd(); // form 1 long linGetOEMSleepInd(char slaveName[]); // form 2
description: Description With this function it's possible to query the sleep indication bit of a Slave node. Without parameter, the sleep indication bit of the calling slave is returned or zero if the caller is not a slave.
parameters: Parameters slaveName Name of the queried Slave node as specified in the database.
returnValues: Return Values -1 Function call not allowed, execution failed or invalid slave name given. 0 Queried sleep indication bit is not set. >0 Queried sleep indication bit is set.

name: LINGetOEMWakeupInd
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetOEMWakeupInd(); // form 1 long linGetOEMWakeupInd(char slaveName[]); // form 2
description: Description With this function it's possible to query the wakeup indication bit of a Slave node. Without parameter, the wakeup indication bit of the calling slave is returned or zero if the caller is not a slave node.
parameters: Parameters slaveName Name of the queried Slave node as specified in the database.
returnValues: Return Values -1 Function call not allowed, execution failed or invalid slave name given. 0 Queried wakeup indication bit is not set. >0 Queried wakeup indication bit is set.

name: LINGetProtectedID
syntax: Function Syntax dword linGetProtectedID (long frameID);
description: Description With this function it is possible to calculate protected ID for the corresponding LIN frame identifier (i.e. the frame identifier with parity bits).
parameters: Parameters frameID LIN frame identifier whose protected ID will be calculated. Value range: 0 .. 63
returnValues: Return Values Returns the calculated protected identifier or a value greater than 255 on failure.

name: LINGetRespError
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetRespError(); // form 1 long linGetRespError(long NAD); // form 2
description: Description With this function it's possible to query the response error flag of the calling Slave node or of the Slave node specified by its node address (NAD) as last seen on the LIN bus.
parameters: Parameters NAD Configured node address of the Slave node to be queried.
returnValues: Return Values Returns one if the queried response_error flag is set, zero if it is not set. (Special case J2602:2012 – Here the error state ranging from 0 to 7 is returned). Returns -1 if the response error flag for the slave node has not been sent yet, or if the supplied NAD does not map to a single slave node.

name: LINGetRespErrorCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetRespErrorCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of response errors of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of response errors, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetResponseData
syntax: Function Syntax dword LinGetResponseData(linFrame frameObject);
description: Description Queries the LIN frame response data which is set to be transmitted next for the specified FrameId on certain LIN channel. This is only possible for frame IDs which are configured to be transmitted (via the LIN Interactive Scheduler, a simulated slave node, linConfigureResponse , etc.). The response data reflects the current state of the internal transmission buffer. There is not necessarily going to be a frame on the bus containing this response data, since the response data could be
parameters: Parameters frameObject The data structure to be filled in. Note, that the following selectors have to be set prior to calling this function: ID MsgChannel
returnValues: Return Values Non-zero on success. Zero on failure or when there is no response data defined. The non-zero value in real mode is the initial and in simulated mode the actual response counter value.

name: LINGetRespSpace
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetRespSpace(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the duration of the frame response space of a LIN channel in nanoseconds. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Duration of the frame response space in nanoseconds, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetSleepCommandCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetSleepCommandCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of go-to-sleep requests of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of go-to-sleep requests, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetToleranceHeader
syntax: Function Syntax double linGetToleranceHeader(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the header tolerance of a LIN channel in percent. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Header tolerance in percent, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetToleranceResp
syntax: Function Syntax double linGetToleranceResp(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the response tolerance of a LIN channel in percent. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Response tolerance in percent, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetWakeupCount
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetWakeupCount(long channel);
description: Description Gets the total count of wakeup requests of a LIN channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64).
returnValues: Return Values Total count of wakeup requests, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGetWakeupDuration
syntax: Function Syntax long linGetWakeupDuration(long channel, ValueSelector selector);
description: Description Gets the duration of wakeup requests of a LIN channel in microseconds. The kind of value to be returned (average, current, maximum or minimum) can be selected.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1…64). selector Value selector: eCurrValue = current eMinValue = minimum eMaxValue = maximum eAvgValue = average
returnValues: Return Values Duration of wakeup requests in microseconds, if LIN statistics is enabled; 0 otherwise.

name: LINGotoSleep
syntax: Function Syntax long linGotoSleep();
description: Description Leads to a transmission of a go-to-sleep-command.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINGotoSleepInternal
syntax: Function Syntax long linGotoSleepInternal();
description: Description Sets the LIN hardware to Sleep mode without sending a go-to-sleep-command on the network. Calling this function in the event procedure on prestart leads to measurement start in sleep mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINInvertHeaderBit
syntax: Function Syntax dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex); // form 1 dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level); // form 2 dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions); // form 3 dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges); // form 4 dword linInvertHeaderBit(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level, dword numberOfExecut
description: Description Inverts the specified bit in the next LIN header.
parameters: Parameters byteIndex Index of the byte. -1: Sync Break 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte bitIndex The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index values will cause the interbyte-space after the data byte to be manipulated. In this case, the user should make sure that the interbyte-space is large enough. Since the bit index is based on a UART-byte, index 0 will not disturb the very first bit
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINInvertHeaderBitEx
syntax: Function Syntax dword linInvertHeaderBitEx(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword level); // form 1 dword linInvertHeaderBitEx(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges); // form 2 dword linInvertHeaderBitEx(dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword lev
description: Description Inverts the specified number of 1/16 th bits at the specified position in the next LIN header. Note Partial bit disturbances require the activation of the flash mode in order to get the edges of the disturbance fast enough. Call linActivateFlashMode for that purpose.
parameters: Parameters byteIndex Index of the byte. -1: Sync Break 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte bitIndex The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index values will cause the interbyte-space after the data byte to be manipulated. In this case, the user should make sure that the interbyte-space is large enough. Since the bit index is based on a UART-byte, index 0 will not disturb the very first bit
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINInvertMultipleHeaderBits
syntax: Function Syntax dword linInvertMultipleHeaderBits(dword byteIndices[],dword bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[],dword levels[],dword arrSize); // form 1 dword linInvertMultipleHeaderBits(dword byteIndices[],dword bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[],dword levels[],dword arrSize, dword numberOfExecutions); // form 2 dword linInvertMultipleHeaderBits(dword byteIndices[],dword bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[],dword levels[],dword arrSize, dword numberOfExecutions, long distur
description: Description Inverts the multiple bits in specified locations in the next LIN header. Note Some driver versions only support inverting multiple (consecutive) bits in one position (arrSize == 1).
parameters: Parameters byteIndices An array of the index of the bytes. -1: Sync Break 0: Sync Byte 1: ProtectedId Byte bitIndices The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index values will cause the interbyte-space after the data byte to be manipulated. In this case, the user should make sure that the interbyte-space is large enough. Since the bit index is based on a UART-byte, index 0 will not distur
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINInvertMultipleRespBits
syntax: Function Syntax dword linInvertMultipleRespBits (long frameID, dword byteIndices[],dword bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[], dword levels[], dword arrSize); // form 1 dword linInvertMultipleRespBits (long frameID, dword byteIndices[],dword bitIndices[],dword numberOfDisturbedBits[], dword levels[], dword arrSize, dword numberOfExecutions); // form 2
description: Description Inverts the multiple bits in specified locations in the next LIN header. Note Some driver versions only support inverting multiple (consecutive) bits in one position (arrSize == 1). Please note hardware-related restrictions .
parameters: Parameters frameID ID of the bus event to be manipulated. Value range: 0..63 byteIndices An array of the indices of the bytes to be manipulated (use 0 for the first byte). If an index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be manipulated. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length bitIndices An array of the indices of the bits to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will mani
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINInvertRespBit
syntax: Function Syntax dword linInvertRespBit (long frameID, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex); // form 1 dword linInvertRespBit (long frameID, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level); // form 2 word linInvertRespBit (long frameID, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions); // form 3 dword linInvertRespBit (long frameID, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges); // form 4
description: Description This function inverts the specified bit when the next bus event for the specified ID occurs, provided that the bus event contains the specified data byte.
parameters: Parameters frameID ID of the bus event to be manipulated. Value range: 0..63 byteIndex The index of the byte to be manipulated (use 0 for the first byte). If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be manipulated. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value range: 0..N, where N is frame length bitIndex The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher index
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINInvertRespBitEx
syntax: Function Syntax dword linInvertRespBitEx(long frameID, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword level); // form 1 dword linInvertRespBitEx(long frameID, dword byteIndex, dword bitIndex, dword bitOffsetInSixteenthBits, dword distLengthInSixteenthBits, dword level, dword numberOfExecutions, dword reportFallingEdges); // form 2
description: Description Inverts the specified number of 1/16 th bits at the specified position in the next LIN frame's response with the specified frame ID. Note Partial bit disturbances require the activation of the flash mode in order to get the edges of the disturbance fast enough. Call linActivateFlashMode for that purpose.
parameters: Parameters frameID ID of the bus event to be manipulated. Value range: 0..63 byteIndex The index of the byte to be manipulated (use 0 for the first byte). If the index is equal to the frame's length, then the checksum byte will be manipulated. An index larger than the frame length is invalid. Value Range: 0..N: where N is the frame length bitIndex The index of the bit to be manipulated. An index in the range 0-7 will manipulate a data bit, while the index 8 will manipulate the stop bit. Higher i
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINisBitStreamBusy
syntax: Function Syntax dword linIsBitStreamBusy (); // form 1 dword linIsBitStreamBusy (dword channel); // form 2
description: Description Queries the current LIN bitstream sending status.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel number which will be queried.
returnValues: Return Values Returns 1 if a bitstream is currently being sent, otherwise 0 .

name: LINIsChannelMasterMode
syntax: Function Syntax long linIsChannelMasterMode();
description: Description This function returns 1 if the network channel given by the current bus context is running in master mode, 0 otherwise. See SetBusContext for how to change the current bus context. The channel's master mode setting can be changed at any time using the function linSetChannelMasterMode . See also Master Mode of the LIN Hardware for details on the LIN channel master mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 if the current bus context's LIN channel is in master mode, 0 otherwise

name: LINIsFlashModeActive
syntax: Function Syntax dword linIsFlashModeActive();
description: Description This function reports the flash mode state on high-speed capable transceivers.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Returns 1 if flash mode is active, otherwise 0.

name: LINIsMasterNode
syntax: Function Syntax long linIsMasterNode();
description: Description This function returns 1 if the calling node is the current LIN master node of the network given by the current bus context. This will be the node assigned to the LIN master from the LDF if linSetMasterNode has not been called before, or the node referred to by the previous call of linSetMasterNode . See SetBusContext for how to change the current bus context.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 if the calling node is the current LIN master node, 0 otherwise.

name: LINMeasEdgeTimeDiffs
syntax: Function Syntax dword linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs(dword numIndices, long indices[]); // form 1 dword linMeasEdgeTimeDiffs(dword numIndices, long indices[], dword id); // form 2
description: Description This function activates the measurement of the falling edges of the specified bytes in the next frame with the correct id if specified. If the first form is used (without specifying an id), or by specifying the id 0xff/-1 , the measurement takes place in the next frame. The measured values can be queried with linGetMeasEdgeTimeDiffs . The bitrates of the measured bytes can be queried via linGetMeasByteBitRates .
parameters: Parameters numIndices The number of specified byte indices. A pending measurement may be canceled by specifying numIndices=0 , before the frame to be measured occurs on the bus. A pending, uncompleted measurement must be canceled, before a new measurement can be activated. indices An array containing the indices of the bytes to be measured. Note that the minimum size for this array is numIndices . The array is sorted ascending internally. Therefore, the results of the measurement are sorted as w
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINMeasHeaderBaudrate
syntax: Function Syntax long linMeasHeaderBaudrate(int index);
description: Description With this function it is possible to set request to measure the baudrate value for next LIN header event. Channel, where the baudrate is measured, is determined by the CAPL program context. This function only initiates the baudrate measurement. The result becomes available when the next LIN header occurs and it remains valid until next measurement request. To retrieve the result the function linGetMeasBaudrate() shall be used. Note When LIN hardware is in Master mode calling this fun
parameters: Parameters index This parameter specifies a field of LIN header which will be used for baudrate measurement. 0: Synch field 1: Identifier field
returnValues: Return Values On successful request returns zero, otherwise -1.

name: LINMeasRespBaudrate
syntax: Function Syntax long linMeasRespBaudrate(long frameID, int index);
description: Description With this function it is possible to send a request to measure the baudrate value of a certain data byte of a certain LIN frame. The channel where the baudrate is measured is determined by the CAPL program context. This function only initiates the baudrate measurement. The result becomes available when the next bus event for the specified ID occurs and it remains valid until the next measurement request. To retrieve the result the function linGetMeasBaudrate() shall be used. Note The
parameters: Parameters frameID LIN frame identifier whose baudrate will be measured. Value range: 0…63 index Data byte index. Value range: 0..N, where N = length [in bytes] of specified LIN frame [0..N-1]: data bytes 1..N N: checksum byte
returnValues: Return Values On successful request returns zero, otherwise -1.

name: LINOutput
syntax: Function Syntax void output(linFrame msg);
description: Description This function can be used for two purposes: To apply frame header on the bus, i.e. to transmit the frame. In that case RTR selector must be set to 1. Note When LIN hardware is not in Master mode calling this function will have no effect.
parameters: Parameters Msgext Variable of type linFrame . List of available selectors for this type of objects can be found under linFrame selectors .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: LINRegisterOnFrameResponseDataChangedCallback
syntax: Function Syntax dword linRegisterOnFrameResponseDataChangedCallback (char[] callbackName);
description: Description Registers a callback function which is invoked whenever the response data of a LIN frame has been changed. The invocation of the callback happens right before the data is passed to the LIN hardware or the LIN bus simulator. The data of the linFrame parameter can be modified by this function. This can be used to ensure consistency of the frame's data before it is sent. Note This function may only be called in the event procedure on preStart .
parameters: Parameters callbackName The name of the callback function which should be registered. The callback must have the following signature: void func (linFrame* frame)
returnValues: Return Values Returns 1 if the registration succeeded, otherwise 0 .

name: LINRegisterSchedulerStartStopCallback
syntax: Function Syntax dword linRegisterSchedulerStartStopCallback (char[] callbackName);
description: Description Registers a callback function which is invoked if a LIN scheduler is either started or stopped. Note This function may only be called in the event procedure on preStart .
parameters: Parameters callbackName The name of the callback function which should be registered. The callback must have the following signature: void func (dword channel, dword started, int64 eventTime)
returnValues: Return Values Returns 1 if the registration succeeded, otherwise 0 .

name: LINResetManualChecksum
syntax: Function Syntax void linResetManualChecksum(linFrame linFrame);
description: Description Sets the correct checksum of a LIN frame, whose checksum has been changed by using linSetManualChecksum() function. The checksum is calculated using the frame data.
parameters: Parameters linFrame LIN frame for which the correct checksum will be used again.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: LINResetMasterNode
syntax: Function Syntax long linResetMasterNode();
description: Description Calling this function reverses the effect of linSetMasterNode . Sending LIN headers (by calling linTransmitHeader or similar functions) is no longer restricted to the currently assigned LIN master node. If a node is assigned as LDF master node, the LIN scheduler will be reset to its state before linSetMasterNode was called.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Node ID of the previous master node. If the return value is 0 (zero), the function failed, or there was no previous LIN master node.

name: LINResetMaxHeaderLength
syntax: Function Syntax dword linResetMaxHeaderLength();
description: Description Resets the maximum header length to the protocol version dependent default.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINResetNad
syntax: Function Syntax long linResetNAD();
description: Description This function resets NAD (Node Address for Diagnostic) of the Slave node determined by the CAPL program context to its initial NAD. See Option .LIN: Notes to the way initial NAD is determined to understand how initial NAD is determined in CANoe. Note If the CAPL program calling this function, does not model a LIN 2.x Slave node, then this function will have no effect.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values On success this function returns a value unequal to -1, otherwise -1.

name: LINResetRespBaudrate
syntax: Function Syntax long linResetRespBaudrate(long frameId);
description: Description Resets a response baudrate for a specified frame to the master baudrate.
parameters: Parameters frameId The identifier of the frame for which the response baudrate will be reset.
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINResetRespBitStream
syntax: Function Syntax long linResetRespBitStream (long frameId);
description: Description Deactivates the bitstream response of the specified frame.
parameters: Parameters frameId LIN frame identifier in the range 0 .. 63
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINResetRespDisturbance
syntax: Function Syntax long linResetRespDisturbance (long frameId);
description: Description Deactivates the disturbance in the response space of the specified frame.
parameters: Parameters frameId LIN frame identifier in the range 0 .. 63
returnValues: Return Values On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

name: LINResetSlave
syntax: Function Syntax long linResetSlave (); // form 1 long linResetSlave(dword resetToLastConfiguration); // form 2
description: Description This function resets the NAD (Node Address for Diagnostic) of the modeled Slave node and marks all its protected identifiers as invalid or it will load the last saved configuration if resetToLastConfiguration is set. See Notes to the way initial NAD is determined to understand how initial NAD is determined in CANoe. Note If the CAPL-program calling this function, does not model a LIN 2.x Slave node, then this function will have no effect.
parameters: Parameters resetToLastConfiguration 0: perform a complete reset non-zero: load last saved configuration
returnValues: Return Values On success this function returns a value unequal to -1, otherwise -1.

name: LINResetStatistics
syntax: Function Syntax void linResetStatistics(); // form 1 void linResetStatistics(long channel); // form 2
description: Description Resets LIN channel statistics. Note There are two ways to call this function: Calling this function without the explicit channel is only possible from a CAPL program defined in the Simulation/Test Setup . The statistics for the channel determined by the current bus context are reset. The function with the explicit channel can be called from a CAPL program defined in Measurement Setup only.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel (1-based)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: LINRunSchedTableNtimes
syntax: Function Syntax long linRunSchedTableNtimes (dword tableIndex, dword numberOfRepetitions, dword onSlotIndex, dword returnToTableIndex);
description: Description Switches from the current schedule table to another one, runs the table n times and returns to the initial table or to a different table. Note This function only works with LIN core network interfaces.
parameters: Parameters tableIndex Index of the schedule table to be changed to. Value range: 0..n-1, where n is a total number of defined schedule tables numberOfRepetitons Number of repetitions of the schedule table. onSlotIndex Index of last slot in the current schedule table to be sent before changing to the new schedule table. Default value: -1, make change on reaching the end of current schedule table. Value range: -1..x-1, where x is a total number of slots in the current schedule table. returnToTable
returnValues: Return Values Index of the current schedule table or -1 if no active schedule table exists and on failure.

name: LINSendAsSporadic
syntax: Function Syntax long linSendAsSporadic (long frameID);
description: Description This function can be used to configure an associated frame as being ready for transmission. At the next time slot, corresponding to its sporadic frame, the associated frame will be sent once.
