The entries of the array will be filled with the following information: Index Information 0 Milliseconds (0 - 999) 1 Seconds (0 - 59) 2 Minutes (0 - 59) 3 Hours (0 - 23) 4 Day of month (1 - 31) 5 Month (0 - 11) 6 Year (0 - xxx, offset of 1900, e.g. 117 = 2017) 7 Day of week (0 - 6, Sunday is 0)
returnValues: Return Values 1 if successful, 0 if not (e.g. array to small).

name: arccos
syntax: Function Syntax double arccos(double x);
description: Description Calculates arccosine of x.
parameters: Parameters x Value between –1 and 1 whose arccosine is to be calculated. Values outside this range cause a CAPL runtime error .
returnValues: Return Values Arcus Cosine of x.

name: arcsin
syntax: Function Syntax double arcsin(double x);
description: Description Calculates arcsine of x.
parameters: Parameters x Value between –1 and 1 whose arcsine is to be calculated. Values outside this range cause a CAPL runtime error .
returnValues: Return Values Arcus Sine of x.

name: arctan
syntax: Function Syntax double arctan(double x);
description: Description Calculates arctangent of x.
parameters: Parameters x Value whose arctangent is to be calculated.
returnValues: Return Values Arcus Tangent of x.

name: Atodbl
syntax: Function Syntax double atodbl(char s[]);
description: Description The function converts the string s into a double number. Normally, the base is decimal and must have the following format: [Blank space] [Sign] [Digits] [.Digits] [ {d | D | e | E}[Sign]Digits] String parsing ceases at the first non-compliant character. If the string cannot be converted into a number, 0.0 is returned. If the string starts with 0x, the base used is 16. Only integer numbers can be read in.
parameters: Parameters Input string to be converted.
returnValues: Return Values Double number, the converted string.

name: atoi64
syntax: Function Syntax int64 _atoi64(char s[]);
description: Description Converts the string s to a 64bit integer. The number base is decimal.
parameters: Parameters s String to be converted.
returnValues: Return Values The converted number. The return value is 0 if the string can't be converted to a number. It is the largest positive/negative number in case of overflow.

name: Atol
syntax: Function Syntax long atol(char s[]);
description: Description Converts the string s to a LONG number. The number base is decimal. Starting with string 0x, base 16 is used. Leading blanks are not read.
parameters: Parameters s String to be converted.
returnValues: Return Values The converted number. 0 will be returned if the string is no number.

name: BytesToString
syntax: Function Syntax long BytesToString(byte input[], char format[], char sep[], char output[]); // form 1 long BytesToString(byte input[], char format[], char sep[], char output[], dword outputStartIndex); // form 2
description: Description Converts a byte array to a formatted string. For each byte in the input, the format is applied and the result is written to the output. Between the written bytes, the separator string is inserted. It is not inserted before the first byte or after the last byte.
parameters: Parameters input The byte array to be written to the output. format The format in which to write the bytes to the output. It can be an arbitrary printf format string so long as only a single parameter is written (i.e. only a single % is present in the string). sep A separator string that is written in between. A separator string that is written in between the formatted bytes. output The string in which the formatted input is written. If it is not sufficiently long to hold the output, the string
returnValues: Return Values Returns a number that indicates the success of the function or the length of the resulting string. >=0 Indicates a successful function execution and is the number of characters written into the output (without the trailing null byte). -1 The output was not large enough to hold the result. -2 The format string was malformed.

name: CancelTimer
syntax: Function Syntax void cancelTimer(msTimer t); // from 1 void cancelTimer(timer t); // from 2
description: Description Stops an active timer.
parameters: Parameters Timer or msTimer variable.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CanOffline
syntax: Function Syntax void canOffline(); // form 1: deprecated dword canOffline(dword flags); // form 2
description: Description Limits the connection between the node and the bus. Messages sent from the node are not passed through to the bus. The function canOnline() will restore the connection. If the node is set to offline, output instructions for sending messages in CAPL or modeling library are ignored (refers to a node locally only). Regardless of the status, all messages are received in the CAPL program/NodeLayer. Form 1 only has an effect on the CAPL-program. In form 2 you can choose between the CAPL-pr
parameters: Parameters Flags Indicates the deactivated part of the node. 1 Cuts the connection of the CAPL-program to the bus 2 Cuts the connection of the Nodelayer to the bus 3 Cuts the connection of the CAPL-program and the Nodelayer to the bus and separates the diagnostic ECU simulation from the network, if one is configured for the node. Diagnostic requests via diagSendRequest are blocked.
returnValues: Return Values Form 2 returns the part of the node being online before the function call. Equal to the flags.

name: CanOnline
syntax: Function Syntax void canOnline(); // form 1: deprecated dword canOnline(dword flags); // form 2
description: Description Restores the connection of the node to the bus. After a call to the function canOffline() the node can be connected to the bus with the function canOnline() . Messages send from the node are passed through to the bus. If the node is set to offline, output instructions for sending messages in CAPL or modeling library are ignored (refers to a node locally only). Regardless of the status, all messages are received in the CAPL program/NodeLayer. Form 1 only has an effect on the CAPL-prog
parameters: Parameters Flags Indicates the activated part of the node. 1 Restores the connection of the CAPL-program to the bus. 2 Restores the connection of the Nodelayer to the bus. 3 Restores the connection of the CAPL-program and the Nodelayer to the bus and attaches the diagnostic ECU simulation to the network, if one is configured for the node.
returnValues: Return Values Form 2 returns the part of the node being online before the function call. Equal to the flags.

name: Ceil
syntax: Function Syntax float _ceil(float x);
description: Description Calculates the ceiling of a value, i.e. the smallest integer larger or equal to the value.
parameters: Parameters x Value of which the ceiling shall be calculated.
returnValues: Return Values Ceiling of x.

name: ClockControlReset
syntax: Function Syntax void ClockControlReset(char[] panel, char[] control);
description: Description Resets the Clock Control designed as stop watch with the Panel Designer (setting Mode = StopWatch ). The displayed time is reset to 00:00:00 or 00:00 depending on the Panel Designer setting Display Seconds . Note The stop watch cannot be reset when it is already running.
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ClockControlStart
syntax: Function Syntax void ClockControlStart(char[] panel, char[] control);
description: Description Starts the Clock Control designed as stop watch in the Panel Designer (setting Mode = StopWatch ). The stop watch starts with 00:00:00 or 00:00 depending on the Panel Designer setting Display Seconds . The start time cannot be changed. The stop watch is updated every second. Note The stop watch cannot be started when it is already running.
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ClockControlStop
syntax: Function Syntax void ClockControlStop(char[] panel, char[] control);
description: Description Stops the Clock Control designed as stop watch with the Panel Designer (setting Mode = StopWatch ). The displayed time stays unchanged unless the user starts the stop watch again or resets it. If the stop watch is started again without resetting it, the start time is the current displayed time (not zero).
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CloseLogFile
syntax: Function Syntax void closeLogFile(char loggingBlockName[]);
description: Description Explicitly closes the logging file of a given Logging Block. If the Logging Block is running, it will be stopped. Note If the Logging Block is started again without changing the name of the logging file, the old file will be overwritten.
parameters: Parameters loggingBlockName Name of the Logging Block. Note If you use the function in standalone mode and if you activated Logging and Trigger Block in the standalone mode configuration settings you can address the (single) Logging Block available in this case by passing an empty string as parameter.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ClosePanel
syntax: Function Syntax void closePanel(char panelName[]);
description: Description Closes a panel.
parameters: Parameters panelName Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CompleteStop
syntax: Function Syntax void CompleteStop ();
description: Description Indicates completion of pre-stop actions carried out in a certain node after a measurement stop has been deferred by DeferStop . Note If several CAPL nodes or internal components defer a measurement stop, measurement continues until they have all indicated completion of their pre-stop actions (either by explicitly calling CompleteStop or implicitly when the timeout whose interval has been defined in the DeferStop call has passed). CompleteStop may not be called before a stop request
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ConvertGPSTimestamp
syntax: Function Syntax void convertGPSTimestamp(dword timestamp, dword& year, byte& month, byte & day, byte & hour, byte & minute, byte & second);
description: Description Converts a GPS time stamp into UTC based date and time information.
parameters: Parameters timestamp The GPS time stamp (seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00). year Receives the year of the time stamp. month Receives the month of the time stamp (between 1 and 12). day Receives the day of the time stamp (between 1 and 31). hour Receives the hour of the time stamp (between 0 and 23). minute Receives the minute of the time stamp (between 0 and 59). second Receives the second of the time stamp (between 0 and 59).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ConvertString
syntax: Function Syntax long ConvertString(byte output[], long& encodedSize, long maxOutputSize, dword outputCodepage, byte input[], long inputSize, dword inputCodepage);
description: Description Converts a string from one encoding to another encoding. The length of the converted string depends on the requested encoding. The number of bytes used in the output byte array is written to encodedSize . If the size of the output array maxOutputSize is not sufficient to hold the converted string and a terminating "\0", the function returns an error and the content of output is undefined. Characters that cannot be represented in the requested encoding, are replaced with the best matc
parameters: Parameters output Target byte array. encodedSize ConvertString writes the number of bytes used in output (including the terminating "\0") to this parameter. maxOutputSize Size of the output array. outputCodepage Windows code page number for output . The include file Encoding.cin defines the following code pages: CP_UTF8 CP_UTF16 CP_UTF16_BE CP_LATIN1 CP_SHIFT_JIS input The input string in encoding codepage , without BOM. inputSize Length of the input string in bytes. inputCodepage Windows code p
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success, the byte array output and the resulting length encodedSize are valid. -1 Illegal character (e.g. illegal UTF8 code point). -2 Insufficient buffer space, output array is too small. -3 Internal error.

name: ConvertTimestamp
syntax: Function Syntax void convertTimestamp(dword timestamp, dword& days, byte& hours, byte& minutes, byte& seconds, word& milliSeconds, word& microSeconds); // form 1 void convertTimestampNS(qword timestamp, dword& days, byte& hours, byte& minutes, byte& seconds, word& milliSeconds, word& microSeconds, word& nanoSeconds); // form 2
description: Description Converts a time stamp to separate parts (form 1: maximum time: .2^32 * 10 microseconds = 11 hours, 55 minutes, 49 seconds, 672 milliseconds, 96 microseconds).
parameters: Parameters timestamp time stamp in 10 microseconds (form 1) time stamp in nanoseconds (form 2) days Receives the days of the time stamp hours Receives the hours the time stamp (between 0 and 23) minutes Receives the minutes of the time stamp (between 0 and 59) seconds Receives the seconds of the time stamp (between 0 and 59) milliseconds Receives the milliseconds of the time stamp (between 0 and 999) microseconds Receives the microseconds of the time stamp (between 0 and 999) nanoseconds (only f
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ConvertTimestampToNS
syntax: Function Syntax Int64 convertTimestampToNS ( dword days, dword hours, dword minutes, dword seconds );
description: Description Converts a time stamp given in days, hours, minutes and seconds into a nanosecond time stamp.
parameters: Parameters days Number of days of the time stamp. hours Number of hours of the time stamp. minutes Number of minutes of the time stamp. seconds Number of seconds of the time stamp.
returnValues: Return Values Time stamp in nanoseconds.

name: ConvertUTCDateToUnixTimestamp
syntax: Function Syntax Int64 convertUTCDateToUnixTimestamp(dword year, byte month, byte day, byte hour, byte minute, byte second);
description: Description Converts the given UTC time and date into a UNIX timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01).
parameters: Parameters year The year. month The month (between 1 and 12). day The day (between 1 and 31). hour The hour (between 0 and 23). minute The minute (between 0 and 59). second The second (between 0 and 60, since leap seconds are supported).
returnValues: Return Values UNIX timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01) corresponding to the input parameters. -1 if input parameters are invalid

name: Cos
syntax: Function Syntax double cos(double x);
description: Description Calculates cosine of x.
parameters: Parameters Value in radians whose cosine is to be calculated.
returnValues: Return Values Cosine of x.

name: CreateGlobalMarker
syntax: Function Syntax long createGlobalMarker (char markerName[], char makerDesc []);
description: Description Sets a marker in the CANoe Trace Window, Graphics Window and State Tracker. The set marker can be displayed with the shortcut menu Show in the marker bar of these windows.
parameters: Parameters markerName Name of the marker. makerDesc Description of the marker.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DataWindowStartStopLogging
syntax: Function Syntax void dataWindowStartLogging(char[] windowName); void dataWindowStopLogging(char[] windowName);
description: Description Starts or stops logging in a specified Data Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName Name of the Data Window.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DecodeString
syntax: Function Syntax long DecodeString(char output[], long outputSize, byte input[], long inputSize, dword codepage); // form 1 long DecodeString(string output, bytes input, dword codepage); // form 2
description: Description Form 1 : Decodes the byte array input from the encoding codepage to the current CAPL string encoding. Form 2 : Decodes the vCDL "bytes" value input from the encoding codepage to the encoding of the vCDL "string" value output . The length of the decoded string depends on the given encoding. If the size of the output array outputSize is not sufficient to hold the decoded string and a terminating "\0", the functions returns an error and the content of output is undefined. Characters tha
parameters: Parameters output Form 1: Target char array. Form 2: Target vCDL "string" value. outputSize Size of the output array. input Form 1: The input string in encoding codepage , without BOM. Form 2: The vCDL "bytes" values in encoding codepage, without BOM. inputSize Size of the input array. codepage Windows code page number for input . The include file Encoding.cin defines the following code pages: CP_UTF8 CP_UTF16 CP_UTF16_BE CP_LATIN1 CP_SHIFT_JIS
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success, the byte array output is valid. -1 Illegal character (e.g. illegal UTF8 code point). -2 Insufficient buffer space, output array is too small. -3 Internal error.

name: DeferStop
syntax: Function Syntax void DeferStop(dword maxDeferTime);
description: Description Defers measurement stop. The function can be called in the on preStop handler or even at an earlier time instance. Measurement is deferred until CompleteStop is called in the same node or the simulation time has advanced by the amount given in parameter maxDeferTime since the arrival of a stop request (and call of the on preStop handler). DeferStop enables waiting for completion of activities that have to be carried out before measurement stop takes effect, e.g. a reset of an attache
parameters: Parameters maxDeferTime Indicates the time interval in milliseconds after which completion of pre-stop activities is indicated automatically if it has not yet been done explicitly via CompleteStop .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: DeleteControlContent
syntax: Function Syntax void DeleteControlContent(char[] panel, char[] control);
description: Description Deletes the content of the Panel Designer CAPL Output View .
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. "": References all CAPL Output View controls on the panel.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: ElCount
syntax: Function Syntax long elcount(...); // if used with arrays which are function parameters dword elcount(...); // in all other cases
description: Description Determines the number of elements of an array. Note The function elcount always returns 1 when called with an associative field; it is only meant for non-associative fields (arrays).
parameters: Parameters Array of any arbitrary type.
returnValues: Return Values Number of elements.

name: EnableControl
syntax: Function Syntax void enableControl(char panel[], char control[], long enable);
description: Description Selective activation and deactivation of: certain panel controls If a control and display element is configured as a simple display, this command will have no effect on the element in question. The turned on or turned off state of an element remains intact at the start/end of the measurement. Because of this, a defined state should be created for the beginning of the measurement for the elements involved (for example in the CAPL program under System->Start).
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: EncodeString
syntax: Function Syntax long EncodeString(byte output[], long& encodedSize, long maxOutputSize, char input[], dword codepage);
description: Description Encodes the string input with the encoding codepage . The length of the encoded string and a terminating "\0" depend on the requested encoding. The number of bytes used in the output byte array is written to encodedSize . If the size of the output array maxOutputSize is not sufficient to hold the encoded string and the "\0", the functions returns an error and the content of the output array is undefined. Characters that cannot be represented in the requested encoding, are replaced wi
parameters: Parameters output Target byte array. encodedSize EncodeString writes the number of bytes used in output (including the terminating "\0") to this parameter. maxOutputSize Size of the output array. input The input string, using the current CAPL string encoding. codepage Windows code page number for the encoded string. The include file Encoding.cin defines the following code pages: CP_UTF8 CP_UTF16 CP_UTF16_BE CP_LATIN1 CP_SHIFT_JIS
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success, the byte array output and the resulting length encodedSize are valid. -1 Illegal character (e.g. illegal UTF8 code point). -2 Insufficient buffer space, output array is too small. -3 Internal error.

name: EnvVarTimeNS
syntax: Function Syntax float EnvVarTimeNS(envvar envVariable);
description: Description Returns the time stamp of the environment variable envVariable in nanoseconds.
parameters: Parameters envVariable Name of the environment variable.
returnValues: Return Values Time stamp of the environment variable in ns.

name: Exp
syntax: Function Syntax double exp(double x);
description: Description Calculates the exponential function.
parameters: Parameters Value whose exponent is to be calculated.
returnValues: Return Values Exponent to base e .

name: FDXClientHandleTcp
syntax: Function Syntax long FDXClientHandleTcp(dword ipv4Address, dword port); // form 1 long FDXClientHandleTcp(byte ipv6Address[], dword port); // form 2
description: Description This function creates an FDX client handle for the FDX client with the specified address. Prerequisite for the function is to configure TCP/IPv4 or TCP/IPv6 as transport layer for the FDX feature.
parameters: Parameters ipv4Address IPv4 address of the FDX clients ipv6Address IPv6 address of the FDX clients port TCP port number of the FDX clients.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than 0, which means the FDX client handle. Values smaller than or equal to zero indicate an error. -1 The FDX feature is disabled in the CANoe configuration or configured for another transport layer.

name: FDXClientHandleUdp
syntax: Function Syntax long FDXClientHandleUdp(dword ipv4Address, dword port); // form 1 long FDXClientHandleUdp(byte ipv6Address[], dword port); // form 2
description: Description This function creates an FDX client handle for the FDX client with the specified address. Prerequisite for the function is to configure UPD/IPv4 or UDP/IPv6 as transport layer for the FDX feature.
parameters: Parameters ipv4Address IPv4 address of the FDX clients ipv6Address IPv6 address of the FDX clients port UPD port number of the FDX clients.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than 0, which means the FDX client handle. Values smaller than or equal to zero indicate an error. -1 The FDX feature is disabled in the CANoe configuration or configured for another transport layer.

name: FDXConfigureSequenceCounting
syntax: Function Syntax long FDXConfigureSequenceCounting(long fdxClientHandle, long mode);
description: Description This function sets the behavior with which CANoe should fill out the field seqNrOrDgramLen in the FDX datagram header. Prerequisite for the function is to configure UPD/IPv4 or UDP/IPv6 as transport layer for the FDX feature.
parameters: Parameters fdxClientHandle FDX client for which the sequence counting should be configured. mode Mode that should set for the Sequence Counting. 0: Automatic mode (default for new connections) The Sequence Counting automatically adapts to the FDX client. 1: Enabled The Sequence Counting is activated for the client. 2: Disabled The Sequence Counting is deactivated for this client.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful function call -1 The parameter fdxClientHandle is invalid. -2 The parameter mode is invalid. -3 The configured transport layer does not support Sequence Counting .

name: FDXDisableFreeRunningMode
syntax: Function Syntax long FDXDisableFreeRunningMode(long fdxClientHandle, word groupID);
description: Description This function disables the Free Running mode for the specified FDX client. The behavior of the function corresponds to the reception of a FreeRunningCancel command via the FDX protocol . You can find further information about the Free Running mode in the manual CANoe_FDX_Protocol_EN.pdf .
parameters: Parameters fdxClientHandle FDX Client for which the FreeRunningMode should be activated. groupID Identifies the data group inside the FDX description file.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful function call -1 The parameter fdxClientHandle is invalid.

name: FDXEnableFreeRunningMode
syntax: Function Syntax long FDXEnableFreeRunningMode(long fdxClientHandle, word groupID, word flags, dword cycleTime, dowrd firstDuration);
description: Description This function activates the Free Running mode for the specified FDX client. The behavior of the function corresponds to the reception of a FreeRunningRequest command via the FDX protocol . You can find further information about the Free Running mode in the manual CANoe_FDX_Protocol_EN.pdf .
parameters: Parameters fdxClientHandle FDX client for which the Free Running mode should be activated. groupID Identifies the data group inside the FDX description file. flags The flags describe when the data group will be transmitted. 1 : Transmits data group at pre start phase of the CANoe measurement 2 : Transmits data group at stop of measurement 4 : Transmits the data group cyclically while the measurement is running. The transmit cycle is defined by the fields cycleTime and firstDuration . 8 : Transmi
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful function call -1 The parameter fdxClientHandle is invalid.

name: FDXSetByteOrder
syntax: Function Syntax long FDXSetByteOrder(long fdxClientHandle, long byteOrder);
description: Description This function configures the byte order of the FDX protocol that should be used for the communication with the specified client. The default is Little Endian. Note With each received FDX datagram, your CANoe automatically takes over the byte order from the datagram for the further communication with this client. The function FDXSetByteOrder is therefore only relevant in the rare case that CANoe should send datagrams to an FDX client without having received a datagram from this client
parameters: Parameters fdxClientHandle FDX client for which the byte order should be set. byteOrder 1: Little Endian 2: Big Endian
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful function call -1 The parameter fdxClientHandle is invalid. -1 The parameter byteOrder is invalid.

name: FDXSetProtocolVersion
syntax: Function Syntax long FDXSetProtocolVersion(long fdxClientHandle, byte majorVersion, byte minorVersion);
description: Description This function configures the version of the FDX protocol that should be used for the communication with the specified client. Note With each received FDX datagram, CANoe automatically adapts to the protocol version from the received datagram. The function FDXSetProtocolVersion is therefore only relevant in the rare case that CANoe should send datagrams to an FDX client without having received a datagram from this client.
parameters: Parameters fdxClientHandle FDX client for which the protocol version should be set. majorVersion Major version of the FDX protocol minorVersion Minor version of the FDX protocol
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful function call -1 The parameter fdxClientHandle is invalid. -2 The given main version ( majorVersion parameter) is not supported by CANoe .

name: FDXTriggerDataGroup
syntax: Function Syntax long FDXTriggerDataGroup (WORD goupID);
description: Description This function triggers the transmission of a data group via FDX protocol .
parameters: Parameters groupID ID of the FDX data group that should be transmitted.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success -1 The given data group is not configured. -2 No client is registered to receive this data group.

name: FileClose
syntax: Function Syntax long fileClose (dword fileHandle);
description: Description This function closes the specified file.
parameters: Parameters The integer contains the handle to the file.
returnValues: Return Values If an error occurs the return value is 0, else 1.

name: FileGetBinaryBlock
syntax: Function Syntax long fileGetBinaryBlock (byte buff[], long buffsize, dword fileHandle);
description: Description The function reads characters from the specified file in binary format. The source file must be opened in binary format.
parameters: Parameters buff Buffer buffsize Maximum of buffsize characters fileHandle Handle to the file
returnValues: Return Values The function returns the number of characters read.

name: FileGetString
syntax: Function Syntax long fileGetString (char buff[], long buffsize, dword fileHandle);
description: Description The function reads a string from the specified file into the buffer buff . Characters are read until the end of line is reached or the number of read characters is equal to buffsize -1. The end of line is marked either by a single line feed or a carriage return and a line feed (DOS file) The end of line in the buffer is represented by a line feed. See also fileGetStringSZ . If the end of a line was encountered, the returned string contains a new line character. Else, the next call to
parameters: Parameters buff Buffer for the read-out string. buffsize Length of the string. fileHandle Handle to the file.
returnValues: Return Values If an error occurs, the return value is 0, else 1.

name: FileGetStringSZ
syntax: Function Syntax long fileGetStringSZ(char buff[], long buffsize, dword fileHandle);
description: Description The function reads a string from the specified file. Characters are read until the end of line is reached or the number of read characters is equal to buffsize -1. The end of line is marked either by a single line feed or a carriage return and a line feed (DOS file) No line feed character will be contained in the buffer. See also fileGetString.
parameters: Parameters buff Buffer for the read-out string buffsize Length of the string fileHandle Handle to the file
returnValues: Return Values If an error occurs, the return value is 0, else 1.

name: FileName
syntax: Function Syntax void fileName();
description: Description Output of the CAPL program name in the Write Window. Helpful for debugging purposes.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: FilePutString
syntax: Function Syntax long filePutString (char buff[], long buffsize, dword fileHandle); long fileWriteString (char buff[], long buffsize, dword fileHandle);
description: Description These functions write a string in the specified file. There is no difference in behavior between the functions.
parameters: Parameters buff Buffer for the read-in string buffsize Number of characters fileHandle Handle to the file
returnValues: Return Values If an error occurs, the return value is 0, else 1.

name: FileRewind
syntax: Function Syntax long fileRewind (dword fileHandle);
description: Description This function resets the position pointer to the beginning of the file.
parameters: Parameters The integer indicates the file handle.
returnValues: Return Values If an error occurs, the return value is 0, else 1.

name: FileWriteBinaryBlock
syntax: Function Syntax long fileWriteBinaryBlock (byte buff[], long buffsize, dword fileHandle);
description: Description This function writes buffsize bytes in the specified file.
parameters: Parameters buff Buffer, with the binary data to be written. buffersize Number of bytes to be written. fileHandle Handle to the file.
returnValues: Return Values The function returns the number of bytes written.

name: Floor
syntax: Function Syntax float _floor(float x);
description: Description Calculates the floor of a value, i.e. the largest integer smaller or equal to the value.
parameters: Parameters x Value of which the floor shall be calculated.
returnValues: Return Values Floor of x.

name: gcvt
syntax: Function Syntax void _gcvt(double val, int digits, char s[]);
description: Description The number val is converted to a string s containing a decimal point and a possible sign byte.
parameters: Parameters val Number to be converted. digits Number of significant digits. s String, which contains the converted number. If the string size is too small, the string stays unchanged.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GetA664Message
syntax: Function Syntax long GetA664Message (PDUobject aPDU, a664Message aMessage);
description: Description This function can be used within an "ON PDU"-handler to retrieve the A664Message object, to which the PDU corresponds. The a664Message object then allows to retrieve the various runtime parameters like BAG or error flags via message selectors. Note : the message ID is available, but not the message name.
parameters: Parameters <aPDU> The <this> object of the PDU-handler <aMessage> The a664Message object which getting the current message information
returnValues: Return Values <success> - 0 information about corresponding message was successfully retrieved -1 Wrong bus system (callback not from an AFDX channel) -3 PDU-object (this) not of correct type or not initialized properly -6 Frame or PDU not available

name: GetAbsFilePath
syntax: Function Syntax long getAbsFilePath(char relPath[], char absPath[], long absPathLen);
description: Description Gets the absolute path of a file. As parameter the file should be defined with the relative path to the current configuration .
parameters: Parameters relPath A path (with or without a filename) defined relative to the current configuration . If this parameter is empty, then the full path of the current configuration will simply be returned. absPath Buffer to which the full path name should be copied. absPathLen Size of the buffer [in bytes] for the full path name.
returnValues: Return Values On success this function returns length of the full path name, otherwise -1.

name: GetApplicationClockCurrentTimeNs
syntax: Function Syntax byte getApplicationClockCurrentTimeNs(int64& domainTimeNs, long& timescale, int64& leapSeconds);
description: Description Supplies the current / absolute domain-time of the Application-Clock. Additionally, the function provides information about the used timescale and the number of leap seconds. The Application-Clock can be configured using the Vector Hardware Manager.
parameters: Parameters domainTimeNs Absolute time since epoch in nanoseconds. Does not contain any leap seconds. timescale The timescale of the domain. Valid values are: 2 (PTP): Epoch: 1970 TAI. The unit of measure of time is the SI second (International System of Units) as realized on the rotating geoid. One nanosecond is equal to one billionth of an SI-second (1 nanosecond = 1/1,000,000,000 of a second). 3 (PERFORMANCE_COUNTER): Epoch: arbitrary. The timescale is not traceable to international standards.
returnValues: Return Values 0 FAILURE: Failed to get the current domain-time. 1 SUCCESS: The current domain time was successfully retrieved.

name: GetApplicationClockRelativeTimeNs
syntax: Function Syntax byte getApplicationClockRelativeTimeNs(int64& domainTimeNs, long& timescale, int64& leapSeconds);
description: Description Supplies the relative domain-time of the Application-Clock since measurement start. Additionally, the function provides information about the used timescale and the number of leap seconds. The Application-Clock can be configured using the Vector Hardware Manager.
parameters: Parameters domainTimeNs Relative time since measurement start in nanoseconds. Does not contain any leap seconds. timescale The timescale of the domain. Valid values are: 2 (PTP): Epoch: 1970 TAI. The unit of measure of time is the SI second (International System of Units) as realized on the rotating geoid. One nanosecond is equal to one billionth of an SI-second (1 nanosecond = 1/1,000,000,000 of a second). 3 (PERFORMANCE_COUNTER): Epoch: arbitrary. The timescale is not traceable to internationa
returnValues: Return Values 0 FAILURE: Failed to get the relative domain-time. 1 SUCCESS: The relative domain time was successfully retrieved.

name: GetBusContext
syntax: Function Syntax dword GetBusContext();
description: Description Returns the current bus context of the CAPL block.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values The current bus context.

name: GetBusNameContext
syntax: Function Syntax dword GetBusNameContext(char name[]); // form 1 dword GetBusNameContext(dword busType, dword channelNumber); // form 2
description: Description Returns the context of the specified bus.
parameters: Parameters name The name of the bus. The bus name can be taken from the CANoe Simulation Setup : Open the System View Select a network Select the Rename... shortcut menu command Copy the name from the dialog busType Bus system of the bus. The predefined enum BusType should be used for the value of this parameter. channelNumer Number of the channel for the specified bus. Channel numbering starts with 1.
returnValues: Return Values In the case of success, the context of the specified bus is returned. If the specified bus does not exist, 0 is returned.

name: GetCANMessage
syntax: Function Syntax long GetCANMessage (this, message * msg);
description: Description This function can only be called inside of an on PDU handler. The function will return in its second parameter the CAN (or CAN-FD) frame, the PDU was contained.
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. msg Reference to a message object that will contain the overall received message data and information.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system; CAN message is not available. -2 Message does not support this info. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of Rx type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Message or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetChannelHardwareMapping
syntax: Function Syntax int GetChannelHardwareMapping(dword channel, dword busType, ChannelHardwareMappingInformation chmi);
description: Description This function populates the ChannelHardwareMapping struct with hardware mapping information based on the specified channel and bus type. In simulation mode, the struct contains placeholder values.
parameters: Parameters channel Number of the channel for the specified bus. Channel numbering starts with 1. busType Bus system of the bus. The predefined enum BusType should be used for the value of this parameter. ChannelHardwareMappingInformation The channel hardware mapping information for the defined channel and bus type.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No channel hardware mapping could be found. 1 Successful.

name: GetComputerName
syntax: Function Syntax long GetComputerName(char buffer[], dword bufferSize);
description: Description Retrieves the fully qualified name of the computer.
parameters: Parameters buffer Space for the returned name. bufferSize Length of the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values 0 if the function completed successfully, else unequal 0.

name: GetConfigurationName
syntax: Function Syntax long getConfigurationName(char buffer[], dword bufferLength);
description: Description Returns the filename of the currently loaded configuration. It includes neither the filename extension nor any path information, for example CANSystemDemo . Note Difference between StandaloneGetCurrentConfig and getConfigurationName There are two kinds of configurations in standalone mode: the standalone configuration (with filename extension RTCFG) the normal configuration (with filename extension CFG) associated to the standalone configuration. These two filenames may well differ (
parameters: Parameters buffer Space for the returned name. bufferLength Length of the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values -1 Buffer too small. >= 0 Operation successful. The return value specifies the length of the configuration name.

name: GetDrift
syntax: Function Syntax int getDrift();
description: Description Determines the constant deviation when Drift is set.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Drift in parts per thousand.

name: GetEthernetPacket
syntax: Function Syntax long GetEthernetPacket (this, ethernetPacket packet);
description: Description This function can only be called inside of an on PDU handler. The function will return in its second parameter the Ethernet packet, the PDU was contained.
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. packet Reference to an Ethernet Packet object that will contain the overall received packet data and information.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system; Ethernet packet is not available. -2 Packet does not support this info. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of Rx type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Packet or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetEventSortingStatus
syntax: Function Syntax int GetEventSortingStatus(message msg); int GetEventSortingStatus(pg msg); int GetEventSortingStatus(gmLanMessage msg); int GetEventSortingStatus(linFrame msg); int GetEventSortingStatus(j1587Param msg); int GetEventSortingStatus(linBaudrateEvent event); int GetEventSortingStatus(linCsError event); int GetEventSortingStatus(linDlcinfo event); int GetEventSortingStatus(linReceiveError event); int GetEventSortingStatus(linSchedulerModeChange event); int GetEventSortingStatus(linSle
description: Description Determines the Event Sorting state. With active Event Sorting the return value indicates if the given event in the Simulation Setup of CANoe DE product was processed in correct time sequence.
parameters: Parameters Message event variable of type "message", "pg", "gmLanMessage", "linFrame", "j1587Param", "linBaudrateEvent", "linCsError", "linDlcinfo", "linReceiveError", "linSchedulerModeChange", "linSleepModeEvent", "linSyncError", "linTransmError",  "linWakeupFrame", "beanError", "FRSlot", "FRFrame" or "FrStartCycle".
returnValues: Return Values 0 Invalid state The Event Sorting is inactive or it concerns a program internal event that will never be sorted (e.g. environment variable). 1 sorted The Event Sorting is active and the event was processed and sorted in the correct time sequence. 2 unsorted The Event Sorting is active but the event arrived too late or too soon so that the event was processed unsorted.

name: GetFrFrame
syntax: Function Syntax long GetFrFrame (this, FrFrame * frame);
description: Description This function can only be called inside of an on PDU handler. The function will return in its second parameter the FlexRay frame, the PDU was contained.
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. packet Reference to an FlexRay Frame object that will contain the overall received packet data and information.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system; FlexRay frame is not available. -2 Frame does not support this info. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of Rx type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Frame or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetGPSTimeString
syntax: Function Syntax void getGPSTimeString(char timeBuffer[], dword timestamp);
description: Description Copies a printed representation of the GPS time stamp represented as UTC date and time into the supplied character buffer. The format of the string is ddd mmm dd hh:mm:ss jjjj (e.g. "Fri Aug 21 15:22:24 1998").
parameters: Parameters timeBuffer The buffer the string will be written in. This buffer must be at least 26 characters long. timestamp The GPS time stamp (seconds have been displayed in time stamps since 1970-01-01, 00:00). The GPS time stamp is available as system variable as soon as a GPS device is configured.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GetIPAddress
syntax: Function Syntax long GetIPAddress(char buffer[], dword bufferSize);
description: Description Retrieves the default (first) IP address of the computer as a string.
parameters: Parameters buffer Space for the returned address. bufferSize Length of the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values 0 if the function completed successfully, else unequal 0.

name: GetJitterMax
syntax: Function Syntax int getJitterMax();
description: Description Determines the upper limit for the allowable deviation when Jitter is set.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Upper deviation in parts per thousand.

name: GetJitterMin
syntax: Function Syntax int getJitterMin();
description: Description Determines the lower limit for the allowable deviation when Jitter is set.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Lower deviation in parts per thousand.

name: GetLastTimerAccuracyNS
syntax: Function Syntax long GetLastTimerAccuracyNS(msTimer timer, int64& accuracy);
description: Description If you have turned on the accuracy monitoring for a timer with SetTimerAccuracyMonitoringActive , you can read out the last measured accuracy when the timer has elapsed with this function. It returns the difference between the timer interval in simulation time and in real (wallclock) time in nanoseconds. Note A certain difference between realtime and simulation time is normal and not a problem in most cases. However, if you have higher realtime requirements, you may detect problems e
parameters: Parameters timer The timer which shall be monitored. accuracy The last measured accuracy in nanoseconds (out-parameter). If accuracy monitoring is active for the timer, but the timer has not yet elapsed, the value will be 0.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success -1 Accuracy monitoring is not active for the timer.

name: GetLocalTime
syntax: Function Syntax void getLocalTime(long time[]);
description: Description Returns details to the current date and time in an array of type long. Note for use in distributed mode This function always returns the local time of the user computer.
parameters: Parameters An array of type long with at least 9 entries. The entries of the array will be filled with the following information: Index Information 0 Seconds (0 - 59) 1 Minutes (0 - 59) 2 Hours (0 - 23) 3 Day of month (1 - 31) 4 Month (0 - 11) 5 Year (0 - xxx, offset of 1900, e.g. 117 = 2017) 6 Day of week (0 - 6, sunday is 0) 7 Day of Year (0 - 365) 8 Flag for daylight saving time (0 - 1, 1 = daylight saving time)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GetLocalTimeString
syntax: Function Syntax void getLocalTimeString(char timeBuffer[]);
description: Description Copies a printed representation of the current date and time into the supplied character buffer. The format of the string is ddd mmm dd hh:mm:ss jjjj (e.g. "Fri Aug 21 15:22:24 1998"). Note for use in distributed mode This function always returns the local time of the user computer.
parameters: Parameters timeBuffer The buffer the string will be written in. This buffer must be at least 26 characters long.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GetMeasurementIndex
syntax: Function Syntax long GetMeasurementIndex()
description: Description Returns the current measurement index. The measurement index can be set in the global options and is incremented on each measurement start.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GetMeasurementStartTime
syntax: Function Syntax long getMeasurementStartTime(long time[]);
description: Description Returns details about the absolute time the measurement was started. Note In CANoe offline mode , the function returns the oldest start time of those stored in the logging files.
parameters: Parameters time An array of type long with at least 8 entries. The entries of the array will be filled with the following information: Index Information 0 Milliseconds (0 - 999) 1 Seconds (0 - 59) 2 Minutes (0 - 59) 3 Hours (0 - 23) 4 Day of month (1 - 31) 5 Month (0 - 11) 6 Year (0 - xxx, offset of 1900, e.g. 117 = 2017) 7 Day of week (0 - 6, Sunday is 0)
returnValues: Return Values 1 if successful, 0 if not (e.g. array to small).

name: GetMeasurementStartUTCTime
syntax: Function Syntax long getMeasurementStartUTCTime(long time[]);
description: Description Returns details about the absolute UTC date and time, the measurement was started. Note In CANoe offline mode , the function returns the oldest start time of those stored in the logging files.
parameters: Parameters time An array of type long with at least 8 entries. The entries of the array will be filled with the following information: Index Information 0 Milliseconds (0 - 999) 1 Seconds (0 - 59) 2 Minutes (0 - 59) 3 Hours (0 - 23) 4 Day of month (1 - 31) 5 Month (0 - 11) 6 Year (0 - xxx, offset of 1900, e.g. 117 = 2017) 7 Day of week (0 - 6, Sunday is 0)
returnValues: Return Values 1 if successful, 0 if not (e.g. array to small).

name: GetNetworkName
syntax: Function Syntax long GetNetworkName(dword busType, dword channelNumber, char name[]);
description: Description Retrieves the name assigned in the Simulation Setup for a specific channel.
parameters: Parameters busType Bus system of the channel. The predefined enum BusType should be used for the value of this parameter. channelNumer Number of the channel for the specified bus. Channel numbering starts with 1. name Receives the name for the channel.
returnValues: Return Values 0 success 1 invalid bus system or channel number 2 name contains characters which can't be represented in the current CAPL string encoding 3 name buffer is too small for the name

name: GetNumOfflineFiles
syntax: Function Syntax long getNumOfflineFiles();
description: Description Returns the number of configured offline source files.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 If error (e.g., not in offline mode) Number of configured offline source files if no error.

name: GetOfflineFileName
syntax: Function Syntax long getOfflineFileName(char[] buffer, dword bufferSize); //form 1 long getOfflineFileName(long index, char[] buffer, dword bufferSize); // form 2
description: Description Returns the complete path of one file of the currently used offline source files. If form 1 is used, the first file is returned, otherwise the index of the file to return is passed as parameter.
parameters: Parameters buffer Space for the returned string. bufferSize Size of the buffer. index Index of the returned offline file.
returnValues: Return Values 0 If no error 1 If buffer is too small 2 For other errors (e.g. not in offline mode)

name: GetOSEnvironmentVariableValue
syntax: Function Syntax long GetOSEnvironmentVariableValue(char variableName[], char buffer[]);
description: Description Gets the value of an operating system environment variable (i.e. "PATH" or "USERNAME"). Note that these are not the CANoe system variables.
parameters: Parameters variableName The name of the environment variable to be queried. buffer Space for the return value of the environment variable. If it does not contain sufficient space for the result, it will remain unchanged and the function will return 1.
returnValues: Return Values 0 if the function completed successfully, else unequal 0. Possible reasons for failure are that the environment variable does not exist or that the buffer is not large enough to contain the result.

name: GetPDUsTPIPv4DstAddr
syntax: Function Syntax long GetPDUsTPIPv4DstAddr(this, dword &IPv4DestinationAddress);
description: Description This function can only be used within a on PDU handler. If the PDU was received via IPv4, with this function the IPv4 destination address can be requested. Note In on someipMessage you can use either GetDestinationAddress or GetDestinationEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. destinationIPv4Address IPv4 destination address in network byte order.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system. -2 The requested information was not available. Most probably the PDU was not received by an IPv4 layer. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of RX type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Message or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetPDUsTPIPv4SrcAddr
syntax: Function Syntax long GetPDUsTPIPv4SrcAddr(this, dword &IPv4SourceAddress);
description: Description This function can only be used within a on PDU handler. If the PDU was received via IPv4, with this function the IPv4 source address can be requested. Note In on someipMessage you can use either GetSourceAddress or GetSourceEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. IPv4SourceAddress IPv4 source address in network byte order.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system. -2 The requested information was not available. Most probably the PDU was not received by an IPv4 layer. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of RX type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Message or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetPDUsTPIPv6DstAddr
syntax: Function Syntax long GetPDUsTPIPv6DstAddr(this, byte IPv6DestinationAddress[]);
description: Description This function can only be used within a on PDU handler. If the PDU was received via IPv6, with this function the IPv6 destination address can be requested. Note In on someipMessage you can use either GetDestinationAddress or GetDestinationEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. IPv6DestinationAddress IPv6 destination address as 16 byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system. -2 The requested information was not available. Most probably the PDU was not received by an IPv6 layer. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of RX type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Message or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetPDUsTPIPv6SrcAddr
syntax: Function Syntax long GetPDUsTPIPv6SrcAddr(this, byte IPv6SourceAddress[]);
description: Description This function can only be used within a on PDU handler. If the PDU was received via IPv6, with this function the IPv6 source address can be requested. Note In on someipMessage you can use either GetSourceAddress or GetSourceEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. IPv6SourceAddress IPv6 source address as 16 byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system. -2 The requested information was not available. Most probably the PDU was not received by an IPv6 layer. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of RX type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Message or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetPDUsTPTCPDstPort
syntax: Function Syntax long GetPDUsTPTCPDstPort(this, dword &tcpDestinationPort);
description: Description This function can only be used within a on PDU handler. If the PDU was received via TCP, with this function the TCP destination port can be requested. Note In on someipMessage you can use GetDestinationEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. tcpDestinationPort TCP destination port.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system. -2 The requested information was not available. Most probably the PDU was not received by a TCP layer. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of RX type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Message or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetPDUsTPTCPSrcPort
syntax: Function Syntax long GetPDUsTPTCPSrcPort(this, dword &tcpSourcePort);
description: Description This function can only be used within a on PDU handler. If the PDU was received via TCP, with this function the TCP source address can be requested. Note In on someipMessage you can use GetSourceEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. tcpDestinationPort TCP source port.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system. -2 The requested information was not available. Most probably the PDU was not received by a TCP layer. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of RX type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Message or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetPDUsTPUDPDstPort
syntax: Function Syntax long GetPDUsTPUDPDstPort(this, dword &udpDestinationPort);
description: Description This function can only be used within a on PDU handler. If the PDU was received via UDP, with this function the UDP destination port can be requested. Note In on someipMessage you can use GetDestinationEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. udpDestinationPort UDP destination port.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system. -2 The requested information was not available. Most probably the PDU was not received by an UDP layer. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of RX type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Message or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetPDUsTPUDPSrcPort
syntax: Function Syntax long GetPDUsTPUDPSrcPort(this, dword &udpSourcePort);
description: Description This function can only be used within a on PDU handler. If the PDU was received via UDP, with this function the UDP source port can be requested. Note In on someipMessage you can use GetSourceEndpoint .
parameters: Parameters this Handle to the currently handled (received) PDU object. udpSourcePort UDP source port.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Data access successful. -1 Wrong bus system. -2 The requested information was not available. Most probably the PDU was not received by an UDP layer. -3 The PDU object is invalid. -4 PDU is not of RX type. -5 Parameter too small (e.g. array has too less bytes) -6 Message or PDU is not available (any more)

name: GetProductVersion
syntax: Function Syntax void GetProductVersion(dword& major, dword& minor, dword& build, dword& patch, dword& servicePack);
description: Description Retrieves version information for the running product.
parameters: Parameters major The major version of your product, e.g., 18 for CANoe 18.3.118.16 SP3. minor The minor version of your product, e.g., 3 for CANoe 18.3.118.16 SP3. build The build number of your product, e.g., 118 for CANoe 18.3.118.16 SP3. patch The patch number of your product, e.g., 16 for CANoe 18.3.118.16 SP3 or 0 for CANoe 18.0.185. servicePack The service pack number of your product, e.g., 3 for CANoe 18.3.118 SP3 or 0 for CANoe 18.0.185.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GetProFileArray
syntax: Function Syntax long getProfileArray(char section[], char entry[],char buff[], long buffsize, char filename[]); // form 1 long getProfileArray(char section[], char entry[],char buff[], long buffsize, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
description: Description Searches the file under section section for the variable entry . Entry is interpreted as a list of numerical values, separated by comma, tab, space, semicolon or slash. A 0x prefix indicates hex values.
parameters: Parameters section Section of the file as a string. entry Variable name as a string. buff Buffer for the read-in numerical values. Due to the char data type, the values must be in the range -128 .. 127 (inclusive). buffsize Size of buff : Maximum number of read in numerical values (max. 1279 characters). filename File path as a string. utf16 If this flag is set the file will be interpreted as UTF-16LE encoded, if the corresponding BOM is also present.
returnValues: Return Values Number of numerical values read in.

name: GetProFileFloat
syntax: Function Syntax float getProfileFloat(char section[], char entry[], long def, char filename[]); // form 1 float getProfileFloat(char section[], char entry[], long def, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
description: Description Searches the file filename under section section for the variable entry . If its value is a number, this number is returned as the functional result. If the file or entry is not found, the default value def is returned as the functional result. If the entry does not contain a valid number, positive zero is returned. If the value is out of range for float, positive infinity is returned instead.
parameters: Parameters section Section of the file as a string. entry Variable name as a string. def Default value in case of error as a float. filename File path as a string. utf16 If this flag is set the file will be interpreted as UTF-16LE encoded, if the corresponding BOM is also present.
returnValues: Return Values Float that was read in.

name: GetProFileInt
syntax: Function Syntax long getProfileInt(char section[], char entry[], long def, char filename[]); // form 1 long getProfileInt(char section[], char entry[], long def, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
description: Description Searches the file filename under section section for the variable entry . If its value is a number, this number is returned as the functional result. If the file or entry is not found, or if entry does not contain a valid number, the default value def is returned as the functional result.
parameters: Parameters section Section of the file as a string. entry Variable name as a string. def Default value in case of error as an integer. filename File path as a string. utf16 If this flag is set the file will be interpreted as UTF-16LE encoded, if the corresponding BOM is also present.
returnValues: Return Values Integer that was read in.

name: GetProFileString
syntax: Function Syntax long getProfileString(char section[], char entry[], char def[], char buff[], long buffsize, char filename[]); // form 1 long getProfileString(char section[], char entry[], char def[], char buff[], long buffsize, char filename[], dword utf16); // form 2
description: Description Searches the file filename under section section for the variable entry . Its contents (value) are written to the buffer buff . Its length must be passed correctly in buffsize . If the file or entry is not found, the default value def is copied to buffer. If the read string length is longer than the buffer, the string will be cut to the buffer length.
parameters: Parameters section Section of the file as a string. entry Variable name as a string. def Default value in case of error as a string. buff Buffer for the read-in character as a string. buffersize Size of buff in bytes. filename Filename as a string. utf16 If this flag is set the file will be interpreted as UTF-16LE encoded, if the corresponding BOM is also present. The string written to buff will be converted to the CAPL encoding.
returnValues: Return Values Length of the read string. Length of the default string: Fault Key not found -1 : Fault File not found

name: GetStartDelay
syntax: Function Syntax int getStartdelay();
description: Description Determines the value of the start delay configured for this network node in the Simulation Setup.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Start delay in ms. If no start delay was set the function returns the value zero.

name: GetSteadyClockCurrentTimeNS
syntax: Function Syntax int64 GetSteadyClockCurrentTimeNS();
description: Description Returns the current time of a steady clock. This time is only useful in comparison with other times from that clock. The steady clock provides a monotonic realtime . That time has no relation to a wallclock time and may drift slightly in comparison to the time stamps of measured frames, since the used clock in the computer is not synchronized to the one in the network interfaces (e.g. Vector VN devices). The time also has no relation to the simulation time, which is correlated to tho
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Current time of a steady clock in nanoseconds.

name: GetSteadyClockStartDurationNS
syntax: Function Syntax int64 GetSteadyClockStartDurationNS();
description: Description Returns the duration of the measurement start in nanoseconds. The duration returned is the one needed for all on start handlers of simulation nodes and similar functions. It does not include the time needed e.g. for on preStart handlers, for compilation of CAPL programs, initialization of hardware etc. You can use the function mainly for detecting whether the measurement start takes too much time, maybe because of an inefficient on start handler.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Measurement start time of a steady clock in nanoseconds. The function returns 0 if called in the on start or on preStart handler.

name: GetSteadyClockStartTimeNS
syntax: Function Syntax int64 GetSteadyClockStartTimeNS();
description: Description Returns the measurement start time of a steady clock. This time is only useful in comparison with other times from that clock which you can get through GetSteadyClockCurrentTimeNS() . The steady clock provides a monotonic realtime . That time has no relation to a wallclock time and may drift slightly in comparison to the time stamps of measured frames, since the used clock in the computer is not synchronized to the one in the network interfaces (e.g. Vector VN devices). The time also
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Measurement start time of a steady clock in nanoseconds.

name: GetUserFilePath
syntax: Function Syntax long getUserFilePath(char fileName[], char absPath[], long absPathLen);
description: Description Gets the absolute path of a user file . In case of execution in a distributed environment the absolute user file path (including filename) on the remote device (e.g. VN8900) is returned if the user file was predefined. If the file was not predefined an error code is returned. In case of a single Computer environment the registered absolute file path (including filename) of the user file is returned if the user file was predefined. If the file was not predefined the function returns t
parameters: Parameters fileName A filename potentially containing a path. If the filename is predefined the path is ignored. Otherwise the path is interpreted as relative to the current configuration path in a single Computer environment. absPath Buffer to which the full path name should be copied. absPathLen Size of the buffer [in bytes] for the full path name.
returnValues: Return Values On success this function returns the length of the full path name. Otherwise the function returns -1 if parameters do not fit or buffer size is too small or -2 if no user file with the given name was registered in case of a distributed environment.

name: GetUTCTime
syntax: Function Syntax long getUTCTime(long time[]);
description: Description Returns details for the current UTC date and time in an array of type long.
parameters: Parameters An array of type long with at least 9 entries. The entries of the array will be filled with the following information: Index Information 0 Seconds (0 - 59) 1 Minutes (0 - 59) 2 Hours (0 - 23) 3 Day of month (1 - 31) 4 Month (0 - 11) 5 Year (0 - xxx, offset of 1900, e.g. 117 = 2017) 6 Day of week (0 - 6, sunday is 0) 7 Day of Year (0 - 365) 8 Flag for daylight saving time (always 0 for UTC)
returnValues: Return Values 1 if successful, 0 if not (e.g. array too small).

name: GetUTCTimeString
syntax: Function Syntax long getUTCTimeString(char timeBuffer[]);
description: Description Copies a printed representation of the current UTC date and time into the supplied character buffer. The format of the string is ddd mmm dd hh:mm:ss jjjj (e.g. "Wed Apr 16 10:52:32 2025").
parameters: Parameters timeBuffer The buffer the string will be written into. This buffer must be at least 26 characters long.
returnValues: Return Values 1 if successful, 0 if not (e.g. array too small).

name: GMLanID
syntax: Function Syntax dword gmLanId (dbMsg aMessage, dword aSourceId); // form 1 dword gmLanId (dbMsg aMessage, Node aTxNode); // form 2
description: Description This function can be used to create a message ID for a GMLAN message. In addition to the message, you must specify the transmission node or its source ID. The message ID returned can be used, for example, to wait for a specific GMLAN message with the TestWaitForMessage function.
parameters: Parameters aMessage GMLAN message for which a message ID is to be created. aSourceId Source ID of the node to be coded as the transmission node in the message ID. aTxNode Node to be coded as the transmission node in the message ID.
returnValues: Return Values If the message is a GMLAN message, a GMLAN message ID will be returned containing the correct source address and priority. Otherwise, the message ID will be returned.

name: gmLanSetPID
syntax: Function Syntax gmLanSetPID(gmLanMessage msg, long v); gmLanSetSourceId(gmLanMessage msg, long v); gmLanSetPrio(gmLanMessage msg, long v); gmLanGetPID(gmLanMessage msg); gmLanGetSourceId(gmLanMessage msg); gmLanGetPrio(gmLanMessage msg);
description: Description gmLanSetPID sets the parameter ID of the message. gmLanSetSourceId sets the source address of the message. gmLanSetPrio sets the priority of the message. gmLanGetPID gets the parameter ID of the message. gmLanGetSourceId gets the source address of the message. gmLanGetPrio gets the priority of the message.
parameters: Parameters msg Message v Parameter ID, source address or priority
returnValues: Return Values gmLanSetPID , gmLanSetSourceId , gmLanSetPrio : — gmLanGetPID , gmLanGetSourceId , gmLanGetPrio : Parameter ID, source address or priority

name: GraphicsWindowClear
syntax: Function Syntax void graphicsWindowClear (char[] windowName);
description: Description Temporarily deletes the contents of the CANoe Graphics Window . E.g. with TestReportAddWindowCapture a screenshot is created that contains only the data of a certain period of time.
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Graphics Window.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GraphicsWindowFit
syntax: Function Syntax void graphicsWindowFit(char[] windowName, byte mode);
description: Description Scales symbols in a CANoe Graphics Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Graphics Window. mode mode = 1: Fit all signals mode = 2: Fit all signals Y mode = 3: Fit X mode = 4: Fit all signals to a special line mode = 5: Fit all signals to a special line Y
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GraphicsWindowModifyGroup
syntax: Function Syntax void graphicsWindowModifyGroup(char[] windowName, byte mode, char[] groupName);
description: Description Modifies group enabled and expanded states.
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Graphics Window. byte mode 1 = Enable group 2 = Disable group 3 = Expand group 4 = Collapse group 5 = Disable all groups 6 = Collapse all groups 7 = Expand all enabled groups 8 = Collapse all disabled groups groupName The name of the common axis/group to modify (optional parameter).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GraphicsWindowPause
syntax: Function Syntax void graphicsWindowPause(char[] windowName, byte active);
description: Description Pauses and resumes a CANoe Graphics Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Graphics Window. active active = 0: Pause off active = 1: Pause on
returnValues: Return Values —

name: GraphicsWindowTimeInterval
syntax: Function Syntax void graphicsWindowTimeInterval(char[] windowName, int64 from, int64 to);
description: Description Sets the time interval that will be displayed in a CANoe Graphics Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Graphics Window. from / to Defines the time interval in [ns]. The time can be retrieved by timeNowInt64 .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Halt
syntax: Function Syntax void halt();
description: Description The function stops both the debugged program as well as the simulation. The debugger window opens and the halt() command is displayed. The function thus creates a breakpoint while simultaneously interrupting the measurement in simulated mode (also for test modules). The simulation can be continued with <F9> . For usage in Real Mode, it is only activated for test modules and test configurations otherwise the halt instruction is ignored.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: InterfaceStatus
syntax: Function Syntax void InterfaceStatus(long time, long channel, long status);
description: Description The callback can be inserted in the sections callback function or function . The callback occurs when the status of the connection to the interface hardware is changed (e.g. when Windows reports a lost connection to a CAN/WLAN gateway or to a WLAN interface hardware for Car2x communication).
parameters: Parameters time Time, resolution 10us channel The channel number status Status of the channel Values: 3015: The connection is lost
returnValues: Return Values —

name: InterpretAs
syntax: Function Syntax dword interpretAsDword(float x); float interpretAsFloat(dword x); qword interpretAsQword(double x); double interpretAsDouble(qword x);
description: Description These functions interpret the actual bytes of a value as if the value was of another data type. InterpretAsFloat for example takes the four bytes or a dword, interprets them as if they were four bytes of an IEEE single precision floating point number, and returns that number. InterpretAsDouble interprets eight bytes as if they were an IEEE double precision floating point number. InterpretAsDword and InterpretAsQword are the inverse functions.
parameters: Parameters x The number which shall be interpreted.
returnValues: Return Values The interpreted value.

name: IsInf
syntax: Function Syntax byte IsInf(float x);
description: Description Checks if a float value is equal to positive or negative infinity.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 True (value is equal to positive or negative infinity). 0 False (value is not equal to positive or negative infinity).

name: IsNaN
syntax: Function Syntax byte IsNaN(float x);
description: Description Checks if a float value is NaN. Note Unlike other programming languages, CAPL does not guarantee that NaN is not equal to itself. Therefore, comparing a variable to itself is not a reliable method for detecting NaN values in CAPL.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 True (value is NaN). 0 False (value is not NaN).

name: IsOfflineFileActive
syntax: Function Syntax long isOfflineFileActive(long index);
description: Description Returns whether events of an offline source file are currently replayed, i.e., the start of the source file has been reached but the end of the source file has not yet been reached.
parameters: Parameters index Index of the offline source file.
returnValues: Return Values -1 If error (e.g., not in offline mode) 0 Start of offline source file not yet reached, or end of offline source file already reached. 1 Start of offline source file reached, but end of offline source file not yet reached.

name: IsOfflineMode
syntax: Function Syntax long isOfflineMode();
description: Description This function is used to get the information if CANoe is in offline mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 True, CANoe is in offline mode. 0 False, CANoe is in online mode.

name: IsRunningInStandaloneMode
syntax: Function Syntax dword IsRunningInStandaloneMode();
description: Description This function is used to get the information whether the CAPL code is executed in standalone mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 True (execution in standalone mode) 0 False (execution not in standalone mode)

name: IsRunningOnRemoteKernel
syntax: Function Syntax long IsRunningOnRemoteKernel();
description: Description This function is used to get the information whether the CAPL code is executed on the local computer or by a RT kernel running on a remote device (RT server).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 True (execution on remote RT kernel) 0 False (execution on local computer)

name: IsSimulated
syntax: Function Syntax long isSimulated();
description: Description This function is used to get the information if CANoe is in simulated mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 True, CANoe is in simulated mode. 0 False, CANoe is in real mode.

name: IsTimerActive
syntax: Function Syntax int isTimerActive(timer t); // form 1 int isTimerActive(mstimer t); // form 2
description: Description Return value indicates whether a specific timer is active. This is the case between the call to the setTimer function and the call to the on timer event procedure.
parameters: Parameters timer or mstimer variable
returnValues: Return Values 1, if the timer is active; otherwise 0. 0 is also returned within the on timer event procedure.

name: KeyPressed
syntax: Function Syntax dword keypressed();
description: Description This function returns the key code of a currently pressed key. If no key is being pressed it returns 0.. For example, pressing of a key can be queried in a timer function. The reaction can also be to letting go of a key.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Key code of pressed key. If the 8 lower bits do not equal 0, keypressed returns the ASCII code of the next key in the keyboard buffer. If the 8 lower bits do not equal 0, the 8 upper bits represent the extended key code (see IBM PC Technical Reference Manual).

name: Log
syntax: Function Syntax double _log(double x);
description: Description Calculates the natural logarithm.
parameters: Parameters Value of which the logarithm shall be calculated.
