name: lookupFrFrame
syntax: Function Syntax dbFrFrame * lookupFrFrame(char frameName[]);
description: Description Searches for a FlexRay frame definition in the database(s). If the frame is not found or if the name is not unique, test modules/units report an error in test system while simulation/analysis nodes write a warning into the Write Window, and the function returns an invalid dbFrFrame . Notes It is recommended to use this function only in special cases or during measurement start, since searching for the database definition may impact realtime performance.
parameters: Parameters frameName The qualified name of the frame. The syntax is [NetworkName::][NodeName::]FrameName , i.e., both network name and node name are optional.
returnValues: Return Values The found unique frame definition or an invalid object.

parameters: Parameters pduName The qualified name of the PDU. The syntax is [NetworkName::][NodeName::]PDUName , i.e., both network name and node name are optional.
returnValues: Return Values The found unique PDU definition or an invalid object.

name: lookupSignal
syntax: Function Syntax signal * lookupSignal(char signalName[]);// form 1 signal * lookupSignal(dbSignal signal); // form 2 signal * lookupSignal(dbSignal signal, dbNode node); // form 3
description: Description Searches for a signal in the database(s). If the signal is not found or if the name is not unique, test modules/units report an error in test system while simulation/analysis nodes write a warning into the Write Window, and the function returns an invalid signal . Notes It is recommended to use this function only in special cases or during measurement start, since searching for the database definition may impact realtime performance. Use form 2 or 3 to program generically with a vari
parameters: Parameters signalName The qualified name of the signal. The syntax is [Channel::][Database name (alias)::][Node::][Message::]Signal ; the order and completeness of the objects from right to left is important. Note Using drag and drop of a signal from the Symbol Explorer of the CAPL Browser results in a signal qualification where the database name is missing. You must add the database name to the signal qualification at the correct order, so that the function could find the signal at runtime.
returnValues: Return Values The found unique signal or an invalid object.

name: ltoa
syntax: Function Syntax void ltoa(long val, char s[], long base);
description: Description The number val is converted to a string s . In this case, base indicates a number base between 2 and 36. s must be large enough to accept the converted number!
parameters: Parameters val Number to be converted. s String, which contains the converted number. base Number base.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: MakeARGB
syntax: Function Syntax long MakeARGB(long Alpha, long Red, long Green, long Blue);
description: Description Calculates the color value from the alpha value and the three primary color components. The value is interpreted as 4 bytes and the color results from which value is contained in which byte: [0] = Alpha (transparency), [1] = Red , [2] = Green , [3] = Blue See also availability for panel controls
parameters: Parameters Alpha The transparency of the color (0 - 255) 0: 0% opacity of the color, totally transparent 255: 100% opacity of the color Red Red color component (0 - 255) Green Green color component (0 - 255) Blue Blue color component (0 - 255)
returnValues: Return Values Color value (type long)

name: MakeRGB
syntax: Function Syntax long MakeRGB(long Red, long Green, long Blue);
description: Description Calculates the color value from the three primary color components. The value is interpreted as 4 bytes and the color results from which value is contained in which byte: [0] = ignored, [1] = Red , [2] = Green , [3] = Blue See also availability for panel controls
parameters: Parameters Red Red color component (0 - 255) Green Green color component (0 - 255) Blue Blue color component (0 - 255)
returnValues: Return Values Color value (type long)

name: Max
syntax: Function Syntax long _max(long x, long y); // form 1 dword _max(dword x, dword y); // form 2 int64 _max(int64 x, int64 y); // form 3 qword _max(qword x, qword y); // form 4 float _max(float x, float y); // form 5
description: Description Returns the maximum of the parameters.
parameters: Parameters x First operand y Second operand
returnValues: Return Values y > x ? y x

name: MbStrLen
syntax: Function Syntax long mbstrlen(char s[]);
description: Description The functional result is the length of the string s in characters .
parameters: Parameters string
returnValues: Return Values Length of the string in characters.

name: MbStrnCmp
syntax: Function Syntax long mbstrncmp(char s1[], char s2[], long len); // form 1 long mbstrncmp(char s1[], char s2[], long s2offset, long len); // form 2 long mbstrncmp_off(char s1[], long s1offset, char s2[], long s2offset, long len); // form 3
description: Description This function compares s1 with s2 for a maximum of len characters. If they are identical the functional result is 0. If s1 is less than s2 the result is -1, else +1. Comparison starts in s1 at s1offset (form 3) and in s2 at s2offset (form 2 and form 3). Note On Windows, the function does not work correctly for unicode symbols which need to be stored as a surrogate pair in UTF-16 (i.e., which are not part of the basic multilingual plane). This is a limitation of the Microsoft APIs on
parameters: Parameters s1 First string s2 Second string len Maximum number of characters to compare s1offset Offset in s1 in characters s2offset Offset in s2 in characters
returnValues: Return Values -1 if s1 is less than s2. 1 if s2 is less than s1. 0 if the strings are equal.

name: MbStrnCpy
syntax: Function Syntax void mbstrncpy(char dest[], char src[], long len); void mbstrncpy_off(char dest[], long destOffset, char src[], long len);
description: Description mbstrncpy function copies src to dest . len indicates the number of characters that shall be copied; use -1 to indicate that as much shall be copied into dest as will fit (maximum until the end of src ). The function ensures that there is a terminating 0 byte; but in contrast to strncpy, that byte is not counted into len . mbstrncpy_off overwrites characters in the destination buffer starting at the character offset destOffset.
parameters: Parameters dest Destination buffer src Source string len Number of characters to be copied, or -1 to copy as much as possible destOffset Offset in characters into the destination buffer
returnValues: Return Values —

name: MbStrStr
syntax: Function Syntax long mbstrstr(char s1[], char s2[]); long mbstrstr_off(char s1[], long offset, char s2[]);
description: Description Searches in s1 for s2 .
parameters: Parameters s1 First string s2 Second string offset Offset in s1 in characters at which the search shall be started
returnValues: Return Values First position in characters of s2 in s1 , or -1 if s2 is not found in s1 .

name: MbSubStrCpy
syntax: Function Syntax void mbsubstr_cpy(char dest[], char src[], long srcStart, long len); void mbsubstr_cpy_off(char dest[], long destOffset, char src[], long srcStart, long len);
description: Description mbsubstr_cpy copies a substring of src to dest . len indicates the number of characters that shall be copied; use -1 to indicate that as much shall be copied into dest as will fit (maximum until the end of src ). The function ensures that there is a terminating 0 byte; but in contrast to substr_cpy / substr_cpy_off , that byte is not counted into len . mbsubstr_cpy_off overwrites characters in the destination buffer starting at the character offset destOffset .
parameters: Parameters dest Destination buffer src Source string srcStart Start index in characters in src of substring len Number of characters to be copied, or -1 to copy as much as possible destOffset Offset in characters into the destination buffer
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Min
syntax: Function Syntax long _min(long x, long y); // form 1 dword _min(dword x, dword y); // form 2 int64 _min(int64 x, int64 y); // form 3 qword _min(qword x, qword y); // form 4 float _min(float x, float y); // form 5
description: Description Returns the minimum of the parameters.
parameters: Parameters x First operand y Second operand
returnValues: Return Values y < x ? y x

name: MsgBeep
syntax: Function Syntax void msgBeep (long soundType);
description: Description The msgBeep function plays back a sound predefined by the Windows system. It replaces the previous beep function.
parameters: Parameters soundType Integer for the predefined sound. Specifically these are: 0 MB_ICONASTERISK SystemAsterisk 1 MB_ICONEXCLAMATION SystemExclamation 2 MB_ICONHAND SystemHand 3 MB_ICONQUESTION SystemQuestion 4 MB_OK SystemDefault 5 Standard beep using the computer speaker (default)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Open
syntax: Constructor Syntax file(char [] filename, dword access, dword mode); // form 1 file(char [] filename, dword access, dword mode, dword fileEncoding); // form 2
description: Description This function opens the filename file. If access = 0, the file is opened for write access; if access = 1 the file is opened for read access. Use mode = 0 to open the file in text mode; Use mode = 1 to open the file in binary mode; Use mode = 2 to append data at the end of the file in text mode; Use mode = 3 to append data at the end of the file in binary mode;
parameters: Parameters filename The name of the file access 0 for write access, 1 for read access mode 0 for text mode, 1 for binary mode, 2 for append in text mode, 3 for append in binary mode. Appending requires write access. fileEncoding Identifier of the Microsoft code page that is used to encode the file.
returnValues: Return Values — (constructor) > 0 on success, 0 on error (method)

name: OpenFileRead
syntax: Function Syntax dword openFileRead (char filename[], dword mode); // form 1 dword openFileRead (char filename[], dword mode, dword fileEncoding); // form 2
description: Description This function opens the filename file for the read access. If mode=0 the file is opened in text mode; if mode=1 the file is opened in binary mode.
parameters: Parameters filename filename mode   fileEncoding Identifier of the Microsoft code page that is used to encode the file.
returnValues: Return Values The return value is the file handle that must be used for read operations. If an error occurs, the return value is 0.

name: OpenFileWrite
syntax: Function Syntax dword openFileWrite (char filename[], dword mode); // form 1 dword openFileWrite (char filename[], dword mode, dword fileEncoding); // form 2
description: Description This function opens the filename file for the write access. If mode=0 writing can be executed in text mode; if mode=1 writing can be executed in binary mode. An already existing file will be overwritten. mode=2 to append data at the end of the file use for text mode. mode=3 to append data at the end of the file for binary mode.
parameters: Parameters filename filename mode   fileEncoding Identifier of the Microsoft code page that is used to encode the file.
returnValues: Return Values The return value is the file handle that must be used for write operations. If an error occurs, the return value is 0.

name: OpenPanel
syntax: Function Syntax void openPanel(char panelName[]);
description: Description Opens a panel.
parameters: Parameters panelName Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Pow
syntax: Function Syntax double _pow(double x, double y);
description: Description Returns x to the power of y.
parameters: Parameters x base y exponent
returnValues: Return Values x to the power of y.

name: PutValueToControl
syntax: Function Syntax Value & String Output void putValueToControl(char panel[], char control[], float val); void putValueToControl(char panel[], char control[], float val, long paragraph); 1 This function requires CANoe 7.5 and higher. void putValueToControl(char panel[], char control[], long val); void putValueToControl(char panel[], char control[], long val, long paragraph); 1 This function requires CANoe 7.5 and higher. void putValueToControl(char panel[], char control[], long val, long paragraph,
description: Description Assigns the value val to the Panel Designer control : CAPL Output View The CAPL Output View is located on the panel with the title panel . Different contents are displayed with the CAPL Output View. In addition to numbers (float and integer) and texts (char[]), in particular different messages (CAN, LIN, ... and J1939 PGNs ) can also be displayed.
parameters: Parameters panel Name of the panel, restricted to 128 characters. control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. val Value to be displayed. paragraph Indicates if the output shall be written to a new line. Parameter Values Description 0 In this case, no new paragraph is set. The text will be append directly to the existing output. This is the default setting. If you want to use the default setting you do not need to set the parameter when calling the function. 1 In this case, the out
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Random
syntax: Function Syntax dword random(dword num);
description: Description Calculates a random number between 0 and num-1.
parameters: Parameters Determines the interval.
returnValues: Return Values Random number between 0 and num-1.

name: RegisterQueueOverflowHandler
syntax: Function Syntax long RegisterQueueOverflowHandler(char aCallbackfunction[], long upperLimit, long lowerLimit);
description: Description Registers a user-defined functions which gets called whenever any events are lost on any hardware queue or on the MainQueue, or if a specific fill level of the MainQueue is reached. Different callback functions can be defined for different sets of queue limits for performance reasons. This user-defined callback functions must be of the form: void QueueOverflowHandler(dword flags, dword load, qword timestamp)
parameters: Parameters aCallbackfunction Name of the user provided callback function upperLimit If the MainQueue load exceeds this percentage limit, the callback is triggered once. lowerLimit If the MainQueue goes below this limit, a callback can be triggered again if the upperLimit is exceeded. User-Defined Callback-Function Parameters flags dword Bit1: events lost on any side (runtime kernel or CANoe ) Bit2: events lost in MainQueue Bit3: events lost in any hardware queue load dword Current load, that tri
returnValues: Return Values —

name: RegisterUserFile
syntax: Function Syntax long RegisterUserFile(char userFilePath[], long isTemporaryRegistration);
description: Description Registers user files dynamically. This function can be used if files names are not known before runtime ( e.g. if they are read out from an XML file or if they contain a counter as part of the filename) to avoid the continuous actualization of the file list in the Options dialog of CANoe .
parameters: Parameters userFilePath Absolute or relative (to the configuration directory) path of the user file's storage location on the user computer. isTemporaryRegistration If set to 0, the registered file will be added permanently to the User Files list of the current CANoe configuration; if set to 1, the registration is only valid for the current measurement run.
returnValues: Return Values 1 File successfully registered -1 File registration failed

name: ReplayResume
syntax: Function Syntax dword ReplayResume (char pName[]);
description: Description Starts the Replay Block with the name pName after it is suspended by ReplaySuspend .
parameters: Parameters pName Name of the Replay Block.
returnValues: Return Values 1 If successful 0 If the Replay Block does not exist or cannot be restarted

name: ReplayStart
syntax: Function Syntax dword ReplayStart (char pName[]);
description: Description Starts the Replay Block with the name pName .
parameters: Parameters pName Name of the Replay Block.
returnValues: Return Values 1 If successful 0 If the Replay Block does not exist or cannot be restarted

name: ReplayState
syntax: Function Syntax dword ReplayState (char pName[]);
description: Description Returns the state of the Replay Block with the name pName .
parameters: Parameters pName Name of the Replay Block
returnValues: Return Values 0 Replay Block is stopped (state: stopped) 1 Execution of the Replay file was started (state: running) 2 Execution of the Replay file was stopped (state: suspended) (dword)-1 when the Replay Block does not exist

name: ReplayStop
syntax: Function Syntax dword ReplayStop (char pName[]);
description: Description Stops the Replay Block with the name pName .
parameters: Parameters pName Name of the Replay Block.
returnValues: Return Values 1 If successful 0 If the Replay Block does not exist or cannot be restarted

name: ReplaySuspend

description: Description Suspends the Replay Block with the name pName .
parameters: Parameters pName Name of the Replay Block
returnValues: Return Values 1 If successful 0 If the Replay Block does not exist or cannot be restarted

name: ResetFilePath
syntax: Function Syntax Syntax; void ResetFilePath(dword mode);
description: Description This function resets the read and/or write path so that files are searched as if setFilePath had not been called.
parameters: Parameters mode 0 Resets path for read functions 1 Resets path for write functions 2 Resets path for both types of functions
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Round
syntax: Function Syntax long _round(double x); // form 1 int64 _round64(double x); // from 2
description: Description Rounds x to the nearest integral number. The rounding method used is symmetric arithmetic rounding.
parameters: Parameters x Number to be rounded
returnValues: Return Values Nearest integral number. For very large numbers, you should use _round64, which returns a 64 bit integer.

name: RunError
syntax: Function Syntax void runError(long err, long);
description: Description Triggers a run error. Outputs the error number to the Write Window indicating the error number and the passed number, and then terminates the measurement.
parameters: Parameters Numbers that are represented in your CANoe as a references for the user. The values under 1000 are reserved for internal purposes. The second parameter is reserved for future expansions.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetBusContext
syntax: Function Syntax dword SetBusContext( dword context);
description: Description Sets the bus context of the CAPL block.
parameters: Parameters context The new bus context to be set.
returnValues: Return Values The bus context that was valid before the call was made is returned.

name: SetClockControlTime
syntax: Function Syntax void SetClockControlTime(char[] panel, char[] control, int hours, int minutes, int seconds); // form 1 void SetClockControlTime(char[] panel, char[] control, int time); // form 2
description: Description Sets the time of the Panel Designer Clock Control.
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetControlBackColor
syntax: Function Syntax void SetControlBackColor(char[] panel, char[] control, long color);
description: Description Sets the background color of: certain panel controls
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetControlColors
syntax: Function Syntax void SetControlColors(char[] panel, char[] control, long backcolor, long textcolor);
description: Description Sets the background and text color of: certain panel controls
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetControlForeColor
syntax: Function Syntax void SetControlForeColor(char[] panel, char[] control, long color);
description: Description Sets the foreground color of: certain panel controls The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer .
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetControlProperty
syntax: Function Syntax void SetControlProperty(char[] panel, char[] control, char[] property, long value); // form 1 void SetControlProperty(char[] panel, char[] control, char[] property, float value); // form 2 void SetControlProperty(char[] panel, char[] control, char[] property, char[] value); // form 3
description: Description Sets a property of a Panel Designer control during runtime. On the next measurement start, the property value selected in the Panel Designer will be reloaded. It is easier to access color properties by makeRGB . Note makeRGB Up to and including CANoe version 12.0 SP2, the function makeRGB returned the blue value in the second byte and the red value in the fourth byte. Functions that received this value as a parameter interpreted this exchange so that the color was displayed correctly
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. "": References all controls on the panel. property Name of the property value Value to be set (long, float or string value)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetControlVisibility
syntax: Function Syntax void SetControlVisibility(char[] panel, char[] control, long visible);
description: Description Sets the visibility of all panel controls.
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetDefaultControlColors
syntax: Function Syntax void SetDefaultControlColors(char[] panel, char[] control);
description: Description Sets back the background and text color of as defined in the Panel Designer : certain panel controls The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer .
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. You can only access the control by its name. In the property dialog of the control it's name is assigned/displayed. "": Access to all controls of a pane
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetDrift
syntax: Function Syntax void setDrift(int drift);
description: Description A constant deviation can be set for the timers of a network node with this function. Inputs for the two values may lie between –10000 and 10000 (corresponds to –100.00% to 100.00%). If the value does not lie within this range, a message is output in the Write Window. Note Setting of a Drift causes any existing Jitter to be reset.
parameters: Parameters drift Integer for the constant deviation.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetFilePath
syntax: Function Syntax void setFilePath (char path[],dword mode);
description: Description This function sets the read and write path to the directory. The path can be given as absolute or relative to the currently active configuration .
parameters: Parameters path The new path. mode 0 Sets path for read functions 1 Sets path for write functions (same as setWritePath ) 2 Sets path for both types of functions
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetJitter
syntax: Function Syntax setJitter(int min, int max);
description: Description The Jitter interval for the timers of a network node can be set with this function. The two values may lie between –10000 and 10000 (corresponds to –100.00% to 100.00%). If one of the two values does not lie within this range, a message is output in the Write Window. Note Setting of a Jitter will cause any existing Drift to be reset. To utilize Jitter and Drift simultaneously please refer to the example.
parameters: Parameters min Integer for the lower interval limit. max Integer for the upper interval limit.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetLogFileName
syntax: Function Syntax setLogFileName(char fileName[]); // form 1 setLogFileName(char strLoggingBlockName[], char fileName[]); // form 2 setLogFileName(char strLoggingBlockName[], char fileName[], int appendIncrementFieldCodes); // form 3
description: Description Sets the name of the logging file. If a valid extension is given it also changes the file type.
parameters: Parameters fileName Note Since Version 8.5 the filename can also contain field codes. If the new filename does not contain any field code, all increment field codes from the old one will be appended (regardless of whether there are other field codes there as well). The auto increment field codes will be added to the filenames as usual. With form 3, this behavior can be suppressed with the parameter appendIncrementFieldCodes . Example field codes: ... setLogFileName( "logFile_M{IncMeasurement}.bl
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetMeanRange
syntax: Function Syntax bool SetMeanRange(char[] panel, char[] control, float lower, float upper);
description: Description Sets the upper and lower value of: certain panel controls
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. "": References all controls on the panel. lower Lower value to be set. upper Upper value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values If the values were set, the return value is 1, else 0.

name: SetMediaFile
syntax: Function Syntax SetMediaFile(panel, control, mediafile);
description: Description Replaces the media file of the Panel Designer control during runtime: Media Player
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. "": References all controls on the panel. mediafile Path and name of the media file.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetMediaStream
syntax: Function Syntax SetMediaStream(panel, control, busType, channel, streamIndex, streamId[]);
description: Description Replaces the media stream at the specified index of the Panel Designer during runtime: Media Stream Control
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. "": References all controls on the panel. busType Bus system of the source media stream (e.g. eCAN, eFlexRay, eEthernet, ...). Currently only bus system
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetMinMax
syntax: Function Syntax bool SetMinMax(char[] panel, char[] control, float min, float max);
description: Description Sets the minimum and maximum value of: certain panel controls
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. "": References all controls on the panel. minimum Minimum value to be set. maximum Maximum value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values If the values were set, the return value is 1, else 0.

name: SetPictureBoxImage
syntax: Function Syntax setPictureBoxImage(panel, control, imagefile);
description: Description Replaces the image of the Panel Designer control during runtime: Picture Box
parameters: Parameters panel Panel name, restricted to 128 characters. The panel is accessed by its individual panel name that is entered in the Panel Designer . "": references all loaded panels If you open a panel the first time, the panel is loaded. If you close the panel, it remains loaded . control Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters. "": References all controls on the panel. iamgefile Path and name of the image file.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetPostTrigger
syntax: Function Syntax setPostTrigger(long PostTriggerTime);
description: Description Sets the posttrigger of the logging. The posttrigger set with this function is valid until the end of the measurement or until the next call of this function. Note : The function stopLogging() does not consider the setting with function ' setPostTrigger ', use the CAPL function trigger() instead.
parameters: Parameters New posttrigger value in milliseconds. If a value of -1 is supplied, the prosttrigger will be set to infinity.
returnValues: Return Values 1 if the posttrigger is set to the given value 0 else

name: SetPreTrigger
syntax: Function Syntax setPreTrigger(long preTriggerTime);
description: Description Sets the pretrigger of the logging. The pretrigger set with this function is valid until the end of the measurement or until the next call of this function. Note : The function startLogging() does not consider the setting with function setPreTrigger , use the CAPL function trigger() instead.
parameters: Parameters New pretrigger value in milliseconds.
returnValues: Return Values 1 if the pretrigger is set to the given value 0 else

name: SetReplayFileName
syntax: Function Syntax dword setReplayFileName(char nameOfReplayBlock[], char fileName[], char fileType[]);
description: Description Replaces the source file of an existing Replay Block. The function call temporarily stops the replay and restarts the Replay Block with the new source file. The function call temporarily stops the replay and restarts the Replay Block with the new source file, if replay is active before file change. The function call will not modify the configuration of the Reply block in the Simulation Setup . The new file will not be stored when saving the configuration. If you change the source fil
parameters: Parameters nameOfReplayBlock Contents of the Replay name field in the replay configuration dialog. fileName The new source file (as absolute or relative path to the configuration file). fileType The source file type (as string): ASC, BLF For all other source file types that are available in the selection dialog of the Replay Block, use BLF as fileType parameter.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetReplayFileNameAndTimeInterval
syntax: Function Syntax dword setReplayFileNameAndTimeInterval(char nameOfReplayBlock[], char fileName[], char fileType[], int64 startTime); // form 1 dword setReplayFileNameAndTimeInterval(char nameOfReplayBlock[], char fileName[], char fileType[], int64 startTime, int64 endTime); // form 2
description: Description Replaces the source file of an existing Replay Block and allows to set a start and stop time of the recorded events. The function call temporarily stops the replay and restarts the Replay Block with the new source file, if replay is active before file change. The function call will not modify the configuration of the Reply Block in the Simulation Setup . The new file will not be stored when saving the configuration. If you change the source file during measurement in the configuratio
parameters: Parameters nameOfReplayBlock Contents of the Replay name field in the replay configuration dialog. fileName The new source file (as absolute or relative path to the configuration file). fileType The source file type (as string): ASC, BLF For all other source file types that are available in the selection dialog of the CANoe Replay Block, use BLF as fileType parameter. startTime Start time of the recorded events in the log file in nano seconds. Use ConvertTimestampToNS to convert a timestamp give
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Setstartdelay
syntax: Function Syntax void setStartdelay(int delay)
description: Description Sets the value of the Start Delay for this network node. This function can only be called in the preStart event procedure. Afterwards the value of the Start Delay cannot be changed any more.
parameters: Parameters delayText Integer for the Start Delay ms. This value may lie between 0 and 99999. If the value is greater or less than the limits of this range a warning appears in the Write Window.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetTimer
syntax: Function Syntax void setTimer(msTimer t, long duration); // form 1 void setTimer(timer t, long duration); // form 2 void setTimer(timer t, long durationSec, long durationNanoSec); // form 3
description: Description Sets a timer.
parameters: Parameters Timer or msTimer variable and an expression which specifies the duration of the timer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetTimerAccuracyMonitoringActive
syntax: Function Syntax long SetTimerAccuracyMonitoringActive(msTimer timer, dword active);
description: Description With this function you can turn on an accuracy monitoring for a timer. If the monitoring is active, the real (wallclock) time which passes between the start of the timer and its execution is measured and can be read out with GetLastTimerAccuracyNS . Note A certain difference between realtime and simulation time is normal and not a problem in most cases. However, if you have higher realtime requirements, you may detect problems early by monitoring the accuracy of the important timers.
parameters: Parameters timer The timer which shall be monitored. active Whether monitoring shall be active (1) or inactive (0).
returnValues: Return Values 0 Monitoring is now not active for the timer. 1 Monitoring is now active for the timer. -1 Monitoring cannot be configured because of wrong execution mode. It is only possible to use the monitoring in real mode, not in simulated mode or in offline mode.

name: SetTimerCyclic
syntax: Function Syntax void setTimerCyclic(msTimer t, long firstDuration, long period); // form 1 void setTimerCyclic(msTimer t, long period); // form 2 void setTimerCyclic(timer t, int64 periodInNs); // form 3
description: Description Sets a cyclical timer. With form 2, firstDuration is implicitly the same as period, i.e. the timer runs precisely according to period the first time.
parameters: Parameters t The timer to be set. firstDuration Timer duration in milliseconds for the first run of the timer. period Timer duration in milliseconds for the following cycles. periodInNs Timer duration in nanoseconds for the following cycles.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetVideoOfflineLoggerCam
syntax: Function Syntax SetVideoOfflineLoggerCam(char windowName[], long camType, [char filePrefix[]]);
description: Description Sets the offline mode logger cam (type and prefix) for a CANoe Video Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Video Window. camType The type of logger cam: 0|cCamlog2 1|cHostCam For details see the Image Files Properties in the CANoe Video Configuration dialog. filePrefix The corresponding prefix of the image files generated by the cam. For cCamlog2 this can be omitted. For details see the Image Files Properties in the CANoe Video Configuration dialog.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetVideoOfflineSource
syntax: Function Syntax SetVideoOfflineSource(char windowName[], long sourceType, char sourcePath[]);
description: Description Sets the offline source (type and path) for a CANoe Video Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Video Window. sourceType The type of offline source: 0|cNoVideoInput 1|cVideoFile 2|cImageFolder For details see the Offline Source in the CANoe Video Configuration dialog. sourcePath The corresponding source path: For cNoVideoInput the path will be ignored. For cVideoFile this must be a existing video file. For cImageFolder this must be a existing folder.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetVideoRecordFile
syntax: Function Syntax SetVideoRecordFile(char windowName[], char recordFile[]);
description: Description Sets the record file for a CANoe Video Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Video Window. recordFile The path to the record file. This parameter can be an absolute path. All non-existing directories of the path will be created. a relative path. In that case the record file will be placed in the path relative to the configuration file. All non-existing directories of the path will be created. a single filename. In that case the record file will be placed in the directory of the current configuration file. The directories of the path
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetVideoTriggerTimes
syntax: Function Syntax SetVideoTriggerTimes(char windowName[], dword preTriggerTime, dword postTriggerTime);
description: Description Sets the trigger times for a CANoe Video Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Video Window. preTriggerTime The pre trigger time (in ms). For details see the pre trigger time in the CANoe Video Configuration dialog. postTriggerTime The pre trigger time (in ms). For details see the pre trigger time in the CANoe Video Configuration dialog.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetWriteDbgLevel
syntax: Function Syntax void setWriteDbgLevel (unsigned int priority);
description: Description This function sets the priority level for the writeDbgLevel CAPL function. The output priority must be set for every network node.
parameters: Parameters priority Priority of current CAPL node for output to the Write Window. Rays for priority: 0 to 15 0 Only write output with a priority of 0 are shown in the Write Window. 5 Write output with a priority ranging from 0 to 5 are shown. 15 All outputs are shown.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SetWritePath
syntax: Function Syntax void setWritePath (char relativeOrAbsolutePath[]);
description: Description This function sets the write path for the functions openFileWrite writeProfileString , writeProfileInt and writeProfileFloat . The path can be given as absolute or relative to the currently active configuration .
parameters: Parameters The file path as a string.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Sin
syntax: Function Syntax double sin(double x);
description: Description Calculates sine of x.
parameters: Parameters Value in radians whose sine is to be calculated.
returnValues: Return Values Sine of x.

name: SnPrintf
syntax: Function Syntax long snprintf(char dest[], long len, char format[], ...);
description: Description This function corresponds to the C function sprintf . Supplementally, the parameter len indicates the maximum length of the array dest . The format string has the same meaning as with the function write and is described there. Note CAPL supports a function call with maximum 64 parameters.
parameters: Parameters dest Character buffer to print to. len Maximum number of characters printed to buffer including terminating '\0'. Must be at most the size of the buffer. format Formatted string printed to buffer.
returnValues: Return Values The number of characters written.

name: Sqrt
syntax: Function Syntax double sqrt(double x);
description: Description Calculates the square root.
parameters: Parameters Value whose square root is to be calculated.
returnValues: Return Values Square root of x.

name: StandaloneConfigOpen
syntax: Function Syntax dword StandaloneConfigOpen(CHAR rtcfgFileName[], dword stopCurrentMeasurement, dword startNewMeasurement, dword returnToActiveConfig)
description: Description Opens the RTCFG file with the given name as standalone configuration. The given standalone configuration file must have been downloaded to the device before. The command is only allowed while standalone mode is activated. If standalone measurement is currently running the command is deferred until end of measurement (unless overwritten by another subsequent open command). If standalone measurement is not running but standalone mode is activated the file is loaded but measurement is n
parameters: Parameters rtcfgFileName Name of the standalone configuration file (without path) to be set as default file. stopCurrentMeasurement If this parameter is 1 measurement is stopped immediately (but still waiting for deferred stop clients). If the parameter value is 0 the request to load another configuration is queued until end of measurement. startNewMeasurement If this parameter is set to 1 measurement will start immediately after the new configuration has been loaded. returnToActiveConfig If thi
returnValues: Return Values The function returns an error code with 0 representing successful operation.

name: StandaloneConfigSetDefault
syntax: Function Syntax dword StandaloneConfigSetDefault(CHAR rtcfgFileName[]);
description: Description Changes the default configuration file, i.e. the standalone configuration to be loaded when standalone mode is activated or the RT kernel is started after reboot. Thus, it results in a persistent change on the VN8900 . The given standalone configuration file must have been downloaded to the device before. The command can be used while standalone mode is activated or deactivated. Basic behavior corresponds to changing the default standalone configuration file (see Home ribbon tab |Gro
parameters: Parameters rtcfgFileName Name of the standalone configuration file (without path) to be set as default configuration file.
returnValues: Return Values The function returns an error code with 0 representing successful operation.

name: StandaloneGetCurrentConfig
syntax: Function Syntax dword StandaloneGetCurrentConfig(char cfgNameBuffer[], dword cfgNameBufferLength);
description: Description Returns the filename of the currently loaded configuration in standalone mode. It includes the filename extension, but no path information, for example CANSystemDemo.rtcfg . The function can be used in standalone mode . It is also useful to determine whether standalone mode is active or not. Note Difference between StandaloneGetCurrentConfig and getConfigurationName There are two kinds of configurations in standalone mode: the standalone configuration (with filename extension RTCFG)
parameters: Parameters cfgNameBuffer Space for the returned name. cfgNameBufferLength Length of the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful operation 1 Buffer too small 2 Not in standalone mode

name: StandaloneGetDefaultConfig
syntax: Function Syntax dword StandaloneGetDefaultConfig(char cfgNameBuffer[], dword cfgNameBufferLength);
description: Description Gets the name of the default configuration in standalone mode. This is the standalone configuration, which gets loaded when standalone mode is activated or the RT kernel is started after reboot. The command can be used while standalone mode is running. It is also useful to determine whether standalone mode is running or not. A returned configuration name consists of the filename and the extension, for example CANSystemDemo.rtcfg .
parameters: Parameters cfgNameBuffer Space for the returned name. cfgNameBufferLength Length of the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful operation 1 Buffer too small 2 Not in standalone mode

name: StartDOReplayFile
syntax: Function Syntax dword StartDOReplayFile (char filename[]); // form 1 dword StartDOReplayFile (char filename[], dword role); // form 2 dword StartDOReplayFile (char filename[], dword role, dword ignoreSubscriptions, dword ignoreAnnouncements); // form 3 dword StartDOReplayFile (char filename[], dword role, dword ignoreSubscriptions, dword ignoreAnnouncements, dword enforceConnection); // form 4
description: Description Starts replaying a file with distributed objects named fileName .
parameters: Parameters filename Filename of the file to be replayed. role Role to be replayed. 0x0 Provider + Consumer (default) 0x1 Provider 0x2 Consumer ignoreSubscriptions If set, subscriptions are ignored. 0x0 Send subscriptions (default) 0x1 Subscriptions are ignored ignoreAnnouncements If set, announcements are ignored. 0x0 Send announcements (default) 0x1 Announcements are ignored enforceConnection Enables enforcing connections between participants via the first virtual network. 0x0 Uses the settings
returnValues: Return Values The returned handle is required to stop the replay file .

name: StartLogging
syntax: Function Syntax void startLogging(); // from 1 void startLogging(char strLoggingBlockName[]); // from 2 void startLogging(char strLoggingBlockName[], long preTriggerTime); // from 3
description: Description Starts all Logging Blocks immediately bypassing all logging trigger settings. Starts a Logging Block with name strLoggingBlockName immediately bypassing all logging trigger settings. Starts a Logging Block with name strLoggingBlockName bypassing all logging trigger settings. Function also sets a pre-trigger time to a value of the preTriggerTime .
parameters: Parameters strLoggingBlockName Name of the Logging Block. Note If you use the function in standalone mode and if you activated logging and Trigger Block in the standalone mode configuration settings you can address the (single) Logging Block available in this case by passing an empty string as parameter. preTriggerTime Pre-trigger time interval in ms. Note While using the preTriggerTime please pay attention to the fact, that the buffer size in the trigger configuration dialog of the Logging Bloc
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StartMacroFile
syntax: Function Syntax dword StartMacroFile(char fileName[]);
description: Description Starts playing the macro with the fileName filename.
parameters: Parameters fileName The macro file.
returnValues: Return Values The returned handle is required to stop the macro playback .

name: StartReplayFile
syntax: Function Syntax dword StartReplayFile(char fileName[]);
description: Description Starts playing the replay file with the name fileName . This function replays events of the following types: CAN messages CAN Error Frames CAN overload frames (not sent, only visible in Measurement Setup) J1708 messages Ethernet packets WLAN packets AFDX packets Diagnostic requests GPS events Signal values System variable updates Environment variable updates In particular, replay of LIN, FlexRay events using this function is not possible. All events are replayed on the same channel a
parameters: Parameters fileName Replay file.
returnValues: Return Values The returned handle is required to stop the replay file .

name: StartValuesUpdateList
syntax: Function Syntax void startValuesUpdateList(); // form 1 void startValuesUpdateList(char groupName[]); // form 2 void startValuesUpdateList(char groupName[], long onlyActiveStartValues); // form 3
description: Description Sets the start values of variables/signals in the CANoe Start Value Window to the currently measured values.
parameters: Parameters groupName Name of a group containing start values. If groupName is defined, then only the active start values contained in this group get the currently measured values. If groupName is not defined or is equal to "", then only the active start values of all groups get the currently measured values. onlyActiveStartValues Determines if active start values get the currently measured values or not. If missing, then only the active start values are considered. 1 : Only the active start valu
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StartValuesUpdateSymbols
syntax: Function Syntax void startValuesUpdateSymbols(char groupName[]);
description: Description Sets the variables/signals contained in a specific group to their individual start values in the CANoe Start Value Window .
parameters: Parameters groupName Name of a group containing variables/signals that are set to their individual start values. If groupName is equal to "", then all variables/signals contained in the Start Values Window are set to their individual start values.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StartVideoRecording
syntax: Function Syntax StartVideoRecording(char windowName[]);
description: Description Starts/resumes the recording for a CANoe Video Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Video Window.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Stop
syntax: Function Syntax void stop();
description: Description Programmed interrupt of the ongoing measurement. If the function is called in an event procedure, the event procedure will always be executed completely before the measurement is stopped. In offline mode this function interrupts but does not end the measurement. In offline mode the measurement can only be ended with <ESC>.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StopLogging
syntax: Function Syntax void stopLogging(); // from 1 void stopLogging(char strLoggingBlockName[]); // from 2 void stopLogging(char strLoggingBlockName[], long postTriggerTime); // from 3
description: Description Stops all Logging Blocks immediately bypassing all logging trigger settings. Stops a Logging Block with name strLoggingBlockName immediately bypassing all logging trigger settings. Stops a Logging Block with name strLoggingBlockName bypassing all logging trigger settings. Functions also sets a post-trigger time to a value of the postTriggerTime .
parameters: Parameters strLoggingBlockName Name of the Logging Block. Note If you use the function in standalone mode and if you activated logging and Trigger Block in the standalone mode configuration settings you can address the (single) Logging Block available in this case by passing an empty string as parameter. postTriggerTime Post-trigger time interval in ms.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StopMacroFile
syntax: Function Syntax void StopMacroFile(dword handle);
description: Description Stops the macro from playing with the handle handle.
parameters: Parameters handle Handle of the started macro. The handle is returned from the StartMacroFile function when starting the macro.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StopReplayFile
syntax: Function Syntax void StopReplayFile(dword handle);
description: Description Stops the replay file from playing with the handle handle.
parameters: Parameters handle Handle of the started replay file. The handle is returned from the StartReplayFile or StartDOReplayFile function when starting the reply file.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StopVideoRecording
syntax: Function Syntax StopVideoRecording(char windowName[]);
description: Description Stops/suspends the recording for a CANoe Video Window .
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Video Window.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StringToBytes
syntax: Function Syntax long StringToBytes(char input[], char format[], char sep[], byte output[]); // form 1 long StringToBytes(char input[], dword inputStartIndex, char format[], char sep[], byte output[]); // form 2
description: Description Parses a byte array from a formatted string. It is the opposite of the intrinsic BytesToString and can parse the output of that function. The input string must contain a series of numbers formatted with the format specifier and optional separators in between. Given that the output is read into a byte array, no number can be larger than 255.
parameters: Parameters input The input string from which to parse the byte array. It must be formatted according to the arguments format and sep in order for the parsing to succeed. inputStartIndex The index at which to start parsing the input. All characters before the start index are ignored. format The printf format specifier with which the input is formatted. It must contain the formatting for a single number, i.e. exactly one % must be present in the format. sep The separator that separates individual
returnValues: Return Values Returns a number that indicates the success of the function or the number of bytes parsed and written into the output. >=0 Indicates a successful function execution and is the number of bytes parsed and written into the output. -1 The output was not large enough to hold the result. -2 The input does not match the format string and separator, a number in the input did not fit into a byte, or the format string was malformed.

name: StrLen
syntax: Function Syntax long strlen(char s[]);
description: Description The functional result is the length of the string s in bytes . Note A character may need several bytes depending on the string encoding, e.g. Japanese characters in Windows ANSI (932) encoding or any special characters in UTF-8 encoding. In that case, you may want to use mbstrlen instead.
parameters: Parameters string
returnValues: Return Values Length of the string in bytes.

name: StrMatchRegex
syntax: Function Syntax long str_match_regex(char s[], char pattern[]);
description: Description Checks whether a string completely matches a regular expression pattern.
parameters: Parameters s String to be checked. pattern Regular expression against which the string is matched. For the regular expression, the same syntax is used as in the Perl programming language.
returnValues: Return Values 1 if the string matches the pattern, 0 if it doesn't match the pattern.

name: StrnCat
syntax: Function Syntax void strncat(char dest[], char src[], long len);
description: Description This function appends src to dest . len indicates the maximum length of the composite string. The function ensures that there is a terminating "\0". Thus, a maximum of len - strlen(dest) - 1 characters are copied. Note Unlike the strncat C-function, rather than the number of characters to be appended, len indicates the maximum length of the composite string, including the terminating "\0".
parameters: Parameters dest Target string to which characters are appended. src Appended string. len Maximum length of composite string including terminating '\0'.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StrnCmp
syntax: Function Syntax long strncmp(char s1[], char s2[], long len); // form 1 long strncmp(char s1[], char s2[], long s2offset, long len); // form 2
description: Description This function compares s1 with s2 for a maximum of len bytes. If they are identical the functional result is 0. If s1 is less than s2 the result is -1, else +1. Form 2 starts the comparison in s2 at the specified offset. Note A character may need several bytes depending on the string encoding, e.g. Japanese characters in Windows ANSI (932) encoding or any special characters in UTF-8 encoding. In that case, you should use mbstrncmp instead.
parameters: Parameters s1 First string s2 Second string s2offset Offset in s2 in bytes len Maximum number of bytes to compare
returnValues: Return Values -1 if s1 is less than s2 1 if s2 is less than s1 0 if the strings are equal

name: StrnCmpOff
syntax: Function Syntax long strncmp_off(char s1[], long s1offset, char s2[], long s2offset, long len);
description: Description This function compares s1 with s2 for a maximum of len bytes. If they are identical the functional result is 0. If s1 is less than s2 the result is -1, else +1. Comparison starts in s1 at s1offset and in s2 at s2offset . Note A character may need several bytes depending on the string encoding, e.g. Japanese characters in Windows ANSI (932) encoding or any special characters in UTF-8 encoding. In that case, you should use mbstrncmp_off instead.
parameters: Parameters s1 First string s2 Second string s1offset Offset in s1 in bytes s2offset Offset in s2 in bytes len Maximum number of bytes to compare
returnValues: Return Values -1 if s1 is less than s2 1 if s2 is less than s1 0 if the strings are equal

name: StrnCpy
syntax: Function Syntax void strncpy(char dest[], char src[], long max);
description: Description This function copies src to dest . max indicates the size of dest in bytes. The function ensures that there is a terminating '\0'. Thus, a maximum of max-1 bytes are copied. Note A character may need several bytes depending on the string encoding, e.g. Japanese characters in Windows ANSI (932) encoding or any special characters in UTF-8 encoding. In that case, you should use mbstrncpy instead.
parameters: Parameters dest Destination buffer src Source string max Is used to determine the maximum number of copied bytes. Must not be larger than the size of dest . A maximum of max-1 bytes are copied. If src is shorter than that, bytes are only copied until a terminating '\0' is encountered.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StrnCpyOff
syntax: Function Syntax void strncpy_off(char dest[], long destOffset, char src[], long max);
description: Description This function copies src to dest . max indicates the size of dest in bytes. The function ensures that there is a terminating '\0'. Thus, a maximum of max-1-destOffset bytes are copied. Bytes are overwritten in dest starting at destOffset . Note A character may need several bytes depending on the string encoding, e.g. Japanese characters in Windows ANSI (932) encoding or any special characters in UTF-8 encoding. In that case, you should use mbstrncpy_off instead.
parameters: Parameters dest Destination buffer destOffset Offset in destination buffer src Source string max Is used to determine the maximum number of copied bytes. Must not be larger than the size of dest . A maximum of max-1-destOffset bytes are copied. If src is shorter than that, bytes are only copied until a terminating '\0' is encountered.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: StrReplace
syntax: Function Syntax long str_replace(char s[], char searched[], char replacement[]); // form 1 long str_replace(char s[], long startoffset, char replacement[], long length); // form 2
description: Description Form 1: Replaces all occurrences of a text in a string with another string. Form 2: Replaces a part of a string with another string.
parameters: Parameters s String to be modified. searched Text which shall be replaced. startoffset Offset at which to start replacing characters. replacement Text which replaces the original characters. length Maximum number of characters to replace.
returnValues: Return Values 1 if successful, 0 if the resulting string would be too long for the buffer s .

name: StrReplaceRegex
syntax: Function Syntax long str_replace_regex(char s[], char pattern[], char replacement[]);
description: Description Replaces all occurrences of pattern in a string with another string.
parameters: Parameters s String to be modified. pattern Regular expression which determines the parts in s which shall be replaced. For the regular expression, the same syntax is used as in the Perl programming language. replacement Replacement for the parts which match the pattern.
returnValues: Return Values 1 if successful, 0 if the resulting string would be too long for the buffer s .

name: StrStr
syntax: Function Syntax long strstr(char s1[], char s2[]); long strstr_off(char s1[], long offset, char s2[]);
description: Description Searches in s1 for s2 . Note A character may need several bytes depending on the string encoding, e.g. Japanese characters in Windows ANSI (932) encoding or any special characters in UTF-8 encoding. In that case, you should use mbstrstr / mbstrstr_off instead.
parameters: Parameters s1 First string offset Offset in s1 in bytes at which the search shall be started s2 Second string
returnValues: Return Values First position in bytes of s2 in s1 , or -1 if s2 is not found in s1 .

name: StrstrRegex
syntax: Function Syntax long strstr_regex(char s[], char pattern[]); long strstr_regex_off(char s[], long offset, char pattern[]);
description: Description Searches for a regular expression pattern in a string.
parameters: Parameters s String to be searched. offset Offset in s at which the search shall be started. pattern Regular expression which is searched. For the regular expression, the same syntax is used as in the Perl programming language.
returnValues: Return Values The position in s where the pattern was found, or -1 if it wasn't found.

name: Strtod
syntax: Function Syntax int strtod(char s[], double& result); // from 1 int strtod(char s[], dword startIndex, double& result); // form 2
description: Description Converts the string s to a floating point number. The string must have the format [whitespace][sign][digits][.digits][{d|D|e|E}[sign]digits] . Whitespace : may consist of space and tab characters, which are ignored sign : is either plus (+) or minus (-) digits : are one or more decimal digits. If no digits appear before the radix character, at least one must appear after the radix character. The decimal digits can be followed by an exponent, which consists of an introductory letter (
parameters: Parameters s String to be converted. result Contains the converted value after the call. The value is 0 if the string can't be converted to a number. It is the largest possible positive/negative number in case of overflow. startIndex Position in s where the conversion shall begin.
returnValues: Return Values -2 If s is empty or startIndex is larger than strlen(s) . -1 An overflow occurs. Otherwise, returns the index of the first character after the number.

name: Strtol
syntax: Function Syntax int strtol(char s[], long& result); // form 1 int strtol(char s[], dword startIndex, long& result); // from 2
description: Description Converts the string s to a 32bit integer. The number base is hexadecimal if the string starts with '0x' octal if it starts with '0' decimal otherwise Whitespace (spaces or tabs) at the start of the string is ignored.
parameters: Parameters s String to be converted. result Contains the converted value after the call. The value is 0 if the string can't be converted to a number. It is the largest possible positive/negative number in case of overflow. startIndex Position in s where the conversion shall begin.
returnValues: Return Values -2 If s is empty or startIndex is larger than strlen(s) . -1 An overflow occurs. Otherwise, returns the index of the first character after the number.

name: Strtoll
syntax: Function Syntax int strtoll(char s[], int64& result); // from 1 int strtoll(char s[], dword startIndex, int64& result); // from 2
description: Description Converts the string s to a 64bit integer. The number base is hexadecimal if the string starts with '0x' octal if it starts with '0' decimal otherwise Whitespace (spaces or tabs) at the start of the string is ignored.
parameters: Parameters s String to be converted. result Contains the converted value after the call. The value is 0 if the string can't be converted to a number. It is the largest possible positive/negative number in case of overflow. startIndex Position in s where the conversion shall begin.
returnValues: Return Values -2 If s is empty or startIndex is larger than strlen(s) . -1 An overflow occurs. Otherwise, returns the index of the first character after the number.

name: Strtoul
syntax: Function Syntax int strtoul(char s[], dword& result); // from 1 int strtoul(char s[], dword startIndex, dword& result); // from 2
description: Description Converts the string s to an unsigned 32bit integer. The number base is hexadecimal if the string starts with '0x' octal if it starts with '0' decimal otherwise Whitespace (spaces or tabs) at the start of the string is ignored.
parameters: Parameters s String to be converted. result Contains the converted value after the call. The value is 0 if the string can't be converted to a number. It is the largest possible positive/negative number in case of overflow. startIndex Position in s where the conversion shall begin.
returnValues: Return Values -2 If s is empty or startIndex is larger than strlen(s) . -1 An overflow occurs. Otherwise, returns the index of the first character after the number.

name: Strtoull
syntax: Function Syntax int strtoull(char s[], qword& result); // from 1 int strtoull(char s[], dword startIndex, qword& result); // from 2
description: Description Converts the string s to an unsigned 64bit integer. The number base is hexadecimal if the string starts with '0x' octal if it starts with '0' decimal otherwise Whitespace (spaces or tabs) at the start of the string is ignored.
parameters: Parameters s String to be converted. result Contains the converted value after the call. The value is 0 if the string can't be converted to a number. It is the largest possible positive/negative number in case of overflow. startIndex Position in s where the conversion shall begin.
returnValues: Return Values -2 If s is empty or startIndex is larger than strlen(s) . -1 An overflow occurs. Otherwise, returns the index of the first character after the number.

name: SubStrCpy
syntax: Function Syntax void substr_cpy(char dest[], char src[], long srcStart, long len, long max);
description: Description This function copies a substring of src to dest . max indicates the size of dest in bytes. The function ensures that there is a terminating '\0'. Thus, a maximum of max-1 bytes are copied. Note A character may need several bytes depending on the string encoding, e.g. Japanese characters in Windows ANSI (932) encoding or any special characters in UTF-8 encoding. In that case, you should use mbsubstr_cpy instead.
parameters: Parameters dest Destination buffer src Source string srcStart Start index in bytes in src of substring len Length of the substring in bytes, or -1 to copy the string until the end max Size of dest in bytes
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SubStrCpyOff
syntax: Function Syntax void substr_cpy_off(char dest[], long destOffset, char src[], long srcStart, long len, long max);
description: Description This function copies a substring of src to dest . max indicates the the size of dest in bytes. The function ensures that there is a terminating '\0'. Thus, a maximum of max-1-destOffset bytes are copied. Note A character may need several bytes depending on the string encoding, e.g. Japanese characters in Windows ANSI (932) encoding or any special characters in UTF-8 encoding. In that case, you should use mbsubstr_cpy_off instead.
parameters: Parameters dest Destination buffer destOffset Offset in bytes in destination buffer src Source string srcStart Start index in bytes in src of substring len Length of the substring in bytes, or -1 to copy the string until the end max Size of dest in bytes
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SwapWord
syntax: Function Syntax word swapWord(word x); // form 1 int swapInt(int x); // form 2 dword swapDWord(dword x); // form 3 long swapLong(long x); // form 4 int64 swapInt64(int64 x); // form 5 qword swapQWord(qword x); // form 6
description: Description Swaps bytes of parameters. CAPL arithmetics follows the "little-endian-format" (Intel). The swap-functions serve for swapping bytes for the transition to and from the "big-endian-format" (Motorola).
parameters: Parameters Value whose bytes are to be swapped.
returnValues: Return Values Value with swapped bytes.

name: SymbolMappingSetDynamicMappingSet
syntax: Function Syntax void SymbolMappingSetDynamicMappingSet(char mappingSetName[]);
description: Description Changes the dynamic mapping set that will be used for mapping in the CANoe Symbol Mapping dialog. Only the mapping relations contained in the given dynamic mapping set and in the static mapping set are considered.
parameters: Parameters mappingSetName Name of a dynamic set which shall be activated.
returnValues: Return Values -1 if no mapping set with the given name is found 0 if the mapping set with the given name is found and its contained mapping relations are mapped

name: SysExecSysExecCmd
syntax: Function Syntax long sysExec(char cmd[], char params[]); // form 1 long sysExec(char cmd[], char params[], char directory[]); // form 2 void sysExec(char cmd[], char params[], char directory[], dword timeoutMs, SysExecCallbackFunction callback); // form 3   long sysExecCmd(char cmd[], char params[]); // from 1 long sysExecCmd(char cmd[], char params[], char directory[]); // form 2
description: Description Executes an external command. Does not wait until the command has completed its execution. sysExec must be given an executable; sysExecCmd calls cmd.EXE /K with the first parameter, which opens a command window where the command is executed as if it was entered directly. Form 3 takes an additional callback that is executed once the process terminates and is given the exit code of the process. The callback is only executed if the process terminates while the measurement is still runni
parameters: Parameters cmd The command to be executed. Either the full absolute path or a path relative to the current working directory must be given or the command must be in the system path. params Parameters to the command. A parameter which contains spaces must be enclosed in " ". directory Working directory for the command. Either an absolute path or a path relative to the current working directory must be given. timeoutMs The maximum time to wait for the process termination in milliseconds. After the
returnValues: Return Values Form 1, 2 1 if the command was successfully started, else 0. Form 3 Nothing is returned, the success indicator is given as the first argument of the callback. Note Note that this function does not wait for the process to finish. Thus, forms 1 and 2 do not return the return code of the process itself.

name: SysExit
syntax: Function Syntax void sysExit();
description: Description Exits the application from within a CAPL program.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SysMinimize
syntax: Function Syntax void sysMinimize();
description: Description The application window of will be minimized or restored. The first call of the function minimizes the window, afterwards the window will be restored to normal size and minimized alternaltly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TimeDiff
syntax: Function Syntax long timeDiff(message m1, NOW); // from 1 long timeDiff(message m1, message m2); // from 2
description: Description Time difference between messages or between a message and the current time in ms (msg2 - msg1 or now - msg1). Starting with CANalyzer 2.xx this difference can be calculated directly (Units of 10 microseconds).
parameters: Parameters Variable of type message Variable of type now
returnValues: Return Values Time difference in ms.

name: TimeNow
syntax: Function Syntax dword timeNow();
description: Description Supplies the current simulation time (maximum time: .2^32 * 10 microseconds = 11 hours, 55 minutes, 49 seconds, 672 milliseconds, 96 microseconds) The simulation time can be correlated with the hardware results of the network interface. The resolution of this time is dependent upon the hardware used (usually a millisecond or better). Depending on the hardware configuration, the simulation time will be the same as the message time calculated by the network interface. or the message ti
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Simulation time in 10 microseconds.

name: TimeNowFloat
syntax: Function Syntax float timeNowFloat();
description: Description Supplies the current simulation time. The simulation time can be correlated with the hardware results of the network interface. The resolution of this time is dependent upon the hardware used (usually a millisecond or better). Depending on the hardware configuration, the simulation time will be the same as the message time calculated by the network interface. or the message times will have a higher accuracy
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Simulation time in 10 microseconds.

name: TimeNowNS
syntax: Function Syntax float TimeNowNS(); int64 timeNowInt64();
description: Description Supplies the current simulation time. The simulation time can be correlated with the hardware results of the network interface. The resolution of this time is dependent upon the hardware used (usually a millisecond or better). Depending on the hardware configuration, the simulation time will be the same as the message time calculated by the network interface. or the message times will have a higher accuracy
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Simulation time in nanoseconds.

name: TimeToElapse
syntax: Function Syntax long timeToElapse(timer t); // from 1 long timeToElapse(mstimer t); // from 2
description: Description Returns a value indicating how much more time will elapse before an on timer event procedure is called. For form 1, the time value is returned in seconds; for form 2, the time value is returned in milliseconds. If the timer is not active, -1 is returned. This is also the case in the on timer event procedure itself.
parameters: Parameters timer or mstimer variable
returnValues: Return Values Time to go until the timer elapses and the event procedure is called.

name: TimeToString
syntax: Function Syntax long TimeToString(int64 timeNS, char buffer[]);
description: Description Converts a timestamp or a duration (given in nanoseconds) into a string in the format days:hours:minutes:seconds,microseconds . Hours, minutes and seconds are always with two digits; microseconds are with six digits so they show the fraction of the second.
parameters: Parameters timeNS Time/duration in nanoseconds. This could be a timestamp of a frame, the time given by timeNowInt64 , or a time measured with the CAPLProfiler class . buffer Receives the string. Must be at least 18 characters long (more if the duration is >= 10 days).
returnValues: Return Values 0 Success -1 Error, e.g. buffer too small.

name: ToLower
syntax: Function Syntax char toLower(char c); // form 1 void toLower(char dest[], char source[], dword bufferSize); // form 2
description: Description Transforms a character or string to lower case. Only characters a-z and A-Z are supported.
parameters: Parameters c Character to be transformed. source String to be transformed. dest Destination buffer for the transformed string. bufferSize Size of the destination buffer.
returnValues: Return Values Lower case of the character (form 1).

name: ToUpper
syntax: Function Syntax char toUpper(char c); // form 1 void toUpper(char dest[], char source[], dword bufferSize); // form 2
description: Description Transforms a character or string to upper case. Only characters a-z and A-Z are supported.
parameters: Parameters c Character to be transformed. source String to be transformed. dest Destination buffer for the transformed string. bufferSize Size of the destination buffer.
returnValues: Return Values Upper case of the character (form 1).

name: TraceSetEventColors
syntax: Function Syntax long traceSetEventColors(message msg, dword font, dword bkgnd); // form 1 long traceSetEventColors(errorFrame msg, dword font, dword bkgnd) // form 2 long traceSetEventColors(pg msg, dword font, dword bkgnd) // form 3 long traceSetEventColors(linFrame msg, dword font, dword bkgnd) // form 4 long traceSetEventColors(frFrame msg, dword font, dword bkgnd) // form 5 long traceSetEventColors(frError msg, dword font, dword bkgnd) // form 6 long traceSetEventColors(FrFrameError msg, dwo
description: Description Sets the text and background color for displaying msg in the Trace Window. The makeRGB function can be used for the colors. Note This function can only be used in Measurement Setup and is only applied to the Trace Window. The setting of colors with this function has a higher priority than the color settings for events in the Trace Window. Note makeRGB Up to and including CANoe version 12.0 SP2, the function makeRGB returned the blue value in the second byte and the red value in the f
parameters: Parameters msg Variable type: message, errorFrame, pg, linFrame, frFrame, frError, frameError, nullFrame, pdu, pocState, slot, symbol, startCycle font Font color (RGB value) bkgnd Background color (RGB value)
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Invalid RGB value of a color.

name: TraceWindowClear
syntax: Function Syntax void traceWindowClear(char[] windowName);
description: Description Clears the contents of the Trace Window.
parameters: Parameters windowName The name of the Trace Window.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: Trigger
syntax: Function Syntax void trigger();
description: Description Sends a trigger event to all CANoe Logging or Trigger Blocks. For a Logging Block, the trigger event starts and stops logging, depending on the trigger mode (single or toggle trigger) the trigger conditions for toggle on and toggle off set in the Trigger Configuration dialog of this block. For a trigger block, the trigger event starts and stops the data stream (like a filter) for the whole analysis branch or a single analysis window, depending also on trigger mode and trigger conditi
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TriggerEx
syntax: Function Syntax void triggerEx(char name[]);
description: Description Sends a trigger event to a CANoe Logging or Trigger Block specified by name. For a Logging Block, the trigger event starts and stops logging, depending on the trigger mode (single or toggle trigger) the trigger conditions for toggle on and toggle off set in the Trigger Configuration dialog of this block. For a Trigger Block, the trigger event starts and stops the data stream (like a filter) for the whole analysis branch or a single analysis window, depending also on trigger mode and
