parameters: Parameters sysVarNamespace The namespace of the channel or ECU.
returnValues: Return Values Returns a SensorErrorCode .

name: SensorReverseBits
syntax: Function Syntax SensorErrorCode sensorReverseBits(byte buffer[], dword bufferByteCount, dword bitCount);
description: Description Reverses the given number of bits in the given array.
parameters: Parameters buffer The array in which the bits shall be reversed. bufferByteCount The length of the array in bytes. bitCount The number of bits in the array that shall be reversed.
returnValues: Return Values Returns a SensorErrorCode .

name: SensorSetSupplyVoltage
syntax: Function Syntax SensorErrorCode sensorSetSupplyVoltage(char[] sysVarNamespace, double SupplyVoltage);
description: Description Sets the supply voltage on the specified SENT/PSI5 channel.

name: SensorSetSyncPulseTolerance
syntax: Function Syntax SensorErrorCode sensorSetSyncPulseTolerance(char[] sysVarNamespace, dword tolerance);
description: Description Sets the allowed sync pulse tolerance in percent on the specified SENT channel.
parameters: Parameters sysVarNamespace The namespace of the channel or sensor. tolerance The tolerance value in percent. Valid range: 1 – 50 % (Default value 20%)
returnValues: Return Values Returns a SensorErrorCode .

name: SensorSwitchSupplyVoltage
syntax: Function Syntax SensorErrorCode sensorSwitchSupplyVoltage(char[] sysVarNamespace, dword onOff);
description: Description Enables or disables the sensor supply voltage of a simulated ECU.
parameters: Parameters sysVarNamespace The namespace of the channel or ECU. onOff 1 = Supply voltage on 0 = Supply voltage off
returnValues: Return Values Returns a SensorErrorCode .

name: GetGeneralSignalType
syntax: Function Syntax long GetGeneralSignalType (char signalName[])
description: Description Determines the general type of a signal, i.e., whether it is a database signal, a service signal, etc.
parameters: Parameters signalName The qualified name of the signal. The syntax is [Channel::][Database name (alias)::][Node::][Message::]Signa; the order and completeness of the objects from right to left is important. See the example for getSignal .
returnValues: Return Values 0 Signal was not found. 1 Signal is a database signal. 2 Signal is a service signal.

name: GetSignalDescriptionForValue
syntax: Function Syntax long getSignalDescriptionForValue(Signal signal, int64 rawValue, char buffer[], long bufferSize); // form 1 long getSignalDescriptionForValue(char signalNamel[], int64 rawValue, char buffer[], long bufferSize); // form 2 long getSignalDescriptionForValue(serviceSignalNumber signal, int64 rawValue, char buffer[], long bufferSize); // form 3
description: Description Retrieves the description for the value of a database signal. In this way, you can access the value table of the signal.
parameters: Parameters signal The signal (form 1). rawValue The value for which the description shall be retrieved. This must be the raw value, not the physical value. buffer String buffer which receives the value description. bufferSize Size of the buffer. signalName The name of the signal (form 2). Note Signal Ambiguity You have to use further objects to identify the signal uniquely. They are: channel, database name (alias), node name and message name. The exact qualification syntax is: [Channel::][Databa
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error, function successful. -1 The signal is not valid. -2 There is no value table for the signal or the value table doesn't contain the value. -3 The provided buffer is too small. -4 The description contains special characters which can't be represented in the current CAPL string encoding. -5 The signal was not found (form 2 only).

name: GetSignalValueForDescription
syntax: Function Syntax long getSignalValueForDescription(Signal signal, char description[], int64& rawValue); // form 1 long getSignalValueForDescription(char signalNamel[], char description[], int64& rawValue); // form 2 long getSignalValueForDescription(serviceSignalNumber signal, char description[], int64& rawValue); // form 3
description: Description Retrieves the value for a value description of a database signal. In this way, you can access the value table of the signal.
parameters: Parameters signal The signal (form 1). description The value description for which the value shall be retrieved. rawValue The value for the description. This is the raw value, not the physical value. signalName The name of the signal (form 2). Note Signal Ambiguity You have to use further objects to identify the signal uniquely. They are: channel, database name (alias), node name and message name. The exact qualification syntax is: [Channel::][Database name (alias)::][Node::][Message::]Signal Th
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error, function successful. -1 The signal is not valid. -2 There is no value table for the signal or the value table doesn't contain the description. -4 The description contains special characters which can't be represented on the current computer. -5 The signal was not found (form 2 only).

name: ReadByteArraySignalAsInteger
syntax: Function Syntax dword ReadByteArraySignalAsInteger(signal aSignal, qword& value, dword direction, dword byteOrder, dword byteFillMode) // form 1 dword ReadByteArraySignalAsInteger(pdu aPDU, char signalName[], qword& value, dword byteOrder, dword byteFillMode) // form 2
description: Description Reads the value of a byte array signal and treats it as a 64-bit integer. Use this function if you previously read the value of a byte array signal with the .raw64 selector. This selector works only for integer signals, but byte array signals were treated incorrectly as integer signals in CANoe versions < 12.0 SP3.
parameters: Parameters aSignal A byte array signal aPDU An AUTOSAR PDU with a byte array signal signalName Name of the signal on the PDU value The read value (out parameter) direction Whether the rx signal (parameter value 0) or the tx signal value buffer (parameter value 1) shall be read. byteOrder Byte order for interpreting the signal bytes. 0: little-endian (intel) 1: big-endian (motorola) See examples below. byteFillMode How the remaining bits of the 64-bit integer shall be filled if the signal has les
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error 1 The signal has more than 64 bits. -2 The signal is not a byte array. 3 Signal not found on the PDU (form 2).

name: ResetSignalValues
syntax: Function Syntax void resetSignalValues(dword mode); // form 1 void resetSignalValues(dword mode, eBusType busType, dword channelNumber); // form 2
description: Description Function to reset signals to their initial values. Depending on the mode parameter, the signals may be reset either to their initial value as configured in the database or to their start value as configured in der Start Values dialog.
parameters: Parameters mode Reset signals either to their initial value or to their start value. Values: 0: Reset signals to their initial database value 1: Reset signals to their start value busType Reset only the signals of the specified bus type. channelNumber Reset only the signals of the specified channel number.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: WriteByteArraySignalAsInteger
syntax: Function Syntax dword WriteByteArraySignalAsInteger(signal aSignal, qword value, dword byteOrder, dword byteCutMode) // form 1 dword WriteByteArraySignalAsInteger(pdu aPDU, char signalName[], qword value, dword byteOrder, dword byteCutMode) // form2
description: Description Writes the value of a byte array signal, treating it as a 64-bit integer. Use this function if you previously wrote the value of a byte array signal with the .raw64 selector. This selector works only for integer signals, but byte array signals were treated incorrectly as integer signals in CANoe versions < 12.0 SP3.
parameters: Parameters aSignal A byte array signal aPDU An AUTOSAR PDU with a byte array signal signalName Name of the signal on the PDU value The to be written value. byteOrder Byte order for interpreting the signal bytes. 0: little-endian (intel) 1: big-endian (motorola) See examples below. byteCutMode How the additional bits of the 64-bit value shall be cut if the signal has less than 64 bits. 0: ignore bits a the end 1: ignore bits at the beginning See examples below.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error 1 The signal has more than 64 bits. -2 The signal is not a byte array. 3 Signal not found on the PDU (form 2).

name: CCSPayloadPreSendInd
syntax: Function Syntax void PayloadPreSendInd(inout bytes payload, MessageIdType messageID, uint32 payloadLength);
description: Description The callback is called each time a TPC/UDP packet containing a Vehicle2Grid or SECC Discovery message is about to be sent by the DO. It allows to peek for the payload and its length before sending. Additionally, the payload and by doing so also the length may be changed for the purpose of fault injection. It is available for the whole Distributed Object.
parameters: Parameters payload The UDP/TCP payload data (writable) messageId ID of the message to be sent. See MessageIDs for more information. payloadLength The length of the UDP/TCP payload at the time of the callback. Will automatically match the length of the buffer, once modified payload is passed back to the DO that is about to send this message.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: CertChainVerificationInd
syntax: Function Syntax void CertChainVerificationInd(MessageIdType messageId, string chainName, bool chainIsValid);
description: Description The callback is called when a received certificate chain (e.g. ContractSignatureCertChain) is verified. Certificate chains are sent as part of several V2G messages when in PnC mode. The callback may be called multiple times for a V2G message, if that message contains multiple chains.
parameters: Parameters messageId ID of the received message. See MessageIDs for more information. chainName Name of the verified certificate chain inside the V2G message. chainIsValid 0: invalid 1: valid
returnValues: Return Values —

name: IndGenericVSHostMessageInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_Generic_VS_HostMessage_Ind (byte sourceMacAddress[], dword hostMessageInd);
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a VS_Host_Action.Ind or equivalent message is received. The interpretation of the message depends on the vendor OUI and will then be mapped to the respective hostMessageInd values. Currently supported OUIs: Qualcomm (0x00B052) MStar, MediaTek and Vertexcom (0x0013D7) Lumissil (0x0016E8)
parameters: Parameters SourceMacAddress MAC address of sender. hostMessageInd Notification: DEVICE_READY = 0x00 READY_TO_JOIN = 0x01 AVLN_JOINED = 0x02 AVLN_DISCONNECTED = 0x03 NOT_SUPPORTED = 0xFF
returnValues: Return Values —

name: IndGenericVSVersionQueryCnf
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_Generic_VS_VersionQuery_Cnf (byte sourceMacAddress[], char versionString[]);
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a VS_Get_Version.Cnf or equivalent message is received. The versionString Parameter will be formatted based on the type of confirmation message received. The version can be requested by using the SCC_CreateGenericVS_VersionQuery_Req function. Currently supported OUIs: Qualcomm (0x00B052) MStar, MediaTek and Vertexcom (0x0013D7) Lumissil (0x0016E8)
parameters: Parameters SourceMacAddress MAC address of sender. versionString Version string formatted based on the type of chip that answers the request.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: InvalidMessageReceivedInd
syntax: Function Syntax void InvalidMessageReceivedInd(EGBT27930MessageType lastTxMessageId)
description: Description The callback is called when an unknown message or message with an invalid format is received and is therefore not further processed. It is available for the whole Distributed Object.
parameters: Parameters lastTxMessageId ID of the message received (if it was possible to determine this)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnLastReceivedMessageIdChanged

description: Description This callback is called as soon as a message is received.
parameters: Parameters LastRxMessageId ID of the last message received. See MessageIDs for more information.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnLastTransmittedMessageIdChanged
syntax: Function Syntax void OnLastTransmittedMessageIdChanged ( LastTxMessageIdType LastTxMessageId )
description: Description This callback is called after a message was transmitted.
parameters: Parameters LastTxMessageId ID of the last message received. See MessageIDs for more information.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCAuthorizationReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_AuthorizationReq ( byte SessionID[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as an Authorization Request is received. The challenge optionally transmitted in this request can be queried with SCC_GetGenChallenge . Alternatively the callback SCC_ContractAuthenticationReq can be used. Both functions are called when the message is received. SCC_ContractAuthenticationReq is called first.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCAuthorizationRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_AuthorizationRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode)
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Authorization Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this response can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetProcessing SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCCableCheckReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_CableCheckReq ( byte SessionID[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Cable Check Request is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following function: GetDC_EVStatus
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCCableCheckRes

description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Cable Check Response is received. Further details (EVSEProcessing) that are transmitted in this response can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetProcessing SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCCertChainVerificationInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_CertChainVerificationInd ( byte SessionId[], char ChainName[], dword ChainIsValid )
description: Description The callback is called when a received certificate chain (e.g. ContractSignatureCertChain ) is verified. Certificate chains are sent as part of several V2G messages when in PnC mode. The callback may be called multiple times for a V2G message, if that message contains multiple chains. In case of errors, the specific error cause(s) can be queried with the function SCC_GertCertChainVerificationDetails . A list of individual verification errors can be queried with: SCC_GetCertChainVerif
parameters: Parameters SessionId 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ChainName Name of the verified certificate chain inside the V2G message. ChainIsValid 1 if the chain was verified as valid, 0 if the chain was verified as invalid.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCCertificateUpdateReq

description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Certificate Installation Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this response can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetDHPublicKey SCC_GetEncryptedPrivateKey SCC_GetEMAIDIdAttr SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED ContractID ID of the contract to which the certificate is assigned, max. 128 character string. (eMAID)

NumSounds Number of M-Sounds used to generate the ATTEN_PROFILE. NumGroups Number of attenuation groups. AAG Average Attenuation Group (array length is indicated by the parameter NumGroups ).

SAScheduleTupleID (ISO 15118) ID of the selected SAScheduleTuple. ReceiptRequired (ISO
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCErrorStateInd

name: SCCInvalidMessageInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_InvalidMessageInd ( long MessageType, long ErrorCode )
description: Description The callback is called when a SDP or V2G message is received with an invalid content (i.e. it is rejected by the session layer).
parameters: Parameters MessageType Type of the invalid message: 0: SDP (SECC Discovery Protocol) 1: V2G (Vehicle2Grid) ErrorCode Type of error: 0: Wrong version number, or version number format 1: Unknown payload type 2: Invalid payload length 3: Error while decoding (EXI or XML parsing failed) – V2G only 4: Invalid signature – V2G only
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCInvalidMessageReceivedInd
syntax: Function Syntax void InvalidMessageReceivedInd(InvalidMessageErrorType error);
description: Description The callback is called when a SDP or V2G message is received with an invalid format and is therefore not further processed. It is available for the whole Distributed Object.
parameters: Parameters error Type of error: 0: Wrong version number or inverse version number 1: Unknown payload type 2: Invalid payload length
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCLinkStatusChangeInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_LinkStatusChangeInd ( dword OldStatus, dword NewStatus )
description: Description The callback is called each time the internally stored link status changes. To use this callback, the XML-parameter <SLAC_ChipMACAddress> must either be set to the correct value or removed. Otherwise, the polling message is ignored. Note It is recommended to use VS_Host_Action.Ind as described in SCC_Generic_VS_HostMessage_Ind instead of link status polling.
parameters: Parameters OldStatus Previous link status (0 = no link, 1 = link, 2 = unkown). NewStatus New link status (0 = no link, 1 = link, 2 = unkown).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCMessageRxInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_MessageRxInd(byte SessionID[], dword MessageID)
description: Description The callback is called each time a Vehicle2Grid, SECC Discovery or SLAC message is received by the simulation DLL.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of the connection, range: 0 - 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF MessageID Type of received message: For SLAC messages, MMType (2 byte) according to specification. For V2G messages see help page MessageID .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCMessageTxInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_MessageTxInd ( byte SessionOrRunID[], dword MessageID, long Error )
description: Description The callback is called each time a Vehicle2Grid , SECC Discovery or SLAC message is sent by the simulation DLL.
parameters: Parameters SessionOrRunID 8-byte long SessionID (V2G) or RunID (SLAC) of the connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. MessageID Type of sent message: For SLAC messages, MMType (2 byte) according to specification. For V2G messages see help page MessageID . Error 0 if the sending was successful. > 0 if the send call failed (sending may fail due to socket errors or the absence of a receiver which sends ACK packages).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCMeteringReceiptReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_MeteringReceiptReq ( byte SessionID[], byte MessageSessionID[], long ScheduleTableEntryID )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Metering Receipt Request is received. With this request, the vehicle confirms receipt of the metering information sent by the charge point. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the helper function SCC_GetMeterInfoData .
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. MessageSessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF, as transferred within the message body. ScheduleTableEntryID ID of the selected SAScheduleTuple.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCMeteringReceiptRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_MeteringReceiptRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Metering Receipt Response is received. . Further details that are transmitted in this message can be queried with the following function: SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCOnGenericVSHostActionInd
syntax: Function Syntax void OnGenericVSHostActionInd( byte oui [], LinkStatusType linkStatus);
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a VS_Host_Action.Ind or equivalent message is received. The interpretation of the message depends on the vendor OUI and will then be mapped to the respective link status values. Currently supported OUIs: Qualcomm (0x00B052) MStar, MediaTek and Vertexcom (0x0013D7) Lumissil (0x0016E8) It is available for the whole Distributed Object.
parameters: Parameters oui The OUI of the received host action indication. linkStatus The link status that was indicated by the received message: 0: Disconnected 1: ReadyToJoin 2: Connected 3: Undefined
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCOnPreSendMessageIdChanged
syntax: Function Syntax void OnPreSendMessageIdChanged ( LastTxMessageIdType txMessageId )
description: Description This callback is called before a message is sent. Using the active simulation, the individual values of the corresponding message can be changed here. This includes message parameters with meaningful values that were set by the state machine before sending. For correct usage refer to the CAPL example. It is available for the whole Distributed Object.
parameters: Parameters txMessageId ID of the message that will be sent. See MessageIDs for more information.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCOnSocketReceivedInd
syntax: Function Syntax void OnSocketReceivedInd(MessageIdType messageId, uint32 sourcePort, uint32 destinationPort, inout bytes payload, uint32 payloadLength);
description: Description The callback is called each time a TCP/UDP packet containing a Vehicle2Grid or SECC Discovery message is received on the socket by the DO. It allows to query the payload and its length as well as for the source and destination port. It is available for the whole Distributed Object. The received UDP/TCP payload can be manipulated after EXI decoding and before it is processed by the simulation. In this case the manipulated payload will be EXI decoded again and the corresponding message
parameters: Parameters messageId The id of the received message contained in the packet. See  MessageIDs  for more information. sourcePort The source port of the received packet. destinationPort The destination port of the received packet. payload The payload of the received packet. payloadLength The length of the payload of the received packet.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCOnSocketReceivedRawInd
syntax: Function Syntax void OnSocketReceivedRawInd(SocketProtocolType protocolType, uint32 sourcePort, uint32 destinationPort, inout bytes payload, uint32 payloadLength);
description: Description This callback is called each time a TCP/UDP packet is received on the socket by the DO. It allows querying the payload and its length, as well as the source and destination ports. Compared to OnSocketReceivedInd it does not check the content before and is called regardless of whether a V2G / SDP message is contained or not. Note : Is called when closing a TCP connection with 0 bytes. The received UDP/TCP payload can be manipulated before it is processed by the simulation. In this cas
parameters: Parameters protocolType Protocol type of the socket on which data is received: SocketProtocolType::UDP SocketProtocolType::TCP sourcePort The source port of the received packet. destinationPort The destination port of the received packet. payload The payload of the received packet. payloadLength The length of the payload of the received packet.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPayloadPreSendInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PayloadPreSendInd(byte SessionOrRunID[], dword MessageID, byte Payload[], dword& PayloadLength)
description: Description The callback is called each time a Vehicle2Grid, SECC Discovery or SLAC message is ready to be sent by the simulation DLL. It allows to peek for the payload and its length before sending. Also, both payload and length may be changed for the purpose of fault injection.
parameters: Parameters SessionOrRunID 8-byte long SessionID (V2G) or RunID (SLAC) of the connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. MessageID Type of sent message: For SLAC messages, MMType (2 byte) according to specification. For V2G messages see help page MessageID . Payload The ETH/UDP/TCP payload data (writable) PayloadLength The length of the ETH/UDP/TCP payload (writable)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPaymentDetailsReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PaymentDetailsReq ( byte SessionID[], char ContractID[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Payment Details Request is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetCertificateChainData SCC_GetCertificateChainCertificate
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ContractID ID of the contract to which the certificate is assigned, max. 128 character string. (eMAID)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPaymentDetailsRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PaymentDetailsRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Service Payment Selection Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetTimestamp SCC_GetGenChallenge SCC_GetResponseCodeString With SCC_SetEnergyTransferType the transfer type/mode sent in the following message can be set.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPaymentServiceSelectionReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PaymentServiceSelectionReq ( byte SessionID[], char SelectedPaymentOption[], long ServiceListCount )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Payment Service Selection Request is received. The Request contains the selected services and the chosen method of payment. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetSelectedServiceID SCC_GetSelectedParameterSetID
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. SelectedPaymentOption The selected payment option in form of a string. ServiceListCount Number of selected services: Up to 8 entries are contained in the list. The list entries must be queried via separate help functions.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPaymentServiceSelectionRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PaymentServiceSelectionRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Payment Service Selection Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPowerDeliveryReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PowerDeliveryReq ( byte SessionId[], long ChargeProfileCount, long ChargeState, long ScheduleID)
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Power Delivery Request is received. With this request, the vehicle requests the charge point to switch on the current and to send the charging profile. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetChargingProfileData SCC_GetBulkChargingComplete (DC) SCC_GetChargingComplete (DC)
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ChargingProfileCount Number of received charging profiles. ChargeState 1 if start of charging is requested, 0 if stop of charging is requested, 2 if ReNegotiation is requested (only ISO 15118). Corresponds to ReadyToChargeState in DIN 70121. ScheduleID ID of the chosen SAScheduleTuple.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPowerDeliveryRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PowerDeliveryRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Power Delivery Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following function: SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED EVSEID ID of charge point.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPreChargeReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PreChargeReq ( byte SessionID[], float TargetVoltage, float TargetCurrent )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a PreCharge Request is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: GetDC_EVStatus
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF TargetVoltage Voltage demand TargetCurrent Current demand
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPreChargeRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PreChargeRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode, float EVSEPresentVoltage )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a PreCharge Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following function: SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED . EVSEPresentVoltage Present voltage value of charge point.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPreSendInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PreSendInd ( byte SessionID[], dword MessageID, char ResponseCode[] )
description: Description The callback is called in active mode before a response message is sent. It enables checking the message's response code and, if desired, overwriting it with another value. Additionally, overwriting the following parameters is supported: EVSEStatusCode EVSEIsolationStatus Note The function is called before a manually set ResponseCode is applied (see function SCC_SetResponseCode ).
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. MessageID Type of message to be sent. ResponseCode Response code string of the message. The list of possible response codes you find on page SCC_SetResponseCode .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCPrivateKeyVerificationInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_PrivateKeyVerificationInd ( byte SessionId[], dword KeyIsValid )
description: Description The callback is called when a received encrypted private key (e.g. ContractSignatureEncryptedPrivateKey) is verified. The private key is sent as part of Certificate Installation and Certificate Update Responses.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. KeyIsValid 1 if the key was verified as valid, 0 if the key was verified as invalid.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCProtocolFinishedInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_ProtocolFinishedInd ( byte SessionID[] )
description: Description The callback is called when a protocol run has been successfully finished.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCRestartInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_RestartInd ( byte SessionID[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as the vehicle restarts the protocol after a disconnection. With SCC_GetRetriesLeft the number of restarts remaining is queried.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of the last SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSchemaSelectionInd

parameters: Parameters Namespace Namespace string of the selected schema. VersionMajor Major version of the selected schema. VersionMinor Major version of the selected schema.
returnValues: Return Values —

syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SECCDiscoveryReq ( dword TLSEnabled, dword TransportProtocol )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a SECC Discovery Request is received.
parameters: Parameters TLSEnabled 1 for TLS, 0 for no transport layer security . TransportProtocol 0 for TCP, 0x10 for UDP.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSECCDiscoveryRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SECCDiscoveryRes ( dword TLSEnabled, dword TransportProtocolType )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as an SECC Discovery Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetEVSEIP SCC_GetEVSEPort
parameters: Parameters TLSEnabled 1 for TLS, 0 for no transport layer security . TransportProtocol 0 for TCP, 0x10 for UDP.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCServiceDetailReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_ServiceDetailReq ( byte SessionID[], dword ServiceId )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Service Detail Request is received.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ServiceId ID of the requested service (16 bit unsigned number).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCServiceDetailRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_ServiceDetailRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode, dword ServiceID, long ParameterSetCount )

Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetServiceParameterSetData SCC_GetServiceParameterData SCC_GetServiceParameterNumericalValue SCC_GetServiceParameterPhysicalValue SCC_GetServiceParameterStringValue SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED . ServiceId ID of the requested service (16 bit unsigned number). ParameterSetCount Number of transmitted parameter sets.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCServiceDiscoveryReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_ServiceDiscoveryReq ( byte SessionId[], char ServiceScope[], char ServiceCategory[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Service Discovery Request is received.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ServiceScope Typically designates a company/organization in the form of a URI. ServiceCategory Type of requested services.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCServiceDiscoveryRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_ServiceDiscoveryRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode, long ServiceCount )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Service Discovery Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetServiceData SCC_GetPaymentOptions SCC_GetEnergyTransferType (DIN 70121) SCC_GetEnergyTransferMode Count (ISO 15118) SCC_GetEnergyTransferMode (ISO 15118) SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED . ServiceCount Number of ServiceTags or Services transmitted.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCServicePaymentSelectionReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_ServicePaymentSelectionReq ( byte SessionID[], char SelectedPaymentOption[], long ServiceListCount )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Service Payment Selection Request is received. The Request contains the selected services and the chosen method of payment. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetSelectedServiceID SCC_GetSelectedParameterSetID
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. SelectedPaymentOption The selected payment option in form of a string. ServiceListCount Number of selected services: Up to 8 entries are contained in the list. The list entries must be queried via separate help functions.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCServicePaymentSelectionRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_ServicePaymentSelectionRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Service Payment Selection Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following function: SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSessionSetupReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SessionSetupReq ( byte SessionID[], byte EVCCID[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Session Setup Request is received.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. If a new session is started, this ID is transferred. EVCCID 6-byte long in ISO and 8-byte long in DIN.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSessionSetupRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SessionSetupRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode, char EVSEID[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Session Setup Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following functions: SCC_GetTimestamp . SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED . EVSEID ID of charge point.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSessionStopReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SessionStopReq ( byte SessionID[], long Terminate )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Session Stop Request is received.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF Terminate 1 if charging status == terminate; for all other values: 0
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSessionStopRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SessionStopRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Session Stop Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following function: SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSignaturePreSendInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SignaturePreSendInd(byte SessionId[], dword MessageId, byte Signature[], dword& SigLength)
description: Description This callback is called when a signature for a message has been created. It allows to peek for the signature and its length before it is sent with the indicated message. Also, both signature and length may be changed for the purpose of fault injection.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. MessageID Type of the message that will use the signature. Signature The signature data. sigLength The number of signature data bytes. If set to zero, the signature will be absent in the indicated message.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSignatureVerificationInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SignatureVerificationInd ( dword MessageID, dword SignatureValid )
description: Description The callback is called when a signed V2G message is verified with the ECDSA algorithm.
parameters: Parameters MessageID Type of the received message. SignatureValid 1 if the signature was verified as valid, 0 if the signature was verified as invalid.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACFinishedInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLACFinishedInd ( dword Result )
description: Description The callback is called when a SLAC run has been completed. It marks the point in time when the SECC Discovery process can be started, if a match has been found and as soon as the link is established. The vehicle can use the function SCC_SLAC_GetAttenResults during this callback to get the measured attenuation values.
parameters: Parameters Result Result of the SLAC run (2 = PLC link established and interval for Amplitude Map Exchange elapsed,1 = match, 0 = no match).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCStateTransitionInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_StateTransitionInd ( byte SessionID[], dword OldState, dword NewState )
description: Description The callback is called each time the internal state machine switches its state, i.e. usually when a new message is received. Use SCC_GetStateName for a string representation of the state IDs.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. OldState Enum value of previous state. NewState Enum value of new state.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSupportedAppProtocolReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SupportedAppProtocolReq ( dword AppProtocolCount )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a SupportedAppProtocol Request is received. The list entries must be queried via the separate help function SCC_GetAppProtocolData .
parameters: Parameters AppProtocolCount Number of transmitted AppProtocol elements.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSupportedAppProtocolRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SupportedAppProtocolRes ( long ResponseCode, dword SchemaID )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Supported App Protocol Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following function: SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED . SchemaID The ID of the schema that was selected by the charge point.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCTCPConnectInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_TCPConnectInd ( dword Port, long IsInitiator )
description: Description The callback is called when a TCP connection is opened.
parameters: Parameters Port Port number of the TCP connection. IsInitiator 1 if the node layer initiated the connection (client case). 0 if the node layer received the connection request (server case).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCTCPShutdownInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_TCPShutdownInd ( dword Port, long IsInitiator )
description: Description The callback is called when a TCP connection is closed.
parameters: Parameters Port Port number of the TCP connection. IsInitiator 1 if the node layer initiated the shutdown. 0 if the node layer received the shutdown request.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCTLSHandshakeInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_TLSHandshakeInd( long Status, long IsServer)
description: Description The callback is called when a TLS handshake has been attempted after the SECC Discovery procedure. It is not called, however, if TLS is disabled or TCP was negotiated during SECC Discovery. To enable TLS, call SCC_SetTLSEnabled(1) in CAPL or set the parameter <UseTLS> to 1 in the XML configuration file.
parameters: Parameters Status 1 – TLS handshake has successfully been completed. 2 – TLS handshake has failed. The Write window may contain more details about the cause of the failure. 3 – TLS handshake has most likey failed due to a bad certificate. IsServer 0 – Callback for EV. 1 – Callback for EVSE. You may usually ignore this parameter as your CAPL code is linked to the EV or EVSE simulation DLL.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCV2GEVCCCableCheckTimeoutInd
syntax: Function Syntax (void SCC_V2G_EVCC_CableCheck_TimeoutInd byte SessionID[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as an V2G_EVCC_CableCheck_Timeout occurs.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of the SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCV2GEVCCMsgTimeoutInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_V2G_EVCC_Msg_TimeoutInd ( byte SessionID[], dword MessageID )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as an V2G_EVCC_Msg_Timeout occurs.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of the SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. MessageID Type of message to be sent.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCV2GEVCCPreChargeTimeoutInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_V2G_EVCC_PreCharge_TimeoutInd ( byte SessionID )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as an V2G_EVCC_PreCharge_Timeout occurs.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of the SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCVSGetStatusCnf
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_VS_Get_Status_Cnf(byte SourceMAC[], long Result, long Status, long LinkState, long Ready)
description: Description This callback is called when a VS_Get_Status.Cnf message (from a VertexCom PLC chip) is received. The callback function SCC_Generic_VS_HostMessage_Ind (byte sourceMacAddress[], dword hostMessageInd) will also be called immediately after this callback. Note Only the most relevant values from the VS_Get_Status.Cnf message are returned by this callback. Other values are currently not accessible by application code.
parameters: Parameters SourceMac The MAC address of the sender. Result The "Result" value of the VS_Get_Status.Cnf message. Status The "Status" value of the VS_Get_Status.Cnf message. LinkState The "Link State" value of the VS_Get_Status.Cnf message. Ready The "Ready for PLC operation" value of the VS_Get_Status.Cnf message.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCVSGetStatusReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_VS_Get_Status_Req(byte SourceMAC[])
description: Description This callback is called when a VS_Get_Status.Req message (from a VertexCom PLC chip) is received.
parameters: Parameters SourceMac The MAC address of the sender.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCVSHostActionInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_VS_HostAction_Ind ( byte SourceMacAddess[], dword hostActionReq )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a VS_Host_Action.Ind message is received. This indication is enabled only in QCA7000/QCA7005 device with SLAC enabled configuration. (Additional data cannot be queried at the moment.)
parameters: Parameters SourceMacAddress MAC address of sender. hostActionReq Notification: QCA_HAR_READY_TO_JOIN_AVLN = 0x09 QCA_HAR_AVLN_JOINED = 0x0A QCA_HAR_AVLN_DISCONNECTED = 0x0B
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCVSNwInfoCnf
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_VS_Nw_Info_Cnf ( byte SourceMacAddress[], long NumAvLn, byte NID[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a VS_Nw_Info.Cnf message is received. This is a response of the QCA7000 chip when using link status polling. (Additional data cannot be queried at the moment.)
parameters: Parameters SourceMacAddress MAC address of sender. NumAvLn Number of AVLANs (> 0 denotes an established link). NID Network ID (7 byte).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCVSPLLnkStatusCnf
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_VS_PL_Lnk_Status_Cnf ( byte SourceMacAddress[], dword MStatus, dword LinkStatus )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a VS_PL_Lnk_Status.Cnf message is received. This is the response of the QCA7000 chip when using link status polling. (Additional data cannot be queried at the moment.)
parameters: Parameters SourceMacAddress MAC address of sender. MStatus MStatus code: 0x00 = success 0x01 = failure LinkStatus Notification if the link is established: 0x00 = no link 0x01 = link
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCWeldingDetectionReq
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_WeldingDetectionReq ( byte SessionID[] )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Welding Detection Request is received.
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCWeldingDetectionRes
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_WeldingDetectionRes ( byte SessionID[], long ResponseCode, float EVSEPresentVoltage )
description: Description The callback is called as soon as a Welding Detection Response is received. Further details that are transmitted in this request can be queried with the following function: SCC_GetResponseCodeString
parameters: Parameters SessionID 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 – 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF. ResponseCode 1 if OK , 0 if FAILED EVSEPresentVoltage Current voltage value of charge point.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SignatureVerificationInd
syntax: Function Syntax void SignatureVerificationInd(MessageIdType messageId, bool signatureIsValid);
description: Description The callback is called when a signed V2G message is verified with the ECDSA algorithm.
parameters: Parameters messageId ID of the received message. See MessageIDs for more information. signatureIsValid 0: invalid 1: valid
returnValues: Return Values —

name: AvoidTCPConnection
syntax: Function Syntax MethodReturnValueType AvoidTCPConnection (bool avoidConnection)
description: Description This function can be used to avoid the TCP connection from the client or the server. The TCP handshake will not be started from client or avoided from server. The port is kept open by the simulation and continues to be listened to. Note : Internal flag is reset after first avoided connection, during the reset ( ResetSimulation ) and during the TCP close, so that the function must be called again for next connection establishment.
parameters: Parameters avoidConnection 1: enable 0: disable
returnValues: Return Values MethodReturnValueType All function calls (except for indications) will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values: 0  OK  1  InvalidArgument  2  NoMatchingElementFound  3  UnknownError

name: AvoidTLSAuthentication
syntax: Function Syntax MethodReturnValueType AvoidTLSAuthentication (bool avoidAuthentication)
description: Description This function can be used to avoid the TLS authentication from the client or the server. The TLS handshake will not be started from client or avoided from server. The port is kept open by the simulation and continues to be listened to. Note : Internal flag is reset after first avoided handshake, during the reset ( ResetSimulation ) and during the TCP close, so that the function must be called again for next connection establishment.
parameters: Parameters avoidAuthentication 1: enable 0: disable

The Standby procedure will be started by sending a Power Delivery Request with ChargeProgress Standby . Afterwards the communication will continue in the respective Charge Loop message pair and can be exited any time using the ExitStandby function (EV) or sending a EVSENotification ExitStandby (EVSE). The Pause procedure will stop the cu

If the EV is not currently in the Standby procedure, calling this function will have no effect and will generate a warning. Note The EVSE may choose to not allow entering the Standby procedure, by replying with a WARNING_StandbyNotAllowed ResponseCode in the Power Delivery Response message. name: TCPShutdown
syntax: Function Syntax long TcpShutdown( dword socket);
description: Description The function disables send operations on the specified socket. This function may be used to shutdown a TCP connection. Data can thereby still be received. When this function is used the socket (in contrast to TcpClose ) is closed only for the sending direction . The remote station is informed about this with event procedure OnTcpClose . Note The remote station cannot recognize whether TcpClose or TcpShutdown was used. For this reason the application or protocol must clearly control h
parameters: Parameters socket The socket handle.
returnValues: Return Values 0 The function completed successfully. WSA_INVALID_PARAMETER (87) The specified socket was invalid. SOCKET_ERROR (-1) The function failed. Call IpGetLastSocketError to get a more specific error code.

returnValues: Return Values —

returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCGetServiceIds
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_GetServiceIds(dword ChargeServiceIDs[], dword ServiceListIDs[], long& ChargeServiceIDsCount, long& ServiceListIDsCount);
description: Description Returns the ServiceID from the ChargeService and the ServiceIDs from the ServiceList sent with the current ServiceDiscoveryRes on EVSE side. This function is meant to help with matching the SerivceIDs received from an EV against the information sent within a ServiceDiscoveryRes by the EVSE. Note: This function is only valid during a SCC_MessageTxInd() callback with MessageID = 8 (ServiceDiscoveryRes).
parameters: Parameters ChargeServiceIDs The destination array of at least size 1 to receive the ServiceID from the ChargeService. ServiceListIDs The destination array is large enough to receive the (maximum) number of expected ServiceIDs from the ServiceList as per your SCC_ChargePoint XML configuration file. ChargeServiceIDsCount In: dimension of the destination array Out: The number of charge services ServiceListIDsCount In: dimension of the destination array Out: The number of ServiceIDs returned in the
returnValues: Return Values 0 error, the message was called in the wrong V2G context, i.e., not during a SCC_MessageTxInd() with message = ServiceDiscoveryRes. 1 success 2 success, but the specified array dimension was too short. Increase the arrays dimension to receive all data.

The function SCC_LoadCommunicationConfig loads the configuration globally, i.e. for all connections to be created. This call is only possible when simulation is deactivated. The function SCC_LoadV2GConfig loads the configuration for an already active connection, applying only those paramete

name: SCCSetChargingProfileData
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetChargingProfileData(long Index, long Start, double MaxPower, long NumberOfPhases);
description: Description This function allows to dynamically adapt a pre-loaded ChargingProfile to the SASchedule previously received from an EVSE. It overwrites the fields ChargingProfileEntryStart , ChargingProfileEntryMaxPower and/or ChargingProfileEntryMaxNumberOfPhasesInUse in a pre-loaded ChargingProfile of a PowerDeliveryReq (start) message. The ChargingProfile must have previously been loaded from the vehicle XML-config by calling SCC_SetOptionalParameterFromConfig . An error will be returned if no p
parameters: Parameters Index The index of the ProfileEntry to be modified, starting at 0 (zero). An error will be returned if the index is invalid. Start The new value for ChargingProfileEntryStart . No changes will be made if the value is <0. MaxPower The new value for ChargingProfileEntryMaxPower . No changes will be made if the value is <0. NumberOfPhases The new value for ChargingProfileEntryMaxNumberOfPhasesInUse . This optional field must already exist in the (XML) profile, it will not be created. No
returnValues: Return Values 0 error 1 success

parameters: Parameters SessionID The SessionID of the ChargeParameterDiscovery.Res message that provides the SAScheduleList from which the ChargingProfile is to be derived. SelectedTupleID Tuple that should be used as a basis for the created ChargingProfile.
returnValues: Return Values 0 error 1 success
