parameters: Parameters StatusCode String buffer that is written to the status code.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetEnergyToBeDelivered
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetEnergyToBeDelivered ( float EnergyToBeDelivered )
description: Description Sets the target energy value, which is sent in the ChargeParameterDiscoveryRes message.
parameters: Parameters EnergyToBeDelivered Value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetEnergyTransferType
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetEnergyTransferType ( char EnergyTransferType[] ) //form 1 (EV only) long SCC_SetEnergyTransferType ( char EnergyTransferType[], dword Force ) // form 2 (EV only) long SCC_SetEnergyTransferType ( char EnergyTransferType[] ) // form 3 (EVSE only)
description: Description EV: Sets the desired charging mode for a running SCC session . For the list of all valid charging modes, see the SCC specification. EVSE: Sets the offered energy transfer type (DIN 70121) resp. -mode (ISO 15118) for a running SCC session . In case of ISO 15118, a list with one element is created. For the list of all valid values, see the SCC specification.
parameters: Parameters EnergyTransferType (EV/EVSE) Desired charging mode as string. Please make sure the string entered is a valid according to the schema. Force (EV) If set to 1, the vehicle is forced to set the charging mode even if the charge point hasn't offered it previously. If set to 0, another mode may be selected if this one is unavailable.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetEVReady
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetEVReady ( long Ready )
description: Description The function sets the ready flag.
parameters: Parameters Ready 1 = ready, 0 = not ready
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetEVSENotification
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetEVSENotification ( char EVSENotification[] )
description: Description Sets the EVSE notification enum.
parameters: Parameters EVSENotification String that must match an enum value according to the schema. Possible values are: None StopCharging ReNegotiation
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetExternalPaymentAllowed
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetExternalPaymentAllowed ( dword Allowed )
description: Description Changes the available PaymentOptions to be sent in the ServiceDiscovery response message.
parameters: Parameters Allowed 1 to allow the PaymentOption, 0 to disallow. The other option will automatically be activated if an option is disallowed.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetFaultNotification
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SetFaultNotification ( char FaultCode[], char FaultMsg[] )
description: Description Sets the fault code and message for the next V2G message. The fault code is contained within the V2G header.
parameters: Parameters FaultCode Desired fault code, which must be a valid enum value according to the specification. FaultMsg Desired fault message string. If this string is empty, the optional message element is omitted.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetGenChallenge
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetGenChallenge ( byte GenChallenge[] )
description: Description Sets the challenge for the PaymentDetailsRes message, which is randomly generated otherwise.
parameters: Parameters GenChallenge Value to be set. Array length must be 16 byte.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetIsolationStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetIsolationStatus ( char IsolationStatus[] )
description: Description Sets the isolation status enum.
parameters: Parameters IsolationStatus String buffer that is written to the isolation status. The string should be a valid enum value according the schema.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetMaxCurrent
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetMaxCurrent ( float MaxCurrent )
description: Description Sets the limit for current. These limits are used in various messages for both AC and DC mode (the actual element name depends on the charging mode and the protocol version). Defaults can be set using the respective configuration file.
parameters: Parameters MaxCurrent Limit value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetMaxPower
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetMaxPower ( float MaxPower )
description: Description Sets the limit for power. These limits are used in various messages for both AC and DC mode (the actual element name depends on the charging mode and the protocol version). Defaults can be set using the respective configuration file.
parameters: Parameters MaxPower Limit value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetMaxVoltage
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetMaxVoltage ( float MaxVoltage )
description: Description Sets the limit for voltage. These limits are used in various messages for both AC and DC mode (the actual element name depends on the charging mode and the protocol version). Defaults can be set using the respective configuration file.
parameters: Parameters MaxVoltage Limit value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetMessageDelay
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SetMessageDelay ( dword Delay ) // form 1 void SCC_SetMessageDelay ( dword Delay, float JitterPercent ) // form 2
description: Description Specifies the delay time before an SCC message is sent. This allows you to slow down the protocol sequence. The delay applies to all active connections of the module.
parameters: Parameters Delay Desired delay value in milliseconds. JitterPercent Desired jitter (max. random variation) in % of Delay.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSetMeterReading
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetMeterReading( float CurrentMeter )
description: Description Sets the current consumption in the charge point.
parameters: Parameters CurrentMeter Meter reading to be set in Wh.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetMinCurrent
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetMinCurrent ( float MinCurrent )
description: Description Sets the minimum current for the message ChargeParameterDiscoveryReq . A default value can be set using the configuration file.
parameters: Parameters MinCurrent Minimum current value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetMinVoltage
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetMinVoltage ( float MinVoltage )
description: Description This function sets the EVSEMinimumVoltageLimit of DC_EVSEChargeParameter.
parameters: Parameters MinVoltage Minimum voltage of EVSE [V].
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetNominalVoltage
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetNominalVoltage ( float NominalVoltage )
description: Description This function sets the EVSENominalVoltage of AC_EVSEChargeParameter.
parameters: Parameters NominalVoltage Nominal voltage of EVSE [V]
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetNotificationMaxDelay
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetNotificationMaxDelay ( long MaxDelay )
description: Description Sets the maximally allowed delay time for the vehicle to react on the provided notification.
parameters: Parameters MaxDelay Target delay time.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetOptionalParameter
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetOptionalParameter ( dword Parameter, long ParameterValue ) // form 1 long SCC_SetOptionalParameter ( dword Parameter, float ParameterValue ) // form 2 long SCC_SetOptionalParameter ( dword Parameter, char ParameterValue[] ) // form 3 long SCC_SetOptionalParameter ( dword Parameter, byte ParameterValue[] ) // form 4 long SCC_SetOptionalParameter ( dword Parameter, byte ParameterValue[], dword length ) // form 5
description: Description Sets an optional parameter after creating a message. Form 5 only copies the number of bytes specified by the length parameter. Only available for the SigMeterReading element.
parameters: Parameters Parameter ID number of the parameter, as denoted in the lists of optional parameters below each Create-function. ParameterValue Desired value of the parameter in the matching data type.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetOptionalParameterFromConfig
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetOptionalParameterFromConfig ( dword Parameter, dword ConfigSection )
description: Description Sets an optional parameter of type complex by referring to one of the TestConfiguration sections.
parameters: Parameters Parameter ID number of the parameter, as denoted in the lists of optional parameters below each Create-function. ConfigSection ID of the config section where the parameter can be found.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetOptionalParameterUnsigned
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetOptionalParameterUnsigned ( dword Parameter, dword ParameterValue )
description: Description Sets an optional parameter using a DWORD value after creating a message.
parameters: Parameters Parameter ID number of the parameter, as denoted in the lists of optional parameters below each Create-function. ParameterValue Desired value of the parameter in the matching data type.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetPaymentOption
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetPaymentOption ( char PaymentOption[], dword Force )
description: Description Sets the desired payment option for a running SCC session .
parameters: Parameters PaymentOption Desired payment option as string. For ISO 15118, the value must be either Contract (indicates PnC mode) or ExternalPayment (indicates EIM mode). Force If set to 1, the vehicle is forced to set the payment option even if the charge point hasn't offered it previously. If set to 0, the payment option is only selected if it has been offered.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetPeakCurrentRipple
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetPeakCurrentRipple ( float PeakCurrentRipple)
description: Description This function sets the EVSEPeakCurrentRipple of DC_EVSEChargeParameter.
parameters: Parameters PeakCurrentRipple Nominal voltage of EVSE [A].
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetPMaxScheduleEntryData
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetPMaxScheduleEntryData(long i1, long i2, long Start, long Duration, float PMax);
description: Description Overwrites the start , duration and PMax values of the PMaxScheduleEntry at the given indices. The modified SAScheduleList will be sent with the next ChargeParameterDiscovery response. Note This function applies only to the SAScheduleList of ISO 15118. This function is used to programmatically modify a PMaxScheduleEntry of an SAScheduleList previously loaded from the charge point XML configuration . It does not create any entries.
parameters: Parameters i1 The index of the SAScheduleTuple, starting at 0 (zero). An error will be returned if no SAScheduleTuple exists at the given index. i2 The index of the PMaxScheduleEntry in the selected SAScheduleTuple to be modified, starting at 0. An error will be returned if no PMaxScheduleEntry exists at the given index. Start The new start time within the RelativeTimeInterval field of the PMaxScheduleEntry. Duration The new duration within the RelativeTimeInterval field of the PMaxScheduleEntry
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetPreparedMsgFaultNotification
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetPreparedMsgFaultNotification( char FaultCode[], char FaultMessage[] )
description: Description Sets the fault code and fault message for a prepared message.
parameters: Parameters FaultCode Desired fault code, which must be a valid enum value according to the specification. FaultMsg Desired fault message string. If this string is empty, the optional message element is omitted.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetPreparedMsgHeaderData
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SetPreparedMsgHeaderData ( byte Header[] )
description: Description Overwrites the SDP/V2G header for a prepared message. (Refer to the DIN / ISO specifications for the header layout.)
parameters: Parameters Header 8 byte V2G header
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSetPresentCurrent
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetPresentCurrent ( float PresentCurrent )
description: Description Sets the present current output. This value is used in various DC messages, and for calculating the MeterInfo in AC mode. If no value is set, the charge point will automatically calculate a default.
parameters: Parameters PresentCurrent Value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetPresentVoltage
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetPresentVoltage ( float PresentVoltage )
description: Description Sets the current voltage output. This value is used in various DC messages, and for calculating the MeterInfo in AC mode. If no value is set, the charge point will automatically calculate a default.
parameters: Parameters PresentVoltage Value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetProcessing
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetProcessing ( long Processing )
description: Description Sets the processing status of the charge point. This status is used for controlling various message loops, where the vehicle will continue sending the same request until EVSEProcessing is set to Finished within the response message. Note The charge point will never send EVSEProcessing = Ongoing unless this function is called. The special value Ongoing_WaitingForCustomerInteraction of ISO IS will automatically be applied according to [V2G2-854] if EVSEProcessing was set to 1.
parameters: Parameters EVSEProcessing 0 if Finished 1 if Ongoing resp. Ongoing_WaitingForCustomerInteraction (ISO 15118)
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetRCD
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetRCD ( long RCD )
description: Description The function sets the residual current device.
parameters: Parameters RCD Open, error = 1 Closed, no error = 0
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetReceiptRequired
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetReceiptRequired ( long ReceiptRequired )
description: Description Sets the flag ReceiptRequired , and adapts the simulation state to await a MeteringReceiptReq next. Although MeteringReceipt is a specific message for PnC mode, this is also possible when using EIM mode.
parameters: Parameters ReceiptRequired 1 if a MeteringReceiptReq is expected next. 0 if another ChargingStatusReq is expected.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetResponseCode
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetResponseCode ( char ResponseCode[] )
description: Description Sets the return code of the next message to be sent.
parameters: Parameters ResponseCode Response code string, which must match a valid enum value from the XMLSchema as defined by DIN70121 and ISO15118. SAP Response Codes OK_SuccessfulNegotiation OK_SuccessfulNegotiationWithMinorDeviation Failed_NoNegotiation
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetRESSConditioning
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetRESSConditioning ( long RESSConditioning )
description: Description The function sets the RESS conditioning flag.
parameters: Parameters RESSConditioning 1 = conditioning on, 0 = conditioning off
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetSchema
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SetSchema ( char Namespace[] ) void SCC_SetSchema ( char Namespace[], long VersionMajor, long VersionMinor )
description: Description Sets the preferred schema version, corresponding to <SchemaNamespace> and optionally a specific version, in the XML configuration file. The configured schema will be used unless a different one is derived from the SupportedAppProtocol handshake, and it will be prioritized during the handshake. If unspecified, the latest schema will have the highest priority. When no version numbers are specified, depending on the context, it will mean any or latest version.
parameters: Parameters SchemaNamespace Desired namespace. SchemaVersionMajor Desired major version number, may be -1 for EVSE simulation. SchemaVersionMinor Desired minor version number, may be -1 for EVSE simulation.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetSelectedScheduleTableEntry
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetSelectedScheduleTableEntry ( long ID )
description: Description Sets the ID of the selected SAScheduleTuple, which is sent in the messages PowerDeliveryReq and MeteringReceiptReq . Note For a valid charge session, use one of the SAScheduleTupleIDs sent by the charge point in the message ChargeParameterDiscoveryRes . You can query them using the function SCC_GetSAScheduleTupleID . If no tuple is selected, the vehicle will automatically select the first or newest one.
parameters: Parameters ID Desired SAScheduleTupleID.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetSelectedSchema
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetSelectedSchema ( long SchemaID )
description: Description Sets the ID of the schema to be selected in the SupportedAppProtocolRes message. This overrides the automatic selection done based on the vehicle's priority values. Note Due to technical reasons, the schema ID must belong to a schema actually offered by the vehicle (the charge point cannot continue with an undefined schema).
parameters: Parameters SchemaID ID of the selected schema, based on the vehicle's list.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetSelectedService
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetSelectedService ( long ServiceID, long ParameterSetID )
description: Description Adds a service to the SelectedServiceList of the PaymentService- SelectionReq (former ServicePaymentSelectionReq) message. Note For a correct message, use one of the ServiceIDs sent by the charge point in the message ServiceDiscoveryRes. The charge service is always selected automatically by the vehicle.
parameters: Parameters ServiceID ID of the desired service. ParameterSetID ID of the desired parameter set (if set to 0, this optional parameter will be omitted).
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetServiceDetailRequest
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetServiceDetailRequest ( dword ServiceId )
description: Description Requests the vehicle to send a Service Detail Request during the ServiceDiscoveryRes or ServiceDetailRes callback. It is possible for multiple Service Detail Requests, or none at all, to be sent. If this function is not called, the vehicle skips this message. Note This function must be called during a callback before a Service Detail Request may be sent, i.e. the callback for the messages Service Discovery Response or Service Detail Response.
parameters: Parameters ServiceID ID of service that is to be requested, in hexadecimal representation.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetSessionId
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetSessionId (byte sessionId[])
description: Description Sets the new Session IDduring the callback SCC_SessionSetupReq .
parameters: Parameters SessionId 8-byte long SessionID of the connection, range: 0 - 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetShutdownRequest
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetShutdownRequest ( long ShutdownRequest )
description: Description Demands the stop of the charging session from the vehicle, the appropriate mechanism for the active schema version, or withdraws this request. Note You can still initiate a shutdown using the specific parameter of the target schema version instead of using this convenience function.
parameters: Parameters ShutdownRequest 1 to demand a shutdown. 0 to withdraw a shutdown request.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetTargetCurrent
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetTargetCurrent ( float TargetCurrent )
description: Description Sets the desired current. These limits are used in various DC messages. If no values are set, the vehicle will automatically calculate defaults.
parameters: Parameters TargetCurrent Value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetTargetVoltage
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetTargetVoltage ( float TargetVoltage )
description: Description Sets the desired voltage. These limits are used in various DC messages. If no values are set, the vehicle will automatically calculate defaults.
parameters: Parameters TargetVoltage Value to be set.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSetTLSEnabled
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SetTLSEnabled ( long Enabled )
description: Description Specifies if TLS is used. This function will affect both the Security flag in the SECC Discovery message and the subsequent communication setup. By calling this function, the configuration parameter <UseTLS> is overwritten. When no behavior is specified by any of these methods, the remote station may decide for either TCP or TLS. Note Communication between EV and EVSE can only be established if both sides configure the TLS equally. You can use the information from SCC_SECCDiscoveryRe
parameters: Parameters Enabled 1 to enable TLS support, 0 to disable TLS support.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSetVerbosity
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SetVerbosity ( dword Level )
description: Description Sets the Verbosity parameter of the DLL. The higher the value is, the more information will be output in the write window.
parameters: Parameters Level The desired verbosity level. Selector Description of return value 0 Signals only critical errors. 1 Signals errors due to erroneous configurations, etc. 2 Warns, if an unexpected protocol status is reached, and signals missing obligatory callbacks. 3 Informs about missing optional callbacks and minor problems. >3 Signals low level events such as setting and expiring of timers.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSetWeldingDetectionCount
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SetWeldingDetectionCount ( dword Count )
description: Description Sets the number of WeldingDetection requests for the next welding detection phase.
parameters: Parameters Count Number of WeldingDetection messages, may be zero.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCShutdown
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_Shutdown ( long Terminate )
description: Description Stops the charging session when inside the charge loop, by starting a regular shutdown procedure.
parameters: Parameters Terminate If 1, the SessionStopReq is sent with ChargingSession = Terminate , else with ChargingSession = Pause (ISO 15118 only, else this value is ignored).
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSimulationReset
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SimulationReset ( )
description: Description This function resets the SCC simulation to its initial state, corresponding to the start of a new measurement. All connections and stored parameters are deleted.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSimulationResume
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SimulationResume ( )
description: Description This function pauses/resumes the SCC simulation. This functionality can be used to freeze the protocol flow in a certain state.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSimulationWait
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SimulationWait ( )
description: Description This function pauses/resumes the SCC simulation. This functionality can be used to freeze the protocol flow in a certain state.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACGenerateApplyKey
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_GenerateApplyKey ( )
description: Description Immediately sends a CM_Set_Key.Req message with a new NMK and NID. If a NMK is defined in the XML configuration file, this NMK is permanently discarded.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACGetApplicationType
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_GetApplicationType ( )
description: Description Queries the application type.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 PEV-EVSE Association (mandatory) 0x01-0xFF Reserved

name: SCCSLACGetAttenResults
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetAttenResults ( float Results[], long& ResultCount )
description: Description Gets the measured average attenuation values during the callback SCC_SLACFinishedInd .
parameters: Parameters Results Queries the attenuation results (to the given float buffer). The results are returned sorted, starting with the lowest attenuation. ResultCount Gets the number of results in the array (via reference).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetCMSetKeyCnfData
syntax: Function Syntax void SLAC_GetCMSetKeyCnfData ( dword& MyNonce, dword& YourNonce, dword& PID, dword& PRN, dword& PMN, dword& CCOCapability )
description: Description Gets the additional parameters of a CM_Set_Key.Cnf message, all at once, via references.
parameters: Parameters MyNonce Gets the random number that will be used to verify next message (usually 0) (via reference). YourNonce Gets the last nonce received; used to verify this message (usually 0) (via reference). PID Gets the protocol (usually 0x04) (via reference). PRN Gets the protocol Run Number (usually 0) (via reference). PMN Gets the protocol Message Number (usually 0) (via reference). CCOCapability Gets the STA's CCo capability (usually 0) (via reference).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetDestinationAddress
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetDestinationAddress ( byte[] MACAddress )
description: Description Gets the destination MAC address of a SLAC message to the output buffer. Note The destination address may differ from the simulation node's address when the node is in passive mode. This function is intended to allow filtering for the node's address in this case.
parameters: Parameters MACAddress Queries the destination address of the MME frame (to the given byte buffer).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetEthPayloadLength
syntax: Function Syntax dword SCC_SLAC_GetEthPacketLength ( )
description: Description Gets the Ethernet packet of a received MME frame.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetKeyData
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetKeyData ( byte NID[],byte NMK[] )
description: Description Gets the currently stored NID and NMK values (when automatic SLAC is used).
parameters: Parameters NID Queries the Network ID (7 byte hexadecimal number) (to the given byte buffer). NMK Queries the Network Membership Key (16 byte) (to the given byte buffer).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetLinkStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_GetLinkStatus ( )
description: Description This function retrieves the internally stored link status of the HomePlug Green PHY chip.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 No link available. 1 Link is available. 2 Link status is unknown (chip is not responding).

name: SCCSLACGetMSoundTarget
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetMSoundTarget ( byte MSoundTarget[] )
description: Description Gets the target MAC Address for the M-Sounds (mandatory value: broadcast address).
parameters: Parameters MSoundTarget Queries the address (6 byte) (to the given byte buffer).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetMVFLength
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_GetMVFLength ( )
description: Description Queries the length of the match variable field.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Length of the field.

name: SCCSLACGetOUI
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetOUI(byte OUI[]);
description: Description Gets the 3 byte OUI of a vendor specific message. Currently supported OUIs: Qualcomm (0x00B052) MStar, MediaTek and Vertexcom (0x0013D7) Lumissil (0x0016E8)
parameters: Parameters OUI Queries the vendor OUI of the chip (to the given byte buffer).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetPEVAndEVSEId
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetPEVAndEVSEId ( byte PEVID[], byte EVSEID )
description: Description Queries the PEV and EVSE IDs.
parameters: Parameters PEVID Queries the 17 byte PEV ID (to the given byte buffer). EVSEID Queries the 17 byte EVSE ID (to the given byte buffer).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetRandomValue
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetRandomValue ( byte Rnd[] )
description: Description Gets the random value transmitted with CM_MNBC_Sound.Ind .
parameters: Parameters Rnd Queries the random value transmitted with CM_MNBC_Sound.Ind (16 byte) (to the given byte buffer).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetReservedField
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_GetReservedField ( dword Index, byte Data[], dword& DataSize )
description: Description Gets one of the reserved fields of the message. For a valid message, these fields must contain only zeroes.
parameters: Parameters Index Number of the reserved field (0 or 1). Data Queries the field (to the given byte buffer). DataSize Gets the byte length of the returned data (via reference).
returnValues: Return Values Data and length of the reserved field. 0 If the field could not be retrieved. 1 otherwise

name: SCCSLACGetResponseId
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetResponseId ( byte ResponseID[] )
description: Description Queries the Response ID.
parameters: Parameters ResponseID Queries the 17 byte ID (to the given byte buffer).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACGetRespType
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_GetRespType ( )
description: Description Queries the response type.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 HLE of the STA. 0x01 Another GP STA (mandatory). 0x02-0xFF Reserved.

name: SCCSLACGetResult
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_GetResult ( )
description: Description Queries the result code (from different SLAC messages).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 Success 0x01-0xFF Reserved 0x00 Not Ready 0x01 Ready 0x02 Success 0x03 Failure 0x04 Not Required 0x05-0xFF Reserved

name: SCCSLACGetSecurityType
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_GetSecurityType ( )
description: Description Queries the security type.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 No Security (mandatory) 0x01 Public Key Signature 0x02-0xFF Reserved

name: SCCSLACGetSignalType
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_GetSignalType ( )
description: Description Queries the signal type.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 PEV S2 toggles on CPLT line (mandatory). 0x01-0xFF Reserved.

name: SCCSLACGetSourceAddress
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetSourceAddress( byte[] MACAddress );
description: Description Gets the source MAC address of the received message (might differ from local address).
parameters: Parameters MACAddress Queries the Source MAC address of the received message as byte array (to the given byte buffer).

name: SCCSLACGetSourceId
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SLAC_GetSourceId ( byte SourceID[] )
description: Description Queries the Source ID.
parameters: Parameters SourceID Queries the 17 byte ID (to the given byte buffer).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SCCSLACSetAdditionalParameter
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_SetAdditionalParameter ( dword Parameter, dword ParameterValue ) // form 1 long SCC_SLAC_SetAdditionalParameter ( dword Parameter, byte ParameterValues[] ) // form 2
description: Description Sets an additional parameter after creating a message, thus overwriting a value predefined by the specification (DIN 70121:2014-12).
parameters: Parameters Parameter ID number of the parameter, as denoted in the lists of optional parameters below each Create-function. ParameterValue Desired value of the parameter in the matching data type.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACSetAttenuation
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_SetAttenuation ( float AttenuationMean, float AttenuationDev )
description: Description Changes the probability distribution used to create attenuation characteristics when simulating a HomePlug Green PHY chip. The values apply to all SLAC sessions, yet may be set each time an M-Sound is received using the indication SCC_CM_MNBC_Sound_Ind .
parameters: Parameters AttenuationMean Attenuation Mean Value in dB. AttenuationDev Standard deviation of the distribution in dB.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACSetAttenuationRx
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_SetAttenuationRx ( float AttenuationRx )
description: Description Sets the attenuation of the Rx path, which is subtracted from the AAG values. This is can also be used to influence the calculation of attenuation values when a real chip is used, i.e. the attenuation may be artificially raised or lowered.
parameters: Parameters AttenuationRx Attenuation value.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACSetChipPresent
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_SetChipPresent ( dword ChipPresent )
description: Description Configures the SLAC protocol to run with a real HomePlug Green PHY chip, or without it. This toggles the sending of artificial attenuation data. Note This function overrides the configuration parameter <SLAC_ChipPresent>. The supplied EV / EVSE configurations for VT8770 are already configured to use a real chip - there is no need to adapt these.
parameters: Parameters ChipPresent 1 to disable chip simulation, 0 to enable chip simulation.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACSetLinkStatus
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_SetLinkStatus ( long LinkStatus )
description: Description This function overrides the internally stored link status of the HomePlug Green PHY chip. This is useful if no hardware is present.
parameters: Parameters LinkStatus Value to be set: 0: No link available 1: Link is available 2: Link status is unknown (chip is not responding)
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACSetLinkStatusPollingInterval
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_SetLinkStatusPollingInterval ( long Interval )
description: Description This function changes the polling interval for sending VS_PL_Lnk_Status or VS_Nw_Info requests to the chip. Set the interval to 0 to disable polling. Note It is recommended to use VS_Host_Action.Ind as described in SCC_Generic_VS_HostMessage_Ind instead of link status polling.
parameters: Parameters Interval Desired time interval in milliseconds.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACSetLinkStatusPollingType
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_SetLinkStatusPollingType ( dword Type ) Note It is recommended to use VS_Host_Action.Ind as described in SCC_Generic_VS_HostMessage_Ind instead of link status polling.
description: Description Changes the message(s) used for querying the link status (if activated). This overwrites the configuration parameter < SLAC_LinkStatusPollingType >.
parameters: Parameters Type Desired message configuration, where the following applies: 0 = use both available messages 1 = use only VS_PL_Lnk_Status 2 = use only VS_Nw_Info
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACSetNewKey
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_SetNewKey(byte NMK[], byte NID[])
description: Description This function stores the NMK and NID as user defined Key.
parameters: Parameters NMK Network Membership Key (16 byte) NID Network ID (7 byte hexadecimal number)
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACSetPSD
syntax: Function Syntax SCC_SLAC_SetPSD ( byte PSD[], dword PSDLength)
description: Description Fills the power spectral density map as a prerequisite for Amplitude Map Exchange. The data format is equal to the message CM_SLAC_AmpMap.Req, so the data will be sent exactly as set with this function. To deactivate Amplitude Map Exchange (default), set a map of {0..0} . To activate Amplitude Map Exchange, set a map that contains values != 0 . Note : Because the PSD is defined in values of 4 bit, only the lower half of each byte is considered.
parameters: Parameters PSD The Power Spectral Density in differences to the specified mean value and in [-dBm/Hz]. This must be a 58 or 917 byte array, depending on the setting in the XML configuration (see help pages Configuration of Vehicle SCC Nodes (Smart Charging) and Configuration of Charge Point SCC Nodes (Smart Charging) ). PSDLength Length of the byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSLACSetToggleNum
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_SLAC_SetToggleNum ( int ToggleNum )
description: Description Sets the number of toggles for the next CM_Validate.Cnf message. If this function is not used, either the value from the XML configuration or the default (2) is used. Note The number of toggles is only applied to the second CM_Validate.Cnf in a validation sequence. The first one is specified to always have ToggleNum = 0.
parameters: Parameters ToggleNum Number of received toggles (max. 255). Real systems use toggle numbers <= 3.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCStartCertificateInstallation
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_StartCertificateInstallation ( )
description: Description Schedules a CertificateInstallationReq message to be sent after the ServiceAndPaymentSelectionReq . This is only possible when in ISO 15118 PnC mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCStartCertificateUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_StartCertificateUpdate ( )
description: Description Schedules a CertificateUpdateReq message to be sent after the ServiceAndPaymentSelectioReq . This is only possible when in ISO 15118 PnC mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCStartPassive
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_StartPassive ( ) long SCC_StartPassive ( dword SLACMode )
description: Description This function activates the modeling library, but does not start an SCC simulation (i.e. a state machine). In this state, callbacks can be received, and test functions can be called.
parameters: Parameters SLACMode Controls the behavior regarding SLAC, which overrides the parameter <UseSLAC> from the XML configuration: 0 = SLAC is skipped (may be done manually) 1 = SLAC is used 2 = EV: use SLAC depending on link status (not recommended due to HomePlug Green PHY chip instabilities)
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCStartRenegotiation
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_StartRenegotiation ( )
description: Description Initiates a renegotiation procedure while inside the charge loop. Note The charge point may initiate a renegotiation procedure by sending an EVSENotification with the value ReNegotiation (see SCC_SetEVSENotification ).
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCStartSimulation
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_StartSimulation ( ) // form 1 long SCC_StartSimulation ( dword SLACMode ) // form 2
description: Description These functions start the simulation DLL in active mode. Note Until the simulation is started, no messages are sent and the API (except SCC_StartSimulation() ) has no effect!
parameters: Parameters SLACMode Controls the behavior regarding SLAC, which overrides the parameter <UseSLAC> from the XML configuration: 0 = SLAC is skipped (may be done manually) 1 = SLAC is used 2 = EV: use SLAC depending on link status (not recommended due to HomePlug Green PHY chip instabilities)
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCStopSession
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_StopSession ( dword CloseTcpConnection ) // form 1 long SCC_StopSession ( dword CloseTcpConnection, dword CloseDataLink ) // form 2
description: Description The function stops a connection immediately. If it is used within a message callback, a response to the message is not sent.
parameters: Parameters CloseTcpConnection If 1, an additional TCP close is executed CloseDataLink If 1, an additional data link close via CM_SetKey.Req is executed. This is the default behavior for CloseTcpConnection == 1
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCStopSimulation
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_StopSimulation ( )
description: Description These functions activate/deactivate the SCC simulation. Note If the simulation is deactivated, no messages are sent and the API (except SCC_StartSimulation() ) has no effect!
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCCSuspendTx
syntax: Function Syntax void SCC_SuspendTx ( long NumberOfMessages )
description: Description Skips the sending of the following messages depending on the parameter value.
parameters: Parameters NumberOfMessages Value Behavior -1 Sending of all further messages is suspended. 0 Resume. Starting from current state messages are send. >0 The following NumberOfMessages are suspended.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: SCC_TCPShutdown
syntax: Function Syntax long SCC_TCPShutdown ()
description: Description The function can be used to trigger a closing of the TCP connection. If the remote side does not respond, the TCP connection is reset.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not successful 1 Successful

name: CloseWebSocketConnection
syntax: Function Syntax MethodReturnValueType CloseWebSocketConnection ( )
description: Description This function is used to close the websocket and underlying TCP/TLS connection of the respective participant. The CSMS closes its active connection but keeps listening for new connections. The ChargingStation closes its connection to the CSMS.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values MethodReturnValueType All function calls will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values: 0 OK 1 InvalidArgument 2 NoMatchingElementFound 3 UnknownError

name: OpenWebSocketConnection
syntax: Function Syntax MethodReturnValueType OpenWebSocketConnection ( )
description: Description This function is used to open the websocket and underlying TCP/TLS connection of the respective participant. The Charging Station tries to connect to the CSMS. The CSMS starts listening for new connections.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values MethodReturnValueType All function calls will return a MethodReturnValueType indicating whether the call was successful or not. Possible values: 0 OK 1 InvalidArgument 2 NoMatchingElementFound 3 UnknownError

name: ConnectionErrorIndication
syntax: Function Syntax void ConnectionErrorIndication(WebsocketConnectionErrorType error);
description: Description The callback is called when an error regarding the WebSocket connection occured.
parameters: Parameters error Type of error: 1: Error during TCP connection setup 2: Wrong subprotocol in WebSocket handshake detected
returnValues: Return Values —

name: MemCmp
syntax: Function Syntax int memcmp(struct * dest, byte source[]); // form 1 int memcmp(byte dest[], struct * source); // form 2 int memcmp(struct * dest, struct * source); // form 3 int memcmp(byte dest[], byte source[], dword size); // form 4 int memcmp(struct dest, struct source); // form 5 int memcmp(array dest, array source); // form 6 int memcmp(union dest, union source); // form 7 int memcmp(struct dest, byte source[]); // form 8 int memcmp(array dest, byte source[]); // form 9 int memcmp(union de
description: Description Compares the bytes of the parameters. In form 3, both structs must have the same type. Forms 5-13 compare vCDL objects with themselves or byte arrays. In form 5-7, dest and source must habe the same type. In form 8-13, the vCDL object must have a fixed layout.
parameters: Parameters dest A struct / byte array / vCDL object source Another struct / byte array / vCDL object size Size of the arrays (number of bytes to compare).
returnValues: Return Values 0 if the bytes are equal; a value different from 0 if they are unequal

name: MemCpy
syntax: Function Syntax void memcpy(byte dest[], struct * source); // form 1 void memcpy(char dest[], struct * source); // form 2 void memcpy(byte dest[], long offset, struct * source); // form 3 void memcpy(char dest[], long offset, struct * source); // form 4 void memcpy(struct * dest, byte source[]); // form 5 void memcpy(struct * dest, char source[]); // form 6 void memcpy(struct * dest, byte source[], long offset); // form 7 void memcpy(struct * dest, char source[], long offset); // form 8 void mem
description: Description Copies bytes from a source to a destination. In form 9, both structs must have the same type. In other forms with structs, the arrays must be large enough to contain the struct data. In forms 18 to 22, the payload size and the struct size must be identical.
parameters: Parameters source (form 1-4, 9, 16, 17, 21): Struct whose bytes shall be copied. (form 18, 22): Payload data of a PDU whose bytes shall be copied. (form 27, 28): vCDL "bytes" value that shall be copied. (other forms): Array whose bytes shall be copied. dest (form 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 22): Struct into which the bytes shall be copied. (form 18, 19, 20, 21): Payload data of a PDU into which the bytes shall be copied. (form 23, 24, 25, 26): vCDL "bytes" value into which the bytes shall be copied. (other f
returnValues: Return Values —

name: MemCpyH2n
syntax: Function Syntax void memcpy_h2n(byte dest[], struct source); // form 1 void memcpy_h2n(byte dest[], int offset, struct source); // form 2
description: Description Copies the bytes from the struct into the array, and translates the byte order of the elements from little-endian to big-endian (h2n stands for "host to network").
parameters: Parameters source Struct whose bytes shall be copied dest Array into which the bytes shall be copied offset (form 2) Offset into the array
returnValues: Return Values —

name: MemCpyN2h
syntax: Function Syntax void memcpy_n2h(struct dest, byte source[]); // form 1 void memcpy_n2h(struct dest, byte source[], int offset); // form 2
description: Description Fills the struct with bytes from the array, and translates the byte order of the elements from big-endian to little-endian (n2h stands for "network to host").
parameters: Parameters source Array whose bytes shall be copied dest Struct into which the bytes shall be copied offset (form 2) Offset in the array where the data begins
returnValues: Return Values —

name: MemCpy_off
syntax: Function Syntax void memcpy_off( struct type dest, dword destOffset, byte source[], dword sourceOffset, dword length); // form 1 void memcpy_off( struct type dest, dword destOffset, char source[], dword sourceOffset, dword length); // form 2 void memcpy_off( byte dest[], dword destOffset, struct type source, dword sourceOffset, dword length); // form 3 void memcpy_off( char dest[], dword destOffset, struct type source, dword sourceOffset, dword length); // form 4 void memcpy_off( byte dest[], dw
description: Description Copies bytes from a source to destination, giving a destination start offset. The size of the destination must be at least destOffset + length.
parameters: Parameters dest (form 1, 2): Struct into which the bytes shall be copied. (form, 9, 10, 11): vCDL object into which the bytes shall be copied. (other forms): Array into which the bytes shall be copied. source (form 3, 4): Struct from which the bytes shall be copied. (form 12, 13, 14): vCDL object from which the bytes shall be copied. (other forms): Array from which the bytes shall be copied. destOffset Start offset in the destination struct or array. sourceOffset Start offset int the source stru
returnValues: Return Values —

name: SysBeginVariableStructUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax long sysBeginVariableStructUpdate(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1 long sysBeginVariableStructUpdate(sysvarName); // form 2
description: Description Starts the update of several elements of a system variable of type struct or generic array . Use this function and the corresponding sysEndVariableStructUpdate to change several elements at the same time without the variable having an intermediate value where only some elements are changed. Each call must be followed by a call to sysEndVariableStructUpdate , else the variable value will not change.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable SysVarName: Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". SysVarName Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::".
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful -1 variable was not found

name: SysCreateVariableFilter
syntax: Function Syntax long sysCreateVariableFilter(long filterType); // form 1 long sysCreateVariableFilter(long filterType, char filterName[]); // form 2
description: Description Creates a new variable filter behind the node calling the function. If no filterName is given, the default filter with an empty name is created.
parameters: Parameters filterType Specifies the filter behavior: 0: Stop Filter. Stops all variables that are added to it. 1: Pass Filter. Stops all variables that are not added to it. filterName The name of the new filter. The name is used to identify the filter later, when variables or namespaces are added. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error, function successful. 1 The filter type is not valid. The filter cannot be created. 4 A filter with the given name already exists. The filter cannot be created. Note: if no name or an empty name was given, this return value means that the default filter already exists. It cannot be created a second time.

name: SysDefineNameSpace
syntax: Function Syntax long sysDefineNamespace(char namespace[]);
description: Description Defines a namespace.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace.
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function

name: SysDefineVariableData
syntax: Function Syntax long sysDefineVariableData(char namespace[], char variable[], byte initialData[], long initialSize);
description: Description Defines a variable of data type.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable. initialData Initial value of the variable. initialSize Initial size (in bytes) of the variable. Must not exceed the length of the initialData array.
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function

name: SysDefineVariableFloat
syntax: Function Syntax long sysDefineVariableFloat(char namespace[], char variable[], double initialValue); // form 1 long sysDefineVariableFloat(char namespace[], char variable[], double initialValue, double minimum, double maximum); // form 2
description: Description Defines a variable of the float type.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable. initialValue Initial value of the variable. minimum The minimum value of the variable. maximum The maximum value of the variable.
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function 5 given minimum is larger than the given maximum

name: SysDefineVariableFloatArray
syntax: Function Syntax long sysDefineVariableFloatArray(char namespace[], char vari-able[], float initialValues[], long arraySize); // form 1 long sysDefineVariableFloatArray(char namespace[], char variable[], double initialValues[], long arraySize, double minimum, double maximum); // form 2
description: Description Defines a variable of the float[] type.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable. initialValue Initial value of the variable. arraySize Number of values in the array. minimum The minimum value of the variable. maximum The maximum value of the variable.
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function 5 given minimum is larger than the given maximum

name: SysDefineVariableLong
syntax: Function Syntax long sysDefineVariableLong (char namespace[], char variable[], long initialValue); // form 1 long sysDefineVariableLong (char namespace[], char variable[], long initialValue, long minimum, long maximum); // form 2
description: Description Defines a variable of the long type.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable. initialValue Initial value of the variable. minimum The minimum value of the variable. maximum The maximum value of the variable.
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function 5 given minimum is larger than the given maximum

name: SysDefineVariableLongArray
syntax: Function Syntax long sysDefineVariableLongArray (char namespace[], char variable[], long initialValues[], long arraySize); // form 1 long sysDefineVariableLongArray (char namespace[], char variable[], long initialValues[], long arraySize, long minimum, long maximum); // form 2
description: Description Defines a variable of the long[] type.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable. initialValue Initial value of the variable. arraySize Number of values in the array. minimum The minimum value of the variable. maximum The maximum value of the variable.
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function 5 given minimum is larger than the given maximum

name: SysDefineVariableLongLong
syntax: Function Syntax long sysDefineVariableLongLong(char namespace[], char variable[], int64 initialValue);
description: Description Defines a variable of the Int64 type.
parameters: Parameters namespace name of the namespace variable name of the variable initialValue initial value of the variable

name: SysDefineVariableString
syntax: Function Syntax long sysDefineVariableString(char namespace[], char variable[], char initialValue[]);
description: Description Defines a variable of the String (char[]) type.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable. initialValue Initial value of the variable.
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function

name: SysEndVariableStructUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax long sysEndVariableStructUpdate(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1 long sysEndVariableStructUpdate(sysvarName); // form 2
description: Description Ends the update of several elements of a system variable of type struct or generic array . Use this function and the corresponding sysBeginVariableStructUpdate to change several elements at the same time without the variable having an intermediate value where only some elements are changed.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable SysVarName: Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". SysVarName Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::".
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful -1 variable was not found

name: SysFilterAddNamespace
syntax: Function Syntax long sysFilterAddNamespace(char namespace[]); // form 1 long sysFilterAddNamespace(char namespace[], char filterName[]); // form 2
description: Description Adds a namespace to the variable filter. If no filterName is given, the namespace is added to the default filter.
parameters: Parameters namespace The namespace to add. The existence of the namespace is not checked, and adding a namespace that is not defined is not an error. If the namespace gets defined after it is added to the filter, it is affected by the filter normally. filterName The name of the filter the namespace should be added to. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error, function successful. 2 The variable filter does not exist. The namespace cannot be added. 3 The variable is already added to the filter. The namespace cannot be added.

name: SysFilterAddVariable
syntax: Function Syntax long sysFilterAddVariable(sysVar variable); // form 1 long sysFilterAddVariable(sysVar variable, char filterName[]); // form 2 long sysFilterAddVariable(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 3 long sysFilterAddVariable(char namespace[], char variable[], char filterName[]); // form 4
description: Description Adds a variable to a filter. If no filterName is given, the variable will be added to the default filter.
parameters: Parameters variable The variable to add. Note If a struct member is specified, the whole struct will be added. filterName The name of the filter the variable should be added to. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely. namespace The namespace that contains the variable. variable (char[]) The name of the variable.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error, function successful. 2 The variable filter does not exist. The variable cannot be added. 3 The variable is already added to the filter. 6 The variable does not exist. The variable cannot be added.

name: SysFilterRemoveNamespace
syntax: Function Syntax long sysFilterRemoveNamespace(char namespace[]); // form 1 long sysFilterRemoveNamespace(char namespace[], char filterName[]); // form 2
description: Description Removes a namespace from the variable filter. If no filterName is given, the namespace is removed from the default filter.
parameters: Parameters namespace The namespace to remove. filterName The name of the filter the namespace should be removed from. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error, function successful. 2 The variable filter does not exist. The namespace cannot be removed. 3 The namespace is not filtered by the filter. The namespace cannot be removed.

name: SysFilterRemoveVariable
syntax: Function Syntax long sysFilterRemoveVariable(sysVar variable); // form 1 long sysFilterRemoveVariable(sysVar variable, char filterName[]); // form 2 long sysFilterRemoveVariable(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 3 long sysFilterRemoveVariable(char namespace[], char variable[], char filterName[]); // form 4
description: Description Removes a variable from a variable filter. If no filterName is given, the variable will be removed from the default filter.
parameters: Parameters variable The variable to remove. Note If a struct member is specified, the whole struct will be removed. filterName The name of the filter the variable should be removed from. Note An empty filterName refers to the default filter and can be omitted completely. namespace The namespace that contains the variable. variable (char[]) The name of the variable.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error, function successful. 2 The variable filter does not exist. The variable cannot be removed. 5 The variable is not filtered by the filter. The variable cannot be removed. 6 The variable does not exist. The variable cannot be removed.

name: SysGetOrigTimeNS
syntax: Function Syntax int64 sysGetOrigTimeNS(SysVarName);
description: Description Returns the original time stamp of the last update to the variable value, before time synchronization may have changed it.
parameters: Parameters SysVarName Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::".
returnValues: Return Values Original time stamp of the last update to the variable value in nanoseconds since measurement start.

name: SysGetVariableArrayLength
syntax: Function Syntax dword sysGetVariableArrayLength(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1 dword sysGetVariableArrayLength(SysVarName); // form 2
description: Description Returns the length of the array of the system variable. Note Direct access to values from system variables . An access to the whole array is with this variant not possible. The function can also be used for specific elements of a system variable of type struct or generic array . For this, add the element to the name of the variable. If you directly give the element name to the function instead of using strings, precede the name by sysvarMember:: instead of sysvar:: . Example : sysvar
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable. SysVarName Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::".
returnValues: Return Values For system variables of type data, returns the current size (in bytes) of the value. For system variables of type long or float array, returns the number of elements in the array. For system variables of type string, returns the length of the string, excluding the terminating 0 character. For system variables of type long or float, returns 1.

name: SysGetVariableArraySize
syntax: Function Syntax dword sysGetVariableArraySize(sysvar variable) // form 1 long sysGetVariableArraySize(char namespace[], char variable[], dword& arraySize) // form 2
description: Description Retrieves the current array size of a system variable which has a generic array data type. Note This function can't be used for system variables of type Data, IntArray or FloatArray; use sysGetVariableArrayLength for these. The function can also be used for specific elements of a system variable of type struct or generic array. For this, add the element to the name of the variable. If you directly give the element name to the function instead of using strings, precede the name by sys
parameters: Parameters variable (form 1) Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::". namespace (form 2) Name of the namespace variable (form 2) Name of the variable arraySize (form 2) current size of the array (out-parameter)
returnValues: Return Values Form 1 current size of the array Form 2 error code: 0 – no error 2 – variable not found 4 – variable is not of a generic array type

name: SysGetVariableData
syntax: Function Syntax long sysGetVariableData(char namespace[], char variable[], byte buffer[], long& copiedBytes); // form 1 long sysGetVariableData(SysVarName, byte buffer[], long& copiedBytes); // form 2
description: Description Returns the value of a variable of the type data, string, struct or generic array. Note The function can also be used for specific elements of a system variable of type struct or generic array . For this, add the element to the name of the variable. If you directly give the element name to the function instead of using strings, precede the name by sysvarMember:: instead of sysvar:: . Example : sysvarMember::SomeNamespace::SomeStructVariable.SomeArrayMember[0] For variables of type st
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable. buffer Buffer which takes the value of the variable. copiedBytes Receives the number of bytes which were copied into the buffer. If the buffer is too small, no bytes will be copied; else the parameter will contain the current size of the variable. SysVarName Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::".
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful 1 namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 3 no writing right for the namespace available 4 the variable has no suitable type for the function 5 the buffer is too small for the value

name: SysGetVariableDescriptionForValue
syntax: Function Syntax long sysGetVariableDescriptionForValue(SysVarName, long value, char buffer[], long bufferSize); // form 1 long sysGetVariableDescriptionForValue(char namespace[], char variable[], long value, char buffer[], long bufferSize); // form 2
description: Description Retrieves a description for a value of a system variable of type long or long array. In this way, you can access the value table of the system variable.
parameters: Parameters namespace Name of the namespace. variable Name of the variable. value Value for which the description shall be retrieved. buffer Buffer which receives the description. bufferSize Size of the buffer. SysVarName Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::".
returnValues: Return Values 0 no error, function successful 2 variable was not found or second try to define the same variable 5 there is no description of this value for the variable 6 the buffer is not large enough for the description

name: SysGetVariableDWord
syntax: Function Syntax dword sysGetVariableDWord(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1 dword sysGetVariableDWord(SysVarName); // form 2 long sysGetVariableDWord(char namespace[], char variable[], dword& value); // form 3 long sysGetVariableDWord(SysVarName, dword& value); // form 4
description: Description Returns the value of a variable of type unsigned 32 bit integer. Note Direct access to values from system variables . The function can also be used for specific elements of a system variable of type struct or generic array . For this, add the element to the name of the variable. If you directly give the element name to the function instead of using strings, precede the name by sysvarMember:: instead of sysvar:: . Example : sysvarMember::SomeNamespace::SomeStructVariable.SomeArrayMemb

returnValues: Return Values 1 if the variable has a signed numeric type 0 otherwise

name: SysResetValues

returnValues: Return Values 0 Success 1 At least one variable in the namespace has no initial value assigned -1 The namespace doesn't exist

name: SysSetAnalysIsOnlyVariable
syntax: Function Syntax void sysSetAnalysisOnlyVariable(SysVarName, long anlyzLocal);
description: Description Determines whether the variable is meant to be used for analysis purposes. If this is true (anlyzLocal is set to 1), and the variable is changed in a CAPL program in the Measurement setup, the value change is not transmitted to the realtime part of CANoe, but used immediately in the analysis area. This is the default. If it is false (anlyzLocal is set to 0), value changes are always transmitted to the realtime part. Note Direct access to values from system variables .
parameters: Parameters anlyzLocal Defines whether the variable shall be used only in the analysis area of CANoe. SysVarName Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::".
returnValues: Return Values —

For this, add the element to the name of the variable. If you dir
parameters: Parameters variable (form 1) Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". returnValues: Return Values 0 no error 2 variable not found 4 variable is not of a generic array type, or the array type doesn't allow changing the size, or the new size is smaller than the minimum or bigger than the maximum size of the array type.

Floating point or integer numbers and arrays are assigned to the corresponding types of system variables (form 2 – 6). The assignment is executed asynchronously. name: SysVarFilterCreate

description: Description Creates a new variable filter behind the node calling the function.
parameters: Parameters filterType Specifies the filter behavior. 0: Stops all variables that are added to the list 1: By-Pass all variable values that are added to the list on change 2: By-Pass all variables values that are added to the list on update filterName Name of the specific filter.
returnValues: Return Values 0 No error, function successful. 1 Invalid CAPL filterType . 2 CAPL variable filter does not exist. 3 Variable or namespace already filtered. 4 CAPL SysVarFilter name already taken. 5 Variable or namespace not filtered. 6 Variable or namespace does not exist.

returnValues: Return Values 0 No error, function successful. 1 Namespace was not found or second try to define the same namespace. 2 Variable was not found or second try to define the same variable. 3 No writing right for the namespace available. 4 Variable has no suitable type for the function. 5 Given minimum is larger than the given maximum.

name: OnIpsecSadbAcquire
syntax: Function Syntax void OnIpsecSadbAcquire(ip_Endpoint source, ip_Endpoint destination, long policyHandle);
description: Description Get a parameter from a security policy record.
parameters: Parameters source Source endpoint for which the security association is acquired. destination Destination endpoint for which the security association is acquired. policyHandle Handle to the security policy which caused the acquire callback.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TCPIPOnIpAddressAdded
syntax: Function Syntax void OnIpAddressAdded(dword ifIndex, ip_address address, dword prefix, dword origin);
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called when a new address is added to a network interface. Note The callback is only available for simulation nodes using the individual TCP/IP stack instance. Check this setting in the CANoe DE product TCP/IP Stack configuration dialog.
parameters: Parameters ifIndex The 1-based network interface index. address The added IP address. prefix The prefix of the added address. It defines the number of bits belonging to the network part of the address. origin Defines the initiator for adding this address. Possible values are: ORIGIN_UNKNOWN = 0, ORIGIN_USER = 1, ORIGIN_SYSTEM = 2, ORIGIN_DHCPv4CLIENT = 3, ORIGIN_DHCPv6CLIENT = 4, ORIGIN_RFC3927CLIENT = 5
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TCPIPOnIpAddressRemoved
syntax: Function Syntax void OnIpAddressRemoved(dword ifIndex, ip_address address, dword prefix, dword origin);
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called when a address gets removed from a network interface. Note The callback is only available for simulation nodes using the individual TCP/IP stack instance. Check this setting in the CANoe DE product TCP/IP Stack configuration dialog.
parameters: Parameters ifIndex The 1-based network interface index. address The removed IP address. prefix The prefix of the removed address. It defines the number of bits belonging to the network part of the address. origin Defines the initiator for removing this address. Possible values are: ORIGIN_UNKNOWN = 0, ORIGIN_USER = 1, ORIGIN_SYSTEM = 2, ORIGIN_DHCPv4CLIENT = 3, ORIGIN_DHCPv6CLIENT = 4, ORIGIN_RFC3927CLIENT = 5
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TCPIPOnIpGetHostByName
syntax: Function Syntax void OnIpGetHostByName(long result, char hostname[], dword ipv4Address[], dword count); // from 1 void OnIpGetHostByName(long result, char hostname[], byte ipv6Address[][], dword count); // from 2
description: Description The callback is called if a previous call of IpGetHostByName is returned with error code WSAEWOULDBLOCK (10035) and the dissolve of the address is completed.
parameters: Parameters result The result of the function. See the Winsock 2 Error Codes for possible errors. Typical values are: result: 0: The function completed successfully. WSAHOST_NOT_FOUND (11001): Host not found. hostname The hostname which was resolved by the function. ipv4Address Array in which the determined IPv4 addresses are written. ipv6Address Array in which the determined IPv6 addresses are written. count Number of determined addresses.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TCPIPOnIpReceivePrepare
syntax: Function Syntax long OnIpReceivePrepare(ethernetPacket * packet);
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called right before a received packet will be dispatched to the TCP/IP stack. It is possible to manipulate the content of the packet or to block the receiving of the package from the bus. Note The callback is only available for simulation nodes using the individual TCP/IP stack instance. Check this setting in the CANoe DE product TCP/IP Stack configuration dialog.
parameters: Parameters packet The received Ethernet packet.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Block the Ethernet packet. 1 Pass the Ethernet packet to the TCP/IP stack.

name: TCPIPOnIpSendPrepare
syntax: Function Syntax long OnIpSendPrepare( dword socket, ethernetPacket * packet);
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called right before a packet will be sent by the TCP/IP stack. It is possible to manipulate the content of the packet or to block the sending of the package on the bus. Note The callback is only available for simulation nodes using the individual TCP/IP stack instance. Check this setting in the CANoe DE product TCP/IP Stack configuration dialog.
parameters: Parameters socket The socket handle. packet The Ethernet packet which will be send.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Block the Ethernet packet. 1 Send the Ethernet packet.

name: TCPIPOnTcpClose
syntax: Function Syntax void OnTcpClose( dword socket, long result);
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called when a TCP socket receives a close notification, when the remote station terminates the connection with TcpClose or TcpShutdown . To release the resources of the socket, TcpClose must be called.
parameters: Parameters socket The socket handle or socket object. result The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is 0. Otherwise the value is an error code . Typical errors are interrupt of the connection, timeout or reset.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TCPIPOnTcpConnect
syntax: Function Syntax void OnTcpConnect( dword socket, long result);
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called when an asynchronous connection operation completes. This means that the connection to the remote station has been established. It is not yet certain at this time that the remote station has accepted the connection with TcpAccept . If a listen socket of a remote station is blocked, error 10061 (Connection refused) is output in the result parameter.
parameters: Parameters socket The socket handle or socket object. result The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is 0. Otherwise the value is an error code .   Error code 10061 (Connection refused): The listen socket of a server is occupied by another connection that has not yet been accepted with TcpAccept . Because the internal queue for incoming connections is set to 1 in the CANoe DE product , only one node can ever queue up there. All other
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TCPIPOnTcpListen
syntax: Function Syntax void OnTcpListen( dword socket, long result)
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called when TcpListen was called and a connection request for the specified socket is received. The connection must be accepted with TcpAccept because the listen socket otherwise remains blocked for other clients. This block is evident for a client in OnTcpConnect from the output of error code 10061 (Connection refused).
parameters: Parameters socket The socket handle or socket object. If the handle is valid (not equal to ~0), it corresponds to the socket that was used for TcpListen . result The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is zero. Otherwise the value is an error code .
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TCPIPOnTcpReceive
syntax: Function Syntax void OnTcpReceive( dword socket, long result, dword address, dword port, char buffer[], dword size); // form 1 void OnTcpReceive( dword socket, long result, dword address, dword port, byte buffer[], dword size); // form 2 void OnTcpReceive( dword socket, long result, byte ipv6Address[], dword port, char buffer[], dword size); // form 3 void OnTcpReceive( dword socket, long result, byte ipv6Address[], dword port, byte buffer[], dword size); // form 4 void OnTcpReceive( d
description: Description If the CAPL program implements this callback it is called when a receive operation on a TCP socket completes. The callback is called even if the receive operation completes synchronously. The stack contains a data queue that is reduced by TcpReceive as soon as data are located there. So that additional data from the data queue will be received in the future for the socket, TcpReceive must be called up again within the callback. In this case it is possible that the OnTcpReceive callba
parameters: Parameters socket The socket handle or socket object. result The specific result code of the operation. If the operation completed successfully the value is zero. Otherwise the value is an error code . If result = 0 and size = 0, socket was closed by the communication peer, see example. address The numerical remote IPv4 address of the location which sent the data. ipv6Address The remote IPv6 address in a 16 byte array . port The remote port of the location which sent the data in host-byte order.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: TCPIPOnTcpSend
syntax: Function Syntax void OnTcpSend( dword socket, long result, char buffer[], dword size);
