description: Description Sets the threshold value for differentiating between high and low levels of the channels 9…16 of a digital module VT2516 . There is only one threshold setting for all eight channels together. Voltages at the input exceeding this threshold value are evaluated as high level and voltages undershooting it are evaluated as low level.
parameters: Parameters Target Name of the system variable/namespace that will be affected by this function call. Threshold Voltage value in volts in the range from 0…25 V.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful call -1 Call error -2 The namespace on which the command was called does not exist, is not a valid VT System namespace or does not support this command. -3 The specified threshold is not valid -4 The function wasn't called in the context of the main method of a test module. So it is not possible to wait until the setting will be taken over from the VT System . Otherwise the call was successful but it is not sure if the settings have been taken over already when th

name: VTSvtsSetTransferCycle
syntax: Function Syntax long vtsSetTransferCycle (char Target[], double CycleTime);
description: Description Sets the cycle time for retrieving the measured value of a system variable from the VT System and writing it to the corresponding system variable.
parameters: Parameters Target Name of the system variable/namespace that will be affected by this function call. CycleTime Defines the cycle time in seconds. The only available cycle times are 1 ms (= 0,001), 2 ms, 5 ms, 10 ms, 20 ms, 50 ms, 100 ms, 200 ms, 500 ms, 1 s, 2 s, 5 s and 10 s. Please note that not all cycle times are available for all measurement values. See possible settings in the VT System configuration dialog. If an invalid cycle time is specified, the call will fail with a feedback value of
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful call -1 Call error, i.e. system variable does not belong to a valid VT System read value or cycle time is invalid. -4 Invalid system variable

name: VTSvtsSetTriggerParams
syntax: Function Syntax long vtsSetTriggerParams (char Target[], eVTSTrigger Trigger, eVTSTriggerSourceChannel SourceChannel, eVTSTriggerEdgeType EdgeType);
description: Description The function configures basic parameters for triggers. Further parameters can be set with the function vtsSetTriggerParamsEx . You can find additional information about VT System triggers on the page Triggers .
parameters: Parameters Target Specifies which trigger is configured: Values see eVTSTrigger Trigger Specifies which trigger is configured ( VT1004 module: 1-8) SourceChannel Specifies to which channel the configured trigger is connected: Values see eVTSTriggerSourceChannel EdgeType Values see eVTSTriggerEdgeType If a triggered is configured to level type using vtsSetTriggerParamsEx this parameter sets which level is detected.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful call -1 Call error -2 The namespace on which the command was called does not exist, is not a valid VT System namespace or does not support this command.

name: VTSvtsSetTriggerParamsEx
syntax: Function Syntax long vtsSetTriggerParamsEx (char Target[], eVTSTrigger Trigger, double MinPulseWidth, double RestartTime, dword PreTriggers, dword RestartTriggers, dword ThresholdEventCount, dword ExtSettings);
description: Description The function configures additional parameters for VT System triggers. Basic parameters are configured using vtsSetTriggerParams . You can find additional information about VT System triggers on the page Triggers .
parameters: Parameters Target Name of the system variable/namespace that will be affected by this function call. Trigger Specifies which trigger is configured. VT1004 module: Values see eVTSTrigger MinPulseWidth Specifies that the triggers fires only if no opposite edge or level event occurs within the specified time after the triggering event. This way you can restrict the trigger to pulses that take at least as long as specified with this parameter. E.g. for a trigger that detects rising edges and a MinPu
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful call -1 Call error -2 The namespace on which the command was called does not exist, is not a valid VT System namespace or does not support this command.

name: VTSvtsSetWFParams
syntax: Function Syntax long vtsSetWFParams (char Target[], double timeIncrement, double pause, dword numOfRepeats); // form 1 long SysVarNamespace.SetWFParams (char Target[], double timeIncrement, double pause, dword numOfRepeats, double startDelay, dword startPoint); // form 2
description: Description The function configures the parameters for the output of a voltage or resistance curve or bitstream.
parameters: Parameters Target Name of the system variable/namespace that will be affected by this function call. timeIncrement Specifies how long the value of a specified interpolation point is to be stimulated before transitioning to the next interpolation point. Depending on the module and the wave form different ranges for timeIncrement are valid: Voltage curves on a VT7001 module: timeIncrement can be 0.000001(1 µs)…0.065 (65 ms). Note that output accuracy depends highly on environmental conditions for
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful call -1 Error -2 The namespace on which the command was called does not exist, is not a valid VT System namespace or does not support this command. -3 One of the specified parameters is not valid.

name: VTSvtsStartStimulation
syntax: Function Syntax long vtsStartStimulation (char Target[]);
description: Description Starts the output of a stimulation signal. A corresponding output mode must be set in advance.
parameters: Parameters Target Name of the system variable/namespace that will be affected by this function call.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful call -1 Call error -2 The namespace on which the command was called does not exist, is not a valid VT System namespace or does not support this command. -4 The function wasn't called in the context of the main method of a test module. So it is not possible to wait until the setting will be taken over from the VT System. Otherwise the call was successful but it is not sure if the settings have been taken over already when the call returns.

name: VTSvtsStartTrigger
syntax: Function Syntax long SysVarNamespace.SetTriggerParams (eVTSTrigger Trigger, dword SourceChannel, dword EdgeType);
description: Description The function starts the specified trigger. It also resets the event counter system variable that is associated to the trigger to 0. To use a trigger it has to be configured using the functions vtsSetTriggerParams and vtsSetTriggerParamsEx . You can find additional information about VT System triggers on the page Triggers .
parameters: Parameters Target Name of the system variable/namespace that will be affected by this function call. Trigger Specifies which trigger is configured. VT1004 / VT1104 module: Values see eVTSTrigger
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful call -1 Call error -2 The namespace on which the command was called does not exist, is not a valid VT System namespace or does not support this command.

name: VTSvtsStopStimulation
syntax: Function Syntax long vtsStopStimulation (char Target[]);
description: Description Stops the output of a stimulation signal. This resets the stimulation mode. Therefore it is not sufficient to call vtsStartStimulation to start the output again. You also have to restore the stimulation mode, e.g. by calling vtsSetStimulationMode . At the end of the execution of the command there is a short break before other commands will be executed. This means that the next functions will be executed after a short delay. With this procedure ensures that the stop command is execute
parameters: Parameters Target Name of the system variable/namespace that will be affected by this function call.
returnValues: Return Values 0 Successful call -1 Call error -2 The namespace on which the command was called does not exist, is not a valid VT System namespace or does not support this command. -4 The function wasn't called in the context of the main method of a test module. So it is not possible to wait until the setting will be taken over from the VT System. Otherwise the call was successful but it is not sure if the settings have been taken over already when the call returns.

name: OnXcpError
syntax: Function Syntax void OnXcpError (char ecuQualifier[], byte xcpCmd, byte xcpErrorCode);
description: Description Whenever the XCP slaves answers with a negative response (i.e. not 0xFF) the OnXCPError callback function is called.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window . xcpCmd XCP command sent by CANoe . xcpErrorCode Error code returned by the XCP slave.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: OnXcpEvent
syntax: Function Syntax void OnXcpEvent(char ecuQualifier[], byte data[], long dataSize);
description: Description This callback provides access to data that is sent from an XCP or CCP slave in an Event packet. The first data byte contains the type of the Event (named Code in the XCP specification).
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window . data Byte array with the event data. dataSize Size of the event data.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: XCPActivate
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpActivate (char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1 long xcpActivate (sysvar sysvar);
description: Description Activates an a2l parameter for upload and DAQ measurement. Note Using system variables structs , the function must be used for the variable valid for the entire struct but not for single members.
parameters: Parameters namespace Namespace of the corresponding system variable. variable Name of the corresponding system variable. sysVar Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::"
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 System variable was not found -2 Operation not allowed

name: XCPActivateMeasurementGroup
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpActivateMeasurementGroup(char[] ecuQualifier, char[] groupName);
description: Description Changes the measurement group that will be used for the next connection to the XCP/CCP device.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window . groupName Name of the measurement group.
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Device with this name is not existing -2 Operation not allowed – already connected -5 groupName does not exist

name: XCPConnect
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpConnect (char ecuQualifier[]);
description: Description Establishes a connection to the XCP/CCP device and starts the configured DAQ measurement. If the connection is successfully depends on the response of the device. Use xcpIsConnected to be aware of the connection. After establishing the connection and before start of the DAQ measurement the callback function OnXcpConnect is called. Only for FlexRay: Iit is possible to use same NAX in several XCP devices. But only one such device can be connected at the moment. To do this set AutoConne
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window .
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Device with this name is not existing -2 Operation not allowed – already connected -6 Only for FlexRay: Another ECU with the same FlexRay NAX is already connected. Hint : Disconnect connected ECU first.

name: XCPDeactivate
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpDeactivate (char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1 long xcpDeactivate (sysvar sysvar); // form 2
description: Description Upload and DAQ measurement will be deactivated. Note Using system variables structs , the function must be used for the variable valid for the entire struct but not for single members.
parameters: Parameters namespace Namespace of the corresponding system variable. variable Name of the corresponding system variable. sysvar Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysVar::"
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 System variable was not found -2 Operation not allowed

name: XCPDeactivateAll
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpDeactivateAll(char[] ecuQualifier);
description: Description Deactivates auto read of all configured parameters of the active measurement group.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window .
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Device was not found

name: XCPDisconnect
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpDisconnect(char ecuQualifier[]);
description: Description Disconnects from a XCP/CCP device. Use xcpIsConnected to be aware of the disconnection. The callback function OnXcpDisconnect is called after the ECU acknowledged the disconnect command.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window .
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 Device with this name is not existing -2 Operation not allowed – already disconnected

name: XCPGetCalPage
syntax: Function Syntax void void xcpGetCalPage(char ecuQualifier[], byte mode, byte segmentNr);
description: Description If the XCP slave device supports calibration data page switching, this command gets the currently active page and access mode. The callback returns the currently active calibration data page of the ECU.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window . mode Access mode of the currently active page. The values may be 0x01 (ECU access) or 0x02 (XCP access). All other values are invalid. segmentNr Logical data segment number. pageNr Logical data page number. Usually 0 identifies the page in RAM and 1 the page in FLASH memory. Option AMD/XCP can only work on parameters stored in RAM.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: XCPGetPollingCycle
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpGetPollingCycle(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1 long xcpGetPollingCycle(sysvar sysvar); // form 2
description: Description Returns the cycle time of a parameter, if the read mode of the parameter is set to polling .
parameters: Parameters namespace Namespace of the corresponding system variable. variable Name of the corresponding system variable. sysvar Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysvar::"
returnValues: Return Values > 0 Cycle time -1 System variable of the parameter was not found -2 Operation not allowed

name: XCPIsConnected
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpIsConnected (char ecuQualifier[]);
description: Description Returns the current connection status of a XCP/CCP device.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window .
returnValues: Return Values 0 Not connected 1 Connected -1 A device with this name does not exist

name: XCPSendRaw
syntax: Function Syntax void xcpSendRaw(char ecuQualifier[], byte data[], long dataSize, byte expectResponse);
description: Description Sends any data as control command to an XCP/CCP device. The first data byte defines the control command code. The callback is called with the response to a raw XCP command sent by xcpSendRaw .
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window . data Byte array of the data to be sent. dataSize Length of the data to be sent. expectResponse 0: The XCP slave will not respond to this command 1: The XCP slave is expected to respond to this command and the callback will be called
returnValues: Return Values —

name: XCPSetCalPage
syntax: Function Syntax void xcpSetCalPage(char ecuQualifier[], byte mode, byte segmentNr, byte pageNr);
description: Description If the XCP slave device supports calibration data page switching, this command sets the current page and access mode. The callback returns that the ECU switched the calibration data page.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window . mode Bit field defined in the XCP specification. Bit 7 Use for all segments, i.e. parameter segmentNr is ignored. Bit 1 The slave device XCP driver will access the given page. Bit 0 The given page will be used by the slave device application. segmentNr Logical data segment number. Ignored if bit 7 of the mode parameter is set to 1. pageNr Logical data page number. Usually 0 identifies the page in RAM and 1 the pag
returnValues: Return Values —

name: XCPSetPollingCycle
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpSetPollingCycle(char namespace[], char variable[], long cycleTime); // form 1 long xcpSetPollingCycle(sysvar sysvar, long cycleTime); // form 2
description: Description Sets the cycle time of a parameter, if the read mode of the parameter is set to polling .
parameters: Parameters namespace Namespace of the corresponding system variable. variable Name of the corresponding system variable. sysvar Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysvar::" cycleTime Cycle time with which the parameter is measured.
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 System variable of the parameter was not found -2 Operation not allowed

name: XCPUpload
syntax: Function Syntax long xcpUpload(char namespace[], char variable[]); // form 1 long xcpUpload(sysvar sysvar); // form 2
description: Description Initiates an upload of the XCP parameter from ECU and updates the dedicated system variable. After finishing of the upload the callback function OnXcpUpload is called to indicate the upload status. Use the return value of xcpUpload to check if an error occurred during initiation of the upload. Use the errorIndication parameter of the OnXcpUpload callback function to check for errors occurred during the upload, if an error during call of xcpUpload occurs OnXcpUpload is not called.
parameters: Parameters namespace Namespace of the corresponding system variable. variable Name of the corresponding system variable. sysvar Name of the fully qualified name of the system variable, including all namespaces, separated by "::". The name must be preceded by "sysvar::" errorIndication 0: OK -4: Device is not connected
returnValues: Return Values 0 OK -1 System variable of the parameter was not found -2 Operation not allowed

name: XCPUserCommand
syntax: Function Syntax void xcpUserCommand(char ecuQualifier[], byte data[], long dataSize);
description: Description Sends user-defined data to the XCP slave. The callback returns the response on the user command.
parameters: Parameters ecuQualifier Name of the device, configured within the XCP/CCP Window . data Byte array for user-defined data. dataSize Size of the user-defined data.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclAbstractConsumedEventGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclConsumedEventID cclAbstractConsumedEventGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns an abstract binding specific consumer side service event port ID, which can be used for event registration. The returned ID can be used in all consumer side service event port functions (not abstract binding specific) but not vice versa. All event port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a consumer side service event port with abstract binding. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the event port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service event port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

syntax: Function Syntax cclConsumedFieldID cclAbstractConsumedFieldGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns an abstract binding specific consumer side service field port ID, which can be used for field registration. The returned ID can be used in all consumer side service field port functions (not abstract binding specific) but not vice versa. returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the field port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDFIELDPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service field port known to the system. CCL_FIELDNOTSUBSC
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclAbstractCreateAddress
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclAddressID; cclAddressID cclAbstractCreateAddress(cclServiceID serviceID, const char* endPointName);
description: Description Creates an abstract binding specific address, which can be used for dynamically adding service endpoints. All address IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to create an address. endPointName The name of the endpoint for which to create the address. The name must be unique within all endpoints of a service when the resulting address is used to dynamically add service endpoints (functions cclAddConsumerByAddress , cclAddProviderByAddress ).
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the address. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclAbstractEventProviderGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclEventProviderID cclAbstractEventProviderGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns an abstract binding specific service event provider ID, which can be used for event registration. The returned ID can be used in all service event provider functions (not abstract binding specific) but not vice versa. All event provider IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a service event provider with abstract binding. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the event provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDEVENTPROVIDERPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service event port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

The returned ID can be used in all service field provider functions (not abstract binding specific) but not vice versa. All field provider IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.

In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDFIELDPROVIDERPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service field port known to the system. CCL_FIELDNOTS

name: cclAbstractGetBindingID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclAbstractGetBindingID(cclAddressID addressID, int32_t* bindingID);
description: Description Returns the binding identifier associated with an abstract binding specific address. The binding identifier can be used to get access to service endpoints and ports.
parameters: Parameters addressID The ID of an abstract binding specific address (returned by cclAbstractCreateAddress function or obtained from a service discovery event routine). bindingID This variable will be set to the binding identifier associated with given address.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The binding identifier was successfully read. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDBINDING The addressID parameter is not bound by an abstract binding block.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclAbstractGetDisplayName
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclAbstractGetDisplayName(cclAddressID addressID, char* buffer, int32_t bufferSize);
description: Description Returns the human readable name associated with an abstract binding specific address.
parameters: Parameters addressID The ID of an abstract binding specific address (returned by cclAbstractCreateAddress function or obtained from a service discovery event routine). buffer Buffer to be filled with the address name. bufferSize Maximum number of characters to write to given buffer (including zero-termination).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The name was successfully read. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The name does not fit into the given buffer size.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclAbstractIsEventRequested
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclAbstractIsEventRequested(cclConsumedEventID consumedEventID, bool* isRequested);
description: Description Checks whether a consumer side service event port is currently subscribing for an event.
parameters: Parameters consumedEventID The ID of a consumer side service event port with abstract binding (returned by cclAbstractConsumedEventGetID function). isRequested This variable returns whether the event port is currently subscribed or trying to subscribe for the event.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The current state of the event port was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTID The consumedEventID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service event port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The current state of the field port was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDFIELDID The consumedFieldID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service field port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

The returned ID can be used in all PDU provider functions (not abstract binding specific) but not vice versa. returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the PDU provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDUPROVIDERPATH The path parameter does not identify a service PDU provider known to the system.

name: cclAddConsumerByAddress
syntax: Function Syntax cclConsumerID cclAddConsumerByAddress(cclServiceID serviceID, cclAddressID addressID);
description: Description Dynamically adds a new consumer side endpoint to a service. The new endpoint is immediately bound to given binding specific address information.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to add a dynamic consumer. addressID ID of some binding specific address, either obtained from a service discovery event routine or by manually creating an address through a binding block API.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the new service consumer. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CANNOTADDDYNAMICCONSUMER The service does not allow dynamic consumer creation.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclAddConsumerByName
syntax: Function Syntax cclConsumerID cclAddConsumerByName(cclServiceID serviceID, const char* name, bool isSimulated);
description: Description Dynamically adds a new consumer side endpoint to a service. The new endpoint is not bound since it is not yet associated with binding specific address information.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to add a dynamic consumer. name The (unique) name of the new consumer. isSimulated Determines whether the new consumer should be simulated by the tool or rather is implemented by a known real ECU.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the new service consumer. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CANNOTADDDYNAMICCONSUMER The service does not allow dynamic consumer creation.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the new service provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CANNOTADDDYNAMICPROVIDER The service does not allow dynamic provider creation.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS Service announcement is in progress (the provider is not yet necessarily connected). CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.

returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service was successfully announced to the consumer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object. CCL_INVALIDBINDING Either the provider
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallAsync
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclCallHandler)(cclTime time, cclCallContextID ccID); int32_t cclCallAsync(cclCallContextID ccID, cclCallHandler resultHandler);
description: Description This function invokes a network function call and registers an event routine for the response.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context which holds the prepared input parameters of the call. Each call context can only be invoked once (while in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING) and if successful transitions to state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED. resultHandler An event routine to be called back when the network function call has returned at the consumer side (it will then be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_RETURNED). Pass the literal nullptr if no event routine should be called back, e.g. if you have a global e
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function call was successfully invoked. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The call context must identify a consumer side network function call port. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTSTATE The call context must be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING, which is the result of function cclCreateCallContext .
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextGetFunctionID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextGetFunctionID(cclCallContextID ccID, cclFunctionID* functionID);
description: Description Returns the network function port ID associated with a call context.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context for which to obtain the network function port. functionID The ID of the network function port is written to this variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function port ID was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextGetFunctionName
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextGetFunctionName(cclCallContextID ccID, char* buffer, int32_t bufferSize);
description: Description Returns the network function communication object name associated with a call context.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context for which to obtain the network function name. buffer The name of the network function is written to this buffer in UTF-8 encoding. bufferSize The size of the allocated buffer for the name (including the terminating 0).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function name was successfully written to the buffer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The network function's name doesn't fit the given buffer size.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextGetFunctionPath
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextGetFunctionPath(cclCallContextID ccID, char* buffer, int32_t bufferSize);
description: Description Returns the network function port path associated with a call context.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context for which to obtain the network function port path. buffer The port path of the network function is written to this buffer in UTF-8 encoding. bufferSize The size of the allocated buffer for the name (including the terminating 0).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function port path was successfully written to the buffer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The network function port path doesn't fit the given buffer size.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextIsSynchronousCall
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextIsSynchronousCall(cclCallContextID ccID, bool* isSynchronous);
description: Description Returns whether a specific call context was used for a synchronous call. It is meant to be used during a call handler registered with cclFunctionSetHandler for a distributed object method.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context which is currently active. isSynchronous Out parameter which will be set to true if the call was made synchronously and false otherwise. In particular, it will be set to false if the call was not yet made.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was successful. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object.

name: cclCallContextMakePermanent
syntax: Function Syntax cclCallContextID cclCallContextMakePermanent(cclCallContextID ccID);
description: Description Creates a permanent clone of a call context, which needs explicit release via function cclCallContextRelease and can be used beyond the current program scope. This is typically only applied to call contexts passed into event routines, which are valid only for the execution of that routine. All not released permanent call contexts are automatically removed at measurement end but you should be aware that repeated permanent call context creation from an event routine may lead to accumul
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context to be cloned (typically a parameter to an event routine).
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the permanent call context. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextRelease
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextRelease(cclCallContextID ccID);
description: Description Releases a permanent call context. The call context ID cannot be used anymore during the measurement. All not released permanent call contexts are automatically removed at measurement end but you should be aware that repeated permanent call context creation from an event routine may lead to accumulating memory consumption.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the permanent call context to be released (returned by cclCallContextMakePermanent ).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The call context was successfully released. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextReturn
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextReturn(cclCallContextID ccID, cclTime timeOffset);
description: Description Sets the relative simulation time when a network function call shall return at provider side. This function must only be invoked from an event routine handling the provider side state transition to CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED, i.e. when simulating the provider side function implementation. If no return time is set in such a handler, the function call will return immediately.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the network function call being simulated. timeOffset The time at which the function call should return at provider side, relative to the current simulation time. You can specify the constant CCL_TIME_OFFSET_NEVER to defer the response for an indeterminate time.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The return time was successfully set. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTSTATE The call context must be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The call context must identify a provider side network function port. CCL_INVALIDTIME The timeOffset parameter must greater than or equal to 0 or the constant CCL
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextSetDefaultAnswer
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextSetDefaultAnswer(cclCallContextID ccID);
description: Description Copies the simulation server configured default result and output parameters to the given call context. This function must only be invoked from an event routine handling the provider side state transition to CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED, i.e. when simulating the provider side function implementation.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the network function call being simulated.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The result and out parameters were successfully set to defaults. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTSTATE The call context must be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The call context must identify a provider side network function port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclValueID cclCallContextValueGetID(cclCallContextID ccID, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns a communication object value ID, which can be used for setting and reading call context parameter values and state. The values are only valid while the context itself is valid (especially call context parameters of event routines are valid only within that routine).
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context in which a parameter shall be accessed. member Path to a parameter, state or sub-member of such. Any member path into a call context must start with one of the predefined constants CCL_MEMBER_IN_PARAM concatenated with an input parameter name and an optional sub-member path CCL_MEMBER_OUT_PARAM concatenated with an output parameter name and an optional sub-member path CCL_MEMBER_RESULT concatenated with an optional sub-member path for accessing the network
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_PARAMETERINVALID The repr parameter must be one of the enumerated values in cclValueRepresentation. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH The member p
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallSync
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclCallHandler)(cclTime time, cclCallContextID ccID); int32_t cclCallSync(cclCallContextID ccID, cclCallHandler resultHandler);
description: Description This function synchronously invokes a distributed object method call and registers an event routine for the response. The function can only be called for internal distributed object methods and specific methods provided by bindings. In contrast to cclCallAsync , it invokes the method synchronously. The resultHandler will therefore be called before the call to cclCallAsync returns.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context which holds the prepared input parameters of the call. Each call context can only be invoked once (while in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING) and if successful transitions to state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED. resultHandler An event routine to be called back when the method call has returned (it will then be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_RETURNED). Pass the literal nullptr if no event routine should be called back, e.g. if you are not interested in the result of the functi
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function call was successfully invoked. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The call context must identify a consumer side network function call port. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTSTATE The call context must be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING, which is the result of function cclCreateCallContext . CCL_NO_SYNCHRONOU

name: cclCanGetChannelNumber
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCanGetChannelNumber(const char* networkName, int32_t* channel);
description: Description Retrieves the CAN channel number for the given network name.
parameters: Parameters networkName The name of the network, for which a CAN channel should be retrieved. Channel Pointer to a variable, where the function should store the retrieved CAN channel.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The channel was successfully retrieved. CCL_NETWORKNOTDEFINED The CANoe configuration does not contain a CAN network with the given name. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED Functionality is not implemented, version 11.0 SP3 or newer is required. CCL_INVALIDNAME The networkName parameter is a NULL pointer or an empty string.
example: Example int32_t gChannelChassis = 0; void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclCanGetChannelNumber("Chassies", &gChannelChassis); }

name: cclCanIsExtendedIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCanIsExtendedIdentifier(uint32_t identifier);
description: Description Checks parameter for extended CAN identifier (29 bit). Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32 bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29 bit extended identifier or an 11 bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier CAN identifier
returnValues: Return Values This function returns 1 if the parameter describes an extended CAN identifier (11 bit). Otherwise, it returns 0 .

name: cclCanIsStandardIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCanIsStandardIdentifier(uint32_t identifier);
description: Description Checks parameter for standard CAN identifier (11 Bit). Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32-bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier CAN identifier
returnValues: Return Values This function returns 1 if the parameter describes an standard CAN identifier (11-bit). Otherwise, it returns 0 .

name: cclCanMakeExtendedIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax uint32_t cclCanMakeExtendedIdentifier(uint32_t identifier);
description: Description This function creates an extended identifier (29-bit) from the specified value. Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32-bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier Value of the extended identifier.
returnValues: Return Values CAN identifier as required for the function cclCanSetMessageHandler .

name: cclCanMakeStandardIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax uint32_t cclCanMakeStandardIdentifier(uint32_t identifier);
description: Description This function creates an standard identifier (11-bit) from the specified value. Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32-bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier Value of the standard identifier.
returnValues: Return Values CAN identifier as required for the function cclCanSetMessageHandler .

name: cclCanOutputMessage
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCanOutputMessage(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, uint32_t flags, uint8_t dataLength, const uint8_t data[]);
description: Description This function outputs a message over the CAN bus. Calling this function is not blocking, i.e. the function only writes the message in the send buffer of the CAN driver and then returns immediately. The actual sending of the message occurs asynchronously with the program flow of the C library.
parameters: Parameters channel The CAN channel on which the message is to be output. identifier The CAN identifier of the message. The most-significant bit of this parameter determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier. Use the functions cclCanMakeExtendedIdentifier and cclCanMakeStandardIdentifier to create this parameter. flags Using the parameter flag, some additional properties of the message to be sent can be set: CCL_CANFLAGS_RTR Protocol Bit RTR (Remote Trans
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified CAN channel is invalid.

name: cclCanSetMessageHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclCanMessage {   int64_t time;   int32_t channel;   uint32_t id;   uint32_t flags;   uint8_t dir;   uint8_t dataLength;   uint8_t data[64]; }; int32_t cclCanSetMessageHandler(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, void (*function) (struct cclCanMessage* message) );
description: Struct cclCanMessage The struct that is transferred to the event routine, contains the following fields: time Time stamp (end-of-frame) of the event in nanoseconds. channel The CAN channel on which the message was received or sent. id CAN identifier of the message. The most significant bit of this parameter determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier. Using the functions cclCanIsExtendedIdentifier and cclCanIsStandardIdentifier , you can determine wheth
parameters: Parameters channel The CAN channel for which the event routine is to be active. identifier The CAN identifier of the message for which the event routine is to be registered. The most significant bit of this parameter determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier. Use the functions cclCanMakeExtendedIdentifier and cclCanMakeStandardIdentifier to create this parameter. The special value CCL_CAN_ALLMESSAGES registers the event routine for all messages of the
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified CAN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer.

name: cclCanValueOfIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax uint32_t cclCanValueOfIdentifier(uint32_t identifier)
description: Description Returns the value of a CAN identifier. Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32-bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier CAN identifier
returnValues: Return Values Value of the CAN identifiers.

name: cclConnectAsyncConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclConnectionEstablishedHandler)(); typedef void(*cclConnectionFailedHandler)(const char* error); int32_t cclConnectAsyncConsumer(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID, cclConnectionEstablishedHandler success, cclConnectionFailedHandler failure);
description: Description
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID function). success Event routine to be called back when the connection was successfully established. failure Event routine to be called back when the connection failed. This event routine receives an error message string indicating the cause of the connection failure.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The consumer side service port is trying to establish a service connection. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConnectAsyncProvider
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclConnectionEstablishedHandler)(); typedef void(*cclConnectionFailedHandler)(const char* error); int32_t cclConnectAsyncProvider(cclProvidedServiceID providedServiceID, cclConnectionEstablishedHandler success, cclConnectionFailedHandler failure);
description: Description Tries to establish a service connection from a provider side service port.
parameters: Parameters providedServiceID The ID of a provider side service port (returned by cclProvidedServiceGetID function). success Event routine to be called back when the connection was successfully established. failure Event routine to be called back when the connection failed. This event routine receives an error message string indicating the cause of the connection failure.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The provider side service port is trying to establish a service connection. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDSERVICEID The providedServiceID parameter does not identify a known provider side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedEventGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclConsumedEventID; cclConsumedEventID cclConsumedEventGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a consumer side service event port ID, which can be used in event registration functions. All event port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a consumer side service event port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the event port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service event port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedEventSetSubscriptionStateIsolated
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclConsumedEventSetSubscriptionStateIsolated(cclConsumedEventID consumedEventID, bool subscribed);
description: Description Sets the low level subscription state of a consumer side service event port.
parameters: Parameters consumedEventID The ID of a consumer side service event port (returned by cclConsumedEventGetID function). subscribed If this parameter is set to true, the event port will transition to state CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_SUBSCRIBED, otherwise it will transition back to CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_AVAILABLE.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The subscription state was successfully changed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTID The consumedEventID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service event port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclPDUReceiverID; cclPDUReceiverID cclConsumedPDUGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a consumer side service PDU port ID, which can be used for accessing PDU signal values and in PDU registration functions. Consumer side service PDU port IDs can be used in place of receiver side non-service PDU port IDs but not vice-versa. All PDU port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.

returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the PDU port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDPDUPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service PDU port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

description: Description Sets the low level subscription state of a consumer side service PDU port.

returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The subscription state was successfully changed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDPDUID The consumedPDUID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service PDU port.

name: cclConsumedServiceGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclConsumedServiceID; cclConsumedServiceID cclConsumedServiceGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a consumer side service port ID, which can be used in service discovery functions. All service port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a consumer side service port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the consumer side service port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedServiceValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclValueID cclConsumedServiceValueGetID(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns the communication object value identifier for a consumer side service port. The function is a convenience shortcut for accessing service port parameters when the service port is already available and its path is unknown.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of consumer side service port for which to access a value (e.g. connection state). member A member path for addressing a specific value of the port (only CCL_MEMBER_CONNECTION_STATE for consumer side service ports). repr The representation parameter can take the values CCL_IMPL, CCL_RAW or CCL_PHYS for access to the application implementation level, network transport or physical representation of the value.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumerGetBindingID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclConsumerGetBindingID(cclConsumerID consumerID, int32_t* bindingID);
description: Description Returns a binding specific identifier for a service consumer, which can be used to access ports of dynamically added consumers.
parameters: Parameters consumerID The ID of a service consumer (returned by cclAddConsumerByAddress , cclAddConsumerByName and cclConsumerGetID functions). bindingID The binding specific identifier is written to this variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The binding ID was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object. CCL_CONSUMERNOTBOUND The consumer must be bound to an address.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumerGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclConsumerID; cclConsumerID cclConsumerGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a consumer side service endpoint ID, which can be used in service discovery functions. All consumer IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a service consumer. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the service consumer. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEPATH The path parameter does not identify a service consumer known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCreateCallContext
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclCallContextID; cclCallContextID cclCreateCallContext(cclFunctionID functionID);
description: Description Creates a call context, which is typically the preparation of a network function call. The context is initialized with the latest call input parameters and can subsequently be configured with input parameter values before invoking the network function call. The function can only be called on consumer side network function ports. The returned call context is initially in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING.
parameters: Parameters functionID ID of the network function (returned by cclFunctionGetID).
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the call context. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONID The functionID parameter does not identify a known function object. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The functionID parameter must identify a consumer side network function port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclDisconnectConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclDisconnectConsumer(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID);
description: Description Tears down a service connection from a consumer side service port.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The connection was successfully terminated. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclDisconnectProvider

description: Description Tears down a service connection from a provider side service port.

returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The connection was successfully terminated. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDSERVICEID The providedServiceID parameter does not identify a known provider side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclDiscoverProviders
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclDiscoverProviders(cclConsumerID consumerID);
description: Description Triggers a service consumer to send service discovery requests.

name: cclEthernetGetChannelNumber
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclEthernetGetChannelNumber(const char* networkName, int32_t* channel);
description: Description Retrieves the Ethernet channel number for the given network name.
parameters: Parameters networkName The name of the network, for which an Ethernet channel should be retrieved. channel Pointer to a variable, where the function should store the retrieved Ethernet channel.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The channel was successfully retrieved. CCL_NETWORKNOTDEFINED The CANoe configuration does not contain an Ethernet network with the given name. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED Functionality is not implemented, version 15 SP2 or newer is required. CCL_INVALIDNAME The networkName parameter is a NULL pointer or an empty string.

name: cclEthernetOutputPacket
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclEthernetOutputPacket(int32_t channel, uint32_t packetSize, const uint8_t data[]);
description: Description This function outputs a packet over the Ethernet bus. Calling this function is not blocking, i.e., the function only writes the message in the send buffer of the Ethernet driver and then returns immediately. The actual sending of the message occurs asynchronously with the program flow of the C library.
parameters: Parameters channel The Ethernet channel on which the message is to be output. packetSize Data length in bytes of the packet to be sent. data The data of the packet to be sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The channel was successfully retrieved. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified Ethernet channel is invalid. CCL_NOTSUPPORTED The configuration uses a network with switched-based access.

name: cclEthernetSetPacketHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclEthernetPacket {   int64_t time;   int32_t channel;   uint8_t dir;   uint8_t packetSize;   uint8_t *packetData; }; int32_t cclEthernetSetPacketHandler(int32_t channel, void (*function) (struct cclEthernetPacket* packet) );
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of Ethernet packets. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel The Ethernet channel for which the event routine is to be active. function The event routine to be registered. The event routine is called as soon as an Ethernet packet has been received or sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified Ethernet channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_NOTSUPPORTED The configuration uses a network with switched-based access.

CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The specified buffer size if too small for the currently subscribed consumers.
