name: cclFieldProviderGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclFieldProviderID; cclFieldProviderID cclFieldProviderGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a service field provider ID, which can be used in field registration functions. All field provider IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a service field provider. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the field provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDFIELDPROVIDERPATH The path parameter does not identify a service field provider known to the system. CCL_FIELDNOTSUBSCRIBABL
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclFunctionGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32 t cclFunctionID; cclFunctionID cclFunctionGetID(cclDomain domain,const char* path);
description: Description Returns a function ID, which can be used for invocation of network function calls or simulation of network functions. All function IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement. Network functions can be free function communication objects or service methods or distributed object methods.
parameters: Parameters domain Use CCL_COMMUNICATION_OBJECTS for a communication object method and CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS for a distributed object method. path The path to a network function port or distributed object member. See Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the network function. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPATH The path parameter does not identify a network function port/distributed object member known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclFunctionSetHandler
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclFunctionSetHandler(cclFunctionID functionID, cclCallState callState, cclCallHandler handler);
description: Description Registers an event routine for a network function call state transition. The event routine is called back whenever any function call at the specified network function port changes its state.
parameters: Parameters functionID ID of the network function call port to be observed. callState The call state (enumerated value from cclCallState) for which to register the handler. When that state is entered, the handler is called back. handler The event routine to be called back. It receives the simulation time when the state transition occurred and the call context ID for accessing parameters and state of the call.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The handler was successfully registered. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONID The functionID parameter does not identify a known network function port object. CCL_PARAMETERINVALID The callState parameter must be an enumerated value from cclCallState.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetConsumedService
syntax: Function Syntax cclConsumedServiceID cclGetConsumedService(cclServiceID serviceID, int32_t consumerID, int32_t providerID);
description: Description Searches a consumer side service port by its binding specific endpoint identifiers.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to find a consumer side port. consumerID The binding specific identifier of the consumer. providerID The binding specific identifier of the provider.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the found service port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CONSUMERNOTFOUND No consumer with given binding identifier could be found for the service. CCL_PROVIDERNOTFOUND
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax cclConsumerID cclGetConsumer(cclServiceID serviceID, int32_t bindingID);
description: Description Searches a service consumer by its binding specific identifier.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to find a consumer. bindingID The binding specific identifier of the consumer.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the found service consumer. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CONSUMERNOTFOUND No consumer with given binding identifier could be found for the service.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetProvidedService
syntax: Function Syntax cclProvidedServiceID cclGetProvidedService(cclServiceID serviceID, int32_t consumerID, int32_t providerID);
description: Description Searches a provider side service port by its binding specific endpoint identifiers.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to find a provider side port. consumerID The binding specific identifier of the consumer. providerID The binding specific identifier of the provider.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the found service port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CONSUMERNOTFOUND No consumer with given binding identifier could be found for the service. CCL_PROVIDERNOTFOUND
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetProvider
syntax: Function Syntax cclProviderID cclGetProvider(cclServiceID serviceID, int32_t bindingID);
description: Description Searches a service provider by its binding specific identifier.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to find a provider. bindingID The binding specific identifier of the provider.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the found service provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_PROVIDERNOTFOUND No provider with given binding identifier could be found for the service.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetUserFilePath
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclGetUserFilePath(const char* filename, char* pathBuffer, int32_t pathBufferLength);
description: Description Gets the absolute path of a user file. In case of execution in a distributed environment the absolute user file path (including filename) on the remote device (e.g. VN8900 ) is returned if the user file was predefined. If the file was not predefined an error code is returned. In case of a single PC environment the registered absolute file path (including filename) of the user file is returned if the user file was predefined. If the file was not predefined the function converts a path
parameters: Parameters filename A filename potentially containing a path. If the filename is predefined the path is ignored. Otherwise the path is interpreted as relative to the current configuration path in a single PC environment. pathBuffer Buffer to which the full path name should be copied. pathBufferLength
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_USERFILENOTDEFINED The file was not predefined. CCL_INVALIDNAME Parameter filename is a null pointer or does not contain any character. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED CANoe / CANalyzer version 7.5 SP2 or newer is required for this function. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and during a running measurement. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The specified buffer is too small for the absolute path of th

name: cclIncrementTimerBase
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclIncrementTimerBase(int64_t newTimeBaseTicks, int32_t numberOfSteps);
description: Description Increases the simulation time by newTimeBaseTicks . Use numberOfSteps steps to reach the requested timestamp. For each step, the simulation time is increased. Events that are due are delivered and handlers are called. The function returns immediately and should not be called again unless your CANoe finished its simulation step. This function can be called from any thread.
parameters: Parameters newTimeBaseTicks The time span in nanoseconds by which the simulation time should be increased. numberOfSteps The number of steps for the increment of the simulation time.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The time span was set successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement and when your CANoe is running with simulated bus with external time source. CCL_PARAMETERINVALID Both parameters must be greater zero.
example: Example See description and example for simulated bus with external time source .

name: cclIsRunningInSlaveMode
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclIsRunningInSlaveMode(bool *isSlaveMode);
description: Description Returns whether the simulation is running with simulated bus with external time source or not. Note The slave mode in CANoe versions prior to 19 has been replaced by the direct configuration of the time source. Select External: Software to get the behavior of the slave mode, or External: SIL Kit if SIL Kit should take over the time control.
parameters: Parameters isSlaveMode Pointer to Boolean indicating if simulation is running with simulated bus with external time source.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS Always succeeds.
example: Example void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; bool isSlaveMode; rc = cclIsRunningInSlaveMode(&isSlaveMode); }

name: cclIsServiceProvided
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclIsServiceProvided(cclProviderID providerID, bool* isProvided);
description: Description Returns whether a service provider is currently accepting service requests.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions). isProvided This variable returns whether the service is currently provided.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The current state of the provider was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclIsServiceRequested
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclIsServiceRequested(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID, bool* isRequested);
description: Description Checks whether a consumer side service port currently requests the service.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID function). isRequested This variable returns whether the service is currently requested.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The current state of the provider side service port was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclLinChangeSchedtable
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinChangeSchedtable(int32_t channel, uint32_t tableIndex);
description: Description Switches from the current schedule table to another one.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the scheduler is to be changed. tableIndex Index of the schedule table to be changed to.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinGetChannelNumber
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinGetChannelNumber(const char* networkName, int32_t* channel);
description: Description Retrieve the LIN channel number for the given network name.
parameters: Parameters networkName The name of the network, for which a LIN channel should be retrieved. Channel Pointer to a variable, where the function should store the retrieved LIN channel.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The channel was successfully retrieved. CCL_NETWORKNOTDEFINED The CANoe configuration does not contain a LIN network with the given name. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED Functionality is not implemented, version 11.0 SP3 or newer is required. CCL_INVALIDNAME The networkName parameter is a NULL pointer or an empty string.
example: Example int32_t gChannelChassis = 0; void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinGetChannelNumber("Chassies", &gChannelChassis); }

name: cclLinGotoSleep
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinGotoSleep(int32_t channel);
description: Description Send a goto-sleep master request and set hardware channel into sleep mode.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel that shall be set to sleep mode.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinGotoSleepInternal
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinGotoSleepInternal(int32_t channel);
description: Description Set hardware channel into sleep mode, without sending a goto-sleep master request.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel that shall be set to sleep mode.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinSendHeader
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinSendHeader(int32_t channel, uint32_t id);
description: Description The function sends a LIN header over the specified channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel on which the LIN header is to be sent. id Identifier of the LIN header to be sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinSetErrorHandler
syntax: Function Syntax typedef enum {   CCL_LIN_ERROR_CHECKSUM,   CCL_LIN_ERROR_TRANSMISSION,   CCL_LIN_ERROR_RECEIVE,   CCL_LIN_ERROR_SYNC,   CCL_LIN_ERROR_SLAVETIMEOUT } cclLinErrorType; struct cclLinError {   int64_t time;   int32_t channel;   uint32_t id;   cclLinErrorType type;   uint16_t crc;   uint8_t dlc;   uint8_t dir;   uint8_t FSMId;   uint8_t FSMState;
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN errors. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the event routine is to be active. identifier LIN identifier. function The event routine to be registered.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement.
example: Example extern void OnLinErrorHandler(struct cclLinError* error); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetErrorHandler(1, CCL_LIN_ALLMESSAGES, &OnLinErrorHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set error handler"); } } void OnLinErrorHandler(struct cclLinError* error) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: error received:"); switch (error->type) { case CCL_LIN_ERROR_CHECKSUM: cclPrintf("Checksum error, Frame-ID=%i, CRC=%i", error->id, error->crc); break; case CCL_LIN_ERROR_TRANSMISSION: cclPrintf("Transmission error, FSM-ID=%i", error->FSMId); break; case CCL_LIN_ERROR_RECEIVE: cclPrintf("Receive error, FSM-ID=%i, offending byte:%i", error->FSMId, error->offendingByte); break; case CCL_LIN_ERROR_SYNC: cclPrintf("Sync error"); break; case CCL_LIN_ERROR_SLAVETIMEOUT: cclPrintf("Slave timeout error, slave ID=%i", error->slaveId); break; default: break; } }

name: cclLinSetFrameHandler
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN frames. The handler is also called if the frame is sent with CANalyzer . Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler of the measurement; the registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the event routine is to be active. identifier The LIN identifier of the message for which the event routine is to be registered. The special value CCL_LIN_ALLMESSAGES registers the event routine for all messages of the specified LIN channel. function The event routine to be registered. The event routine is called as soon as an appropriate LIN frame has been received or sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer.

name: cclLinSetPreDispatchMessageHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclLinPreDispatchMessage {   int64_t time;   int32_t channel;   uint8_t dir;   uint32_t id;   uint32_t flags;   uint32_t dlc;   uint8_t data[8]; }; int32_t cclLinSetPreDispatchMessageHandler(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, void (*function) (struct cclLinPreDispatchMessage *frame));
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN messages before they are dispatched. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel identifier LIN identifier of the message to be received. function The event routine to be registered.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement.
example: Example extern void OnLinPreDispatchMessageHandler(struct cclLinPreDispatchMessage* message); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetPreDispatchMessageHandler(1, 34, &OnLinPreDispatchMessageHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set pre dispatch message handler"); } } void OnLinPreDispatchMessageHandler (struct cclLinPreDispatchMessage* message) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: pre dispatch message for ID = %i received", message->id); // Modify data of the message: if (message->dlc >= 1) // Make sure there is at least one byte in the message { message->data[0] = 0xff; // Set it to some value } }

name: cclLinSetSlaveResponseChangedHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclLinSlaveResponse {   int64_t time;   int32_t channel;   uint8_t dir;   uint32_t id;   uint32_t flags;   uint8_t dlc;   uint8_t data[8]; }; int32_t cclLinSetSlaveResponseChangedHandler(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, void (*function) (struct cclLinSlaveResponse* frame) );
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of changed data bytes of LIN frames. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the event routine is to be active. function The event routine to be registered. The event routine is called as soon as a LIN Slave changed the data bytes of the frame.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer.
example: Example extern void OnLinSlaveResponseChangedHandler(struct cclLinSlaveResponse* frame); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetSlaveResponseChangedHandler(1, CCL_LIN_ALLMESSAGES, &OnLinSlaveResponseChangedHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set slave response changed handler"); } } void OnLinSlaveResponseChangedHandler(struct cclLinSlaveResponse* response) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: slave response changed: %i", response->id); }

name: cclLinSetSleepModeEventHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclLinSleepModeEvent {   int64_t time;   int32_t channel;   uint8_t external;   uint8_t isAwake;   uint8_t reason;   uint8_t wasAwake; }; int32_t cclLinSetSleepModeEventHandler(int32_t channel, void (*function) (struct cclLinSleepModeEvent *event));
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN sleep mode events. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel or -1 (for all channels) function The event routine to be registered.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement.
example: Example extern void OnLinSleepModeEventHandler(struct cclLinSleepModeEvent* event); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetSleepModeEventHandler(1, &OnLinSleepModeEventHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set sleep mode event handler"); } } void OnLinSleepModeEventHandler (struct cclLinSleepModeEvent* event) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: sleep mode event received:"); if (event->isAwake) { cclPrintf("Channel %i is NOT sleeping", event->channel); } else { cclPrintf("Channel %i is sleeping", event->channel); } }

name: cclLinSetWakeupBehavior
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinSetWakeupBehavior(int32_t channel, uint32_t restartScheduler, uint32_t wakeupIdentifier, uint32_t sendShortHeader, uint32_t wakeupDelimiterLength);
description: Description Specifies the action to take right after the hardware channel has been set into awake mode. Note : A short header (SynchBreak / SynchField) pair will be sent after the HW channel entered awake state, if wakeupIdentifier is an invalid LIN identifier AND sendShortHeader == 1 AND no master node defining schedule tables has been assigned to it's corresponding LIN DB node.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel restartScheduler Restart scheduler after HW channel entered awake state. wakeupIdentifier LIN frame identifier to be sent additionally directly after sending a wakeup signal. sendShortHeader Activates/deactivates short header automatism (use 1 for default behavior as in CANoe). wakeupDelimiterLength Time, after wakeup, when to send the first header, if restartScheduler == 1 (use 0 for default value).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinSetWakeupCondition
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinSetWakeupCondition(int32_t channel, uint32_t numWakeupSignals, uint32_t forgetWupsAfterMS);
description: Description Sets wakeup signal parameters, defining the condition when to actually set the hardware channel into awake mode.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel numWakeupSignals Number of wakeup signals received, after which to set the hardware channel into awake mode. forgetWupsAfterMS Time span after which the count of received wakeup signals will be reset (while not causing a HW channel wakeup).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinSetWakeupFrameHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclLinWakeupFrame {   int64_t time;   int32_t channel;   uint8_t external;   uint8_t signal; }; int32_t cclLinSetWakeupFrameHandler(int32_t channel, void (*function) (struct cclLinWakeupFrame *frame));
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN wakeup frames. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel or -1 (for all channels) function The event routine to be registered.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement.
example: Example extern void OnLinWakeupFrameHandler(struct cclLinWakeupFrame* frame); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetWakeupFrameHandler(1, &OnLinWakeupFrameHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set wakeup frame handler"); } } void OnLinWakeupFrameHandler (struct cclLinWakeupFrame* frame) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: wakeup frame received"); }

name: cclLinSetWakeupTimings
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinSetWakeupTimings(int32_t channel, uint32_t ttobrk, uint32_t wakeupSignalCount, uint32_t widthinMicSec);
description: Description Sets timing parameters, on the current node, for consecutive LINWakeup invocation.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel ttobrk Time difference (bit times) between two consecutive frames. wakeupSignalCount The number of Wakeup frames to be sent. widthinMicSec The length of each wakeup signal, in microseconds.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinStartScheduler
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinStartScheduler(int32_t channel);
description: Description This function starts the internal scheduler, which begins a cyclical traversal of a last con-figured schedule table.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the scheduler is to be started.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinStopScheduler
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinStopScheduler(int32_t channel);
description: Description This function stops the internal scheduler. This way a cyclical traversal of the current schedule table is stopped.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the scheduler is to be stopped.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinUpdateResponseData
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinUpdateResponseData(int32_t channel,uint32_t id,uint8_t dlc,uint8_t data[8]);
description: Description This function sets the response data for an LIN frame.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel for which the response data is to be changed. id Identifier of the LIN frame for which the response data is to be changed. data length code Length of the response in bytes. data Data bytes.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinWakeup
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinWakeup(int32_t channel);
description: Description Send wakeup master signal(s) and set hardware channel into awake mode. Node-specific timing parameters will be used if available, default parameters based on protocol version and type otherwise.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel that shall be set to awake mode.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinWakeupInternal
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinWakeupInternal(int32_t channel);
description: Description Sets hardware channel into awake mode, without sending wakeup signals.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel that shall be set to awake mode.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclOnDllLoad
syntax: Function Syntax void cclOnDllLoad();
description: Description This function is the entry function of a C library and must be implemented by you. It is called up by the framework shortly after the library was loaded by your CANoe . The function is used to initialize the library and to register additional event routines. The following functions are typically used within cclOnLoad : cclSetMeasurementPreStartHandler cclSetMeasurementStartHandler cclSetMea-surementStopHandler cclsetDllUnloadHandler
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclPDUGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclPDUGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers(cclPDUProviderID pduProviderID, int32_t* nrOfConsumers);
description: Description Returns the number of consumers currently subscribed at given service PDU provider.
parameters: Parameters pduProviderID The ID of a service PDU provider (returned by cclPDUProviderGetID function). nrOfConsumers This variable returns the number of currently subscribed consumers.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The number of subscribed consumers was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDUPROVIDERID The pduProviderID parameter does not identify a known service PDU provider.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclPDUGetSubscribedConsumers
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclPDUGetSubscribedConsumers(cclPDUProviderID pduProviderID, cclConsumerID* consumerBuffer, int32_t* bufferSize);
description: Description Returns a list of the consumers currently subscribed at given service PDU provider.
parameters: Parameters pduProviderID The ID of a service PDU provider (returned by cclPDUProviderGetID function). consumerBuffer This buffer is filled with the IDs of subscribed service consumers. bufferSize The maximum number of IDs to be written to the buffer. This parameter is updated with the actual number of consumer IDs written to the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The list of subscribed consumers was successfully read. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDUPROVIDERID The pduProviderID parameter does not identify a known service PDU provider. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The specified buffer size if too small for the currently subscribed consumers.

name: cclPrintf

The function uses the printf function of the standard C library of your development environment to format the output. For details about the format string look at the description of printf in the help of your development environment.
parameters: Parameters format The format string, which controls the output of the text.

returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the event port. syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclProvidedEventSetSubscriptionStateIsolated(cclProvidedEventID providedEventID, bool subscribed);
description: Description Sets the low level subscription state of a provider side service event port.
parameters: Parameters providedEventID The ID of a provider side service event port (returned by cclProvidedEventGetID function). subscribed If this parameter is set to true, the event port will transition to state CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_SUBSCRIBED, otherwise it will transition back to CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_AVAILABLE.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The subscription state was successfully changed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDEVENTID The providedEventID parameter does not identify a known provider side service event port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

Provider side service PDU port IDs can be used in place of sender side non-service PDU port IDs but not vice-versa. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDPDUPATH The path parameter does not identify a provider side service PDU port known to the system.

name: cclProvidedServiceValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclValueID cclProvidedServiceValueGetID(cclProvidedServiceID providedServiceID, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns the communication object value identifier for a provider side service port. The function is a convenience shortcut for accessing service port parameters when the service port is already available and its path is unknown.
parameters: Parameters providedServiceID The ID of provider side service port for which to access a value (e.g. connection state). member A member path for addressing a specific value of the port (CCL_MEMBER_CONNECTION_STATE or CCL_MEMBER_SERVICE_STATE for provider side service ports). repr The representation parameter can take the values CCL_IMPL, CCL_RAW or CCL_PHYS for access to the application implementation level, network transport or physical representation of the value.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDSERVICEID The providedServiceID parameter does not identify a known provider side service port object. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions). bindingID The binding specific identifier is written to this variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The binding ID was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object. CCL_PROVIDERNOTBOUND The provider must be bound to an address.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProvideService
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclProvideService(cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Switches a service provider into service state CCL_SERVICE_STATE_AVAILABLE for sending out service announcements and responding to service requests by consumers.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service is successfully provided. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclReleaseService
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclReleaseService(cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Switches a service provider back to service state CCL_SERVICE_STATE_UNAVAILABLE, which makes it stop sending service announcements and accepting service requests.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS Providing the service was successfully stopped. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclReleaseServiceRequest
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclReleaseServiceRequest(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID);
description: Description Stops requesting the service from a consumer side service port. If currently connected, the connection will be released as well.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID function).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The port no more requests the service and any connection is released. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclRemoveConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclRemoveConsumer(cclServiceID serviceID, cclConsumerID consumerID);
description: Description Removes a dynamically added consumer side endpoint from a service.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object from which to remove a dynamic consumer. consumerID ID of the consumer to be removed (returned by cclAddConsumerByName or cclAddConsumerByAddress ).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The consumer was successfully removed from the service. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object. CCL_STATICCONSUMER The identified consumer was not dynamically added to the service and thus cannot be removed.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclRemoveProvider
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclRemoveProvider(cclServiceID serviceID, cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Removes a dynamically added provider side endpoint from a service.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object from which to remove a dynamic provider. providerID ID of the provider to be removed (returned by cclAddProviderByName or cclAddProviderByAddress ).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The provider was successfully removed from the service. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object. CCL_STATICPROVIDER The identified provider was not dynamically added to the service and thus cannot be removed.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclRequestService
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclRequestService(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID);
description: Description Switches a consumer side service port to start requesting the service. As long as the consumer side service port is in that state, it will try to connect to the service provider.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The port now requests the service. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclServiceGetID

description: Description Returns a service communication object ID, which can be used for service method simulation and dynamic endpoint discovery. All service IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.

returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the service. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEPATH The path parameter does not identify a service communication object known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclServiceSetSimulator
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclProvidedServiceID; int32_t cclServiceSetSimulator(cclProvidedServiceID providedService, cclCallHandler simulator);
description: Description Registers a service method simulation event routine for all methods of a provider side service port.
parameters: Parameters providedService ID of the provider side service port. simulator The event routine to be called back when any method of the service transitions into state called at provider side. The event routine receives the simulation time when the state transition occurred and the call context ID for accessing parameters and state of the call.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function port path was successfully written to the buffer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDSERVICEID The providedService parameter does not identify a known provider side service port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclConnectionRequestedHandler)(cclConsumerID consumerID); int32_t cclSetConnectionRequestedHandler(cclProviderID providerID, cclConnectionRequestedHandler handler);

CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSetConsumerAddress
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSetConsumerAddress(cclConsumerID consumerID, cclAddressID addressID, cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Notifies the system about a service consumer's binding specific address. The function can either notify the service consumer about its own address or notify a service provider about the consumer's address.
parameters: Parameters consumerID The ID of a service consumer (returned by cclAddConsumerByAddress , cclAddConsumerByName and cclConsumerGetID functions) for which to set given address. addressID The ID of a binding specific service consumer address. providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions) to be notified about the consumer's address. The constant CCL_PROVIDER_NONE should be used for notifying the consumer about its
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service was successfully announced to the consumer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object. CCL_INVALIDADDRESSID The addressID parameter does not identify a known address object. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSetDllUnloadHandler
syntax: Function Syntax void cclSetDllUnloadHandler (void (*function)());
description: Description This function registers an event routine with the framework. The transferring event routine is called up by the framework shortly before the library is cleared by your CANoe .
parameters: Parameters function Event routine that is called up by the framework shortly before the DLL is cleared.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclSetGpbOSI3Data
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSetGpbOSI3Data (uint8_t * data, int32_t dataSize)
description: Description Passes OSI3 sensor data which are serialized as Google Protocol Buffers (GPB) to CANoe. CANoe will generate Distributed Objects (DOs) based on the sensor data.
parameters: Parameters data The byte array containing the serialized OSI3 sensor data. dataSize The length of the array in byte.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The functional call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDDATA The provided data can't be decoded. The byte array either isn't a Google Protocol Buffer or isn't a in a compatible OSI3 sensor data format.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ADAS.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples\ADAS).

name: cclSetMeasurementPreStartHandler
syntax: Function Syntax void cclSetMeasurementPreStartHandler(void (*function)());
description: Description This function registers an event routine with the framework. The transferring event routine is called up during the initialization of a measurement by your CANoe . The measurement has not yet been started at this time, which is why longer-lasting tasks, e.g. file access, may also be carried out in this routine. Typical tasks of the event routine are the creation of timer objects, the determination of system variable IDs the registration of event routines for the reception of bus mess
parameters: Parameters function Event routine that is called up by the framework shortly before a measurement is started.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclSetMeasurementStartHandler
syntax: Function Syntax void cclSetMeasurementStartHandler(void (*function)());
description: Description This function registers the event routine with the framework. The transferring event routine is called up directly after a measurement start in your CANoe . A typical task of the transferring event routine is the starting of timers. Since the measurement is already running when the event routine is called up, the event routine should not execute any longer-lasting tasks.
parameters: Parameters function Event routine that is called up by the framework directly after a measurement is started.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclSetMeasurementStopHandler
syntax: Function Syntax void cclSetMeasurementStopHandler(void (*function)());
description: Description This function registers an event routine with the framework. The transferring event routine is called up directly after a measurement stop in your CANoe . The measurement has already stopped by this time, i.e. no further messages can be output over the buses.
parameters: Parameters function Event routine that is called up by the framework directly after a measurement is stopped.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclSetProviderAddress
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSetProviderAddress(cclProviderID providerID, cclAddressID addressID, cclConsumerID consumerID);
description: Description Notifies the system about a service provider's binding specific address. The function can either notify the service provider about its own address or notify a service consumer about the provider's address.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions) for which to set given address. addressID The ID of a binding specific service consumer address. consumerID The ID of a service consumer (returned by cclAddConsumerByAddress , cclAddConsumerByName and cclConsumerGetID functions to be notified about the provider's address. The constant CCL_CONSUMER_NONE should be used for notifying the provider about its o
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service was successfully announced to the consumer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object. CCL_INVALIDADDRESSID The addressID parameter does not identify a known address object. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSetProviderDiscoveredHandler
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclProviderDiscoveredHandler)(cclAddressID addressID); int32_t cclSetProviderDiscoveredHandler(cclConsumerID consumerID, cclProviderDiscoveredHandler handler);
description: Description Registers an event routine to be called back when a provider side service endpoint is discovered by a service consumer.
parameters: Parameters consumerID The ID of a service consumer (returned by cclAddConsumer… and cclConsumerGetID functions) for which to register the handler.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The handler was successfully registered. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSetServiceConsumerDiscoveredHandler
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclConsumerDiscoveredHandler)(cclAddressID addressID); int32_t cclSetServiceConsumerDiscoveredHandler(cclServiceID serviceID, cclConsumerDiscoveredHandler handler);
description: Description Registers an event routine to be called back when any consumer side service endpoint is discovered for a service. Use function cclSetConsumerDiscoveredHandler to register a handler for a specific service provider.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of a service communication object. handler Event routine to be called back when a service consumer is discovered.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The handler was successfully registered. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSetServiceDiscoveryHandler

description: Description Registers an event routine to be called back when a service consumer sends service discovery requests.

returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The handler was successfully registered. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSignalGetID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSignalGetID(char* signalname);
description: Description This function returns a signal ID. This ID is required for setting and reading signal values. All system variable IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters signalname The name of the signal. The identification string of a signal has the following form: [[[[network_name::]db_name::]node_name::]msg_name::]signal_name   The field network_name is the name of network in the Simulation Setup of CANoe . It is only required if a communication description is assigned to more than one bus.   If you are working with a communication description, where the signals are directly assigned to communication frames, the field msg_name is the name
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the signal. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_INVALIDNAME Parameter signalname is a null pointer or does not contain any character. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED CANoe / CANalyzer version 8.1 or newer is required for this function. CCL_SIGNALNOTDEFINED The signal does not exists. CCL_SIGNALAMBIGUOUS The given signal name is ambiguous.

name: cclSignalGetRxPhysDouble
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSignalGetRxPhysDouble(int32_t signalID, double* value);
description: Description Reads the Rx value as physical value of the given signal.
parameters: Parameters signalID ID of the signal (return value of the function cclSignalGetID ). value The physical value of the signal is written to this variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSIGNALID The ID of the signal is invalid. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED The signal has no physical value.

name: cclSignalGetRxRawDouble
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSignalGetRxRawDouble(int32_t signalID, double* value);
description: Description Reads the Rx value of the given signal as floating point raw value.
parameters: Parameters signalID ID of the signal (return value of the function cclSignalGetID ). value The raw value of the signal is written to this variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSIGNALID The ID of the signal is invalid. CCL_WRONGTYPE The raw value of the signal is not of floating point type.

name: cclSignalSetHandler
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSignalSetHandler(int32_t signalID, void (*function)(int32_t signalID) );
description: Description The function assigns an event routine to the specified signal. The event routine is called each time a frame with the given signal was transmitted on the bus.
parameters: Parameters signalID ID of the signal (return value of the function cclSignalGetID ). function The event routine to be called up when the system variable is set. The event routine receives the current time of the measurement in nanoseconds and the ID of the system variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSIGNALID The ID of the signal is invalid.

description: Description Write the Tx value as physical value of the given signal.
parameters: Parameters signalID ID of the signal (return value of the function cclSignalGetID ). value The Tx value to be assigned to the signal.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSIGNALID The ID of the signal is invalid. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED The signal is read only or has no physical value.

name: cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupGetEvents
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupGetEvents(cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupID consumedEventGroupID, cclConsumedEventID* buffer, int32_t* bufferSize);
description: Description Returns the event port IDs contained in a consumer side service event group port.
parameters: Parameters consumedEventGroupID The ID of a consumer side service event group port with SOME/IP binding (returned by cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupGetID function). buffer A buffer to be filled with service event IDs contained in the event group. bufferSize The maximum number of service event port IDs to write to the buffer. This parameter is updated with the actual number of event port IDs written to the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service event port IDs were successfully written to the buffer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTGROUPID The consumedEventGroupID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service event group port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

description: Description Returns a SOME/IP binding specific consumer side event group port ID, which can be used for event group registration. All event group port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.

CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTGROUPPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service event group port known to the system.

name: cclSomeIPCreateAddress
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclAddressID; cclAddressID cclSomeIPCreateAddress(const char* udpAddress, int32_t udpPort, const char* tcpAddress, int32_t tcpPort);
description: Description Creates a SOME/IP binding specific address, which can be used for dynamically adding service endpoints. All address IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters udpAddress The IP address for UDP communication in IPv4 or IPv6 format. udpPort The port to use for UDP communication. tcpAddress The IP address for TCP communication in IPv4 or IPv6 format. tcpPort The port to use for TCP communication.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the address. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_CANNOTCREATEADDRESS Any of the address parameters has an incorrect format or value.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSomeIPEventGroupGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSomeIPEventGroupGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers(cclSomeIPEventGroupProviderID eventGroupProviderID, int32_t* nrOfConsumers);
description: Description Returns the number of consumers currently subscribed at given service event group provider.
parameters: Parameters eventGroupProviderID The ID of a service event group provider (returned by cclSomeIPEventGroupProviderGetID function). nrOfConsumers This variable returns the number of currently subscribed consumers.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The number of subscribed consumers was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDEVENTGROUPPROVIDERID The eventGroupProviderID parameter does not identify a known service event group provider.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

description: Description Returns a list of the consumers currently subscribed at given service event group provider.
parameters: Parameters eventGroupProviderID The ID of a service event group provider (returned by cclSomeIPEventGroupProviderGetID function). consumerBuffer This buffer is filled with the IDs of subscribed service consumers. bufferSize The maximum number of IDs to be written to the buffer. This parameter is updated with the actual number of consumer IDs written to the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The list of subscribed consumers was successfully read. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDEVENTGROUPPROVIDERID The eventGroupProviderID parameter does not identify a known service event group provider. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The specified buffer size if too small for the currently subscribed consumers.

name: cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupID; cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupID cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a SOME/IP binding specific provider side event group port ID, which can be used for event group registration. All event group port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a provider side service event group port with SOME/IP binding. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the event group port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDEVENTGROUPPATH The path parameter does not identify a provider side service event group port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupGetPDUs
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupGetPDUs(cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupID providedEventGroupID, cclPDUSenderID* buffer, int32_t* bufferSize);
description: Description Returns the service PDU port IDs contained in a provider side service event group port.
parameters: Parameters providedEventGroupID The ID of a provider side service event group port with SOME/IP binding (returned by cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupGetID function). buffer A buffer to be filled with service PDU port IDs contained in the event group. bufferSize The maximum number of service PDU port IDs to write to the buffer. This parameter is updated with the actual number of service PDU port IDs written to the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service PDU port IDs were successfully written to the buffer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDEVENTGROUPID The providedEventGroupID parameter does not identify a known provider side service event group port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSomeIPReleaseEventGroup
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSomeIPReleaseEventGroup(cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupID consumedEventGroupID);
description: Description Releases an event group request from a consumer side service event group port with SOME/IP binding. If the event group is currently subscribed, the consumer will unsubscribe. In any case it will stop subscribing the event group.
parameters: Parameters consumedEventGroupID The ID of a consumer side service event group port with SOME/IP binding (returned by cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupGetID function).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event group port has unsubscribed for the event group. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTGROUPID The consumedEventGroupID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service event group port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSomeIPRequestEventGroup
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSomeIPRequestEventGroup(cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupID consumedEventGroupID);
description: Description Requests an event group from a consumer side service event group port with SOME/IP binding. If the event group is not currently available on the provider side, it will be subscribed as soon as it becomes available.
parameters: Parameters consumedEventGroupID The ID of a consumer side service event group port with SOME/IP binding (returned by cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupGetID function).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event group port has subscribed for the event group or will do so when it is available. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTGROUPID The consumedEventGroupID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service event group port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSomeIPSetEventGroupSubscribedHandler
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclSomeIPEventGroupSubscriptionHandler)(cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupID providedEventGroupID); int32_t cclSomeIPSetEventGroupSubscribedHandler(cclSomeIPEventGroupProviderID eventGroupProviderID, cclSomeIPEventGroupSubscriptionHandler handler);
description: Description Registers a handler for service event group subscription at an event group provider.
parameters: Parameters eventGroupProviderID The ID of a service event group provider (returned by cclSomeIPEventGroupProviderGetID function). handler Event routine to be called back when a service consumer subscribes at the event group provider. The handler receives the provider side service event group port at which the subscription was received.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The handler was successfully registered. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDEVENTGROUPPROVIDERID The eventGroupProviderID parameter does not identify a known service event group provider.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSomeIPSetEventGroupUnsubscribedHandler
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclSomeIPEventGroupSubscriptionHandler)(cclSomeIPProvidedEventGroupID providedEventGroupID); int32_t cclSomeIPSetEventGroupUnsubscribedHandler(cclSomeIPEventGroupProviderID eventGroupProviderID, cclSomeIPEventGroupSubscriptionHandler handler);
description: Description Registers a handler for service event group desubscription from an event group provider.
parameters: Parameters eventGroupProviderID The ID of a service event group provider (returned by cclSomeIPEventGroupProviderGetID function). handler Event routine to be called back when a service consumer unsubscribes at the event group provider. The handler receives the provider side service event group port at which the desubscription was received.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The handler was successfully registered. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDEVENTGROUPPROVIDERID The eventGroupProviderID parameter does not identify a known service event group provider.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSomeIPSubscribeEventGroup
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSomeIPSubscribeEventGroup(cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupID consumedEventGroupID);
description: Description Subscribes a consumer side service event group port for an event group.
parameters: Parameters consumedEventGroupID The ID of a consumer side service event group port with SOME/IP binding (returned by cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupGetID function).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event group was successfully subscribed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTGROUPID The consumedEventGroupID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service event group port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSomeIPUnsubscribeEventGroup
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSomeIPUnsubscribeEventGroup(cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupID consumedEventGroupID);
description: Description Revokes a consumer side service event group port subscription.
parameters: Parameters consumedEventGroupID The ID of a consumer side service event group port with SOME/IP binding (returned by cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupGetID function).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event group was successfully unsubscribed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTGROUPID The consumedEventGroupID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service event group port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSysVarGetID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSysVarGetID(char* systemVariableName);
description: Description This function returns a system variable ID. This ID is required for setting and reading system variables. All system variable IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters systemVariableName The name of the system variable. In case you want to access a member of a structure, you must extend the name of the system variable by the name of the member and separate them by a dot. In case you want to access an element of an array, you must extend the name of the system variable by the index of the array element. The index must be enclosed in brackets. The first array element has the index zero. Examples: "X::a" System variable with name 'a' in namespace 'X' "
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the system variable. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDNAME The parameter systemVariableName may be neither a zero pointer nor an empty string. CCL_SYSVARNOTDEFINED A system vari

description: Description The function assigns an event routine to the specified system variable. The event routine is called each time the value of the variable is reset.
parameters: Parameters sysVarID ID of the system variable (return value of the function cclSysVarGetID ). function The event routine to be called up when the system variable is set. The event routine receives the current time of the measurement in nanoseconds and the ID of the system variable.

CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSYSVARID The ID of the system variable is invalid.
