name: cclTimerCancel
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclTimerCancel(int32_t timerID);
description: Description Stops the active timer.
parameters: Parameters timerID Timer ID (return value of function cclTimerCreate ).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The timer was successfully stopped or the timer was already inactive. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDTIMERID The transferred TimerID is invalid.

name: cclTimerCreate
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclTimerCreate( void (*function)(int64_t time, int32_t timerID) );
description: Description This function creates a new timer and links the transferred event routine to the timer. Each time the timer expires, the assigned event routine is called. The created timer is activated with the function cclTimerSet and can be ended prematurely with the function cclTimerCancel . The timers required for a measurement are typically created in the PreStartHandler of the measurement (see cclSetMeasurementPreStartHandler ). The created timer is automatically deleted from the framework at
parameters: Parameters function The event routine to be called up when the timer expires. The event routine receives the current time of the measurement in nanoseconds and the ID of the expired timer as parameters.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the timer. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function cclTimerCreate may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The function parameter is zero.

name: cclTimerSet
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclTimerSet(int32_t timerID, int64_t nanoseconds);
description: Description The timer is set with the specified time. After the time expires, the event routine assigned to the timer is called and the timer then becomes inactive.
parameters: Parameters timerID Timer ID (return value of the function cclTimerCreate ). nanoseconds The time span in nanoseconds which the timer is to run. Only positive time specifications are permissible.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The timer was set successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDTIMERID The transferred TimerID is invalid. CCL_INVALIDTIME The transferred time span is less than or equal to zero.

name: cclTimeSeconds
syntax: Function Syntax extern int64_t cclTimeSeconds(int64_t seconds); extern int64_t cclTimeMilliseconds(int64_t milliseconds); extern int64_t cclTimeMicroseconds(int64_t microseconds);
description: Description All three functions convert the transferred time into nanoseconds. Time stamps which are transferred from the framework to the C library typically have the nanosecond unit. The function for setting a timer ( cclTimerSet ) also requires the time specification in nanoseconds.
parameters: Parameters seconds Time in seconds. milliseconds Time in milliseconds. microseconds Time in microseconds.
returnValues: Return Values Time in nanoseconds.

name: cclUnannounceProvider
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclUnannounceProvider(cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Stops sending service announcements from a service provider.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS Service announcement was successfully stopped. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclValueBeginBulkUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclValueBeginBulkUpdate(cclValueID valueID);
description: Description Begins a bulk update of a complex value of a distributed object. Use this if you need to update several elements of a value with a complex data type (e.g., a struct or an array) to improve the performance. Each call to cclValueBeginBulkUpdate must be followed by a call to cclValueEndBulkUpdate . This function can not be used for communication object values or call context values.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the (complex) value, returned by cclValueGetID .
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS No error CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object. CCL_INVALIDDOMAIN The valueID parameter does not identify a distributed object value. CCL_VALUEREADONLY The valueID parameter identifies a value which is read-only.
example: Example cclValueID completeValue, member1, member2; completeValue = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", 0, cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); member1 = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", "member1", cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); member2 = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", "member2", cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); cclValueBeginBulkUpdate(completeValue); cclValueSetInteger(member1, 1); cclValueSetInteger(member2, 4); cclValueEndBulkUpdate(completeValue);

name: cclValueClear
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclValueClear(cclValueID valueID);
description: Description Invalidates a communication object value, i.e. sets an optional value to the invalid state.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the value, returned by cclValueGetID cclCallContextValueGetID cclConsumedServiceValueGetID cclProvidedServiceValueGetID cclPDUSenderSignalValueGetID cclPDUReceiverSignalValueGetID cclPDUProviderSignalValueGetID
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The value was read out successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object. CCL_VALUENOTOPTIONAL The value is not optional and therefore cannot be invalidated.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COSignal.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclValueEndBulkUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclValueEndBulkUpdate(cclValueID valueID);
description: Description Ends a bulk update of a complex value of a distributed object. Use this if you need to update several elements of a value with a complex data type (e.g., a struct or an array) to improve the performance. Each call to cclValueBeginBulkUpdate must be followed by a call to cclValueEndBulkUpdate . This function can not be used for communication object values or call context values.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the (complex) value, returned by cclValueGetID .
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS No error CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object. CCL_INVALIDDOMAIN The valueID parameter does not identify a distributed object value. CCL_VALUEREADONLY The valueID parameter identifies a value which is read-only.
example: Example cclValueID completeValue, member1, member2; completeValue = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", 0, cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); member1 = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", "member1", cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); member2 = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", "member2", cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); cclValueBeginBulkUpdate(completeValue); cclValueSetInteger(member1, 1); cclValueSetInteger(member2, 4); cclValueEndBulkUpdate(completeValue);

name: cclValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32 t cclValueID; cclValueID cclValueGetID(cclDomain domain, const char* path, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns a value ID of a communication object or distributed object, which can be used for setting and reading values. All value IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement. Communication object values can be signal ports, service state, connection state, latest function call or return parameters, PDU sender and receiver values, etc. Distributed object values can be member values, subscription state, announcement s
parameters: Parameters domain Use CCL_COMMUNICATION_OBJECTS for a communication object value and CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS for a distributed object value. path The path to a communication object port or endpoint or to a distributed object member. See Communication Object Paths . member The member identifies a sub-value of a complex type value or a specific value associated with the object. There are predefined constants CCL_MEMBER_... which (depending on the addressed value) can be concatenated with further s
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDDOMAIN The domain parameter must be one of the enumerated values in cclDomain. CCL_INVALIDPORTPATH The path parameter does not identi
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COSignal.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclValueSetHandler
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclValueHandler)(cclTime time, cclValueID valueID); int32_t cclValueSetHandler(cclValueID valueID, bool onUpdate, cclValueHandler handler);
description: Description Registers an event handler for a communication object value. Depending on the onUpdate flag the handler is called each time the value is updated or modified.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the value, returned by cclValueGetID cclCallContextValueGetID cclConsumedServiceValueGetID cclProvidedServiceValueGetID cclPDUSenderSignalValueGetID cclPDUReceiverSignalValueGetID cclPDUProviderSignalValueGetID onUpdate Controls whether the handler is called upon every value update or only when the value actually changes. handler The event routine to be called when the value is updated or modified. The event routine receives the current time of the measurement and the ID
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The value was read out successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object.
